## lattice-system

A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization project targeting general lattice models.
This project subsumes and generalizes the earlier
[ising-model](https://github.com/phasetr/ising-model) project, progressively
covering classical spin systems, quantum spin systems, Hubbard, BCS,
CAR algebras, and eventually lattice QCD.

## Design axis: graphs, not lattices

Despite the name, the **primary combinatorial abstraction in this
library is a graph `(Оӣ, E_Оӣ)`** вҖ” finite for finite-volume work and
infinite for the thermodynamic-limit / algebraic-formulation work
that is a major long-term goal вҖ” not "a lattice". Concrete lattices
(the 1D chain, square / cubic grids, infinite chains, в„Ө^d, вҖҰ) appear
only as specific instances such as `SimpleGraph.pathGraph`,
`SimpleGraph.cycleGraph`, products of these, or their infinite
analogues. This convention follows the standard mathematical-physics
literature on many-body systems on graphs (Lieb's theorem on
bipartite lattices, the MarshallвҖ“LiebвҖ“Mattis theorem, Miyao 2021
В§3, вҖҰ) and aligns the project with mathlib's `SimpleGraph`
foundations.

Finite-volume work uses `Оӣ : Type*` together with `[Fintype Оӣ]`
when needed (e.g. for traces, partition functions, finite sums of
local terms); infinite-volume work drops the `Fintype` assumption
and uses graphs over types like `в„Ө` or `в„Ө^d` instead.

The bridge from a `SimpleGraph` to the pairwise coupling
`J : Оӣ вҶ’ Оӣ вҶ’ в„Ӯ` consumed by `heisenbergHamiltonian` (and similar
operators) lives in `LatticeSystem.Lattice.Graph` (`couplingOf`).
Existing chain coupling families (`openChainCoupling`,
`periodicChainCoupling`) are characterised as
`couplingOf (pathGraph _) _` and `couplingOf (cycleGraph _) _`
respectively.

## Scope

| Area | Stage | Typical references |
|---|---|---|
| Classical spin systems | Inherited from ising-model | Friedli-Velenik, Glimm-Jaffe |
| Quantum spin systems | Current focus | Tasaki, Nielsen-Chuang (cross-check) |
| Hubbard / BCS | Medium term | Tasaki 1998, Bru-Pedra |
| CAR-algebraic formulation | Medium-long term | Araki-Moriya, Bru-Pedra |
| Thermodynamic limit (infinite graphs) | Long term, **major project goal** | Simon, Friedli-Velenik, Bratteli-Robinson |
| Lattice QCD | Longest term | Aarts, Davies |

## Refactoring conventions and review criteria

A **single-source-of-truth document** for refactoring conventions
applied as the review checklist on every pull request:
[Refactoring conventions and review criteria](refactoring-conventions.html).
Topics: test methods (decide / bridge / small-exhaustive / shim /
`#guard_msgs`), module-split criteria, generic / dedup conventions,
deprecation window policy, naming / docstring rules, linter
exceptions, public-doc synchronisation.

The companion page [Deprecation window](deprecations.html) tracks
currently-deprecated public declarations, the `since` date,
recommended replacement, and earliest-removal window.

Generic spin `S`, arbitrary bipartite-antiferromagnetic Heisenberg coupling supported on a connected bipartite graph, with the intermediate-existence hypothesis. The canonical proof witnesses are `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` (PR #3891, toy coupling) and `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_of_bipartiteCompletePositive` (PR #3893, general bipartite `J` positive on the complete bipartite graph). These declarations are the structurally repaired replacements for the older `h_intermediate` capstones removed in PR #3917. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 | Truly-unconditional closure at all `N вүҘ 1` (PR #3891 toy, PR #3893 general) |
| P1o (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, MattisвҖ“Nishimori anisotropic) | **Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4 (MattisвҖ“Nishimori)**: ground-state uniqueness + `ЕңВі_tot \|ОҰ_GSвҹ© = 0` for the anisotropic XXZ + single-ion Hamiltonian (2.5.14) on a connected bipartite lattice with `\|A\| = \|B\|`, under (i) `вҲ’1 < О» вүӨ 1, D вүҘ 0` or (ii) `О» вүҘ 1, D вүӨ 0`. The model is only U(1)-invariant (not SU(2)), so the proof uses even/odd-sector PerronвҖ“Frobenius (at most double degeneracy) + a deformation argument from the SU(2) point `(О»,D)=(1,0)` rather than MLM directly. Ој) = вҲ‘_p finrank в„Ӯ (eig M.submatrix_p Ој)` for parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `P`, (h.4) #3844 submatrix-based вүӨ 2 wrappers. **(i) chain: Hermitian spectral chain** (#3846вҖ“#3855): (i.1) #3846 submatrix Hermiticity, (i.2) #3847 submatrix eigenvalue realness (`Ој.im = 0`), (i.3) #3848 submatrix minimum eigenvalue exists, (i.4) #3849 Hermitian eigenspace `= вҠҘ` below min, (i.5) #3850 block-diag eigenspace `= вҠҘ` below joint per-block min, (i.6) #3851 full eig `вүӨ 2` at min(per-block mins) given per-block `вүӨ 1` at min, (i.7) #3854 bare `ДӨ'` specialisation, (i.8) #3855 bare anisotropic `ДӨ` specialisation via axis-swap (general `N` with `AxisSwapUnitaryS` + spin-1/2 instance) вҖ” the bridge to unconditional `вүӨ 2` modulo PF eigenvalue identification with `hermitianMinEigenvalue`. **(j) chain: PF eigenvalue identification** (#3857вҖ“#3861): (j.1) #3857 PF positive eigenvector exists for unshifted dressed submatrix, (j.2) #3858 lift to complex, (j.3) #3859 `hermitianMinEigenvalue вүӨ Ој` from eigenvector existence, (j.4) #3860 packaged form, (j.5) #3861 consumer-friendly conditional bound at `hermitianMinEigenvalue` (needs the `ОҪ_PF = hermitianMinEigenvalue` hypothesis explicit). **Target balanced-sector PF discharge** (Issue #3739): off-diagonal anisotropic/Heisenberg agreement, real sector matrices, the Marshall-dressed shifted anisotropic sector matrix, structural irreducibility, and CollatzвҖ“Wielandt PF/minimum identification now provide the balanced-sector `finrank в„Ӯ вүӨ 1` input at the spin-1/2 target point and in the general spin-S target sector; the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` callback. **Strict-gap, SU(2)-uniqueness, and MLM endpoint wrappers** now replace the explicit balanced/full equality input by the standard strict gap over all non-balanced non-empty magnetization sectors, derive that strict gap from SU(2)-endpoint global uniqueness, and then construct the SU(2) uniqueness input from the general Theorem 2.3 MLM/Casimir/PF endpoint. **D-boundary extension**: the spin-1/2 parity-block route has a dedicated `D вүҘ 0` version because single-ion `Вұ2` moves are impossible for `Fin (1+1)`. The general spin-S route now also has a bond-only parity reachability replacement for the single-ion branch, giving general `D вүҘ 0` parity-block irreducibility and the MLM/Casimir target wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general`. **О»=1 boundary**: at spin `1/2`, `(S^3)^2 = (1/4)I`, so `singleIonAnisotropyS D 1` is a scalar shift and `anisotropicHeisenbergS J 1 D 1` reduces to the Heisenberg Hamiltonian plus `D|Оӣ|/4`; the SU(2) Theorem 2.3/MLM uniqueness transfers to target uniqueness and zero magnetization at `О» = 1`. For general spin-S, the SU(2)-point wrappers cover `О» = 1`, `D = 0`, and for `2 вүӨ N` the ion-only parity reachability route gives the `О» = 1`, `D > 0` endpoint wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general`. **Case (ii) bridges**: the case-(ii) path-region lemmas show the deformation path stays in `О» вүҘ 1`, `D вүӨ 0`; the first case-(ii) target wrappers reuse the balanced-sector PF bridge while leaving balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2` explicit; the strict-gap wrappers replace the direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input by the strict sector gap; the no-full-finrank wrappers then use sector projection and Hermitian below-min exclusion to remove the full `finrank <= 2` input once strict gap is supplied; the crossing-callback bridge compresses the remaining strict-gap derivation to a single target crossing contradiction callback; the path-callback bridge reduces that target callback to a contradiction for crossings along the case-(ii) path; the crossing-set bridge reduces the path callback to contradiction for non-empty `perMCrossingSet M вҲ© Icc 0 1`; the first-crossing bridge reduces crossing-set non-emptiness to contradiction at `sInf (perMCrossingSet M вҲ© Icc 0 1)`; the argmin-first-crossing bridge reduces the target contradiction to the sector whose first crossing minimises those `sInf` values; the first-crossing finrank bridge proves that argmin callback from a `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first crossing; the path-global finrank bridge moves this to every `t вҲҲ Icc 0 1`; the block-path finrank bridge reduces it to full-ground-energy parity-block submatrix simplicity; and the block-PF/min bridge reduces that to pathwise bare parity-block PF simplicity plus PF/min identification; and the parity-gauge sign layer introduces the case-(ii) block gauge needed to flip exactly the Вұ2 parity-bond and single-ion moves; and the local sign layer proves strict shifted-entry positivity for transverse, parity-bond, and single-ion elementary moves; and the block reachability, block non-negativity, step-support, raw support-classification, and boundary move-set bridges reduce parity-block irreducibility to conditional irreducibility under the bipartite support-zero assumption on `J`. | The public general spin-S `2 вүӨ N` parameter-region wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` package the general spin-S surface. The spin-`1/2` wrappers `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` and `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` now package the exact `N = 1` surface: `-1 < О» < 1`, `D вүҘ 0`; `О» = 1`, using the scalar-shift boundary; and strict case (ii) `1 < О»`, `D вүӨ 0`. |

interaction reuses `fermionTotalSpinPlus_commute_hubbardDoubleOccupancy`; `Ең^-` by adjoint) вҮ’ `ДӨ(Ең^-_tot)^k|ОҰОұвҹ©=0` вҖ” all K+2 multiplet members are zero-energy; **PSD + ground states (`TasakiFlatBandPosSemidef.lean`)**: `ДӨвүҘ0` (`flatBandHamiltonian_posSemidef`, `t,UвүҘ0`) as a nonnegative combination of PSD terms (`bМӮвҖ bМӮ=(bМӮ)бҙҙbМӮ`; `nМӮвҶ‘nМӮвҶ“` Hermitian-idempotent projection), so `rayleighOnVec ДӨ ПҲвүҘ0` everywhere while the tower attains `0` вҮ’ `flatBand_alphaTower_isGroundState`: each `(Ең^-_tot)^k|ОҰОұвҹ©` minimizes the energy. **The existence half of Theorem 11.11 is complete**: a `(2S_max+1)`-dim maximal-spin degenerate ground-state multiplet, `S_max=(K+1)/2`; **frustration-free conditions (`TasakiFlatBandFrustrationFree.lean`, toward uniqueness)**: any flat-band ground state `v` (`rayleighOnVec ДӨ v=0`, `t,U>0`) satisfies `bМӮ_{u,Пғ}v=0` (eq. 11.3.11) and `nМӮ_{xвҶ‘}nМӮ_{xвҶ“}v=0` (no-double-occupancy form of 11.3.12), since `ДӨ` is a sum of PSD terms each of which must annihilate a zero-energy state; **number conservation (`TasakiFlatBandNumberConservation.lean`)**: `[ДӨ,NМӮ]=0` (`flatBandHamiltonian_commute_fermionTotalNumber`; `bМӮ` lowers / `bМӮвҖ ` raises `NМӮ` by one), so ground states split into fixed-`N` sectors; and any ground state lies in the Hubbard hard-core subspace (`flatBand_groundState_mem_hardcoreSubspace`); **uniqueness framework subspaces (`TasakiFlatBandSubspaces.lean`)**: `flatBandAlphaFockSubmodule` (span of Оұ-Slater states, contains `|ОҰОұвҹ©`) and `flatBandBKernelSubmodule = вЁ…_{u,Пғ} ker bМӮ_{u,Пғ}` (contains every ground state, from 11.3.11) вҖ” Tasaki's uniqueness is the inclusion `BKernel вҠҶ ОұFock` + symmetric/maximal-spin classification; **rotated-basis Fock-spanning infrastructure toward it (`TasakiFlatBandModeCreation.lean`, `TasakiFlatBandModeMonomial.lean`, `Math/ListProdMulVec.lean`, Issue #4346)**: the single-particle-mode creation/annihilation maps `ДҲвҖ _Пғ(w)`/`ДҲ_Пғ(w)` (`flatBandModeCreation`/`flatBandModeAnnihilation`, with ГўвҖ /bМӮвҖ /Гў/bМӮ as values at Оұ/ОІ), the operator-level single-particle change of basis (`flatBandModeCreation_eq_repr_sum`), the generic single-particle CAR `{ДҲ_Пғ(w),ДҲвҖ _П„(w')}=(вҲ‘_x w(x)w'(x))Оҙ_ПғП„В·1` (`flatBandMode_annihilation_creation_anticomm`, giving the ОІ-Gram and `{bМӮ,ГўвҖ }=0`), and `flatBandModeFockSubmodule_eq_top` вҖ” the rotated-basis Fock monomials `(вҲҸ ДҲвҖ _Пғ(basis i))|vacвҹ©` span the whole space (every `basisVec c` is an ordered site-creation product on the vacuum, and the span is invariant under each site creation), reindexed by occupation configs (card `2^(4K+4)=finrank`) into the **rotated occupation basis** `flatBandOccBasis`; **the hard inclusion `BKernel вҠҶ AlphaFock` is now PROVED** (`flatBandBKernelSubmodule_le_alphaFockSubmodule`, `TasakiFlatBandUniqueness.lean`, axiom-clean): the ОІ-Gram is invertible (PosDef, `flatBandBetaGram`), its inverse gives dual annihilators `d_{u,Пғ}=вҲ‘_v(GвҒ»В№)_{uv}bМӮ_{v,Пғ}` with `{d,bМӮвҖ }=Оҙ`, `{d,ГўвҖ }=0`, `d|vacвҹ©=0`; the projector `bМӮвҖ _{u,Пғ}d_{u,Пғ}` kills a bМӮ-kernel vector (since `d v=0`), forcing every ОІ-occupied occupation-basis coordinate to vanish, so the vector is a combination of ОІ-free occ monomials (= Оұ-Slater states) вҲҲ Оұ-Fock; with the easy inclusion, `BKernel = AlphaFock`; **dimension-route reduction of the uniqueness `вүӨ` half (`TasakiFlatBandClassification.lean`, Issue #4346)**: `finrank(multiplet)=K+2`, `[Ең^z_tot,ДӨ_flat]=0` (`fermionTotalSpinZ_commute_flatBandHamiltonian`, from `[Ең^Вұ,ДӨ]=0` + su(2) `ЕңвҒәЕңвҒ»вҲ’ЕңвҒ»ЕңвҒә=2Ең^z`), `Ең^z` preserves the ground subspace, the finite `Ең^z`-weight decomposition `G=вЁҶ_{a:Fin(K+2)} GвҠ“eigenspace(Ең^z, aвҲ’(K+1)/2)` (`flatBandHalfFilledGroundSubmodule_eq_iSup_weight`, off-weight blocks `вҠҘ` since `NМӮ=K+1` fixes the half-integer weights), and the capstone `flatBand_groundSubmodule_eq_multipletSpan_of_blocks` вҖ” IF each `Ең^z`-weight block of `G` is `вүӨ1`-dimensional THEN `G=multiplet` (via `Math/EigenspaceWeightFinrank.finrank_le_of_weight_blocks` + `Submodule.eq_of_le_of_finrank_le`, using the proven `multiplet вүӨ G`); the residual `finrank(block)вүӨ1` (symmetric/maximal-spin core) and removal of the axiom are subsequent steps; **easy inclusion (`TasakiFlatBandAlphaFockKernel.lean`)**: `ОұFock вүӨ BKernel` (`flatBandAlphaFockSubmodule_le_BKernelSubmodule`) вҖ” every Оұ-Slater state is annihilated by all `bМӮ` via the 11.3.7 anticommutation + move-through; the hard reverse `BKernel вҠҶ ОұFock` needs the non-orthogonal-basis Fock factorisation, deferred; **Theorem 11.11 CAPSTONE (`TasakiFlatBandTheorem11_11.lean`)**: `flatBand_theorem_11_11_groundSubmodule_eq_multipletSpan` вҖ” the half-filled (`N_e=K+1`) zero-energy ground subspace `ker ДӨ вҠ“ eigenspace(NМӮ,K+1)` equals the ferromagnetic multiplet span (`2S_max+1`-dim, maximal spin), + maximal-spin corollary; `вүҘ` (existence) fully proven, `вүӨ` (uniqueness) uses a **single documented `axiom`** (Tasaki App. A classification, deferred like Theorem 11.8; existence half is axiom-free); **В§11.3.2 Mielke's flat-band ferromagnetism (`MielkeHamiltonian.lean`, Issue #4177)**: `mielkeHamiltonian` on a graph (uniform hopping + `2tВ·NМӮ` shift + `U`) with Hermiticity, `[ДӨ,NМӮ]=0`, SU(2) invariance вҖ” model setup; the line-graph structure + Theorems 11.12 (flat-band) / 11.13 (Mielke ferromagnetism) (`MielkeTheorems.lean`): `mielkeFlatBandDim` D(ОӣМғ,BМғ), `mielkeSingleElectronOp`, line graph via mathlib `SimpleGraph.lineGraph` + `SimpleGraph.Iso`; **`mielke_theorem_11_12`** (flat-band, connected base: single-electron kernel finrank = D) and **`mielke_theorem_11_13`** (Mielke ferromagnetism: biconnected base, N=D вҮ’ ground subspace finrank = N+1 + all maximal spin) were documented **axioms**; **Theorem 11.12 is now PROVEN** (В§11.3.3, see below), leaving only **`mielke_theorem_11_13`** as an axiom (Tasaki states it without proof; the Hamiltonian model + symmetries are axiom-free); **В§11.3.3 incidence-matrix factorisation (`MielkeIncidenceMatrix.lean`, Issue #4180, DISCHARGES the 11.12 axiom)**: `mielkeSingleElectronOpOn` (single-electron operator generalised to an arbitrary finite vertex type; `mielkeSingleElectronOp` is now the `Fin (M+1)` wrapper), `mielkeIncidence` (`S = вҲҡtВ·` mathlib `incMatrix` restricted to genuine edges, eq. (11.3.36)), and **`mielkeIncidence_conjTranspose_mul_self`**: `SбҙҙS = mielkeSingleElectronOpOn (lineGraph G) t` for `t вүҘ 0` (eqs. (11.3.36)вҖ“(11.3.39)) вҖ” the line-graph operator presented as a PSD Gram matrix with diagonal `2t` (edge degree 2) and off-diagonal `t` on shared-vertex edge pairs, kernel `= ker S`; the algebraic core for the rankвҖ“nullity + bipartite zero-mode count (11.3.41) discharging Theorem 11.12, sorry-free; **rank step (PR3)**: `mielke_lineGraph_ker_finrank_eq` вҖ” for `tвүҘ0`, `dim ker T = |B| вҲ’ rank S` (via mathlib `ker_mulVecLin_conjTranspose_mul_self` giving `ker SбҙҙS = ker S` + rankвҖ“nullity), the Lemma 11.14 rank form; **zero-mode count + dim (PR4)**: `mielke_conjTranspose_ker_finrank` вҖ” for `t>0` and a **connected** base, `dim ker(SSбҙҙ) = dim ker Sбҙҙ = (Colorable 2 ? 1 : 0)`, proved directly via `ker Sбҙҙ` (`(Sбҙҙx)_b = вҲҡt(x_u+x_v)`; bipartite вҮ’ 1-dim span of the alternating Вұ1 colouring vector, non-bipartite вҮ’ trivial since a nonzero kernel vector would 2-colour the graph); and `mielke_lineGraph_ker_finrank_eq_dim` вҖ” the assembled flat-band dimension `D = |B| вҲ’ (|ОӣМғ| вҲ’ bip)` (Theorem 11.12 general-base form; inner subtraction keeps it sound under в„• truncation, = Tasaki's `|B|вҲ’|ОӣМғ|+1` once `|ОӣМғ|вүӨ|B|`). **`mielke_theorem_11_12` (PR5, capstone)** вҖ” **now a proved theorem** (formerly the В§11.3.2 axiom): transports the flat-band dimension to the `Fin (M+1)` line-graph realisation along the `SimpleGraph.Iso` via `Matrix.rank_submatrix` (rank/kernel-dim invariance under equiv-reindex), with hypothesis `|ОӣМғ| вүӨ |B|` (base has a cycle вҖ” exactly where Tasaki's `|B|вҲ’|ОӣМғ|+1` is the true dimension). Issue #4180 CLOSED); **В§11.3.4 Theorem 11.15 (general flat-band ferromagnetism, PROVED axiom-free, `GeneralFlatBandTheorem1115.lean`, Issue #4453)**: Mielke's general nec-&-suf condition вҖ” for a Hermitian PSD hopping matrix `T`, flat band `hвӮҖ=ker T`, `DвӮҖ=dim hвӮҖ>0`, `U>0`, filling `N=DвӮҖ`, the model is saturated-ferromagnetic **iff** the `ОӣвӮҖГ—ОӣвӮҖ` projection submatrix `(PвӮҖ)` is irreducible (`ОӣвӮҖ={x|(PвӮҖ)_{x,x}вү 0}`). `PвӮҖ` = orthogonal projection onto `ker T` via `Matrix.toEuclideanLin`вҶ’`Submodule.starProjection`вҶ’`LinearMap.toMatrixOrthonormal`; irreducibility via `Matrix.IsIrreducible` of the real `Complex.normSq(PвӮҖ)` support matrix; ferromagnetism via the `mielke_theorem_11_13` ground-subspace pattern. `theorem tasaki_theorem_11_15` вҖ” DISCHARGED Ој_w(w), Ој_w(w)вү 0 вҹ№ all c=0): the engine of the cut/block argument for the irreducibilityвҶ”connectivity bridge (Codex-validated route, avoids the Gram inverse) (PR #4459). (PвӮҖ)_{xy}вү 0 вҹ№ both x,y active) вҖ” the support graph lives on ОӣвӮҖ (PR #4461). `generalFlatBand_mu_orthogonal_of_disjoint_support` (Ој_z вҠҘ Ој_{z'} when their site supports are disjoint вҖ” inner = ОЈ_x conj Ој_z(x)В·Ој_{z'}(x), each term 0): makes the per-side flat-band subspaces of a basis cut orthogonal (PR #4462). `generalFlatBand_no_shared_site_of_saturated` (for a basis-adjacency-closed J вҠҶ I, no site is covered by both a J-index and an (IвҲ–J)-index вҖ” a shared site would be a basis edge): the active-site side-assignment of a basis cut is well-defined (PR #4463). `generalFlatBand_basisVec_mem_orthogonal_of_side` (if every Ој_z (zвҲҲS) vanishes at x, then e_x вҠҘ span{Ој_z:zвҲҲS}, via span_induction + вҹӘОј_z,e_xвҹ«=conj Ој_z(x)=0): places PвӮҖ e_x in and its reflexive predual basis is the localised `{Ој_z}` (`generalFlatBand_exists_special_index` + `Module.Basis.dualBasis_apply_self`/`apply_evalEquiv_symm_apply`); **Theorem 11.17** (`generalFlatBand_theorem_11_17`: ferromagnetic вҮ” the special basis is connected, via `generalFlatBandBasisGraph`) **PROVED (axiom DISCHARGED, Issue #4363)** вҖ” Mielke's second nec-&-suf condition. Proof discharge of 11.15 is later work; **Theorem 11.17 discharge STARTED (Issue #4363, PR1 foundation, `GeneralFlatBandOperators.lean` + `GeneralFlatBandGroundAnnihilation.lean`, the latter split out in a build-speed refactor): general-flat-band smeared creation/annihilation operators `ГўвҖ _{z,Пғ} := ДҲвҖ _Пғ(Ој_z)` over an arbitrary special basis, full CAR algebra (unified, bilinear `{ДҲ_Пғ(ПҶ), ДҲвҖ _П„(ПҲ)} = Оҙ_{ПғП„}(ОЈПҶПҲ)В·1`, site-dual), Slater states + ГўвҖ -monomial Fock submodule, single + double site-annihilation peel (eq. 11.3.48 engine), `generalCDownUp` + double-occupancy Gram identity, kinetic operator PSD from hopping PSD (`T = CбҙҙC` Gram-sum), Rayleigh decomposition, no-double-occupancy kill, ground state annihilated by every range-T mode (precise `conj(range T)` / `ДҲ_Пғ(star(TВ·w))` and nonzero-eigenvalue-eigenvector `ДҲ_Пғ(Е«)` forms вҖ” the exact orthonormal-eigenbasis occupation-detection operators), and the spectral `ker T вҠ• range T` classification + completeness `exists_ker_add_range_decomp` вҖ” the operator-algebraic premise of eq. 11.3.46. **Theorem 11.17 PR2 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandModeMonomial.lean` + `GeneralFlatBandOccBasis.lean`): general occupation basis over an arbitrary single-particle basis** вҖ” bilinear creationвҖ“creation anticommutation `{ДҲвҖ _Пғ(ПҶ),ДҲвҖ _П„(ПҲ)}=0` + nilpotency `(ДҲвҖ )ВІ=0`; general-basis Fock monomials `ДҲвҖ _Пғ(e i)`-products on the vacuum spanning `вҠӨ` (mode + site creation invariance via the basis `repr`, computational-basis-vector expansion); reorder/permutation-scaling + repeated-mode vanishing; occupation monomials + `generalOccBasis` (a basis of the full Fock space indexed by occupation configs of the `2(M+1)` mode-spin slots, `2^(2(M+1))=finrank`). The Theorem 11.11 occupation-basis machinery, re-developed generically in the basis for instantiation at the spectral eigenbasis of `T`. **Theorem 11.17 PR3 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandEigenbasis.lean`): spectral-eigenbasis annihilation peel** вҖ” the orthonormal eigenbasis `T.IsHermitian.eigenvectorBasis` transported to a `Module.Basis` of `Fin(M+1)вҶ’в„Ӯ` (`eigenbasisAsBasis`), its coordinate orthonormality `ОЈ_x conj(e_j(x))e_k(x)=Оҙ_{jk}`, the dual CAR `{ДҲ_Пғ(Д“_j),ДҲвҖ _П„(e_k)}=Оҙ_{jk}Оҙ_{ПғП„}В·1`, the smeared-annihilator vacuum kill `ДҲ_Пғ(ПҶ)|vacвҹ©=0`, and the eigenbasis-annihilation peel `ДҲ_Пғ(Д“_j)|qsвҹ© = ОЈбөў (-1)^iВ·Оҙ_{j,(qs[i]).1}Оҙ_{Пғ,(qs[i]).2}В·|qsвҲ–iвҹ©` (the occupation-detection engine). **Theorem 11.17 PR4 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpanning.lean`): eq. (11.3.46) Fock spanning (hard direction)** вҖ” the eigenvalue equation for the transported eigenbasis, the ground-state annihilation `ДҲ_Пғ(Д“_j)ОҰ=0` for every nonzero-eigenvalue mode, the eigenmode number operator `nМӮ_{j,Пғ}=ДҲвҖ _Пғ(e_j)ДҲ_Пғ(Д“_j)` with its creation commutation `[nМӮ,ДҲвҖ _П„(e_k)]=Оҙ_{jk}Оҙ_{ПғП„}ДҲвҖ _П„(e_k)`, its diagonality in the occupation basis `nМӮ_{j,Пғ}occMon(g)=g(j,Пғ)В·occMon(g)` (list induction), the **coefficient vanishing** (ground-state occupation-basis coefficient = 0 on any config occupying a nonzero-eigenvalue mode), and the capstone **`flatBand_groundState_mem_flatFockSpan`**: a flat-band ground state lies in the span of the flat-band-supported (zero-eigenvalue) occupation monomials (`IsFlatSupported`). **Theorem 11.17 PR5 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandFilling.lean`): filling-refined eq. (11.3.46)** вҖ” the total fermion number operator is diagonal in the occupation basis (`NМӮ occMon(g)=|occupied(g)|В·occMon(g)`, list induction via `[NМӮ,ДҲвҖ (w)]=ДҲвҖ (w)`), so a `DвӮҖ`-electron ground state has occupation-basis coefficients supported on the `DвӮҖ`-electron configs; combined with the flat-band coefficient vanishing this gives `flatBand_groundState_atFilling_mem_flatFockSpan` (flat-band ground state вҲҲ span of flat-supported occupation monomials with exactly `DвӮҖ` occupied modes). **Theorem 11.17 PR6 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandMuTransport.lean`): transport of eq. (11.3.46) to the special basis** вҖ” the coordinate identity `ker T = span{Ој_z}` (finrank transport via WithLp), the invariance of the `Ој`-Slater Fock submodule `generalFlatBandFockSubmodule Ој` under `ДҲвҖ _Пғ(w)` for `w вҲҲ span{Ој_z}`, and the capstone `flatBand_groundState_mem_generalFlatBandFockSubmodule`: a flat-band ground state at filling lies in the `Ој`-Slater Fock submodule of the special basis (since the flat-supported eigenbasis monomials use only flat eigenvectors, each вҲҲ `ker T = span{Ој_z}`). **Theorem 11.17 PR7 in progress (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpinConfig.lean`): index-mode number-operator machinery toward eq. (11.3.47)** вҖ” the site-dual CAR localized on the index set I (`{Дү_{z,Пғ},ГўвҖ _{Ој_{z'},П„}}=Оҙ_{ПғП„}Оҙ_{zz'}Ој_z(z)`), the index-mode number operator `nМӮ^Ој_{z,Пғ}=ГўвҖ _{Ој_z,Пғ}Дү_{z,Пғ}` with its creation commutation, its diagonality in the Ој-Slater states (`nМӮ^Ој_{z,Пғ}|qsвҹ©=Ој_z(z)В·countВ·|qsвҹ©`, list induction), and the **no double occupancy of index modes** `nМӮ^Ој_{z,вҶ‘}nМӮ^Ој_{z,вҶ“}ОҰ=0` (operator identity = `ГўвҖ ГўвҖ В·generalCDownUp` + `Дү_вҶ“Дү_вҶ‘ОҰ=0`). **Theorem 11.17 PR8 in progress (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpinRep.lean`): tight `I`-mode transport toward eq. (11.3.47)** вҖ” the special basis `{Ој_z}` extends to a full `Fin(M+1)`-basis `eОј` (`exists_extended_special_basis`); an `I`-mode `Ој`-Slater state equals a mode monomial over `eОј` (`generalFlatBandSlaterState_eq_generalModeMonomial`, carrying them into `generalOccBasis eОј` for linear independence); the tight `I`-mode `Ој`-Slater Fock submodule (`generalFlatBandIModeFockSubmodule`) is invariant under index-mode creation, and a flat-band ground state lies in it (`flatBand_groundState_mem_imode`, resolving the `zвҲүI` subtlety). Remaining: coefficient extraction (PR7 no-double-occ + PR5 filling вҹ№ one spin per index, eq. 11.3.47) + sign propagation (11.3.49) + connectivity capstone); **Theorem 11.17 PR9 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpinConfigRepCore.lean` for the index-support / coefficient-extraction machinery [`IsIdxSupported`, `flatBandSpecialIdxInv*`, coefficient-vanishing facts] + `GeneralFlatBandSpinConfigRep.lean` for the assembled representation theorems, split for build speed): eq. (11.3.47) spin-configuration capstone** вҖ” a left inverse `idxInv` of the extended-basis index map on `I` (`flatBandSpecialIdxInv`); the bridge `occMon_eОј g = О _{zвҲҲI} ГўвҖ _{Ој_z,Пғ}` for `idx(I)`-supported `g` (`generalOccMonomial_eq_generalFlatBandSlaterState_of_idxSupported` + preimage-list count); the double index-mode number operator diagonal on supported `occMon_eОј` (eigenvalue `Ој_z(z)ВІ[g(idx z,вҶ‘)][g(idx z,вҶ“)]`); the three coefficient-vanishing facts for `ОҰ`'s `generalOccBasis eОј`-representation вҖ” support (`flatBand_groundState_eОј_repr_eq_zero_of_not_idxSupported`, via `Basis.repr_support_subset_of_mem_span`), no double occupancy (`flatBand_groundState_eОј_repr_eq_zero_of_doublyOccupied`), filling (general-basis `generalOccBasis_repr_eq_zero_of_card_ne`); assembled into `flatBand_groundState_mem_spinConfigSpan` (`ОҰ вҲҲ span` of the one-spin-per-index `Ој`-Slater states вҖ” eq. mirrors `tJ_halfFilling_isMaximalSpinMultiplet` (PR #4443). `hubbardKinetic_mulVec_spinConfigSlater_eq_zero` (kinetic kill for ANY spin config) + `generalCDownUp_mulVec_spinSeparatedSlater_eq_zero` (Дү_{xвҶ“}Дү_{xвҶ‘} kills a spin-separated Slater, double-peel + separation) вҹ№ `generalFlatBandSlaterState_spinSeparated_mem_groundSubmodule`: a spin-separated Ој-Slater (opposite-spin modes with disjoint site support) is a ground state вҖ” the component-colouring seed of the вҹ№ direction (PR #4444). `exists_disconnection_cut_of_not_connected` (В¬connected вҹ№ a non-trivial cut (A, Aб¶ң) of the index set with no crossing Ој-overlap, A = a's connected component) + `disconnection_cut_card_lt` ((|A|+1)(|Aб¶ң|+1) > DвӮҖ+1) вҖ” the graph setup for the вҹ№ direction's `finrank > DвӮҖ+1` contradiction (PR #4445). `cDownUp_canonicalSlaterSum_repr_twoHole_eq_zero_of_edgeSwap_invariant` (the reverse of eq. (11.3.49)): for an edge-swap-invariant D, the D-weighted sum of Дү_{xвҶ“}Дү_{xвҶ‘}-coordinates at the (a,b)-emptied target vanishes вҖ” via `cDownUp_canonicalSum_eq_two_terms` collapse + coordinate negation, either a,b connect at x (edge вҹ№ D(Пғ)=D(Пғ') cancel) or the Ој_a(x)Ој_b(x) factor vanishes (PR #4446). `cDownUp_canonicalSlaterSum_repr_eq_zero_of_not_twoHoleTarget` (the off-target companion): at a g that is NOT a (DвӮҖ-2)-emptied two-hole config, the D-weighted Дү_{xвҶ“}Дү_{xвҶ‘}-coordinate sum vanishes вҖ” each inner rest-Slater coordinate is a Kronecker delta (`generalFlatBandSlaterState_over_I_repr`) at `idxConfigOf` of the twice-erased canonical list (`flatBandSpinConfigList_eraseIdx_eraseIdx`), which g matches for no pair (`hnot`) (PR #4447). `generalCDownUp_mulVec_canonicalSlaterSum_eq_zero_of_edgeSwap_invariant` (interaction-kill main: вҲҖg coordinate dispatch L1/L2 via `generalOccBasis` injectivity, Пғ-modified witness for Пғa=0/Пғb=1) + `canonicalSlaterSum_mem_groundSubmodule_of_edgeSwap_invariant`: **an edge-swap-invariant canonical-Slater sum is a ground state** (kinetic per-Slater + interaction kill + NМӮ=DвӮҖ) вҖ” the converse of the eq. (11.3.49) characterization, placing per-block weight states into ground (PR #4448). `upCountOn_comp_swap_eq` (in-block transposition preserves a block's up-count) + `generalFlatBand_blockWeightState_mem_groundSubmodule`: the per-block fiber sum W_{p,q} = ОЈ_{upCount A=p, upCount Aб¶ң=q} Slater(extend s) is a ground state, since its indicator coefficient is edge-swap-invariant (basis-graph edges lie within one block, so swaps preserve both block up-counts) (PR #4449). `exists_blockUpCount_config` (a config with prescribed per-block up-counts) + `generalFlatBand_disconnected_finrank_gt`: **В¬connected вҹ№ finrank ground > DвӮҖ+1** вҖ” the (|A|+1)(|Aб¶ң|+1) weight states W_{p,q} are linearly independent (at a fiber representative's index config, only its W has a nonzero occupation coordinate, via `idxConfigOf_flatBandSpinConfigList_inj_gen`), so finrank вүҘ (|A|+1)(|Aб¶ң|+1) > DвӮҖ+1; the contradiction with the multiplet's finrank=DвӮҖ+1 (PR #4450). **`generalFlatBand_theorem_11_17` (CAPSTONE, AXIOM DISCHARGED)**: `generalFlatBandFerromagnetic T U вҶ” generalFlatBandBasisConnected I Ој` вҖ” вҮҗ via `generalFlatBand_connected_isMaximalSpinMultiplet`, вҮ’ via the `В¬connected вҹ№ finrank > DвӮҖ+1` contrapositive; the В§11.3.4 Theorem 11.17 axiom is now a proved theorem depending only on `propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound` (PR #4451). Remaining: (вҹ№) direction вҖ” ferromagnetic вҹ№ connected (contrapositive: disconnected вҹ№ finrank > DвӮҖ+1 via per-component product structure) + final iff capstone вҹ№ discharge axiom `generalFlatBand_theorem_11_17`. **REFACTOR (PR #4387): split `GeneralFlatBandSignPropagation.lean` (510вҶ’218 lines) вҖ” the reorder/extraction machinery (Slater swap/perm, canonical list, head/two-head/swap extraction, move-pair-front, per-pair extraction, canonical-Slater D-coefficient expansion; PR16-22) moved into the new `GeneralFlatBandSlaterReorder.lean` (~312 lines), for build speed (independent rebuilds).** **В§11.4 sector minimum energy + ferromagnetism criterion (`SectorMinEnergy.lean`, eq. (11.4.26), Issue #4189)**: `sectorMinEnergy H filling twoS` = `E_min(S)` (minimum energy in the fixed-particle-number-`filling`, total-spin-`S=twoS/2` sector вҖ” `вЁ… rayleighOnVec H` over unit `EuclideanSpace` vectors with `NМӮ_tot ОҰ=filling ОҰ` and `(Ең_tot)ВІОҰ=(twoS/2)(twoS/2+1)ОҰ`) and `exhibitsFerromagnetism H filling twoSmax` (`вҲҖ twoS<twoSmax, E_min(Smax)<E_min(twoS)` at fixed `filling`) вҖ” the precise definition of "exhibits ferromagnetism" used throughout Ch. 11, foundation for the non-singular Hubbard Theorems 11.18вҖ“11.20 (axiomatize-first; later PRs)); **non-singular Hubbard model (`NonsingularHubbardModel.lean`, eq. (11.4.23))**: `nonsingularHubbardHamiltonian = flatBandHamiltonian + (О¶:в„Ӯ)вҖўhubbardKinetic` (flat-band model perturbed by `О¶ ОЈ t_xy ДүвҖ Дү`; `О¶=0` recovers the flat band), with proven Hermiticity, `[ДӨ,NМӮ]=0`, SU(2) invariance `[Ең^Вұ_tot,ДӨ]=0` вҖ” axiom-free model setup for Theorems 11.18вҖ“11.20); **В§11.4 Theorem 11.18 (local stability, AXIOMATIZED, `NonsingularLocalStability.lean`)**: `IsNonsingularHopping` (cyclic translation-invariance (11.4.24) + range-`R` summability (11.4.25) of the perturbation) + `axiom nonsingular_theorem_11_18` вҖ” `вҲғ ОҪвӮҖ,О·вӮҖ,ОҫвӮҖ>0` (uniform in `K`, dep. `d=1,R`) s.t. under the parameter bounds the maximal-spin sector lies below the once-flipped one, `E_min(Smax) < E_min(SmaxвҲ’1)` (`sectorMinEnergy H (K+1) < H (KвҲ’1)`); stability against a single spin flip (eq. (11.4.29)), deferred); **В§11.4.1 Wannier-perturbation example model (`WannierExampleModel.lean`, eq. (11.4.1))**: В§11.4.1 is heuristic (no numbered theorems); its example model вҖ” the 1D flat-band model with the internal on-site potential shifted by `Оі` вҖ” is `wannierExampleModel = nonsingularHubbardHamiltonian K ОҪ t (ОіВ·t) (internal-site indicator) U` (a non-singular instance), with `wannierExampleModel_gamma_zero`: `Оі=0 вҮ’ = flatBandHamiltonian` (eq. (11.4.1)вҶ’(11.3.22)); axiom-free); **В§11.4.2 lattice translation (`TranslationOperator.lean`, towards Theorem 11.19)**: `modeSiteSpinEquiv` (mode `2p+Пғ вҶ” (site p, spin Пғ)`) + `siteTranslationPerm K z` (the `Equiv.Perm` shifting the physical site by `2z` cyclically вҖ” the combinatorial datum for the fermionic `П„МӮ_z` of eq. (11.4.30)), `siteTranslationPerm_zero`; step 1 toward the spin-wave bounds (axiom-free); **fermionic translation operator (`FermionicTranslation.lean`, eq. (11.4.30), step 2)**: `translationJwSign ПҖ Пғ = (-1)^(occupied inversions of ПҖ)` (JW fermion sign, `Вұ1`) + `translationOperator K z` (signed permutation operator) + `translationOperator_mulVec_basisVec` (the defining action `П„МӮ_z|Пғвҹ©=Оө(ПҖ,Пғ)|ПғвҲҳПҖвҒ»В№вҹ©`) + `translationOperator_zero` (`z=0вҮ’1`); axiom-free, `E_SW(k)` + axiom 11.19 to follow); **В§11.4.2 Theorem 11.19 (spin-wave bounds, AXIOMATIZED, `SpinWaveExcitation.lean`)**: `momentumPhase K k = e^{-2ПҖik/(K+1)}`, `spinWaveEnergy K H k` = `E_SW(k)` (min energy in `Ең^z=S_maxвҲ’1` вҲ© `П„МӮ`-momentum-`k` eigenspace) + `axiom nonsingular_theorem_11_19` вҖ” `вҲғ ОҪвӮҒ,О·вӮҒ,ОҫвӮҒ,ОҫвӮӮ,aвӮҒ..bвӮғ>0` s.t. under (11.4.31)/(11.4.32) the dispersion `E_SW(k)вҲ’E_min(S_max)` is two-sided bounded by `FВ·2ОҪвҒҙU(1вҲ’cos k)` (eq. non-positive real вҖ” the PerronвҖ“Frobenius hypothesis. **The real-symmetric sector matrix is now set up** (`TJSectorMatrix.lean`): the `NМӮ=Ne`, `ЕңВі=ВҪ` sector `TJSpinHalfFillingSector N Ne` (`s` with `#вҶ‘=#вҶ“+1`, `#вҶ‘+#вҶ“=Ne`), the real form `tJEffReMatrix = (tJEffMatrix).re`, its sector restriction `tJEffReMatrixOnSector = submatrix Subtype.val`, and `tJEffReMatrixOnSector_isSymm` (`IsSymm` straight from `tJEffMatrix_isHermitian` вҖ” `M_{q,p}=conj M_{p,q}` gives equal real parts, no global realness needed; the full matrix is only sector-real since the wrap-hop needs odd `Ne`); this is the `Matrix вҖҰ в„қ` feeding Theorem A.18. **The connectivity step relation is set up** (`TJStepRelation.lean`): `TJStep` (one off-diagonal move вҖ” a NN/wrap hop into an adjacent empty site, or an adjacent antiparallel spin exchange `tJSpinSwap`), with `tJSpinSwap_count` (the swap is `s вҲҳ Equiv.swap i j`, a site bijection) and `tJStep_preserves_sector` (a step keeps `#вҶ‘`/`#вҶ“`, so the reachability closure stays in one sector) вҖ” the relation whose positive entries will feed `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_path`. **The per-step matrix-entry positivity is proven** (`TJStepMatrixEntry.lean`): the moved-electron kinetic summand equals `1` (`tJ_cycle_hop_kinetic_summand_eq_one`, via the forward/backward/wrap dispatch), so a hop gives `вҹЁKвҹ©.re вүҘ 1` (`tJKinetic_matrixElement_re_ge_one_of_hop`, by `Finset.single_le_sum` over the `{0,1}`-valued summands); an exchange gives an interaction bond-sum `.re вүҘ 1`, hence `JВ·interaction.re вүӨ вҲ’J/2`; combining (`tJEffMatrix_re_neg_of_step`), for `П„,J > 0` every `TJStep s s'` produces `M_{s',s}.re < 0` (a hop `вүӨ вҲ’П„`, an exchange `вүӨ вҲ’J/2`), i.e. `B_{s',s} = вҲ’M_{s',s} > 0`. **The adjacent-swap bridge is in place** (`TJAdjacentSwap.lean`): an `AdjacentSwapStep` (exchange the values at an adjacent pair of distinct sites, `= s вҲҳ Equiv.swap a b`) is always a `TJStep` (`adjacentSwapStep_to_TJStep` вҖ” a hop if one site is empty, an exchange if the two are opposite spins), so `AdjacentSwapReachable` lifts to `TJStep`-reachability (`tjReachable_of_adjacentSwapReachable`); the sector connectivity then reduces to the model-agnostic combinatorial fact that two `Fin 3`-configs with equal value-counts are adjacent-swap reachable. **The reachability basics are in place** (`TJSwapReachableBasics.lean`): `adjacentSwapReachable_swap` (the value-swapped `s вҲҳ Equiv.swap a b` is reachable), `adjacentSwapReachable_count` (reachability preserves every value-count, so it stays in-sector), and `exists_needed_value_right_of_same_counts` (equal counts + prefix agreement вҹ№ the needed value `s' p` sits right of `p` вҖ” the bubble target). **The sector connectivity is now proven** (`TJSwapReachable.lean`): `bubble_reachable` (move a value leftward to a target by NN swaps, prefix-preserving, induction on the gap) + `reach_of_agree_aux` (selection sort: bubble the needed value into the leftmost disagreement, recurse) assemble into **`adjacentSwapReachable_of_same_counts`** вҖ” any two `Fin 3`-configs with equal value-counts are adjacent-swap reachable, hence (via the bridge) `TJStep`-reachable within a sector. **The shifted PF matrix is set up** (`TJSectorShifted.lean`): `tJSectorShifted = cВ·1 вҲ’ M` with `tJSectorShifted_isSymm`, `tJSectorShifted_nonneg` (entrywise `вүҘ 0` for `c` above the diagonal), `tJStep_ne` (a step changes the config), and `tJSectorShifted_pos_of_step` (a `TJStep` between distinct sector states gives a strictly positive off-diagonal `B`-entry). **The shifted sector matrix is now proven irreducible** (`TJSectorIrreducible.lean`): `tJ_value_count_total` (`#вҲ…+#вҶ‘+#вҶ“=N+1`) + `tJSector_same_counts` (distinct sector states share all value-counts) feed the connectivity, and `tJSectorShifted_pow_pos_of_reachable` lifts a `TJStep`-reachable chain to a positive matrix-power entry (`matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_pow_pos_step`, membership carried via `tJStep_preserves_sector`), giving **`tJSectorShifted_isIrreducible`** (`B = cВ·1 вҲ’ M` irreducible via `isIrreducible_iff_exists_pow_pos`: diagonal positivity + connectivity). **The PerronвҖ“Frobenius ground state is now obtained** (`TJSectorGroundState.lean`): a `Nonempty (TJSpinHalfFillingSector N Ne)` witness (the `ЕңВі=ВҪ` filling with `вҶ‘`'s left of `вҶ“`'s) supplies A.18's `[Nonempty]`, and **`tJEffReMatrixOnSector_perronFrobenius`** applies Theorem A.18 to `M = tJEffReMatrixOnSector` вҖ” `M` has a strictly positive eigenvector at its lowest eigenvalue `Ој` (the sector ground energy), with `finrank вүӨ 1` (non-degeneracy): the unique positive PF ground state of the spin-charge-separated sector. **The sectorвҶ”full bridge is started** (`TJExpansion.lean`): `tJExpansion v = ОЈ_s v_sвҖў\|ОҰ_sвҹ©`, `tJExpansionCoeff`, and the left inverse PМӮhc` by idempotency `PМӮhcВІ=PМӮhc` (`tJKinetic_commute_hubbardHardcoreProjection`), and the per-site `Ең^Вұ_x,ЕңВі_x,nМӮ_x` commuting with every double occupancy `nМӮ_{iвҶ‘}nМӮ_{iвҶ“}` state is proven** (`TJOperatorLift.lean`, `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_tJConfigOf`: `ДӨ_tJ|ОҰ_sвҹ© = ОЈ_{s'} вҹЁОҰ_{s'}|ДӨ_tJ|ОҰ_sвҹ© |ОҰ_{s'}вҹ©`, reassembling its sector-matrix column вҖ” mirrors per-basis-state lift `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_tJConfigOf'` + `tJExpansionCoeff(ДӨ_tJ|ОҰ_sвҹ©)=tJEffMatrix`) and the sector realness `tJEffMatrix_sector_im_zero` (off-diagonals by `ЕңвҒҪВ№вҒҫ`, `ЕңвҒҪВІвҒҫ` (and `preservesTJFillingW_tJHamiltonian` for `ДӨ_tJ`); axiom-free. **The compressed `ДӨ_W`, `ЕңвҒҪбө…вҒҫ_W` satisfy the A.17 hypotheses** (`TJFillingCompressSpinAlgebra.lean`): via the compression homomorphism + `compress` linearity (`tJFillingCompress_smul`/`_sub`/`_isHermitian`), the `W`-compressions inherit Hermiticity, the su(2) relations (`tJFillingCompress_su2_12`/`_23`/`_31`) and `ДӨ_W`-commutativity (`tJFillingCompress_tJHamiltonian_commute_one`/`_two`/`_three`) вҖ” exactly the inputs of the matrix Theorem A.17; axiom-free. **The filling embedding is an isometry + the Rayleigh bridge** (`TJFillingRayleighBridge.lean`): `tJFillingEmbedding_conjTranspose_mul_self` (`Tбҙҙ T = 1`), `tJFillingExpansion_dotProduct_self` (`вҹЁT c, T cвҹ©=вҹЁc,cвҹ©`), and `rayleighOnVec_tJFillingCompress` (`rayleighOnVec ДӨ_tJ (tJFillingExpansion c) = rayleighOnVec ДӨ_W c`) вҖ” connecting `groundEnergyAtFilling` to the matrix `ДӨ_W`; axiom-free. **The eigenvector lift is proven** (`TJFillingEigenLift.lean`, `mulVec_tJFillingExpansion_of_compress_eigen`): an eigenvector of `compress(A)` at `E` lifts (when `A` preserves `W`) to an eigenvector of `A` at `E` on `W`, via the projection identity `T Tбҙҙ = id` on `W`; plus `tJFillingExpansion_mem_tJFillingWSubmodule`; axiom-free. Remaining for E2-вүҘ: apply the matrix A.17 (`exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate`) to the min eigenvector of `ДӨ_W` (its hypotheses are the proven compressed su(2)/Hermitian/commute), lift it (this lemma), use odd `Ne` to force `ЕңВі=ВҪ` (`TJFillingSpinZDiag.lean`: `ЕңВі` is diagonal on filling expansions, `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_tJFillingExpansion`, and the only `ЕңВі=0` filling state is `0` for odd `Ne`, `tJFillingExpansion_eq_zero_of_spinZ_mulVec_eq_zero`) вҹ№ `Ој вүӨ hermitianMinEigenvalue ДӨ_W`, identify `groundEnergyAtFilling = hermitianMinEigenvalue ДӨ_W` via the bridge, completing `groundEnergyAtFilling = Ој`. **E2 is now COMPLETE** (`TJGroundEnergyGe.lean`): `tJ_perronFrobeniusMin_le_hermitianMinEigenvalue` (the W-restricted A.17 application), `tJ_groundEnergyAtFilling_ge_of_sectorMin` (`Ој вүӨ groundEnergyAtFilling`), and the capstone `tJHamiltonian_groundEnergyAtFilling_eq_perronFrobeniusMin` (`groundEnergyAtFilling = Ој`, with the strictly-positive PF eigenvector `v` at `Ој`) вҖ” resting only on Theorem A.17 (`exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate`, **now discharged** В§A.3.2). **E3a (the SU(2) upper-bound seed, `TJGroundSpinHalfFinrank.lean`) is now COMPLETE**: `tJ_groundSubmodule_spinHalf_finrank_le_one` вҖ” `finrank в„Ӯ (groundSubmoduleAtFilling ДӨ_tJ Ne вҠ“ (ЕңВі=ВҪ)) вүӨ 1` вҖ” via `groundEnergyAtFilling = Ој`, the PF real `finrank вүӨ 1`, the realвҶ”complex eigenspace bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real`, and the injective `в„Ӯ`-linear embedding `tJExpansionCoeffвӮ—` (`ОҰ вҶҰ tJExpansionCoeff ОҰ`, injective by sector-support) of the block into the complex sector eigenspace at `Ој`; supporting lemma `tJ_spinHalf_W_complexSectorEigen` (a block element's coefficient vector is a complex sector-matrix eigenvector at `Ој`); no new axioms. **E3b PR1 (`TJSectorRaise.lean`, toward lifting the PF vector to a highest weight) is COMPLETE**: the single-site raising operator `ЕңвҒә_x = ДүвҖ _{xвҶ‘}Дү_{xвҶ“}` is **sign-free** on the sector basis вҖ” `fermionSiteSpinPlus_mulVec_tJConfigOf_of_down` (`s x=вҶ“ вҹ№ ЕңвҒә_x|ОҰ_sвҹ© = |ОҰ_{s with xвҶҰвҶ‘}вҹ©` with net JordanвҖ“Wigner sign `+1`, since the adjacent orbitals `(xвҶ‘,xвҶ“)=(2x,2x+1)` with `2x` empty collapse the two strings) + `fermionSiteSpinPlus_mulVec_tJConfigOf_of_not_down` (`s xвү вҶ“ вҹ№ ЕңвҒә_x|ОҰ_sвҹ©=0`) + the config identity `tJConfigOf_update_raise`; fully axiom-free. This Marshall sign-freeness makes the iterated `(ЕңвҒә)^m` have nonnegative config coefficients, so a strictly-positive coordinate (from `v q > 0`) gives the non-vanishing `(ЕңвҒә)^((Ne-1)/2) ОҰвӮҖ вү  0`. **E3b PR2 (`TJRaisingTower.lean`) is COMPLETE**: eigenvalue tracking along the raising tower вҖ” `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (`ЕңВі (ЕңвҒә)^k v = (m+k)(ЕңвҒә)^k v`, via `[ЕңВі,ЕңвҒә]=ЕңвҒә`), `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (energy `Ој` preserved, `[ДӨ_tJ,ЕңвҒә]=0`), `fermionTotalNumber_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (number `Ne` preserved, `[NМӮ,ЕңвҒә]=0`); so `(ЕңвҒә)^k ОҰвӮҖ`, when nonzero, is a fixed-`Ne`, energy-`Ој`, `ЕңВі=ВҪ+k` eigenvector; fully axiom-free. **E3b PR3 (`TJRaisingTermination.lean`) is COMPLETE**: the raising tower terminates вҖ” `fermionTotalDownNumber_mul_fermionTotalSpinPlus` (`[NМӮ_вҶ“,ЕңвҒә]=вҲ’ЕңвҒә`), `fermionTotalDownNumber_mulVec_spinPlusPow` (`NМӮ_вҶ“ (ЕңвҒә)^k v = (mвҲ’k)(ЕңвҒә)^k v`), and `spinPlusPow_succ_eq_zero_of_downNumber` (`NМӮ_вҶ“ v = m v вҹ№ (ЕңвҒә)^(m+1) v = 0`, since the `NМӮ_вҶ“`-eigenvalue `вҲ’1` forces `(вҶ“count+1)В·ПҲ(w)=0` per config); fully axiom-free. So for a `ЕңВі=ВҪ`, `NМӮ=Ne` ground state (`NМӮ_вҶ“=(NeвҲ’1)/2`), the top `О©=(ЕңвҒә)^((NeвҲ’1)/2)ОҰ` satisfies `ЕңвҒәО©=0` вҖ” a highest weight. **E3b PR4 (`TJHighestWeight.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_raised_highestWeight` combines the tower tracking + termination вҖ” `ОҰ` with `ЕңВіОҰ=ВҪОҰ`, `NМӮ_вҶ“ОҰ=mОҰ`, `ДӨ_tJОҰ=ОјОҰ` вҹ№ `О©=(ЕңвҒә)^m ОҰ` satisfies `ЕңвҒәО©=0`, `ЕңВіО©=(m+ВҪ)О©`, `ДӨ_tJО©=ОјО©` (for `m=(NeвҲ’1)/2`, `ЕңВіО©=(Ne/2)О©`, the maximal-spin highest weight); fully axiom-free. **E3b PR5a (`TJExpansionSpinEigen.lean`) is COMPLETE**: the lifted ground vector's sector eigenvalues вҖ” `tJSpinHalfFillingSector_down_count` (every sector state has `#вҶ“=(NeвҲ’1)/2`), `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_tJExpansion` (`ЕңВі (tJExpansion v)=ВҪ(tJExpansion v)`), `fermionTotalDownNumber_mulVec_tJExpansion` (`NМӮ_вҶ“ (tJExpansion v)=((NeвҲ’1)/2)(tJExpansion v)`), letting the highest-weight extraction apply to `ОҰвӮҖ=tJExpansion(в„ӮвҲҳv)` with `m=(NeвҲ’1)/2`; fully axiom-free. **E3b PR5b-1 (`TJTotalRaiseAction.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `fermionTotalSpinPlus_mulVec_tJConfigOf` вҖ” `ЕңвҒә_tot |ОҰ_sвҹ© = ОЈ_{x:s x=вҶ“} |ОҰ_{s with xвҶҰвҶ‘}вҹ©` (the sign-free expansion, every term `+1`), the keystone for the config-nonnegativity argument toward `(ЕңвҒә)^((NeвҲ’1)/2)ОҰвӮҖ вү  0`; fully axiom-free. **E3b PR5b-2 (`TJRaiseCoeffSum.lean`) is COMPLETE**: the coefficient-sum functional `coeffSum ПҲ = ОЈ_c ПҲ_c` вҖ” `coeffSum_basisVec` (`= 1`) and `coeffSum_fermionTotalSpinPlus_tJConfigOf` (`coeffSum (ЕңвҒә_tot|ОҰ_sвҹ©) = #вҶ“(s)`); with the uniform sector down-count, iterating gives `coeffSum ((ЕңвҒә)^((NeвҲ’1)/2) ОҰвӮҖ) = ((NeвҲ’1)/2)!В·ОЈv_q > 0`, so the raised vector is nonzero; fully axiom-free. **E3b PR5b-3a (`TJFillingCoeffSum.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `coeffSum_tJFillingExpansion` (`= ОЈ_s v_s`) and `coeffSum_fermionTotalSpinPlus_tJFillingExpansion` (`= ОЈ_s v_sВ·#вҶ“(s)`) вҖ” together the recursion `coeffSum (ЕңвҒәПҲ) = dВ·coeffSum ПҲ` for a hard-core `NМӮ_вҶ“`-eigenvector at `d`; fully axiom-free. **E3b PR5b-3b (`TJRaisePositivityStep.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `fermionTotalDownNumber_mulVec_tJFillingExpansion` (`NМӮ_вҶ“` diagonal on a filling expansion) and `coeffSum_fermionTotalSpinPlus_eq_of_downEigen` вҖ” a hard-core `NМӮ=Ne` vector `ПҲ` with `NМӮ_вҶ“ПҲ=dПҲ` satisfies `coeffSum (ЕңвҒәПҲ) = dВ·coeffSum ПҲ` (both sides `= ОЈ_s coeff_sВ·#вҶ“(s)` via `tJ_filling_completeness`); fully axiom-free. **E3b PR5c (`TJRaiseHardcore.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `fermionTotalSpinPlus_mulVec_mem_hardcore` + `fermionTotalSpinPlus_pow_mulVec_mem_hardcore` (`ЕңвҒә_tot` and its powers preserve the hard-core subspace), keeping every tower state `(ЕңвҒә)^k ОҰвӮҖ` hard-core for the recursion; fully axiom-free. **E3b PR5d (`TJRaisePositivity.lean`) вҖ” the Marshall positivity crux вҖ” is COMPLETE**: `coeffSum_tJExpansion` (`= ОЈ_s v_s`) and **`spinPlusPow_ne_zero_of_coeffSum_ne_zero`** (a hard-core `NМӮ=Ne` vector `ОҰвӮҖ` with `NМӮ_вҶ“ОҰвӮҖ=mОҰвӮҖ` and nonzero coefficient sum has `(ЕңвҒә)^m ОҰвӮҖ вү  0`, since each step multiplies `coeffSum` by `mвҲ’k вү  0`). Applied to `ОҰвӮҖ=tJExpansion(в„ӮвҲҳv)` (`coeffSum=ОЈv_q>0`, `m=(NeвҲ’1)/2`), this gives `О© = (ЕңвҒә)^((NeвҲ’1)/2) ОҰвӮҖ вү  0` вҖ” the non-vanishing; fully axiom-free. **E4 PR6a (`TJMaximalSpinGroundState.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_exists_maximalSpin_highestWeight_groundState` assembles the E3b chain into a nonzero highest-weight ground state вҖ” `О©` with `ЕңвҒәО©=0`, `ЕңВіО©=(Ne/2)О©`, `ДӨ_tJО©=ОјО©` at `Ој=groundEnergyAtFilling`, plus `О©вҲҲhardcore` and `NМӮО©=NeВ·О©` (so `О©вҲҲgroundSubmoduleAtFilling`) вҖ” non-vanishing from the Marshall positivity, highest-weight from `tJ_raised_highestWeight`, `groundEnergyAtFilling=Ој` from E2; rests only on A.17 (**now discharged** В§A.3.2). **E4 lower bound (`TJGroundDegeneracyLower.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_groundSubmodule_finrank_ge` вҖ” `Ne + 1 вүӨ finrank (groundSubmoduleAtFilling ДӨ_tJ Ne)`. The highest weight `О©` generates the `Ne+1` LI tower `(ЕңвҒ»)^k О©` (`highestWeight_spinMultiplet_general`); each member is again a ground state (`ЕңвҒ»` commutes `ДӨ_tJ`/`NМӮ`, preserves hard-core), so `LinearIndependent.of_comp` + `fintype_card_le_finrank` give the bound; rests only on A.17. **E5 ladder injectivity (`TJLadderInjective.lean`) is COMPLETE**: the SU(2) norm identity `вҖ–ЕңвҒ»vвҖ–ВІ=вҖ–ЕңвҒәvвҖ–ВІ+2szвҖ–vвҖ–ВІ` (`fermionTotalSpin_ladder_norm`, from `[ЕңвҒә,ЕңвҒ»]=2ЕңВі`+`(ЕңвҒ»)бҙҙ=ЕңвҒә`) gives `ЕңвҒ»` injective on weight `sz>0` and `ЕңвҒә` on `sz<0` вҖ” no rep theory вҖ” embedding each `ЕңВі`-weight space of `G` into `ЕңВі=ВұВҪ`, the key to the upper bound `finrankвүӨNe+1`; fully axiom-free. **E5b weight-finrank step (`TJGroundWeightFinrank.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_ground_weight_finrank_le_succ` вҖ” for `szвүҘ0`, `finrank (GвҠ“ЕңВі=sz+1) вүӨ finrank (GвҠ“ЕңВі=sz)` (`ЕңвҒ»` injects the `sz+1` weight space into the `sz` one, preserving `G` via `fermionTotalSpinMinus_mulVec_mem_groundSubmodule` and lowering the weight by one); iterating to `ВҪ` (and up via `ЕңвҒә`) bounds every weight space by the `ЕңВі=ВҪ` block вүӨ1. **The `ЕңвҒә` mirror (`TJGroundWeightFinrankRaise.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_ground_weight_finrank_le_of_spinZ_neg` вҖ” for `sz<0`, `finrank (GвҠ“ЕңВі=sz) вүӨ finrank (GвҠ“ЕңВі=sz+1)` (`ЕңвҒә` injects `sz` into `sz+1`), so negative-weight blocks are bounded by `ЕңВі=ВҪ` from below too. **Every weight block вүӨ1 (`TJGroundWeightOne.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_ground_weight_finrank_le_one_pos/_neg` вҖ” `finrank (GвҠ“ЕңВі=ВҪ+k) вүӨ 1` and `finrank (GвҠ“ЕңВі=вҲ’ВҪвҲ’k) вүӨ 1` (`k:в„•`), by iterating the two steps to the `ЕңВі=ВҪ` block (вүӨ1, E3a); the half-integers exhaust the `ЕңВі` spectrum at odd filling. **The `ЕңВі` weight decomposition (`TJGroundWeightDirectSum.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `fermionTotalSpinZ_mulVec_mem_groundSubmodule` (`ЕңВі` preserves `G`), `fermionTotalSpinZ_iSup_eigenspace_eq_top` (`ЕңВі` diagonal on the computational basis, so its eigenspaces span `вҠӨ`), and `tJ_groundSubmodule_eq_iSup_inf_eigenspace` вҖ” `G = вЁҶ Ој, G вҠ“ eigenspace ЕңВі Ој` (`Submodule.eq_iSup_inf_genEigenspace` for the invariant `G`); **The degeneracy upper bound (`TJGroundDegeneracyUpper.lean`) is COMPLETE**: `tJ_groundSubmodule_finrank_le` вҖ” `finrank G вүӨ Ne+1` (the `Ne+1` half-integer weight blocks are independent (`eigenspaces_iSupIndep`), hence an internal direct sum of `G` (`DirectSum.coeLinearMap` injective + `finrank_directSum`), and each block is вүӨ1 (#4309), so `finrank G = вҲ‘ finrank (block) вүӨ Ne+1`); paired with the SU(2)-tower lower bound (`tJ_groundSubmodule_finrank_ge`, #4305) this pins `finrank G = Ne+1`. **Proposition 11.24 is now PROVED** (`proposition_11_24` in `TJProposition1124.lean`, E6 capstone): `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule N (groundSubmoduleAtFilling вҖҰ) Ne` вҖ” `finrank G = Ne+1` (le_antisymm of #4305/#4312) and every ground state is an `(Ең_tot)ВІ` eigenvector at `(Ne/2)(Ne/2+1)` (the `Ne+1` LI tower states `(ЕңвҒ»)^k О©` span `G`, each maximal-spin via `highestWeight_spinMultiplet_general`). **This discharges the former `axiom proposition_11_24`**; the entire d=1 ferromagnetic t-J ferromagnetism result rests only on Theorem A.17 (**now discharged** В§A.3.2). Then the total-spin identification (`S_tot = Ne/2`) via the highest weight raised this way, the SU(2)-tower lower bound (paired with this `ЕңВі=ВҪ` block bound for the upper bound), and the capstone replacing the axiom. ground states of `tJExchange` have spin-swap-invariant bond amplitudes вҖ” toward the degeneracy upper bound; axiom-free); PR3i-2 `TJHalfFillingBondGround.lean` `tJ_ground_bond_mulVec_eq_zero` (a half-filling ground state is annihilated by every adjacent bond: `bond_xy v=0` via the PSD sum `вҹЁv,tJExchange vвҹ©=0` вҮ’ each `вҹЁv,bond_xy vвҹ©=0` вҮ’ `О”_xy v=0`; axiom-free); PR3i-3a `TJHalfFillingAmplitude.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_swap_invariant` (half-filling ground amplitudes are spin-swap invariant: `v(tJConfigOf s)=v(tJConfigOf(tJSpinSwap s x y))` for adjacent bonds, via filling-basis expansion + the bond half-swap action + `bond_xy v=0`; `tJFillingSwap` sector permutation; axiom-free); PR3i-3b `TJHalfFillingUpCount.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_eq_of_same_upCount` (half-filling ground amplitudes depend only on the up-count: equal up-counts вҹ№ equal value-counts вҹ№ adjacent-swap reachable [`adjacentSwapReachable_of_same_counts`] вҹ№ amplitudes equal by reachability induction over the per-bond swap invariance; axiom-free); PR3i-3c `TJHalfFillingDegeneracyUpper.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_groundSubmodule_finrank_le` (**upper bound `finrank G вүӨ N+2`**: ground states determined by amplitudes constant on the N+2 up-count classes вҹ№ injective `G вҶӘ (Fin(N+2)вҶ’в„Ӯ)`; with the lower bound #4326 вҹ№ `finrank G = N+2`; axiom-free); **PR3-cap `TJHalfFillingMaximalSpin.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_isMaximalSpinMultiplet`** (the half-filling t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin `(N+2)`-fold multiplet: `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule N G (N+1)`, assembling `finrank=N+2` with the all-up SU(2) tower spanning `G`; boundary case of Theorem 11.26's t-J side; axiom-free, no A.17); **PR-unify `TJMaximalSpinUnified.lean` `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`** (the d=1 t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin multiplet for ALL odd `Ne вүӨ K+1`: metallic `Ne<K+1` via `proposition_11_24` + half-filling `Ne=K+1`; the half-filling chain generalized to drop `0<N` so `K=0` is covered; t-J input to Theorem 11.26, rests on A.17 via Prop 11.24); **PR-final: `theorem_11_26` is now a PROVED theorem** (`MetallicFerroModel.lean`, Issue #4314): discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented strong-coupling-equivalence axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`; `#print axioms theorem_11_26` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound, lemma_11_25]` (A.17's `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` now discharged В§A.3.2). Tasaki В§11.5.3 metallic-ferromagnetism Theorem 11.26 complete + **`theorem_11_26` (PROVED, Issue #4314)** (d=1, `NeвүӨK+1=|E|` odd (Tasaki's `NвүӨL`) вҮ’ `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule (3K+2) (groundSubmoduleAtFilling (decHubbardHamiltonian вҖҰ) Ne) Ne` for large `t,U` вҖ” ground `S_tot=Ne/2`, `Ne+1`-fold; metallic when `Ne<K+1`); discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`, so the model and Theorem 11.26 are proved modulo `lemma_11_25` (A.17 now discharged); Theorem 11.27 still a documented axiom); **generic single-particle-state operators (`SpinfulVectorOperator.lean`)**: `spinfulCreationFromVector`/`spinfulAnnihilationFromVector` (`ДҲ_Пғ(ПҶ)=ОЈ_x ПҶ(x)Дү_{x,Пғ}`), shared helper; axiom-free; **В§11.5.4 TanakaвҖ“Tasaki model + Theorem 11.27 (AXIOMATIZED, `TanakaTasakiModel.lean`, Issue #4198) вҖ” В§11.5/Chapter 11 capstone**: the heavily-decorated lattice `Оӣ=EГ—{1,2,3}вҲӘIГ—{1,2}` (externals triplicated, internals duplicated), d=1 in `Fin(5K+5)` (`ttExtSite`/`ttIntSite`); special states `ttAlpha`/`ttBeta`/`ttDeltaP`/`ttDeltaI` (eqs. (11.5.19)вҖ“(11.5.22), `Гў` with `1/вҲҡ(3+4ОҪВІ)`) + ops via `spinfulCreationFromVector`; `ttHopping` (`ОЈ_{вҹЁp,qвҹ©}(вҲ’s ГўвҖ ГўвҲ’t bМӮвҖ bМӮ)+uвӮҒОЈbМӮвҖ bМӮ+uвӮӮОЈdМӮвҖ dМӮ`, eq. (11.5.24)) + `ttInteraction` + `ttHamiltonian` (finite); genuine `uвӮӮ,UвҶ‘вҲһ` limit objects `ttDKernel` (`dМӮОҰ=0`, via Theorem A.12) + `ttEffectiveHamiltonian` (`uвӮӮ,U=вҲһ` effective hopping); **`axiom theorem_11_27`** (d=1, `uвӮҒ>2(|s|+2|t|)`, `K+1вүӨNeвүӨ2(K+1)` (Tasaki `L^dвүӨNвүӨ2L^d`) вҮ’ **in the limit** every ground state in `groundSubmoduleAtFilling (ttEffectiveHamiltonian вҖҰ) Ne вҠ“ ttDKernel` has max spin `S_tot=Ne/2` вҖ” taken as a genuine limit, not finite thresholds (Tasaki proves it only in the limit, warns finite `uвӮӮ,U` is not expected to work); weaker than the multiplet predicate, Tasaki claims only the spin; metallic when `1<Ne/L<2`); model axiom-free, `theorem_11_27` documented axiom ([63])) |
| P3 | CAR algebras, quasi-local C*-algebras, KMS states | Not started |
| P4 | Thermodynamic limit, phase transitions | Not started |
| P5 | Lattice QCD | Not started |
| Appendix A (Tasaki Mathematical Appendices) | The book-order content after Chapter 11; foundations for the deferred Chapter-11 proof discharges (frustration-free A.9/A.10, limit A.11/A.12, PerronвҖ“Frobenius A.17/A.18 вҖ” the last largely already in `Math/PerronFrobenius*`/`CollatzWielandt*`). **Complete вҖ” A.1вҖ“A.28 all formalized in book order** (Issues [#4205](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4205) + [#4224](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4224), both closed; see the **status & axiomatization policy** note below the Roadmap): **Theorem A.1 (Lie product formula)** `e^{A+B} = lim_N (e^{A/N}e^{B/N})^N` **now proved (axiom-free)** (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/LieProduct.lean`, `lieProductFormula` вҖ” mathlib has only the commuting case): generic `trotterProductFormula` in a complete normed `в„қ`-algebra via exponential-series tail bounds (`вҖ–e^XвҲ’1вҲ’XвҖ– вүӨ вҖ–XвҖ–ВІe^{вҖ–XвҖ–}` etc.) + telescoping power estimate (`вҖ–CвҒҝвҲ’DвҒҝвҖ– вүӨ nВ·M^{nвҲ’1}В·вҖ–CвҲ’DвҖ–`) + `O(sВІ)` product comparison + the exact `n`-th-power identity, instantiated for matrices under the scoped operator norm; **Lemmas A.4** (Hermitian `ГӮ` is `вүҘ0` iff all eigenvalues `вүҘ0`), **A.5** (`ГӮ,BМӮвүҘ0 вҮ’ ГӮ+BМӮвүҘ0`), **A.6** (`BМӮвҖ BМӮвүҘ0`, and conversely every `ГӮвүҘ0` is `ДҲВІ` for a *unique* PSD square root `ДҲ=вҲҡГӮ` via `cfc Real.sqrt`, existence+uniqueness) **proved** (axiom-free, `Math/PosSemidef/Basics.lean`). **Theorem A.7** (Weyl monotonicity: Hermitian `ГӮвүӨBМӮ` вҮ’ `i`-th sorted eigenvalue `ГӮ.eigenvaluesвӮҖ i вүӨ BМӮ.eigenvaluesвӮҖ i`) **now proved (axiom-free)** via the CourantвҖ“Fischer block/pigeonhole argument (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/CourantFischer.lean`: spectral Rayleigh-sum `reвҹЁx,Txвҹ©=вҲ‘О»бөўвҖ–вҹЁbбөў,xвҹ©вҖ–ВІ` + block bounds + the top-`(i+1)`/bottom-`(cardвҲ’i)` eigenspaces meeting by finrank pigeonhole; `EigenvalueMonotone.lean`). **Corollary A.8** (`ГӮвүӨBМӮ вҮ’ Tr e^ГӮ вүӨ Tr e^BМӮ`, eq A.2.31) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`MatrixAnalysis/TraceExpMonotone.lean`: spectral mapping `Tr e^ГӮ = ОЈ e^{a_j}` via `Matrix.exp_conj` + `exp_diagonal` + `trace_mul_cycle`, then A.7 Weyl + `Real.exp` monotone). **Lemmas A.9/A.10** (frustration-free Hamiltonian `ДӨ=ОЈДҘвұј`, `ДҘвұјвүҘОөвұј`: A.9 simultaneous eigenstate вҮ’ ground state at `ОЈОөвұј`; A.10 converse) **proved** (axiom-free, `FrustrationFree.lean`). **Lemma A.11** (`ГӮвүҘ0 вҲ§ вҹЁОҰ|ГӮ|ОҰвҹ©=0 вҮ’ ГӮОҰ=0`; `ГӮ=BМӮвҖ BМӮ вҮ’ BМӮОҰ=0`; + angular-momentum corollary `ДҙВІОҰ=0 вҮ’ ДҙвҒҪбө…вҒҫОҰ=0`) **proved** (axiom-free, `PosSemidef/Kernel.lean`, over the existing `RayleighPosSemidefKernel`). **Theorem A.12** (`ДӨ_v=ДӨвӮҖ+vVМӮ`, `VМӮвүҘ0`: `vвҶ‘вҲһ` finite-energy eigenstate families converging to nonzero `ОҰ` satisfy the effective SchrГ¶dinger eq `PМӮвӮҖДӨвӮҖОҰ=EОҰ` on `HвӮҖ=ker VМӮ`) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`EffectiveLimit.lean`, weak-form + Tendsto: the paired eigenvalue relation gives `vВ·вҹЁОҰ_v|VМӮ|ОҰ_vвҹ© = E_vвҖ–ОҰ_vвҖ–ВІвҲ’вҹЁОҰ_v|ДӨвӮҖ|ОҰ_vвҹ©`, the converging right side times `vвҒ»В№вҶ’0` kills the limiting quadratic form, Lemma A.11 kills `VМӮОҰ`; pairing with `ПҲ вҲҲ ker VМӮ` removes the `vVМӮ` term outright and the limit is the weak equation). **Appendix A.2 complete.** **В§A.3 angular momentum**: **Lemma A.14** (su(2) ladder) вҖ” operator identity `ДҙвҒ»ДҙвҒә=ДҙВІвҲ’ДҙВі(ДҙВі+1)` (eq A.3.7) + ladder non-vanishing (`вҲ’JвүӨM<J`, `ОҰвү 0` вҮ’ `ДҙвҒәОҰвү 0`) + raising **membership** (`ДҙвҒәОҰвҲҲH_{J,M+1}`, via `[ДҙВі,ДҙвҒә]=ДҙвҒә` + `[ДҙВІ,ДҙвҒә]=0`) + **lowering** direction (`ДҙвҒәДҙвҒ»=ДҙВІвҲ’ДҙВі(ДҙВівҲ’1)` eq A.3.8, `ДҙвҒ»ОҰвү 0` for `вҲ’J<MвүӨJ`, `ДҙвҒ»ОҰвҲҲH_{J,MвҲ’1}`) **proved** вҖ” A.14 fully done (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Ladder.lean`). **Lemma A.15 (spin bound, part 1)**: norm identities `вҖ–ДҙВұОҰвҖ–ВІ={J(J+1)вҲ’M(MВұ1)}вҖ–ОҰвҖ–ВІ` (eq A.3.9, Hermitian Дҙ^Оұ) `angRaise/angLower_normSq` + `angMom_abs_le_J` (`вҲ’JвүӨMвүӨJ`, i.e. `JвҲ’M,J+MвүҘ0`) **proved** (axiom-free). **Lemma A.15 (integrality)** + **Theorem A.13 (`J = n/2`)**: ladder-termination `raiseIter` (iterated `ДҙвҒә`) with `raiseIter_eigenspace`/`raiseIter_ne_zero` gives `JвҲ’M вҲҲ в„ӨвүҘ0` (`angMom_sub_mem_nat`); the gauge-reflected `(ДҙВ№,вҲ’ДҙВІ,вҲ’ДҙВі)` gives `J+M вҲҲ в„ӨвүҘ0`, so `2J=(JвҲ’M)+(J+M)вҲҲв„ӨвүҘ0`, i.e. `J=n/2` (`angMom_J_eq_half_nat`) **proved** (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Quantization.lean`). **Theorem A.16 (SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy)**: for SU(2)-invariant `ДӨ` (`[ДӨ,Дҙбө…]=0`), a joint energy eigenstate in `H_{J,M0}` yields nonzero same-energy companions in every `H_{J,JвҲ’k}` (`kвүӨ2J`) вҖ” `ham_su2_multiplet` (energy `E` is вүҘ`(2J+1)`-fold degenerate), proved via raise-to-top + reflected-lowering ladder + `ham_mulVec_raiseIter` (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Multiplet.lean`). **Theorem A.17 (spin-0/1-2 sector suffices)**: every energy eigenvalue of an SU(2)-invariant `ДӨ` has an eigenstate with `ДҙВі=0` or `ДҙВі=1/2` вҖ” `ham_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (corollary of A.16+A.13; the lone simultaneous-diagonalization step `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` is **now proved (axiom-free)** via the generic common eigenvector of two commuting Hermitian operators on an invariant subspace (`Math/CommutingHermitianEigenvector.lean`: `exists_common_eigenvector_of_isHermitian_commute`), the Casimir `ДҙВІ` being PSD and commuting with `ДҙВі`/`ДӨ`, `AngularMomentum/SpinHalfSector.lean`). **Theorem A.18 (PerronвҖ“Frobenius, real symmetric)**: a real symmetric `M` with `(cВ·1вҲ’M)` irreducible (off-diag `вүӨ0` + connectivity) has a nondegenerate lowest eigenvalue with a strictly positive eigenvector вҖ” `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` (reuses the project's CollatzвҖ“Wielandt PF + eigenspace simplicity; the lowest-eigenvalue identification is the variational `|w|`-argument eq A.4.1), proved (axiom-free, `PerronFrobeniusSymmetric.lean`). **Theorems A.19/A.20 (polar + SVD)**: `matrix_polar_decomposition` (`A = W C`, `W` unitary, `C` PSD) and `matrix_singular_value_decomposition` (`A = U D VвҖ `, `U,V` unitary, `D = diagonal d` with `d вүҘ 0`) вҖ” **now proved (axiom-free)**: SVD built from the spectral theorem of `AбҙҙA` (`eigenvectorUnitary` `V`, eigenvalues `О»_i вүҘ 0`, `d_i = вҲҡО»_i`), with the normalised images `u_i = d_iвҒ»В№В·A v_i` extended to an orthonormal basis (`exists_orthonormalBasis_extension_of_card_eq`) giving `U`, and `A V = U D` columnwise; polar follows as `W = U Vбҙҙ`, `C = V D Vбҙҙ` (`MatrixAnalysis/Decomposition.lean`). **Theorems A.21/A.22 (Wigner)**: `wignerAutomorphism_unitary` (linear вҲ—-automorphism of `M(H)` вҹ№ `О“(ГӮ)=ГӣвҖ ГӮГӣ`) + `wignerAutomorphism_antiunitary` (antilinear вҹ№ `ГӣвҖ ГӮМ„Гӣ`) + `wignerProjection` (rank-1-projection map preserving `Tr[P PвҖІ]` вҹ№ (anti)unitary `VМӮ`) вҖ” documented axioms over `Matrix D D в„Ӯ` (`WignerTheorem.lean`, Issue #4224). **Definition A.23 + Theorem A.24 (states / BanachвҖ“Alaoglu)**: `IsState` (weak-вҲ—-continuous `ПҶ : WeakDual в„Ӯ A` with `ПҶ 1 = 1`, `0 вүӨ ПҶ(aвҖ a)`) over an abstract unital C*-algebra + `stateSpace_isCompact` (state space weak-вҲ— compact, documented axiom) вҖ” `CStarAlgebra/State.lean`. **Definitions A.25/A.27 + Theorem A.26 (ground states)**: dynamics modelled by a derivation `Оҙ=[ДӨ,В·]`; `IsGroundState` (`0 вүӨ Пү(aвҖ В·Оҙa)`) + `HasNonzeroGap` (`Оі>0`, `Пү(aвҖ Оҙa) вүҘ ОіПү(aвҖ a)` on `Пү(a)=0`) + `groundState_variational` (ground state вҹә `Пү(ДӨ_L)` least over states agreeing outside Оӣ_L, documented axiom) вҖ” `CStarAlgebra/GroundState.lean`. **Theorem A.28 (GNS construction)**: `gns_construction` вҖ” every state `ПҒ` on a C*-algebra has a GNS triple `(H_ПҒ, ПҖ_ПҒ, О©_ПҒ)` (`вҲ—`-representation `ПҖ : A вҶ’вӢҶвӮҗ[в„Ӯ] (HвҶ’L H)` + cyclic `О©`) with `ПҒ(ГӮ)=вҹЁО©,ПҖ(ГӮ)О©вҹ©` and `{ПҖ(ГӮ)О©}` dense; documented axiom (mathlib has the GNS machinery), `CStarAlgebra/GNS.lean`. **В§A.6вҖ“В§A.7 (Wigner / states / ground states / GNS) complete вҶ’ Appendix A complete.** Prove-first where mathlib supports, axiomatize-first for the heavy analytic results, strict book order (see the status & axiomatization-policy note below). |

### Appendix A: status and axiomatization policy

Tasaki's **Appendix A is fully formalized in book order (A.1вҖ“A.28)**, and the
entire Tasaki text up to and including Chapter 11 (В§11.5) plus this appendix is
now covered. The appendix splits into two kinds of items:

- **Proved (axiom-free)** вҖ” the linear-algebra and angular-momentum core that
  `mathlib` supports directly: **A.1** (the Lie product / Trotter formula, proved
  from scratch in a complete normed `в„қ`-algebra), **A.12** (the strong-coupling
  effective Hamiltonian limit, by the `vвҒ»В№`-squeeze + kernel-pairing continuity
  argument), **A.4вҖ“A.6**
  (positive-semidefinite basics + unique
  PSD square root), **A.7вҖ“A.8** (Weyl eigenvalue monotonicity via CourantвҖ“Fischer,
  and the trace-exp monotonicity through the spectral mapping for `exp`),
  **A.9вҖ“A.11** (frustration-free Hamiltonians, energy-form
  kernels), **A.13вҖ“A.16** (angular-momentum quantization `J = n/2`, the ladder,
  and SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy), **A.17** (spin-0/half sector via the common
  eigenvector of commuting Hermitians), **A.18** (PerronвҖ“Frobenius for a real
  symmetric matrix, via the project's CollatzвҖ“Wielandt development and the
  variational `|w|`-argument), and **A.19вҖ“A.20** (polar + singular-value
  decompositions from the spectral theorem).
- **Documented axioms (faithful statements, deferred proofs)** вҖ” the
  operator-algebraic **A.21вҖ“A.28** (Wigner's
  theorem, states, BanachвҖ“Alaoglu, ground states of infinite systems, the GNS
  construction).

**Value judgment / policy.** The documented axioms above are kept as *faithful,
book-order statements* вҖ” they record exactly what Tasaki proves вҖ” but they are
**not active proof targets** of this project. They fall into two categories:

- **Operator-algebraic results** (Appendix A.21вҖ“A.28): the heavy functional-analytic and
operator-algebraic structures (states on the quasi-local C\*-algebra, weak-вҲ—
compactness, ground states, GNS, Wigner) belong to a dedicated operator-algebra /
functional-analysis development; such a development may well be carried out
**separately** (for instance contributed to `mathlib`), and these axioms simply
**wait for that implementation**.

- **Perturbation-theoretic results** (e.g., **Lemma 10.1** (Tasaki В§10.1, degenerate
perturbation theory) and singular-perturbation arguments in Chapter 10): the analytic
proofs of weak-coupling continuation and adiabatic following for eigenstate families
are **not undertaken** as an active project goal; such techniques naturally belong to
a separate analytic-perturbation development. Results whose only missing link is a
perturbation-theoretic proof вҖ” such as the total-spin values in Lieb's repulsive-Hubbard
theorem (Theorem 10.4) вҖ” are recorded as documented axioms while their companion
finite-coupling combinatorial bounds are proved axiom-free.

- **Book theorems that Tasaki states without proof** (results he quotes from the
external literature rather than proving in the text): **Theorem 10.11** (KuboвҖ“Kishi
finite-temperature charge/pairing susceptibility bound, Tasaki В§10.2.5, citing
KuboвҖ“Kishi, *Phys. Rev. B* **41**, 4866 (1990)) and **Theorem 11.13** (Mielke's
flat-band ferromagnetism, `mielke_theorem_11_13`) are recorded as **faithful documented
axioms** on the concrete finite-volume operators (here the Duhamel susceptibilities),
matching the "Tasaki states it without proof" policy вҖ” the reproving of the cited
external work is not an active project goal.

Accordingly the project's policy is to **axiomatize only the appendix and
perturbation-theory results that Tasaki's formalized main development actually uses**,
to **prove** the remaining ones where `mathlib` provides the tools, and otherwise to
leave a faithful axiom in place rather than invest in large bespoke developments whose
natural home is elsewhere. The `#print axioms` of every theorem in the repository makes
the precise dependency on these documented axioms auditable.

## Formalized theorems

All items below are formally proved with **zero `sorry`**. Full
mathematical statements and proof sketches are in
[`tex/proof-guide.tex`](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/blob/main/tex/proof-guide.tex).

### Single-site Pauli operators

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, В§2.1 eq. (2.1.8), p. 15. Cross-checked with Nielsen-Chuang
В§2.1.3 Figure 2.2 (pp. 65-66, definitions), Ex. 2.19 (p. 70,
Hermiticity), Ex. 2.41 (p. 78, `(Пғ^Оұ)ВІ = I` and anticommutation),
Ex. 2.40 (p. 77, commutator, whence the cyclic products).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `pauliX/Y/Z_isHermitian` | `(Пғ^Оұ)вҖ  = Пғ^Оұ` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX/Y/Z_mul_self` | `(Пғ^Оұ)ВІ = I` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_mul_pauliY` etc. | `Пғ^x Пғ^y = iВ·Пғ^z` (cyclic) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_anticomm_pauliY` etc. | `Пғ^Оұ Пғ^ОІ + Пғ^ОІ Пғ^Оұ = 0` (Оұ вү  ОІ) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |

### Spin-1/2 rotation operators (Tasaki В§2.1 eq. (2.1.26))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, В§2.1 eq. (2.1.26), p. 17 (closed form) and eq. (2.1.23),
p. 16 (`Гӣ_{2ПҖ} = -1` for half-odd-integer spin).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3` | `Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё := cos(Оё/2) В· 1 - 2i В· sin(Оё/2) В· Ең^(Оұ)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_zero` | `Гӣ^(Оұ)_0 = 1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_adjoint` | `(Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё)вҖ  = Гӣ^(Оұ)_{-Оё}` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_two_pi` | `Гӣ^(Оұ)_{2ПҖ} = -1` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.23)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_mul` | group law `Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё В· Гӣ^(Оұ)_ПҶ = Гӣ^(Оұ)_{Оё+ПҶ}` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_unitary` | unitarity `Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё В· (Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё)вҖ  = 1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi` | `Гӣ^(Оұ)_ПҖ = -2i В· Ең^(Оұ)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi_sq` | `(Гӣ^(Оұ)_ПҖ)ВІ = -1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_pi_anticomm_spinHalfRot2_pi` (and cyclic) | `{Гӣ^(Оұ)_ПҖ, Гӣ^(ОІ)_ПҖ} = 0` for `Оұ вү  ОІ` (Tasaki (2.1.25)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi_conjTranspose` | `(Гӣ^(Оұ)_ПҖ)вҖ  = 2i В· Ең^(Оұ)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_pi_mul_spinHalfRot2_pi` (and cyclic) | `Гӣ^(Оұ)_ПҖ В· Гӣ^(ОІ)_ПҖ = Гӣ^(Оі)_ПҖ` (Tasaki (2.1.29), S=1/2) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi_conj_spinHalfOp_*` | axis invariance and sign flip at Оё=ПҖ (Tasaki (2.1.15)/(2.1.21)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{2,3,1}` | `(Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё)вҖ  Ең^(ОІ) Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё = cos Оё В· Ең^(ОІ) - sin Оё В· Ең^(Оі)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.16), even-Оө cyclic triple) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{3,1,2}` | `(Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё)вҖ  Ең^(ОІ) Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё = cos Оё В· Ең^(ОІ) + sin Оё В· Ең^(Оі)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.16), odd-Оө triple) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | same-axis invariance `(Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё)вҖ  Ең^(Оұ) Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё = Ең^(Оұ)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.15)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_half_pi_conj_spinHalfOp_*` | `ПҖ/2`-rotation conjugation `(Гӣ^(Оұ)_{ПҖ/2})вҖ  Ең^(ОІ) Гӣ^(Оұ)_{ПҖ/2} = -Оө^{ОұОІОі} Ең^(Оі)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.22), 6 cases) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_eq_exp` | `Гӣ^(3)_Оё = exp(-iОё Ең^(3))` via `Matrix.exp_diagonal` + Euler (Problem 2.1.b, axis 3) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_mul_spinHalfRot2_mulVec_spinHalfUp` | `Гӣ^(3)_ПҶ Гӣ^(2)_Оё |ПҲ^вҶ‘вҹ© = e^{-iПҶ/2} cos(Оё/2) |ПҲ^вҶ‘вҹ© + e^{iПҶ/2} sin(Оё/2) |ПҲ^вҶ“вҹ©` (coherent state, Problem 2.1.d) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_mul_spinHalfRot2_mulVec_spinHalfDown` | `Гӣ^(3)_ПҶ Гӣ^(2)_Оё |ПҲ^вҶ“вҹ© = -e^{-iПҶ/2} sin(Оё/2) |ПҲ^вҶ‘вҹ© + e^{iПҶ/2} cos(Оё/2) |ПҲ^вҶ“вҹ©` (rotation of spin-down, Problem 2.2.c auxiliary) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_half_pi_mul_spinHalfRot2_half_pi_mulVec_spinHalfUp` | specialization at Оё = ПҶ = ПҖ/2 (Problem 2.1.e) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfDotVec` / `spinHalfDotVec_isHermitian` | vector inner product `Ең В· v := ОЈ_Оұ v_Оұ Ең^(Оұ)` and its Hermiticity (cf. (2.1.19)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_commute_spinHalfOp3_smul` | same-axis rotation commutes with `v В· Ең^(3)` (cf. (2.1.20) along axis) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard` / `hadamard_mul_self` | the Hadamard basis-change matrix `W = (1/вҲҡ2)В·!![1,1;1,-1]` and `WВ·W = 1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard_mul_spinHalfOp1_mul_hadamard` | `W В· Ең^(1) В· W = Ең^(3)` (basis change between Пғ^x and Пғ^z) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard_mul_spinHalfOp3_mul_hadamard` | `W В· Ең^(3) В· W = Ең^(1)` (inverse basis change) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_eq_hadamard_conj` | `Гӣ^(1)_Оё = W В· Гӣ^(3)_Оё В· W` (axis 1 rotation as Hadamard conjugate of axis 3) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_eq_exp` | `Гӣ^(1)_Оё = exp(-iОё Ең^(1))` via Hadamard conjugation + `Matrix.exp_conj` (Problem 2.1.b, axis 1) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `yDiag` / `yDiagAdj` / `yDiag_mul_yDiagAdj` / `yDiag_mul_spinHalfOp3_mul_yDiagAdj` | y-axis basis-change unitary `V` with `VВ·VвҖ  = 1` and `VВ·Ең^(3)В·VвҖ  = Ең^(2)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot2_eq_yDiag_conj` / `spinHalfRot2_eq_exp` | `Гӣ^(2)_Оё = VВ·Гӣ^(3)_ОёВ·VвҖ ` and `Гӣ^(2)_Оё = exp(-iОё Ең^(2))` (Problem 2.1.b, axis 2) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |

### 3D rotation matrices `R^(Оұ)_Оё` (general Оё, Tasaki В§2.1 eq. (2.1.11))

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `rot3D{1,2,3} Оё` | 3Г—3 real rotation matrices by angle Оё about each axis | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |
| `rot3D{1,2,3}_zero` | `R^(Оұ)_0 = 1` | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |
| `rot3D{1,2,3}_pi` | `R^(Оұ)_ПҖ` from general formula matches explicit ПҖ-rotation | `Quantum/Rotation3D.lean` |

### ZвӮӮ Г— ZвӮӮ representation (Tasaki В§2.1 eqs. (2.1.27)-(2.1.34))

The ZвӮӮ Г— ZвӮӮ structure is proved across files:
- S = 1/2 (projective, eq. (2.1.31)): `spinHalfRot*_pi_sq = -1`, anticommutation, products.
- S = 1 (genuine, eq. (2.1.27)): `spinOnePiRot*_sq = 1`, commutation.

See `Quantum/Z2Z2.lean` for the unified documentation.

Systems*, В§2.1 Problem 2.1.a, p. 15.

| Lean name | Statement | File |

| `pauliCoeff0/1/2/3` | explicit coefficient functions | `Quantum/SpinHalfDecomp.lean` |
| `pauli_decomposition` | `A = ОЈбөў cбөў В· Пғ^(i)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfDecomp.lean` |
| `spinHalf_decomposition` | same via `Ең^(Оұ) = Пғ^(Оұ) / 2` | `Quantum/SpinHalfDecomp.lean` |
| `pauli_linearIndep` | `{1, Пғ^x, Пғ^y, Пғ^z}` is linearly independent | `Quantum/SpinHalfDecomp.lean` |

### Polynomial-basis decomposition for S = 1 (Tasaki В§2.1 Problem 2.1.a, S = 1)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum
Many-Body Systems*, В§2.1 Problem 2.1.a, p. 15 + solution S.1, p. 493.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinOneProj{Plus,Zero,Minus}` | the three diagonal projectors `\|ПҲ^Пғвҹ©вҹЁПҲ^Пғ\|` (Пғ вҲҲ {+1, 0, -1}) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneProj{Plus,Zero,Minus}_eq_polynomial` | each diagonal projector equals a polynomial in `Ең^(3)` (Lagrange interpolation) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,02,10,12,20,21}` | the six off-diagonal matrix units `\|ПҲ^П„вҹ©вҹЁПҲ^Пғ\|` (П„ вү  Пғ) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,12}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/вҲҡ2) Ең^- В· P_Пғ` for the two single-step lowering units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{10,21}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/вҲҡ2) Ең^+ В· P_Пғ` for the two single-step raising units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit02_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (Ең^-)ВІ В· P_+` for the double-step lowering unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit20_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (Ең^+)ВІ В· P_-` for the double-step raising unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOne_decomposition` | every 3Г—3 complex matrix is a linear combination of the 9 matrix units (entry-wise); combined with the polynomial expressions above this gives Tasaki Problem 2.1.a for `S = 1` | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
