### Time-reversal map for `S = 1/2` (Tasaki В§2.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, В§2.3 eqs. (2.3.4)вҖ“(2.3.8), pp. 26вҖ“27.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `timeReversalSpinHalf` | `ОҳМӮ(v) = (-conj(v 1), conj(v 0))` (Tasaki eq. (2.3.6), `S = 1/2`); the antilinear unitary `Г»_2 В· KМӮ` of В§2.3 | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfUp` / `_spinHalfDown` | `ОҳМӮ|ПҲ^вҶ‘вҹ© = |ПҲ^вҶ“вҹ©` and `ОҳМӮ|ПҲ^вҶ“вҹ© = -|ПҲ^вҶ‘вҹ©` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_add` | additivity `ОҳМӮ(v + w) = ОҳМӮ(v) + ОҳМӮ(w)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_smul` | **antilinearity** in the scalar: `ОҳМӮ(c вҖў v) = (conj c) вҖў ОҳМӮ(v)` (Tasaki В§2.3 antilinearity warning, pp. 26вҖ“27) | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_sq` | **Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki eq. (2.3.8), half-odd-integer): `ОҳМӮВІ = -1МӮ` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | spin sign flip (Tasaki eq. (2.3.14)): `ОҳМӮ(Ең^(Оұ) В· v) = (-Ең^(Оұ)) В· (ОҳМӮ v)` for `Оұ = 1, 2, 3` вҖ” equivariance form of `ОҳМӮ Ең^(Оұ) ОҳМӮвҒ»В№ = -Ең^(Оұ)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `complexConjugationSpinHalf` | the antilinear complex-conjugation map `KМӮ` of Tasaki В§2.3 eq. (2.3.4) at `S = 1/2`: `KМӮ(v) i := conj(v i)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `complexConjugationSpinHalf_sq` / `_add` / `_smul` | `KМӮ` is involutive (`KМӮВІ = id`), additive, and antilinear in the scalar | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_eq_spinHalfRot2_pi_mulVec` | the **factorisation identity** of Tasaki В§2.3: `ОҳМӮ = Г»_2 В· KМӮ` where `Г»_2 = spinHalfRot2 ПҖ` is the ПҖ rotation about the `2`-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `flipConfig` / `flipConfig_apply` / `flipConfig_involutive` | the spin-flip on a many-body configuration `Пғ : Оӣ вҶ’ Fin 2`: `flipConfig Пғ x := 1 - Пғ x`; involutive | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign` / `timeReversalSign_zero` / `_one` / `_mul_flip` | per-site sign factor `Оө(0) = 1`, `Оө(1) = -1` for the multi-spin time-reversal, with `Оө(s) В· Оө(1 - s) = -1` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` | multi-spin time-reversal map (Tasaki В§2.3 lattice extension, `S = 1/2`): `(ОҳМӮ_tot v) П„ := (вҲҸ_x Оө(П„ x)) В· conj(v (flip П„))` for finite `Оӣ` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_conj` / `timeReversalSign_prod_mul_flip` | the product `вҲҸ_x Оө(П„ x)` is real (conjugation invariant); the cross product equals `(-1)^|Оӣ|` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_sq` | **Multi-spin Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki В§2.3 half-odd-integer extension): `ОҳМӮ_totВІ = (-1)^|Оӣ| В· 1МӮ` вҖ” `+1МӮ` when `|Оӣ|` is even, `-1МӮ` when odd | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec` | action of `ОҳМӮ_tot` on a many-body basis state: `ОҳМӮ_tot |ОЁ_Пғвҹ© = (вҲҸ_x Оө(flip Пғ x)) В· |ОЁ_{flip Пғ}вҹ©` вҖ” natural many-body generalisation of `ОҳМӮ|вҶ‘вҹ© = |вҶ“вҹ©` and `ОҳМӮ|вҶ“вҹ© = -|вҶ‘вҹ©` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliZ_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `Пғ^z` (Tasaki В§2.3 (2.3.14) lifted to many-body): `ОҳМӮ_tot ((onSite x Пғ^z) v) = (-(onSite x Пғ^z))(ОҳМӮ_tot v)`. Diagonal-action case; `Пғ^x`, `Пғ^y` deferred | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `siteFlipAt` / `siteFlipAt_self` / `siteFlipAt_of_ne` / `flipConfig_siteFlipAt_comm` / `siteFlipAt_involutive` | per-site flip helpers: `siteFlipAt П„ x` flips slot `x` only; commutes with `flipConfig`; involutive. The combinatorial primitive underlying off-diagonal `Пғ^x_x` / `Пғ^y_x` action (deferred) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_basisVec` | basis-state action of the off-diagonal site Pauli: `(onSite x Пғ^x).mulVec |ОЁ_Пғвҹ© = |ОЁ_{siteFlipAt Пғ x}вҹ©` (the spin at site `x` is swapped) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `pauliX_eq_indicator` / `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_apply` | closed-form `pauliX a b = if b = 1 - a then 1 else 0`, lifted to `((onSite x Пғ^x).mulVec v) П„ = v (siteFlipAt П„ x)` for any state `v` (general extension of the basis-state action) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_siteFlipAt` | `вҲҸ_y Оө((siteFlipAt П„ x) y) = -(вҲҸ_y Оө(П„ y))` вҖ” the per-site flip swaps `Оө(П„ x)` with `Оө(1 - П„ x) = -Оө(П„ x)`, flipping the total sign | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliX_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `Пғ^x` (Tasaki В§2.3 (2.3.14) at Оұ = 1): `ОҳМӮ_tot ((onSite x Пғ^x) v) = (-(onSite x Пғ^x))(ОҳМӮ_tot v)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliY_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `Пғ^y` (Tasaki В§2.3 (2.3.14) at Оұ = 2): `ОҳМӮ_tot ((onSite x Пғ^y) v) = (-(onSite x Пғ^y))(ОҳМӮ_tot v)`. The proof handles the per-site `Вұi` factor via `conj(pauliY_sign(1 - s)) = pauliY_sign(s)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_add` / `_smul` / `_real_smul` | multi-spin `ОҳМӮ_tot` is additive, antilinear in the scalar (`ОҳМӮ_tot(c вҖў v) = conj(c) вҖў ОҳМӮ_tot v`), and real-linear (special case of antilinearity at real `r`) вҖ” foundational for lifting Pauli-axis equivariance to bilinear / Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian forms | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | Tasaki В§2.3 (2.3.14) for spin-1/2 ops `Ең^(Оұ) = Пғ^(Оұ) / 2`: `ОҳМӮ_tot ((onSite x Ең^(Оұ)) v) = (-(onSite x Ең^(Оұ)))(ОҳМӮ_tot v)` for Оұ = 1, 2, 3 вҖ” direct corollaries of the Pauli versions by scalar (1/2) multiplication | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_spinHalfDot_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the bilinear `Ең_x В· Ең_y`** (Tasaki В§2.3): `ОҳМӮ_tot ((Ең_x В· Ең_y) v) = (Ең_x В· Ең_y)(ОҳМӮ_tot v)` вҖ” two equivariance `-1` factors cancel; sums per-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki В§2.3): for real coupling `J` (`conj(J(x,y)) = J(x,y)`), `ОҳМӮ_tot ((H J) v) = (H J)(ОҳМӮ_tot v)`. Combines per-bond invariance + ОҳМӮ_tot antilinearity (J reality) + additivity (over double-sum) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_conj` / `periodicChainCoupling_conj` | every entry of `openChainCoupling N J` (resp. `periodicChainCoupling N J`) is real (under complex conjugation), since `J : в„қ` makes `(-(J : в„Ӯ))` real-valued | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_openChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_periodicChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareTorusHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_cubicLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` | concrete time-reversal invariance: the open / periodic chain, the 2D open square / torus, and the 3D cubic Heisenberg Hamiltonians all commute with `ОҳМӮ_tot` for any real coupling `J : в„қ`. Backed by `*Coupling_conj` reality lemmas in `HeisenbergChain.lean` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec_upDown` / `_basisVec_basisSwap_upDown` | `ОҳМӮ_tot |вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© = -|вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©` and `ОҳМӮ_tot |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ© = -|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ©` on `Fin 2` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_singlet` | the two-site spin singlet `|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© - |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©` is **time-reversal invariant** (Tasaki В§2.3 / В§A.3): being the SU(2) `S = 0` representation, it survives `ОҳМӮ_tot` unchanged | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_triplet_zero` | the triplet `m = 0` state `|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© + |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©` is **anti-invariant** under `ОҳМӮ_tot`: `ОҳМӮ_tot (|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© + |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©) = -(|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© + |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©)` (the symmetric combination picks up a minus sign from the per-basis-vector flip) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

### Multi-body operator space (abstract lattice)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, В§2.2, pp. 21-26 (tensor-product Hilbert space and site-local
operators). The lattice `Оӣ` is an arbitrary finite set with decidable
equality; specializing to `Оӣ = Fin N` recovers an `N`-site chain.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `ManyBodyOp Оӣ` | `Matrix (Оӣ вҶ’ Fin 2) (Оӣ вҶ’ Fin 2) в„Ӯ` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite i A` | site-embedded operator at `i : Оӣ` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_isHermitian` | `A.IsHermitian вҶ’ (onSite i A).IsHermitian` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_add`, `onSite_sub`, `onSite_zero`, `onSite_smul`, `onSite_one` | linearity of the site embedding and `onSite i 1 = 1` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | distinct-site commutation (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x вү  y`, S = 1/2) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec` / `onSite_mulVec_basisVec` | tensor-product basis states and their action under site operators (Tasaki (2.2.1)/(2.2.4)) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_same` | same-site product `onSite i A В· onSite i B = onSite i (A В· B)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_same` | same-site commutator `[onSite i A, onSite i B] = onSite i [A, B]` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | commuting Hermitians multiply Hermitian (generic; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4342) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |

### Generic matrix-analysis helpers (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/`)

Topic-organized generic linear-algebra facts extracted from the physics files where they had been
re-proved as private helpers (Issue [#4339](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4339)).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` | a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | the product of two commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_isHermitian` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_sq_of_sq_one` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting involutions is an involution | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mul_self_of_idempotent` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting idempotents is idempotent | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mulVec_eq_self` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of matrices each fixing `ПҲ` also fixes `ПҲ` | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | the trace of a Hermitian matrix is real | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `Tr(AВ·B)` of two Hermitian matrices is conjugation-invariant | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |

(The two trace helpers above are also listed in the Gibbs-state section where they are consumed; the
canonical definitions live in `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean`.)

These are consumed by the JordanвҖ“Wigner string / Hubbard hard-core projection layers (PR #4342),
replacing the per-file private copies in `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean`,
`Fermion/JordanWigner/Operators.lean`, and `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean`.

### HorschвҖ“von der Linden low-lying states (Tasaki В§3.4, Theorem 3.1)

Start of the Chapters 3вҖ“10 backfill (Issue
[#4485](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4485); book order, infinite systems in
scope). Tasaki В§3.4, Theorem 3.1, eqs. (3.4.7)вҖ“(3.4.12), pp. 66вҖ“67.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `horsch_vonderLinden_lowLying` | for Hermitian `H` with min eigenvalue `EвӮҖ = eigenvalues iвӮҖ`, a unit state `О“` orthogonal to the ground eigenvector with `вҹЁО“,HО“вҹ© вүӨ EвӮҖ+Оҙ` yields an energy eigenstate `j вү  iвӮҖ` with `EвӮҖ вүӨ E_j вүӨ EвӮҖ+Оҙ` (a low-lying state; possibly another ground state if degenerate, as Tasaki notes) | `Quantum/HorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `kaplan_horsch_vonderLinden_order_lower_bound` | **Theorem 3.2, finite-volume core** (В§3.4; the thermodynamic double limit 3.4.22 is not formalized): for the field-perturbed ground state `ОЁ` of `H вҲ’ hВ·O` (`h>0`) and any trial `Оһ`, the order parameter obeys `вҹЁОһ,OОһвҹ© + (EвӮҖвҲ’вҹЁОһ,HОһвҹ©)/h вүӨ вҹЁОЁ,OОЁвҹ©` (eq. (3.4.21), the variational core; the double limit `LвҶ‘вҲһ,hвҶ“0` gives the вҲҡqвӮҖ SSB bound) | `Quantum/KaplanHorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS` / `staggeredOrderOpS_isHermitian` | **Theorem 4.1** (В§4.1, DysonвҖ“LiebвҖ“Simon, toward; eq. (4.1.7)): the staggered (NГ©el) order operator `Г” = ОЈ_x Оө_x Ең_x^(3)` (`Оө_x = Вұ1` sublattice sign) and its Hermiticity (real Вұ1 combination of Hermitian `Ең_x^(3)`), so `вҹЁОҰ,Г”ВІОҰвҹ©` is a real order parameter. The NГ©el-LRO statement needs the actual hypercubic torus + reflection positivity (false for arbitrary bipartite graphs); deferred pending that infrastructure (infinite-volume in scope) | `Quantum/SpinS/DysonLiebSimon.lean` |
| `shastry_no_symmetry_breaking_1d` | **Theorem 4.2** (В§4.1, Shastry, AXIOM; eqs. (4.1.9)вҖ“(4.1.10)): absence of SSB in 1D. For the 1D AFM Heisenberg ring under a staggered field `ДӨ_h = ОЈ_x Ең_xВ·Ең_{x+1} вҲ’ hВ·Г”_L^(3)`, the per-site staggered order parameter of any normalized ground state vanishes in the iterated limit `lim_{hвҶ“0} lim_{LвҶ‘вҲһ}` (eq. (4.1.10), ОөвҖ“Оҙ form). Tasaki states it without proof; the deep argument is now being formalized as the **reflection-positivity infrastructure project** (#4777, codex-validated 11-PR plan). Defines `ringCoupling`, `ringStaggeredSublattice`, `staggeredFieldChainHamiltonianS`. **RP infra layer 1 (in progress):** `RingBondReflection.lean` вҖ” even-ring bond reflection `ringReflect n x = 2nвҲ’1вҲ’x` (involutive, half-swap, staggered-sign flip); `RingReflectionTheta.lean` вҖ” reflection map `Оё(A) Пғ П„ = conj(A(ПҒПғ)(ПҒП„))`, an antilinear `*`-automorphism, with the single-site bridge `Оё(onSiteS x A) = onSiteS (ringReflect n x) (conj A)`. **RP infra layer 2** (`RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`): the Hamiltonian Оё-decomposition вҖ” orientation reversal `J(r x)(r y)=J y x`, spin-op conjugation (SВ№/SВі real, SВІ imaginary), `Оё(Ең_xВ·Ең_y)=Ең_{r x}В·Ең_{r y}`, `Оё(Г”_L^(3))=вҲ’Г”_L^(3)` (staggered-sign flip), `Оё(ДӨ)=ДӨ` (reindex + adjacency + dot-comm), and `Оё(ДӨ_h)=ДӨ_{вҲ’h}` (the reflection symmetry exchanging the two staggered ground states). **RP infra layer 3** (`RingReflectionPositivity.lean`): the left-half subalgebra `SupportedOnLeftS` = `B(H_left) вҠ— I_right` (two conditions: entries vanish off the right-diagonal **and** are independent of the common right-half value) with closure (zero/one/add/smul, left-site `onSiteS`), `Оё` maps left-supported to right-supported (`theta_right`), and the reflection-positive functional predicate `ReflectionPositiveFunctionalS` (`0 вүӨ Re ПҶ(Оё(A)В·A)` for left-supported `A`). **RP infra layer 4** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`): the `ОІ = 0` base case вҖ” `traceFunctional_reflectionPositive` proves the trace functional `X вҶҰ Tr X` is reflection positive, i.e. `0 вүӨ Re Tr(Оё(A)В·A)` for every left-supported `A`. The proof collapses `Tr(Оё(A)В·A) = вҲ‘_{Пғ,Ој} Оё(A)ПғОјВ·AОјПғ` to its diagonal (both support conditions force `Ој=Пғ`), then factorizes over the left/right configuration split (`configSplitEquiv`, from `finSumFinEquiv`): the diagonal value `A Пғ Пғ` depends only on the left half, giving `Tr(Оё(A)В·A) = conj S В· S = вҖ–SвҖ–ВІ вүҘ 0` with `S = вҲ‘_в„“ D в„“`. This is the infinite-temperature trace cone on which a later layer mounts the Gibbs exponential via Trotter. **RP infra layer 5** (`RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`): the algebraic completion of the cone вҖ” `SupportedOnLeftS.mul` (the left-half subalgebra is closed under products), `SupportedOnLeftS.mul_theta_comm` (a left-supported `A` commutes with `Оё(B)`, which acts on the right half), and the **weighted trace cone** `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive` (`X вҶҰ Tr((Оё(C)В·C)В·X)` is reflection positive for left-supported `C`, via `Tr((ОёCВ·C)В·(ОёAВ·A)) = Tr(Оё(CВ·A)В·(CВ·A)) вүҘ 0`) plus its nonnegative-finite-combination version `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive_finsetSum` вҖ” the cone on which the Trotter/Lie-product factors of the Gibbs exponential will be mounted. **RP infra layer 6** (`RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`): the reflection-positive trace-weight cone вҖ” `RPTraceWeightS M` (`X вҶҰ Tr(MВ·X)` is an RP functional), the cone-representability predicate `RPTraceConeRepS` (nonnegative finite combination `вҲ‘ cбөў Оё(Cбөў)В·Cбөў`) with closure under `one`/`zero`/`add`/`smul_nonneg`/`mul`/`pow`, with the product closure via the **four-operator generalization** `weightGen_mul`: `(Оё(A)В·B)В·(Оё(A')В·B') = Оё(AВ·A')В·(BВ·B')` (weakest hypotheses: only `B, A'` need be left-supported; `A, B'` unconstrained; specializes to `(ОёCВ·C)(ОёDВ·D) = Оё(CD)В·(CD)` for the diagonal cone; the off-diagonal `A вү  B` case closes the field-crossing product of `RPTwoFieldConeRepS.mul`). **Field-dependent crossing cone:** `RPTwoFieldConeRepS n N P` (field-dependent two-field cone representation: a field-independent index `О№` with nonnegative weights `c`, field-dependent generators `C i z` (left-supported at each field), such that `P u v = вҲ‘бөў cбөў вҖў (Оё(C i v) В· C i u)` for every field pair; diagonal `u = v` is a genuine cone; off-diagonal `u вү  v` is the field-crossing form) with closure under `one`/`zero`/`add`/`smul_nonneg`/`mul`/`pow`/`expSeriesPartialSum` (fieldwise from the field-independent cone operations) and the **field-free bridge** `RPTraceConeRepS.toField` (a constant family `C i z := ДҲ i` lifts `RPTraceConeRepS` to `RPTwoFieldConeRepS`, recovering it as the `u = v`, constant-in-field degenerate case вҖ” not a duplicate). These give the shared field-crossing cone family that the three slots of the two-field reflection CauchyвҖ“Schwarz of Tasaki (4.1.51)/(4.1.69) (pp. 89вҖ“93; DLS 1978 В§2вҖ“3) consume. Additional: `RPTraceConeRepS.rpTraceWeight` (representable вҹ№ RP trace weight, from the weighted cone), and `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` (RP trace weights are closed under limits, by finite-dimensional trace continuity) вҖ” the cone on which the next layer mounts the interaction Gibbs exponential `exp(tВ·вҲ‘cбөўОё(Cбөў)В·Cбөў)` as a limit of cone-representable partial sums (PR #4991). **RP infra layer 7** (`RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`): `rpInteractionExp_reflectionPositive` вҖ” for a nonnegative finite interaction `D = tВ·вҲ‘бөў cбөў Оё(Cбөў)В·Cбөў` (`t, cбөў вүҘ 0`, `Cбөў` left-supported), the matrix exponential `exp D` is a reflection-positive trace weight. Each partial sum `вҲ‘_{k<m}(k!)вҒ»В№DбөҸ` of the exponential series is cone-representable (`smul_nonneg`/`add`/`pow`), hence an RP trace weight, and `exp D` is their limit (`expSeries_summable'.hasSum.tendsto_sum_nat`), so `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` applies. Uses the `LвҲһ`-operator-norm Banach structure (`open scoped Matrix.Norms.Operator`), whose topology is the entrywise (Pi) topology вҖ” the same one underlying the trace continuity, so no topology diamond. **RP infra layer 8** (`RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`): the matrix exponential and the left subalgebra / reflection map вҖ” `SupportedOnLeftS.exp` (`X` left-supported вҹ№ `exp X` left-supported: the left subalgebra is closed under products/sums/scalars/entrywise-limits via `SupportedOnLeftS.of_tendsto`, and `exp X` is the limit of its partial sums) and `ringReflectionThetaS_exp` (`Оё(exp X) = exp(Оё X)`: `Оё` is a continuous conjugate-linear `*`-automorphism with real exponential coefficients) вҖ” the building blocks for the `e^{-ОІH_L}` Hamiltonian factor of the full Gibbs reflection-positivity decomposition | `Quantum/SpinS/ShastryNoSSB.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingBondReflection.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTheta.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionPositivity.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean` |
| `refLeftSum` | **RP infra layer 4b** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, В§4.1, Shastry base / PR #4977): the left-diagonal coordinate `S(A) = вҲ‘_в„“ A_{(в„“,в„“),(в„“,в„“)}`, the scalar "square root" of a left-supported operator entering the reflection-positive factorization `Tr(Оё(A)В·B) = conj(S A)В·S B`; fundamental to the genuine bilinear generalization of trace positivity | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean` |
| `trace_theta_mul_eq_refLeftSum_mul` | **RP infra layer 4b** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, В§4.1, Shastry base / PR #4977): the **bilinear reflection base identity** `Tr(Оё(A)В·B) = conj(S A)В·S B` for left-supported operators, the genuine bilinear generalization (and proof engine) of trace reflection positivity; the foundation on which the Gibbs / doubled CauchyвҖ“Schwarz layer of the DysonвҖ“LiebвҖ“Simon / Shastry argument is built (Tasaki В§4.1, Theorem 4.2, as infrastructure toward its proof) | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean` |
| `twoField_product_pairing` | **RP infra layer 7b** (`RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2 / PR #4990): the two-field **SWEEP operator identity** вҖ” the doubled DysonвҖ“LiebвҖ“Simon approximant `(g(x)В·Оё(g(y))В·вҲ‘бөў wбөўВ·(Оё(Cбөў(y))В·Cбөў(x)))^m` (with left-supported kinetic family `g`, nonnegative weights `wбөў вүҘ 0`, and **FIELD-DEPENDENT** left-supported cone generators `Cбөў(z)` вҖ” the reflected pile carrying the `y`-field factor `Cбөў(y)`, the non-reflected pile the `x`-field factor `Cбөў(x)`) collapses into a nonnegative reflection pairing `вҲ‘вӮ– vвӮ–В·(Оё(рқ“ӣвӮ–(y))В·рқ“ӣвӮ–(x))` over the crossing family `Оә = О№бөҗ`, where `vвӮ– = вҲҸ w вүҘ 0` and the pattern-shared family `рқ“ӣвӮ–(z)` (left-supported, depending on the field only through `z`) carries the SAME crossing sequence in both piles вҖ” proved by induction via `sweep_operator_identity` (the single SWEEP step) with the disjoint-support commutation and `Оё`'s multiplicativity. The field-free pairing is the constant specialization `Cбөў(z) := Cбөў` (Tasaki В§4.1 Theorem 4.2, crossing identity (4.1.65)вҖ“(4.1.69), p. 90). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean` |
| `twoField_product_pairing_trace` | **RP infra layer 7b** (`RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2 / PR #4990): taking the trace of the SWEEP identity `twoField_product_pairing` and applying the bilinear reflection base identity `trace_theta_mul_eq_refLeftSum_mul` termwise yields the weighted в„“ВІ Gram form `Tr(U) = вҲ‘вӮ– vвӮ– В· conj(refLeftSum рқ“ӣвӮ–(y)) В· refLeftSum рқ“ӣвӮ–(x)` (the doubled reflection CauchyвҖ“Schwarz pairing consumed by the next PR7b-iii-b/c layer) вҖ” field-dependent crossing generators (Tasaki В§4.1 Theorem 4.2, (4.1.65)вҖ“(4.1.69), p. 90). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldPairing.lean` |
| `falkBruch_double_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkвҖ“Bruch** (`FalkBruchDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the concrete ground-state FalkвҖ“Bruch inequality вҖ” for a ground state `ОҰ`, Hermitian `A` and a potential `y` for `AОҰ` (`(HвҲ’EвӮҖ)y = AОҰ`), `2(ReвҹЁОҰ,AВІОҰвҹ©)ВІ вүӨ ReвҹЁОҰ,[A,[H,A]]ОҰвҹ©В·ReвҹЁy,AОҰвҹ©`; combines the abstract FalkвҖ“Bruch (`K = HвҲ’EвӮҖ` PSD) with the double-commutator = variational-energy identity | `Quantum/SpinS/FalkBruchDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `hermitianSubMin_posSemidef` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkвҖ“Bruch** (`HermitianSubMinPosSemidef.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the Hamiltonian shifted to its ground energy `H вҲ’ EвӮҖ` (`EвӮҖ = hermitianMinEigenvalue`) is positive-semidefinite вҖ” the quadratic form is real (Hermiticity, `isHermitian_dotProduct_mulVec_im_zero`) with nonnegative real part (the variational lower bound); this is the PSD `K` fed into the FalkвҖ“Bruch inequality | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianSubMinPosSemidef.lean` |
| `falkBruch_of_mulVec_eq` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkвҖ“Bruch** (`FalkBruch.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the abstract ground-state FalkвҖ“Bruch inequality вҖ” for PSD `K` and `w` in the range of `K` (`K y = w`), `(ReвҹЁw,wвҹ©)ВІ вүӨ ReвҹЁw,K wвҹ©В·ReвҹЁy,wвҹ©`, an immediate instance of the PSD CauchyвҖ“Schwarz (`posSemidef_re_dotProduct_mulVec_sq_le`) with second argument `y`; no spectral theory / pseudo-inverse needed | `Quantum/SpinS/FalkBruch.lean` |
| `double_commutator_ground_state_nonneg` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkвҖ“Bruch** (`DoubleCommutatorNonneg.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ground-state double commutator `ReвҹЁОҰ, [A,[H,A]] ОҰвҹ© вүҘ 0` for Hermitian `A` and a minimal-eigenvalue eigenvector `ОҰ` вҖ” it equals twice the variational energy of `AОҰ`, nonnegative since the ground energy is the Rayleigh minimum (`hermitianMinEigenvalue_mul_dotProduct_re_le_rayleighOnVec`); the sign of the FalkвҖ“Bruch numerator | `Quantum/SpinS/DoubleCommutatorNonneg.lean` |
| `double_commutator_ground_state_eq` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkвҖ“Bruch** (`DoubleCommutatorVariational.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): for Hermitian `H`, `A` and a ground state `HОҰ = EвӮҖОҰ` (`EвӮҖ` real), the f-sum-rule oscillator strength equals twice the variational energy of `AОҰ` вҖ” `вҹЁОҰ, [A,[H,A]] ОҰвҹ© = 2вҹЁAОҰ, H AОҰвҹ© вҲ’ 2EвӮҖвҹЁAОҰ, AОҰвҹ©` (Hermitian-shift); the denominator of the ground-state FalkвҖ“Bruch inequality | `Quantum/SpinS/DoubleCommutatorVariational.lean` |
| `spinSDot_double_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`SingleBondDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the per-bond double commutator is the negated transverse bond energy `[Ең_a^{(3)}, [Ең_aВ·Ең_b, Ең_a^{(3)}]] = вҲ’(Ең_a^{(1)}Ең_b^{(1)} + Ең_a^{(2)}Ең_b^{(2)})` (the f-sum-rule integrand / oscillator strength of the staggered mode), bounded by the bond energy | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_commutator_onSiteS` | **Cor 4.3 / IR bound** (`HeisenbergSpinSOp3Commutator.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the Heisenberg Hamiltonian commutator distributes over bonds `[ДӨ_J, Ең_z^{(3)}] = ОЈ_x ОЈ_y J_{xy} [Ең_xВ·Ең_y, Ең_z^{(3)}]`; with the per-bond case analysis this reduces the spin-current divergence `[ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}]` to the two bonds incident to `z` | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergSpinSOp3Commutator.lean` |
| `spinSDot_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` / `_right` | **Cor 4.3 / IR bound** (`SingleBondSpinSOp3Commutator.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the single-bond spin current `[Ең_aВ·Ең_b, Ең_a^{(3)}] = i(Ең_a^{(1)} Ең_b^{(2)} вҲ’ Ең_a^{(2)} Ең_b^{(1)})` for `a вү  b` (only the transverse bond components fail to commute with `Ең_a^{(3)}`, via the cyclic SU(2) commutators; `_right` is the symmetric right-endpoint companion `[Ең_aВ·Ең_b, Ең_b^{(3)}]`, and `_eq_zero_of_ne` is the off-bond vanishing `[Ең_xВ·Ең_y, Ең_z^{(3)}] = 0` for `z вҲү {x,y}`); the building block of `[ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}]` and the double commutator in the infrared / f-sum-rule bound | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondSpinSOp3Commutator.lean` |
| `expectation_abs_le_manyBodyOperatorNormS` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`ExpectationNormBound.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the general norm-to-expectation bound вҖ” for any `M` and a normalized state `ОҰ` (`star ОҰ в¬қбөҘ ОҰ = 1`), `|вҹЁОҰ, M ОҰвҹ©.re| вүӨ вҖ–MвҖ–` (normalized-state form of `abs_re_dotProduct_mulVec_le_norm_mul`); converts operator-norm bounds into expectation bounds in the FalkвҖ“Bruch argument | `Quantum/SpinS/ExpectationNormBound.lean` |
| `transverseBondEnergy_expectation_abs_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`TransverseBondEnergyExpectation.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): in a normalized state `ОҰ`, the transverse bond energy expectation `|вҹЁОҰ, (Ең_a^{(1)}Ең_b^{(1)} + Ең_a^{(2)}Ең_b^{(2)}) ОҰвҹ©.re| вүӨ 2NВІ` (the operator-norm bound + the normвҶ’expectation bridge `abs_re_dotProduct_mulVec_le_norm_mul` + `вҖ–ОҰвҖ– = 1`); summed over the `O(L)` adjacent bonds bounds the oscillator strength by `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/TransverseBondEnergyExpectation.lean` |
| `spinSDot_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3_norm_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`SingleBondCommutatorNorm.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the single-bond spin current `[Ең_aВ·Ең_b, Ең_a^{(3)}]` has operator norm `вүӨ 2NВІ` (`вҖ–iвҖ– = 1` + per-site norm bounds + submultiplicativity); the `O(1)` bound per bond, summed over `z`'s two incident bonds, bounds `вҖ–[ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}]вҖ–` by `O(1)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondCommutatorNorm.lean` |
| `transverseBondEnergy_manyBodyOperatorNormS_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`TransverseBondEnergyNorm.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the transverse bond energy (per-bond oscillator strength `= вҲ’[Ең_a^{(3)},[Ең_aВ·Ең_b,Ең_a^{(3)}]]`) has operator norm `вҖ–Ең_a^{(1)}Ең_b^{(1)} + Ең_a^{(2)}Ең_b^{(2)}вҖ– вүӨ 2NВІ` (per-site norm bound `вҖ–Ең^{(Оұ)}вҖ– вүӨ N` + submultiplicativity); summed over the `O(L)` adjacent bonds this bounds the oscillator strength by `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/TransverseBondEnergyNorm.lean` |
| `pair_double_commutator_eq_zero_of_ne` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`PairCommutatorVanish.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): off-pair vanishing `[Ең_x^{(3)}, [ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}]] = 0` for `x вҲү {zвҲ’1, z, z+1}` вҖ” since `[ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}]` is supported on `z`'s two incident bonds, `Ең_x^{(3)}` commutes with it (Commute lemmas); hence the oscillator-strength double sum has only `O(L)` nonzero terms (three per `z`) | `Quantum/SpinS/PairCommutatorVanish.lean` |
| `no_long_range_order_1d_of_susceptibility` | **Cor 4.3 / conditional reduction** (`NoLongRangeOrderConditional.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the exact `Оө`вҖ“`Оҙ` statement of Corollary 4.3 *modulo the susceptibility bound* вҖ” if there is `C вүҘ 0` such that every normalized ground state of an even zero-field ring (`LвүҘ2, Even L`) has a potential `y` for `Г”ОҰ` with `ReвҹЁy,Г”ОҰвҹ© вүӨ CВ·L`, then for every `Оө > 0` there is `LвӮҖ` beyond which every normalized ground state has `\|вҹЁОҰ,Г”ВІОҰвҹ©.re/LВІ\| < Оө` (assembling the `O(L)` oscillator bound + susceptibility reduction + ground-state bridge + an Archimedean `Оө`вҖ“`Оҙ`). This isolates the unconditional Cor 4.3 to the susceptibility bound `ReвҹЁy,Г”ОҰвҹ© вүӨ CВ·L`; that bound is now supplied by the documented Shastry axiom `shastry_staggered_susceptibility_bound`, discharging `no_long_range_order_1d` into a theorem (PR #5003) | `Quantum/SpinS/NoLongRangeOrderConditional.lean` |
| `groundState_mulVec_eq_hermitianMinEigenvalue` | **Cor 4.3 / ground-state bridge** (`HermitianGroundStateEigenvalue.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): a ground-state eigenvalue equals the minimum eigenvalue вҖ” for a Hermitian `H` and a normalized eigenvector `ОҰ` (`HОҰ = EвӮҖвҖўОҰ`) whose `EвӮҖ` has minimal real part over all eigenpairs, `HОҰ = (hermitianMinEigenvalue H)вҖўОҰ` (`EвӮҖ.im = 0` by Hermiticity; `EвӮҖ.re` squeezed between the variational lower bound and the minimizing eigenvector); bridges the "energy-minimizing eigenpair" axiom hypothesis to the FalkвҖ“Bruch `hermitianMinEigenvalue` interface (PR #4847) | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianGroundStateEigenvalue.lean` |
| `staggeredOrder_sq_le_susceptibility` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkвҖ“Bruch** (`StaggeredOrderSusceptibility.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the squared staggered order parameter is bounded by `O(L)` times the susceptibility вҖ” for a ground state `ОҰ` of the zero-field ring (eigenvalue `hermitianMinEigenvalue`) and a potential `y` for `Г”ОҰ` (`(ДӨвҲ’EвӮҖ)y = Г”ОҰ`), `2(ReвҹЁОҰ,Г”ВІОҰвҹ©)ВІ вүӨ 12NВіВ·LВ·ReвҹЁy,Г”ОҰвҹ©` (FalkвҖ“Bruch `2вҹЁГ”ВІвҹ©ВІвүӨoscВ·ПҮ` with the `O(L)` oscillator bound `oscвүӨ12NВіL` and `ПҮ=ReвҹЁy,Г”ОҰвҹ©вүҘ0` PSD); reduces Cor 4.3 to the susceptibility bound `ПҮ вүӨ CВ·L` (PR #4846) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSusceptibility.lean` |
| `oscillatorStrength_abs_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`OscillatorStrengthBound.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the f-sum-rule oscillator strength is `O(L)` вҖ” in a normalized state `ОҰ`, `|вҹЁОҰ, [Г”, [ДӨ, Г”]] ОҰвҹ©.re| вүӨ 12NВіВ·L` (assembling the double-sum form, the off-pair vanishing restricting to three nonzero `z` per `x`, the uniform per-pair norm `вүӨ 4NВі`, and the normвҶ’expectation bridge); the `O(L)` numerator of the ground-state FalkвҖ“Bruch bound (PR #4845) | `Quantum/SpinS/OscillatorStrengthBound.lean` |
| `pair_double_commutator_norm_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`PairCommutatorNorm.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): each summand of the oscillator strength is bounded uniformly in `L` вҖ” `вҖ–[Ең_x^{(3)}, [ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}]]вҖ– вүӨ 4NВі` (commutator norm `вүӨ 2вҖ–Ең^{(3)}вҖ–В·вҖ–[ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}]вҖ–` with `вҖ–Ең^{(3)}вҖ– вүӨ N/2` and `вҖ–[ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}]вҖ– вүӨ 4NВІ`); combined with off-pair vanishing gives `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/PairCommutatorNorm.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3_norm_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingHamiltonianCommutatorNorm.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): since `[ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}]` localizes to `z`'s two incident bonds, its operator norm is `O(1)` вҖ” `вҖ–[ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}]вҖ– вүӨ 4NВІ` (sum of the two single-bond spin-current norms `вүӨ 2NВІ` each); the length-independent locality making the oscillator strength `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingHamiltonianCommutatorNorm.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3_closed` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingHamiltonianCommutatorClosed.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): closed form of the spin-current divergence on a ring `L вүҘ 2` вҖ” `[ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}] = [Ең_zВ·Ең_{z+1}, Ең_z^{(3)}] + [Ең_{zвҲ’1}В·Ең_z, Ең_z^{(3)}]` (the bond sum collapses to `z`'s two incident bonds via `Finset.sum_pair`, every other bond commuting); plus `finRotate_ne_self` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingHamiltonianCommutatorClosed.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingHamiltonianCommutatorBondSum.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ring Hamiltonian commutator with `Ең_z^{(3)}` as a bond sum `[ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}] = ОЈ_x [Ең_xВ·Ең_{x+1}, Ең_z^{(3)}]` (bond-sum form + commutator distribution); since each bond commutator vanishes off `z`, this localizes the spin-current divergence to `z`'s two incident bonds | `Quantum/SpinS/RingHamiltonianCommutatorBondSum.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_eq_bondSum_general` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingBondSumGeneral.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian on `Fin L` of any length as a bond sum `ДӨ = ОЈ_x Ең_xВ·Ең_{finRotate L x}` (directed coupling collapses the defining double sum); the form used to localize `[ДӨ, Ең_z^{(3)}]` to the two bonds incident to `z` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingBondSumGeneral.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_double_commutator_dotProduct` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`OscillatorStrengthForm.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the oscillator strength as a double sum of two-site expectations `вҹЁОҰ,[Г”,[ДӨ,Г”]]ОҰвҹ© = ОЈ_x ОЈ_z Оө_x Оө_z вҹЁОҰ,[Ең_x^{(3)},[ДӨ,Ең_z^{(3)}]]ОҰвҹ©` (mulVec/dotProduct linearity over the operator double sum), whose summands vanish off adjacent sites вҖ” the `O(L)` oscillator strength | `Quantum/SpinS/OscillatorStrengthForm.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_double_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`StaggeredOrderDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the f-sum-rule double commutator as a double site sum `[Г”,[ДӨ,Г”]] = ОЈ_x ОЈ_z Оө_x Оө_z [Ең_x^{(3)},[ДӨ,Ең_z^{(3)}]]` (order-operator commutator distribution applied twice; `_commutator'` reversed variant), whose summands vanish off adjacent sites so the apparent `O(LВІ)` collapses to `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`StaggeredOrderCommutator.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the staggered order operator commutator distributes over sites `[Г”, C] = ОЈ_x Оө_x [Ең_x^{(3)}, C]`; applied twice it expands the f-sum-rule double commutator `[Г”,[ДӨ,Г”]]` into a double site sum, whose terms vanish off adjacent sites (reducing the naive `O(LВІ)` count to `O(L)`) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderCommutator.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_sq_dotProduct` | **Cor 4.3 bridge** (`StaggeredOrderSquareForm.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ground-state quadratic-form expansion `вҹЁОҰ, Г”ВІ ОҰвҹ© = ОЈ_x ОЈ_y Оө_x Оө_y вҹЁОҰ, Ең_x^{(3)} Ең_y^{(3)} ОҰвҹ©`, rewriting the order parameter in the exact form `star ОҰ в¬қбөҘ (Г”ВІ).mulVec ОҰ` of the Corollary through the two-point functions the RP machinery bounds | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSquareForm.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_sq_eq_sum` | **Cor 4.3 bridge** (`StaggeredOrderSquare.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the staggered order operator squared expands as the two-point double sum `Г”ВІ = ОЈ_x ОЈ_y Оө_x Оө_y Ең_x^{(3)} Ең_y^{(3)}` вҖ” the bridge from the order parameter to the two-point functions bounded by the reflection-positivity / infrared machinery (the squared order parameter per `LВІ` is controlled by the total two-point sum, only `O(L)` in one dimension) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSquare.lean` |
| `rpTraceWeight_cauchySchwarz_abs` | **RP infra layer 40** (`RingReflectionCauchySchwarzSqrt.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the square-root (usable) form of the RP CauchyвҖ“Schwarz вҖ” `|Re Tr(MВ·ОёAВ·B)| вүӨ вҲҡ(Re Tr(MВ·ОёAВ·A))В·вҲҡ(Re Tr(MВ·ОёBВ·B))` for a reflection-invariant RP trace weight `M` and left-supported `A,B`, by taking roots in the chessboard inequality (diagonals nonnegative); the form iterated in the DLS spreading argument | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCauchySchwarzSqrt.lean` |
| `ringTranslateConj` / `chessboard_ringTranslateConj_of` | **RP infra layer 39** (`RingReflectionChessboardTransport.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation by the ring translation `П„(X) = TвҖ В·XВ·T` is a trace-preserving `*`-homomorphism (`ringTranslateConj_mul`/`_trace`), so any chessboard estimate transports unchanged to the translation-conjugated data `(П„M, П„(ОёA), П„B)` (`chessboard_ringTranslateConj_of`) вҖ” the weight `П„M` is the Marshall gauge relative to the shifted reflection plane; carries the single-axis chessboard to every bond toward Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionChessboardTransport.lean` |
| `chainTranslation_conj_gibbs` / `thermalAverageReS_chainTranslation` | **RP infra layer 38** (`RingTranslationGibbs.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): unlike the half-system gauge, the physical ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_heisenbergHamiltonianS`), so the physical Gibbs operator `exp(вҲ’ОІВ·ДӨ)` is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_gibbs`) and every physical thermal average is translation covariant `вҹЁTвҖ ATвҹ© = вҹЁAвҹ©` (`thermalAverageReS_chainTranslation`); the thermal-state translation symmetry for the momentum-space infrared analysis | `Quantum/SpinS/RingTranslationGibbs.lean` |
| `chainTranslation_conj_ringReflectionThetaS_onSiteS` / `_spinSDot` | **RP infra layer 37** (`RingReflectionTranslation.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): translation covariance of the fixed-axis reflection map `Оё` вҖ” conjugating `Оё(onSiteS x A)` (resp. `Оё(Ең_xВ·Ең_y)`) by the ring translation `chainTranslationOp` shifts the reflection axis, sending the sites to `finRotate (ringReflect x)` (resp. both endpoints); the foundation for reflections across every bond, needed to spread the single-axis chessboard toward DLS Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTranslation.lean` |
| `ringDLSGibbs_reflection_step` | **RP infra layer 36** (`RingReflectionGibbsChessboard.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the concrete chessboard inequality for the gauged ring Gibbs weight вҖ” combining its reflection positivity (Gibbs capstone) and reflection invariance (`ОёM=M`), for `ОІ вүҘ 0` and left-supported `A,B`, `(Re Tr(MВ·ОёAВ·B))ВІ вүӨ Re Tr(MВ·ОёAВ·A)В·Re Tr(MВ·ОёBВ·B)` with `M = exp(вҲ’ОІВ·toHamiltonian)`; the DLS factorization estimate for the thermal state of the physical ring antiferromagnet | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsChessboard.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_toHamiltonian` / `_ringDLSGibbs` | **RP infra layer 35** (`RingReflectionThetaInvariance.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the gauged DLS Hamiltonian is reflection-invariant `Оё(toHamiltonian) = toHamiltonian` (the `H_L + Оё(H_L)` part is swapped to itself; each crossing bond `Оё(crossBondInteractionS x) = crossBondInteractionS x` since `Ең_x^Оұ` commutes with its right-half reflection `Оё(Ең_x^Оұ)`), hence the gauged ring Gibbs weight `exp(вҲ’ОІВ·toHamiltonian)` is reflection-invariant вҖ” supplying the `ОёM = M` hypothesis for the chessboard/real-diagonal lemmas | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionThetaInvariance.lean` |
| `rpForm_re_symm` / `rpTraceWeight_reflection_step` | **RP infra layer 34** (`RingReflectionChessboard.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): for a reflection-invariant RP trace weight `M` (`ОёM=M`) and left-supported `A,B`, the off-diagonal form value is symmetric `Re Tr(MВ·ОёAВ·B) = Re Tr(MВ·ОёBВ·A)` (since `A` commutes with the right-half `ОёB`); feeding this into the CauchyвҖ“Schwarz discriminant collapses the cross term, giving the chessboard bound `(Re Tr(MВ·ОёAВ·B))ВІ вүӨ Re Tr(MВ·ОёAВ·A)В·Re Tr(MВ·ОёBВ·B)` вҖ” the DLS factorization step underlying Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionChessboard.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_trace` / `rpForm_diag_im_zero` | **RP infra layer 33** (`RingReflectionTraceReality.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflection map conjugates the trace `Tr(ОёX) = conj(Tr X)` (reindex the diagonal by the involutive site reflection), so for a reflection-invariant weight `M` (`ОёM = M`) and left-supported `A` the RP-form diagonal `Tr(MВ·ОёAВ·A)` is real (`ОёВІ=id`, `*`-homomorphism, `A` commutes with `ОёA`); together with the CauchyвҖ“Schwarz this makes `(A,B)вҶҰTr(MВ·ОёAВ·B)` a genuine positive-semidefinite form with real diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceReality.lean` |
| `rpTraceWeight_cauchySchwarz` | **RP infra layer 32** (`RingReflectionCauchySchwarz.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): a reflection-positive trace weight `M` makes `(A,B) вҶҰ Tr(MВ·ОёAВ·B)` a positive-semidefinite sesquilinear form on the left subalgebra; expanding `0 вүӨ Re Tr(MВ·Оё(AвҲ’tB)В·(AвҲ’tB))` as a nonnegative real quadratic in `t` and taking its discriminant (`discrim_le_zero`) gives `(Re Tr(MВ·ОёAВ·B) + Re Tr(MВ·ОёBВ·A))ВІ вүӨ 4В·Re Tr(MВ·ОёAВ·A)В·Re Tr(MВ·ОёBВ·B)` вҖ” the workhorse for the DLS infrared bound | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCauchySchwarz.lean` |
| `thermalAverageReS_toHamiltonian_eq` | **RP infra layer 31** (`RingReflectionThermalTransfer.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half gauge is a similarity transform, so it leaves the partition function invariant (`thermalPartitionFnS_toHamiltonian_eq`) and transports thermal averages вҖ” the physical ring Heisenberg canonical average of `A` equals the reflection-positive DLS canonical average of `rightGauge В· A В· rightGaugeInv`; the bridge carrying RP bounds back to the physical ring antiferromagnet | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionThermalTransfer.lean` |
| `exp_neg_smul_toHamiltonian_eq` | **RP infra layer 30** (`RingReflectionGibbsGauge.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation by the right-half gauge commutes with the matrix exponential (`Matrix.exp_units_conj`), so the physical ring Heisenberg Gibbs operator `exp(вҲ’ОІ ДӨ)` and the reflection-positive DLS Gibbs operator `exp(вҲ’ОІ В· ringDLSDecomposition.toHamiltonian)` are unitarily equivalent вҖ” physical thermal averages become DLS thermal averages of gauge-transformed observables | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsGauge.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_ringHamiltonian` | **RP infra layer 29** (`RingReflectionGaugeAssembly.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge conjugates the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian into the reflection-positive DLS Hamiltonian вҖ” `rightGauge В· heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) В· rightGaugeInv = ringDLSDecomposition.toHamiltonian` (bond Hamiltonians fixed, the two crossing bonds become the negated gauged crossing interactions); the unitary equivalence bridging the physical ring antiferromagnet to its reflection-positive form | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGaugeAssembly.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_ringLeftHamiltonian` / `_theta_*` | **RP infra layer 28** (`RingReflectionNonCrossConj.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): summing the bond-invariance lemmas, the right-half gauge fixes both the left bond Hamiltonian (`rightGauge_conj_ringLeftHamiltonian`) and the reflected right bond Hamiltonian `Оё(ringLeftHamiltonian)` (`rightGauge_conj_theta_ringLeftHamiltonian`) вҖ” only the two crossing bonds are affected | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionNonCrossConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_crossBond` | **RP infra layer 27** (`RingReflectionCrossConj.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the gauge image of a crossing bond is the negated gauged crossing interaction. For a left site `x < n` (reflection `r x` a right site), `rightGauge В· (Ең_x В· Ең_{r x}) В· rightGaugeInv = вҲ’crossBondInteractionS x` вҖ” the left endpoint untouched, the right endpoint gauged (flipping `Ең^1`/`Ең^3` signs), matching `crossBondInteractionS_eq`. Both ring crossing bonds `(nвҲ’1,n)` and `(2nвҲ’1,0)` are of this form | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCrossConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_left`/`_right` | **RP infra layer 26** (`RingReflectionBondConj.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half gauge fixes every non-crossing bond. `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_left` (left bond, both sites `< n`: untouched) and `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_right` (right bond, both sites gauged: the `Ең^1`/`Ең^3` sign flips cancel in the product, `Ең^2` unchanged). Only the two crossing bonds acquire the DLS sign flip. Helpers `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS_left`/`_right` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_mul` (gauge homomorphism) | **RP infra layer 25** (`RingReflectionGaugeConj.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation `X вҶҰ rightGaugeВ·XВ·rightGaugeInv` by the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge is an algebra homomorphism вҖ” `rightGauge_conj_mul` (over products, via `rightGaugeInv_mul_rightGauge`), `rightGauge_conj_add`, `rightGauge_conj_smul`, `rightGauge_conj_sum`. With `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS` these compute the gauge image of any operator built from single-site operators (the ring Hamiltonian in particular) | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGaugeConj.lean` |
| `AxisTwoPiRotS` / `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS` | **RP infra layer 24** (`RingReflectionGauge.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge. `manyBodyTensorS_conj_onSiteS_dep` вҖ” site-dependent tensor conjugation `(вҠ—_i W_i)(onSiteS z A)(вҠ—_i Winv_i) = onSiteS z (W_zВ·AВ·Winv_z)` (for `W_iВ·Winv_i = 1`). `AxisTwoPiRotS` вҖ” the single-site ПҖ-rotation around axis 2 as an interface (following `AxisSwapUnitaryS`; fields `U Ең^1 UвҒ»В№ = вҲ’Ең^1`, `U Ең^2 UвҒ»В№ = Ең^2`, `U Ең^3 UвҒ»В№ = вҲ’Ең^3`, non-vacuous at spin-1/2). `rightGauge`/`rightGaugeInv` вҖ” the gauge unitary applying `U` on the right sites only; `rightGaugeInv_mul_rightGauge` (invertible) and `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS` (conjugation acts as `UВ·AВ·UвҒ»В№` on right sites, trivially on left). The interface is made **non-vacuous at spin-1/2** by `axisTwoPiRotSpinHalf` (the explicit `spinHalfRot2 ПҖ`). The gauge bridging the ungauged ring Hamiltonian to its reflection-positive form | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGauge.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_ungauged_dls` | **RP infra layer 23** (`RingReflectionUngaugedDLS.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ungauged DLS split of the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Combining the bond split (#4798) with `ringRightBondSum = Оё(ringLeftHamiltonian)` (#4799): `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) = ringLeftHamiltonian + Оё(ringLeftHamiltonian) + (Ең_{nвҲ’1}В·Ең_n + Ең_{2nвҲ’1}В·Ең_0)`. The crossing term is the *ungauged* AFM crossing bond, differing from the gauged `crossBondInteractionS` (with `ЕңВІ` sign flipped) by the DLS/Marshall gauge; bridging the two via the gauge unitary is the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionUngaugedDLS.lean` |
| `ringRightBondSum_eq_theta` | **RP infra layer 22** (`RingReflectionRightEqTheta.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right bond Hamiltonian equals the reflection of the left bond Hamiltonian. Reindexing by the bijection `x вҶҰ r(x+1)` (reflection of the cyclic successor `ringBondSucc`, shown bijective `= В·+1` via `Equiv.addRight`) and using `spinSDot` symmetry, `ringRightBondSum = Оё(ringLeftHamiltonian)` (`Fintype.sum_bijective`). This identifies the right part of the bond split with the `Оё(H_L)` term of the DLS decomposition; combined with the bond split (#4798), `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) = ringLeftHamiltonian + Оё(ringLeftHamiltonian) + (ungauged crossing bonds)` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRightEqTheta.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_bond_split` | **RP infra layer 21** (`RingReflectionBondSplit.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian split into left, right, and crossing bonds. The bonds `(x, x+1)` of `Fin (2n)` fall into four disjoint classes by `x.val` (left `x вүӨ nвҲ’2`, crossing `x = nвҲ’1`, right `n вүӨ x вүӨ 2nвҲ’2`, crossing `x = 2nвҲ’1`); summing the bond form over this partition (`bondTerm_four_way` per-bond decomposition + `Finset.sum_add_distrib` + `sum_ite_val_eq`) gives `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) = ringLeftHamiltonian + ringRightBondSum + Ең_{nвҲ’1}В·Ең_n + Ең_{2nвҲ’1}В·Ең_0`. Identifying `ringRightBondSum` with `Оё(ringLeftHamiltonian)` and the crossing bonds with the gauged interaction is the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSplit.lean` |
| `ringDLSDecomposition_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` | **RP infra layer 20** (`RingReflectionConcreteGibbs.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the concrete gauged ring antiferromagnet has a reflection-positive Gibbs weight. Instantiating the ring DLS decomposition with the concrete left part `H_L = ringLeftHamiltonian` (`ringDLSDecomposition`), `ringDLSDecomposition_toHamiltonian` gives the gauged ring Hamiltonian `H = ringLeftHamiltonian + Оё(ringLeftHamiltonian) вҲ’ (crossBondInteractionS 0 + crossBondInteractionS (nвҲ’1))`, and `ringDLSDecomposition_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` concludes (via the Gibbs capstone) that `exp(вҲ’ОІВ·H)` is a reflection-positive trace weight (`ОІ вүҘ 0`). Identification of `H` with the physical ungauged Hamiltonian (bond-split assembly + gauge unitary) is the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionConcreteGibbs.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian` / `ringBondSquareLinField` / `ringBondSquareConst` / `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_eq` / `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_isHermitian` | **Bond-square field Hamiltonian foundation** (`RingReflectionBondSquareField.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 (4.1.48), book p.86, bond-square field Hamiltonian / PR #4987): the faithful quadratic bond-square field Hamiltonian and its reduction to the linear-core form. Tasaki's field Hamiltonian couples the staggered field `f_x = (вҲ’1)ЛЈ h_x` **quadratically** inside the bond-square `ВҪ{ЕңВівӮ“ + ЕңВі_y вҲ’ f_x вҲ’ f_y}ВІ`; this square form is the faithful object. The equivalent linear-core form `ringFieldHamiltonian n N (ringBondSquareLinField n h) + (ringBondSquareConst n h : в„Ӯ) вҖў 1` is a **proved identity `(вҳ…)` `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_eq`**, obtained by expanding the square, completing the isotropic dot product with the `ЕңВівӮ“ЕңВі_y` cross term, cancelling the single-ion terms via successor reindexing, and regrouping into linear and scalar parts. The staggered-field per-site coefficient `ringBondSquareLinField n h z = вҲ’(f_z + f_{z+1}) вҲ’ (f_{zвҲ’1} + f_z)` (the two incident ring-bond field sums, Tasaki `bМғ`-field (4.1.38), book p.84). The scalar constant `ringBondSquareConst n h = ВҪ ОЈ_bonds (f_x + f_{x+1})ВІ` is nonnegative (deferred; C(h)вүҘ0 is consumed in later PR-BS3+ where the collapse inequality and uniform-field bound are applied) and repairs the reflection-bound inequality direction. The bond-square Hamiltonian is Hermitian (`ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_isHermitian`) as the sum of the Hermitian linear-core part and the real scalar constant. This is PR-BS1 (bond-square route) toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareField.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe` / `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_eq_scaled` | **Bond-square field partition function and scaled reduction** (`RingReflectionBondSquareFieldPartition.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 (4.1.49), book p.86, bond-square partition function / PR #4988): the canonical partition function `Z^{BS}_ОІ(h) = Re Tr exp(вҲ’ОІВ·ДӨ_h)` of the bond-square field Hamiltonian `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian` (Tasaki (4.1.48), book p.86). The crux is the **scalar-shift reduction `(вҳ…вҳ…)` `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_eq_scaled`**: since the reduction identity `(вҳ…)` (`ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_eq`, PR-BS1) decomposes `ДӨ_h = ДӨ(kOf h) + C(h)В·1` where `C(h)В·1` is a real scalar central element commuting with `ДӨ(kOf h)`, the matrix exponential splits: `exp(вҲ’ОІВ·ДӨ_h) = exp(вҲ’ОІВ·ДӨ(kOf h)) В· exp(вҲ’ОІC(h)В·1)`, and the scalar exponential `exp(вҲ’ОІC(h)В·1)` evaluates to `e^{вҲ’ОІC(h)}В·1` (via `Matrix.exp_diagonal`). The real scalar `e^{вҲ’ОІC(h)}` pulls out of both the trace (`Matrix.trace_smul`) and the real part (`Complex.re_ofReal_mul`), yielding `Z^{BS}_ОІ(h) = e^{вҲ’ОІC(h)} В· Z^{repo}_ОІ(kOf h)` (Tasaki В§4.1 (4.1.49), book p.86), the finite-`ОІ` form of the uniform-field bound. This equation lets later PR-BS3+ reuse the single-field symmetries of `ringFieldPartitionRe` for the bond-square partition function without redefining `Z` on a field map. This is PR-BS2 of the bond-square route toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareFieldPartition.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareConst_const` / `ringBondSquareLinField_const` / `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_const` / `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_const` | **Constant-field collapse** (`RingReflectionBondSquareField.lean` / `RingReflectionBondSquareFieldPartition.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 footnote 10 (4.1.49), book pp. 84вҖ“86, constant-field collapse / PR #4989): at a constant field `h_x вүЎ c`, the staggered field on any ring bond sums to zero `f_x + f_{x+1} = (вҲ’1)^x c + (вҲ’1)^{x+1} c = 0` via the even-ring staggered sign relation `(вҲ’1)^{x+1} = вҲ’(вҲ’1)^x` (private lemma: `neg_one_pow_ringBondSucc`); consequently both the bond-square constant `C(h^const) = 0` (`ringBondSquareConst_const`) and the bond-square linear field `kOf(h^const) = 0` (`ringBondSquareLinField_const`) vanish (each per-site coefficient is the sum of two incident bond cancellations, proven via private `ringBondSquareStagField_const_bond_cancel`). The **operator collapse** `ДӨ(h^const) = ringFieldHamiltonian n N 0` (`ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_const`) follows from the reduction identity `(вҳ…)` and the two vanishings: the bond-square Hamiltonian at a constant field collapses to the field-free linear-core form `ДӨвӮҖ` (Tasaki В§4.1 (4.1.49), book p.86, finite-`N` form of `E_GS(h^const) = E_GS(0,вҖҰ,0)`). The **partition-function collapse** `Z^{BS}_ОІ(h^const) = Z^{repo}_ОІ(0)` (`ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_const`) is immediate from the operator collapse: both partition functions are the real part of the Gibbs trace of the same Hamiltonian `ringFieldHamiltonian n N 0`, and the scalar exponential `e^{вҲ’ОІC(h^const)} = e^0 = 1` vanishes from the `(вҳ…вҳ…)` reduction (finite-`ОІ` form of `E_GS(h^const) = E_GS(0,вҖҰ,0)`, consumed by the uniform-field Gaussian-domination bound PR-BS10+). This is PR-BS3 of the bond-square route toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareField.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareFieldPartition.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareLeftFieldHamiltonian` / `ringBondSquareLeftFieldHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft` / `ringBondSquareCrossingGen` / `ringBondSquareCrossingGen_supportedOnLeft` / `ringBondSquareFieldCrossing` / `ringBondSquareFieldCrossing_twoFieldConeRep` / `ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight` / `ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight_self` / `ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight_isLimit` | **Bond-square DLS decomposition and two-field weight** (`RingReflectionBondSquareTwoFieldWeight.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 (4.1.65)вҖ“(4.1.69), book p. 90, bond-square DLS structure and Trotter limit / PR #4992, H_L corrected PR #4994): the left-half Hamiltonian, field-dependent crossing generators, and the doubled Gibbs weight for the bond-square model, all in the gauge spin basis. **Left half**: `ringBondSquareLeftFieldHamiltonian n N a` is the intra-left bond terms `+ЕңВ№ЕңВ№ + ЕңВІЕңВІ + ВҪ(ЕңВівӮ“ + ЕңВі_y вҲ’ a_x вҲ’ a_y)ВІ`, the single-ion `вҲ’(ЕңВі)ВІ`, and the boundary half-square `ВҪ(ЕңВівӮ“ вҲ’ a_x)ВІ` (in repo DLS physical Heisenberg frame; sign corrected from TМӮ-form transcription via the `(вҲ’1)ЛЈ` of `TМӮВі = (вҲ’1)ЛЈЕңВі` flipping Оұ=1 to `+ЕңВ№ЕңВ№` and converting longitudinal difference to sum-form; left-supported, proved by `ringBondSquareLeftFieldHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft`; exact physical coefficients deferred to PR-BS8; PR #4994). **Crossing generators**: `ringBondSquareCrossingGen n N p z` is field-free on kinetic slots `Оұ = 0,1` and carries a bare central scalar shift `вҲ’z_x` on the longitudinal slot `Оұ = 2` (Tasaki (4.1.69), book p. 90), left-supported (`ringBondSquareCrossingGen_supportedOnLeft`). **Two-field crossing**: `ringBondSquareFieldCrossing n N a b` is the field-dependent interaction `вҲ‘_c Оё(C_c(b))В·C_c(a)` (with reflected `b` on the right, non-reflected `a` on the left), instantiating the `RPTwoFieldConeRepS` shape (`ringBondSquareFieldCrossing_twoFieldConeRep`). **Two-field weight**: `ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight n N ОІ a b` is the doubled Gibbs operator `exp(вҲ’ОІВ·(H_L(a) + Оё(H_L(b)) вҲ’ crossing))` with independent left and right fields; its diagonal collapse to a single field (`ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight_self`) recovers the symmetric-field DLS form; its Trotter-limit representation (`ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight_isLimit`, via `lieProductFormula`) decomposes as `(exp(вҲ’(ОІ/m)H_L(a)) В· Оё(exp(вҲ’(ОІ/m)H_L(b))) В· exp((ОІ/m)В·crossing))^m` converging as `m вҶ’ вҲһ` (mirroring the linear `ringTwoFieldWeight_isLimit` with the two-field crossing dependence). The physical coefficients and field identification are deferred to PR-BS8 (the DLS form is Hamiltonian definition). This is PR-BS6 of the bond-square route toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareTwoFieldWeight.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight_reflection_cauchySchwarz` | **Bond-square reflection CauchyвҖ“Schwarz capstone** (`RingReflectionTwoFieldCauchySchwarz.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 (4.1.51), book p. 86, bond-square finite-ОІ reflection CauchyвҖ“Schwarz / PR #4993): the **bond-square variant of Tasaki's reflection CauchyвҖ“Schwarz inequality** вҖ” `(Re Tr W^{BS}(a,b))ВІ вүӨ Re Tr W^{BS}(a,a) В· Re Tr W^{BS}(b,b)` where `W^{BS}(a,b) = ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight n N ОІ a b`. The proof structure mirrors the linear-field version (`ringTwoFieldWeight_reflection_cauchySchwarz`, PR #4982) via nested double limit (DLS Trotter), with the finite `(m,r)` CauchyвҖ“Schwarz upgraded by the inner limit `r вҶ’ вҲһ` (exponential cone closure) and outer limit `m вҶ’ вҲһ` (Trotter convergence); both limits preserve the inequality via `cauchySchwarz_of_tendsto`. **Simpler than linear case**: the bond-square crossing is genuinely field-dependent (via the crossing generators instantiating `RPTwoFieldConeRepS`), so the `(b,b)` slot in the Trotter representation directly carries the correct field-dependent crossing from the isLimit statement вҖ” no `interaction_eq` rewrite or new sign lemma is required (contrast with the linear case where the field-independent crossing necessitates `interaction_eq` unification of the (b,b) slot). The `0 вүӨ ОІ` hypothesis ensures cone positivity of `(ОІ/m)D` where `D` is the bond-square crossing interaction. This capstone completes the bond-square reflection CauchyвҖ“Schwarz layer toward Theorem 4.2 (the bond-square Gaussian-domination sub-arc). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldCauchySchwarz.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareBondTermOf` / `ringBondSquareLeftBondSum` / `ringBondSquareRightBondSum` / `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_eq_bondTermOf_sum` / `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_ungauged_dls` / `ringBondSquareLeftBondSum_eq_leftCouplingBulk` | **Bond-square ungauged DLS split** (`RingReflectionBondSquareUngaugedDLS.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 (4.1.48)/(4.1.69), book pp.86,90, ungauged bond-square DLS decomposition / PR #4995): the physical bond-square field Hamiltonian reorganised without expanding any square into four-way directed-bond classification, mirroring the linear ungauged DLS split (`heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_ungauged_dls`). **Per-bond term** `ringBondSquareBondTermOf n N f x`: the summand `ЕңВ№вӮ“ЕңВ№_{x+1} + ЕңВІвӮ“ЕңВІ_{x+1} + ВҪ(ЕңВівӮ“ + ЕңВі_{x+1} вҲ’ f_x вҲ’ f_{x+1})ВІ` at the staggered-field bare form `f` (no gauging). **Directed sums**: `ringBondSquareLeftBondSum` (intra-left bonds `x+1 < n`), `ringBondSquareRightBondSum` (intra-right bonds `n вүӨ x вҲ§ x+1 < 2n`) вҖ” the four-way partition via `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_eq_bondTermOf_sum` and the auxiliary private `sum_four_way_split`. **Main theorem** `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_ungauged_dls`: `ДӨ(h) = intra-left + intra-right + crossing(nвҲ’1) + crossing(2nвҲ’1) вҲ’ single-ion`, the bare-field ungauged form before PR-BS8a-ii gauge conjugation into the DLS crux `H_L(a) + Оё(H_L(b)) вҲ’ crossing(a,b)`. **Bridge** `ringBondSquareLeftBondSum_eq_leftCouplingBulk`: the directed intra-left sum equals the merged `ringBondSquareLeftFieldHamiltonian`'s bulk double sum via `ringLeftCoupling`, the bond-square analogue of `ringLeftHamiltonian_eq_leftBondSum` (PR-RP infra 18), aligning the ungauged split with the DLS left half. Staggered-field visibility: `ringBondSquareStagField` (per-site coefficient `(вҲ’1)^x h_x`, originally defined private in PR-BS1, `RingReflectionBondSquareField.lean`) is de-privatized in this PR (PR-BS8a-i, #4995) for this split's bond reorganisation. The gauge crux, physical-field identification, and reflection step are deferred to PR-BS8a-ii and PR-BS8b. This is PR-BS8a-i of the bond-square route toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareUngaugedDLS.lean` |
| `physBondSquareFieldOf` / `ringBondSquareStagField_physBondSquareFieldOf` / `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian` / `rightGauge_conj_sub` / `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareBondTermOf_left` / `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareLeftBondSum` / `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareSingleIon` / `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareRightBondSum` | **Bond-square right-half gauge crux (G)** (`RingReflectionBondSquareGaugeCrux.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 (4.1.48)/(4.1.65)вҖ“(4.1.69), book pp.86,90, bond-square gauge conjugation and DLS crux / PR #4996): conjugating the ungauged bond-square field Hamiltonian (BS8a-i) by the right-half Marshall gauge into the two-field DLS operator `H_L(a) + Оё(H_L(b)) вҲ’ crossing(a,b)`. The crux input is the **staggered wrapper** `physBondSquareFieldOf n a b z = (вҲ’1)б¶» В· physFieldOf n a b z`, a `(Fin (2n) вҶ’ в„қ)` carrying the spin-basis physical field whose bare (TМӮ-basis) split is the linear `physFieldOf a b`; feeding it into the bond-square Hamiltonian cancels the physical Hamiltonian's internal `(вҲ’1)б¶»` staggering via `((вҲ’1)б¶»)ВІ = 1` (**W1** `ringBondSquareStagField_physBondSquareFieldOf`), so the effective field inside the square is the bare `physFieldOf a b`. The gauge distributes by algebra-homomorphism laws with **no square expanded** in the bulk: intra-left bonds are gauge-fixed to `H_L(a)` bulk; intra-right bonds reindex to `Оё(H_L(b))` bulk (the right-half double-sign cancellation); only the two `O(1)` crossing bonds are completed `ВҪ(AвҲ’B)ВІ = ВҪAВІ + ВҪBВІ вҲ’ AB` into boundary half-squares and the field crossing `вҲ’ringBondSquareFieldCrossing a b`. The single-ion term splits left/right, right half reindexing to `Оё` of the left. Assembling gives the **gauge crux (G)** `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian`, on which PR-BS8b builds the physical-field identification and the one reflection step. Helper lemmas distribute conjugation over difference/sum/product with localized gauge action. **De-privatised** `sum_right_eq_sum_reflect_left` from `RingReflectionFieldPartition.lean` (reindexing the single-ion right half via reflection bijection, statement and proof unchanged, visibility only to avoid duplicate reindex lemma). This is PR-BS8a-ii of the bond-square route toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareGaugeCrux.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_physFieldOf` / `physBondSquareFieldOf_self` / `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_reflection_step` | **Bond-square physical-field identification and reflection step** (`RingReflectionBondSquarePhysId.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 (4.1.48)вҖ“(4.1.51), book pp. 86вҖ“90, bond-square physical-field identification and one reflection step / PR #4997): the physical identification of the bond-square partition function with the two-field Gibbs trace, and the one reflection step in sign-free classical form. **Physical identification** `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_physFieldOf` (the bond-square analogue of `ringFieldPartitionRe_physFieldOf`, PR-BS8b): for the staggered wrapper `physBondSquareFieldOf n a b` (carrying spin-basis staggering `(вҲ’1)^z В· physFieldOf n a b z`), the gauge crux (G) from PR-BS8a-ii conjugates the physical bond-square field Hamiltonian to the DLS two-field operator, so `exp(вҲ’ОІВ·ДӨ^{BS}(physBondSquareFieldOf a b))` conjugates to `exp(вҲ’ОІВ·ДӨ^{BS}(a,b))` via `Matrix.exp_units_conj` + `rightGauge_conj_ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian`, and trace invariance yields `Z^{BS}(physBondSquareFieldOf a b) = Re Tr W^{BS}(a,b)`. **Sign-free classical collapses** (the crux of why bond-square avoids signed-copy variants): the two internal staggered relabels of the wrapper cancel (`physBondSquareFieldOf_eq_relabel`, private bridge), collapsing the three field pairs of the reflection step to Tasaki's classical sign-free mirrors (4.1.50) (book p. 86): `physBondSquareFieldOf_self` (L1, arbitrary physical field decomposed as wrapper split), `physBondSquareFieldOf_diag_left` (L2, left reflection, private), `physBondSquareFieldOf_diag_right` (L3, right reflection, private). **One reflection step** `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_reflection_step` (sign-free classical form, no staggered relabel on right): for `ОІ вүҘ 0`, `Z^{BS}(g)ВІ вүӨ Z^{BS}(reflectLeft n g)В·Z^{BS}(reflectRight n g)` вҖ” the finite-ОІ partition-function form of Tasaki's bond-square reflection bound (4.1.51) вҖ” obtained by expressing `g` as the wrapper split via L1, applying the physical identification three times at the three field pairs (with L2/L3 collapsing to the sign-free classical mirrors), and reducing to the BS7 capstone `ringBondSquareTwoFieldWeight_reflection_cauchySchwarz` (proof pp. 89вҖ“93; DLS 1978 В§2вҖ“3). The `ОІ вҶ’ вҲһ` limit yields the ground-state reflection bound. The private staggered-relabel bridge `physBondSquareFieldOf_eq_relabel` is the key: it exhibits the wrapper as the staggered relabel `P вҲҳ physFieldOf` of the linear split field, so the composition `P вҲҳ physFieldOf вҲҳ P` (two relabels on the right-field slots) simplifies to the bare sign-free reflected copies, **no signed-copy variants** (contrast linear route where the right-side field must carry a sign and produce signed mirrors). This is PR-BS8b of the bond-square route toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquarePhysId.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareConst_reindexCyclic` / `ringBondSquareLinField_reindexCyclic` / `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_pos` / `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_reindexCyclic` / `ringBondSquareFieldPartition_gaussianDomination` | **Bond-square chessboard Gaussian-domination capstone** (`RingReflectionBondSquareGaussianDomination.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 Lemma 4.5 + Theorem 4.2 (4.1.55)вҖ“(4.1.57) + (4.1.51), book pp. 87вҖ“90, bond-square chessboard Gaussian-domination / PR #4998): the **finite-ОІ chessboard Gaussian-domination bound** `Z^{BS}_ОІ(h)^{2n} вүӨ вҲҸ_j Z^{BS}_ОІ(fun _ => h j)` (Tasaki В§4.1 Theorem 4.2, pp. 85вҖ“90; chessboard estimate Lemma 4.5, (4.1.55)вҖ“(4.1.57), pp. 87вҖ“88), obtained by applying the **classical cyclic averaging inequality** (`reflectionPositivity_averaging`, Tasaki Lemma 4.5, (4.1.55)вҖ“(4.1.57), pp. 87вҖ“88) **directly** to the plain functional `F g = вҲ’log Z^{BS}_ОІ(g)` вҖ” no staggered-relabel bridge needed, since PR-BS8b already delivers the reflection step on the sign-free classical mirrors. **Cyclicity hypothesis (вҳ…вҳ… hcyc) (4.1.55)** `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_reindexCyclic` (PR-BS9, `RingReflectionBondSquareFieldPartition.lean`): `Z^{BS}_ОІ(reindexCyclic n g) = Z^{BS}_ОІ(g)`, proved from the **scalar-shift reduction `(вҳ…вҳ…)` (`ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_eq_scaled`)**. The scalar constant `C(h)` is translation-invariant **(A)** `ringBondSquareConst_reindexCyclic` (PR-BS9, `RingReflectionBondSquareField.lean`); the linear field covariance `kOf(reindexCyclic h) = вҲ’kOf(h)` **(B)** `ringBondSquareLinField_reindexCyclic` (PR-BS9, `RingReflectionBondSquareField.lean`), a sign flip absorbed by spin-flip invariance `ringFieldPartitionRe_neg` and translation symmetry `ringFieldPartitionRe_translate` of the linear-core partition. **Positivity domain (4.1.56)** `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_pos` (PR-BS9, `RingReflectionBondSquareFieldPartition.lean`, strict positivity `0 < Z^{BS}_ОІ(h)`) is required for the `вҲ’log` functional; follows from the scalar-shift reduction and positivity of both factors. **Reflection bound (4.1.56)**: the one reflection step (PR-BS8b) `Z^{BS}_ОІ(g)ВІ вүӨ Z^{BS}_ОІ(reflectLeft n g)В·Z^{BS}_ОІ(reflectRight n g)` on classical mirrors, combined with positivity. **Averaging computation**: applying `reflectionPositivity_averaging` yields `(1/2n) ОЈ_j (вҲ’log Z^{BS}_ОІ(fun _ => h j)) вүӨ вҲ’log Z^{BS}_ОІ(h)`. Exponentiating and rearranging gives the product bound. **Forward to PR-BS10**: PR-BS10 collapses each constant-field factor to `Z^{BS}_ОІ(0)`, yielding the uniform-field bound `Z^{BS}_ОІ(h) вүӨ Z^{BS}_ОІ(0)` (Tasaki (4.1.49)/(4.1.52), pp. 85вҖ“86). This is PR-BS9 of the bond-square route toward the reflection-positivity infrastructure for Theorem 4.2. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareGaussianDomination.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_uniform_bound` | **Bond-square uniform-field capstone** (`RingReflectionBondSquareUniformBound.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 Theorem 4.2 (4.1.49)/(4.1.52), book pp. 85вҖ“86, bond-square uniform-field bound / PR #4999): the **terminal capstone of the bond-square reflection-positivity route**: the bound `Z^{BS}_ОІ(h) вүӨ Z^{BS}_ОІ(0)` (Tasaki В§4.1 Theorem 4.2, uniform-field bound (4.1.49)/(4.1.52), pp. 85вҖ“86). Pure algebraic glue atop the chessboard Gaussian-domination capstone (PR-BS9, `ringBondSquareFieldPartition_gaussianDomination`) and the constant-field collapse (PR-BS3, `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_const`). The chessboard bound gives `Z^{BS}_ОІ(h)^{2n} вүӨ вҲҸ_j Z^{BS}_ОІ(fun _ => h j)`; each constant-field factor collapses **exactly** (without `e^{вҲ’ОІC} вүӨ 1` estimate вҖ” the constant field cancels at the operator level via `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_const`, PR-BS3) to `Z^{repo}_ОІ(0) = Z^{BS}_ОІ(0)`, so the product is `Z^{BS}_ОІ(0)^{2n}`. The `2n`-th root monotonicity `le_of_pow_le_pow_leftвӮҖ` (with strict positivity from `ringBondSquareFieldPartitionRe_pos`, PR-BS9) then yields the bound. | `trace_exp_smul_neg_re_eq_sum_exp` / `tendsto_neg_inv_mul_log_trace_exp_re_atTop_hermitianMinEigenvalue` | **(SUM) & (ПҮ1-a) вҖ” Free-energy `T вҶ’ 0` limit: eigenvalue-sum spectral form and ground-energy limit** (`FreeEnergyGroundEnergyLimit.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 (4.1.40)/(4.1.49), book pp. 85вҖ“86, free-energy TвҶ’0 limit / PR #5000): the real-analysis engine for the susceptibility phase. The dual limit `tendsto_neg_inv_mul_log_trace_exp_re_atTop_hermitianMinEigenvalue` is applied in PR-ПҮ1 to both `ДӨ(h)` and `ДӨ(0)` to yield the ground-state inequality `E_GS(0) вүӨ E_GS(h)` from the finite-ОІ partition bound `Z^{BS}_ОІ(h) вүӨ Z^{BS}_ОІ(0)` by limit-preservation. This is PR-ПҮ1 (free-energy layer) toward the susceptibility phase. | `Quantum/SpinS/FreeEnergyGroundEnergyLimit.lean` |
| `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_hermitianMinEigenvalue_ge_field_zero` | **(ПҮ1-b) вҖ” Ground-state energy uniform-field bound `E_GS(h) вүҘ E_GS(0)`** (`RingReflectionBondSquareGroundEnergy.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 (4.1.40)/(4.1.49), book pp. 85вҖ“86, ground-state energy bound / PR #5000): **TEMPORARY capstone of the ПҮ1 (susceptibility free-energy) opening**: the ground-state energy of the bond-square field Hamiltonian at field `h` is at least that at zero field, `E_GS(0) вүӨ E_GS(h)`, on the even ring `Fin (2n)` (`n вүҘ 1`). The `T вҶ’ 0` limit of the finite-`ОІ` partition bound `Z^{BS}_ОІ(h) вүӨ Z^{BS}_ОІ(0)` (PR-BS10): both partition functions are strictly positive (BS9), so `Real.log` is monotone; multiplying the log inequality by `вҲ’(1/ОІ) < 0` reverses it into the free-energy inequality `вҲ’(1/ОІ) log Z^{BS}_ОІ(0) вүӨ вҲ’(1/ОІ) log Z^{BS}_ОІ(h)` for every `ОІ > 0`; applying (ПҮ1-a) to both sides yields the ground-state inequality in the limit `ОІ вҶ’ вҲһ` via `le_of_tendsto_of_tendsto`. This bound is the entry point for the next PR-ПҮ2 (susceptibility sum rule) and the long-range order theorems that follow. | `hermitian_posSemidef_exists_orthogonal_potential` | **(ПҮ2a-i) вҖ” Pseudoinverse existence for Hermitian matrices** (`HermitianPseudoinverse.lean`, generic finite-dimensional linear algebra, Tasaki В§4.1 eq. (4.1.39), book p. 84, susceptibility sum rule infrastructure / PR #5001): for a positive-semidefinite (hence Hermitian) matrix `A`, if `v вҠҘ ker A`, then `вҲғy: A y = v вҲ§ y вҠҘ ker A` (the MooreвҖ“Penrose pseudoinverse image). Since `range A = (ker A)б—®` for self-adjoint `A` (proof: `LinearMap.orthogonal_ker` + adjoint identity), `v` lies in the range; projecting any preimage onto `(ker A)б—®` yields `y` orthogonal to the kernel while preserving the image. Load-bearing for the resolvent identity and susceptibility finite-order perturbation (Tasaki (4.1.39)/(4.1.41), pp. 84вҖ“86). | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianPseudoinverse.lean` |
| `exists_smul_of_mem_finrank_le_one` | **(ПҮ2a-ii) вҖ” Scalar-multiple extraction from one-dimensional eigenspace** (`UniqueEigenspaceInvolution.lean`, generic finite-dimensional linear algebra shared with Theorem 2.4, issue #4777, PR #5001): if a submodule `E` has `finrank вүӨ 1`, and `ОҰ вү  0` and `w` both lie in `E`, then `вҲғc: w = c вҖў ОҰ` (any element is a scalar multiple of any non-zero member). | `exists_involution_eigenvalue_of_unique_eigenspace` | **(ПҮ2a-iii) вҖ” Involution eigenvalue on unique ground state** (`UniqueEigenspaceInvolution.lean`, Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4 (p. 43вҖ“44) and В§4.1 Theorem 4.2 (pp. 84вҖ“86), shared proof infrastructure, issue #4777, PR #5001): if a Hermitian matrix `H` has a `Ој`-eigenspace of `finrank вүӨ 1` (unique ground state), `ОҰ` is a non-zero `Ој`-eigenvector, and `Оҳ` commutes with `H` and is an involution (`ОҳВІ = 1`), then `вҲғОҙ: Оҳ ОҰ = Оҙ вҖў ОҰ вҲ§ ОҙВІ = 1` (`Оҳ` acts as `Вұ1` on the unique eigenstate). The mechanism: `Оҳ ОҰ` lies in the eigenspace (commutation), so equals `Оҙ вҖў ОҰ` (uniqueness via `exists_smul_of_mem_finrank_le_one`), and `ОҳВІ ОҰ = ОҰ` forces `ОҙВІ = 1`. Used in both Theorem 2.4 (no-magnetization) and ПҮ2a (first-order vanishing). | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/UniqueEigenspaceInvolution.lean` |
| `dotProduct_mulVec_eq_zero_of_conj_anti` | **(ПҮ2a-iv) вҖ” Vanishing under symmetric anti-involution** (`AndersonTowerTanakaMoments.lean`, generalized to issue #4777, PR #5001): **shared lemma for Theorem 4.9 transverse-moment vanishing (В§4.2.2) and ПҮ2a first-order vanishing (В§4.1 Theorem 4.2)**. | `ringBondSquareLinField_smul` / `ringBondSquareConst_smul` / `ringFieldHamiltonian_smul_field` | **(ПҮ2b-i/ii/iii) вҖ” Field scaling laws** (`RingReflectionBondSquareSusceptibilitySumRule.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 (4.1.37)вҖ“(4.1.38), book p. 84, susceptibility sum rule / PR #5002): three linear-scaling lemmas for the variational expansion of the susceptibility sum rule. **(i) Linear-field scaling** `ringBondSquareLinField_smul`: `kOf(О»h) = О»В·kOf(h)` (each per-site linear field coefficient scales by `О»`). **(ii) Quadratic-constant scaling** `ringBondSquareConst_smul`: `C(О»h) = О»ВІВ·C(h)`, where `C(h) = ВҪОЈ(f_x+f_{x+1})ВІ` is a sum of squared bond sums (`f` the staggered field); quadratic scaling ensures the `О»`-polynomial in the variational bound is exactly quadratic. **(iii) Field-Hamiltonian scaling** `ringFieldHamiltonian_smul_field`: `ДӨ_field(О»k) = HвӮҖ + О»В·(ОЈ_z k_zВ·ЕңВі_z)` вҖ” the Zeeman sum is linear in the field-coefficient vector `k`, with the field-free part `HвӮҖ = ringFieldHamiltonian n N 0` isolated. (4.1.38)), and `C(h) = ringBondSquareConst n h` is the scalar constant. Combines the three scaling lemmas (ПҮ2b-i/ii/iii) with the reduction `ringBondSquareFieldHamiltonian_eq` (Tasaki (4.1.37)) to yield the polynomial form essential for the variational expansion (Tasaki eq. (4.1.41), pp. 84вҖ“86). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSquareSusceptibilitySumRule.lean` |

(4.1.41), book p. 84, susceptibility sum rule / PR #5002): **CAPSTONE of ПҮ2** (second-order perturbation phase): for a unique ground state `ОҰ` of the field-free ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian `HвӮҖ = ringFieldHamiltonian n N 0`, there is a pseudoinverse potential `y` (orthogonal to `ОҰ`) satisfying `(HвӮҖвҲ’EвӮҖ)y = VОҰ` for the Zeeman perturbation `V`, whose susceptibility is bounded by the scalar constant, `ReвҹЁy, VОҰвҹ© вүӨ C(h) = ВҪОЈ(f_x+f_{x+1})ВІ`. | `ringLeftFieldHamiltonian` / `ringLeftFieldHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft` | **Gaussian-domination layer 1** (`RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the field-augmented left Hamiltonian.
