Its reconstructed Hamiltonian is the symmetric-field ring Hamiltonian `Lfield(a) + Оё(Lfield(a)) вҲ’ crossing`, the DLS form for the uniform-field Gaussian-domination bound `Z_ОІ(h) вүӨ Z_ОІ(0)` (Tasaki В§4.1, eqs. (4.1.49)вҖ“(4.1.51), p. 86). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_ringFieldDLSDecomposition_toHamiltonian` / `ringReflectionThetaS_ringDLSFieldWeightSym` | **Gaussian-domination layer 3** (`RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): reflection invariance of the field-augmented Hamiltonian and weight. The field-augmented DLS Hamiltonian is reflection invariant `Оё(toHamiltonian) = toHamiltonian` (the `Lfield + Оё(Lfield)` part is swapped by the involution `Оё`; each crossing-bond interaction is `Оё`-fixed); consequently the symmetric-field Gibbs weight `ringDLSFieldWeightSym n N ОІ a = exp(вҲ’ОІ В· (ringFieldDLSDecomposition n N a).toHamiltonian)` is reflection-invariant `Оё(M) = M` вҖ” supplies the `Оё M = M` hypothesis for the chessboard/real-diagonal reflection-positivity lemmas in the Gaussian-domination bound. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean` |
| `ringDLSFieldWeightSym` / `ringDLSFieldWeightSym_rpTraceWeight` | **Gaussian-domination layer 4** (`RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): symmetric-field Gibbs weight and reflection positivity. For `ОІ вүҘ 0`, the symmetric-field Gibbs weight `ringDLSFieldWeightSym n N ОІ a = exp(вҲ’ОІ В· H_{sym})` (with `H_{sym} = (ringFieldDLSDecomposition n N a).toHamiltonian` the symmetric-field ring Hamiltonian) is a reflection-positive trace weight вҖ” direct consequence of the Gibbs capstone `RPDecomposition.gibbs_rpTraceWeight` applied to the field-augmented decomposition. This finite-ОІ field-shifted weight is the Fourier-insertion target consumed by the later Gaussian-domination PRs and is the first sound piece of the finite-ОІ Gaussian-domination sub-arc. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionFieldWeight.lean` |
| `ringTwoFieldWeight` / `ringTwoFieldWeight_self` / `ringTwoFieldWeight_isLimit` | **Gaussian-domination layer 5** (`RingReflectionTwoFieldWeight.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, PR #4976, toward Theorem 4.2): the two-field ("doubled") Gibbs weight and Trotter representation. Decoupling the symmetric-field Gibbs weight into independent left and right halves, the doubled weight `W(a,b) := exp(вҲ’ОІВ·(Lfield(a) + Оё(Lfield(b)) вҲ’ D))` (where `D = (ringFieldDLSDecomposition a).interaction`) carries independent fields `a` on the left and `b` on the right (transported by reflection `Оё`). The left part `Lfield(a)` is left-supported and `Оё(Lfield(b))` is right-supported, so they commute вҖ” the kinetic factor splits by `exp_add_of_commute`, giving the DysonвҖ“LiebвҖ“Simon Trotter approximant `(E_G В· Оё(E_{G'}) В· E_D)^m` with exponential factors merging via `ringReflectionThetaS_exp_mul_theta_exp` (asymmetric kinetic merge, placed in `RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean` to avoid a norm-instance diamond). The Lie-product limit representation `ringTwoFieldWeight_isLimit` converges the Trotter sequence to `W(a,b)`. The diagonal case `ringTwoFieldWeight_self` recovers the symmetric-field weight of PR7a. This layer feeds the reflection CauchyвҖ“Schwarz stage (PR7b-iii) for Gaussian domination (eq. (4.1.51), p. 86). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldWeight.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_exp_mul_theta_exp` | **Gaussian-domination layer 5b** (`RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, PR #4976, toward Theorem 4.2): asymmetric kinetic merge for the two-field weight. For left-supported `X` and `Y`, the product `exp X В· Оё(exp Y)` merges into `exp(X + Оё Y)` (the left-supported `exp X` and right-supported `Оё(exp Y)` commute by disjoint supports). This is the two-field analogue of `ringReflectionThetaS_exp_add_eq` with independent left and right generators; placed here rather than in `RingReflectionTwoFieldWeight.lean` to avoid importing `MatrixExponential` and creating a norm-instance diamond. Consumed by `ringTwoFieldWeight_isLimit` as the kinetic-factor building block of the Lie-product convergence. | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean` |
| `ringTwoFieldWeight_reflection_cauchySchwarz` | **RP capstone layer 7b-iii-c** (`RingReflectionTwoFieldCauchySchwarz.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 Theorem 4.2, finite-ОІ reflection CauchyвҖ“Schwarz / PR #4982): the **two-field reflection CauchyвҖ“Schwarz on the doubled Gibbs weight** вҖ” the finite-ОІ matrix form of Tasaki's reflection bound (4.1.51), p. 86 (proof pp. 89вҖ“93; DLS 1978 В§2вҖ“3). For `0 вүӨ ОІ`, `(Re Tr W(a,b))ВІ вүӨ Re Tr W(a,a) В· Re Tr W(b,b)` where `W(a,b) = ringTwoFieldWeight n N ОІ a b`. Proved by a **genuinely nested double limit** (DLS Trotter): the finite `(m,r)` CauchyвҖ“Schwarz `twoField_product_pairing_cauchySchwarz` (PR #4979, iii-b, crossing is a literal finite cone `S_r = вҲ‘_{k<r}(k!)вҒ»В№PбөҸ`) is lifted by the **inner limit `r вҶ’ вҲһ`** (exp-of-cone via `twoField_pairing_cauchySchwarz_exp`, cone partial sums converge to `exp P`) to the per-`m` approximant `T_m = (g_m В· Оё(g_m) В· exp((ОІ/m)D_a))^m` satisfying the same inequality, then by the **outer limit `m вҶ’ вҲһ`** (Trotter via `ringTwoFieldWeight_isLimit`, PR #4976) from `T_m вҶ’ W(a,b)` to the capstone. Both limits reuse the abstract limit-preserving CauchyвҖ“Schwarz `cauchySchwarz_of_tendsto` (via `le_of_tendsto_of_tendsto'` on pointwise `вүӨ` under `Tendsto.pow`/`mul`); the field-free crossing interaction (via `ringFieldDLSDecomposition_interaction_eq`) aligns the `(b,b)` slot where `isLimit(b,b)` carries `D_b` while the per-`m` fact carries `D_a`; the hypothesis `0 вүӨ ОІ` ensures cone positivity (`(ОІ/m)D вүҘ 0`) of the crossing factor. Helpers: `cauchySchwarz_of_tendsto` (limit-preserving CS, reused for both limits), `ringFieldDLSDecomposition_interaction_eq` (field-independence of crossing), `twoField_pairing_cauchySchwarz_exp` (per-`m` CS with exponential crossing). This theorem **completes the reflection CauchyвҖ“Schwarz layer toward Theorem 4.2** (the DLS / Shastry Gaussian-domination bound for long-range order). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTwoFieldCauchySchwarz.lean` |
| `ringFieldPartitionRe` / `ringFieldPartitionRe_physFieldOf` / `ringTwoFieldWeight_self_trace_re_nonneg` / `ringFieldPartitionRe_reflection_step` | **RP capstone layer 7c** (`RingReflectionFieldPartition.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 eqs. (4.1.47)вҖ“(4.1.51), pp. 85вҖ“86, physical field partition identification / PR #4983): identify the physical per-site field partition function `Z_ОІ(h) := Re Tr exp(вҲ’ОІВ·ДӨ_field(h))` with the traced doubled Gibbs weight, yielding the **one-reflection-step finite-ОІ bound** `Z_ОІ(h)ВІ вүӨ Z_ОІ(h_L)В·Z_ОІ(h_R)` (the finite-ОІ form of Tasaki's reflection bound (4.1.51)). The crux: the field-splitting map `physFieldOf n a b : Fin (2n) вҶ’ в„қ`, defined as `if z < n then a_z else вҲ’b(r z)`, realizes `h` as the physical field that reproduces the doubled weight under the right-half Marshall gauge `U`: conjugating `ДӨ_field(physFieldOf a b)` by `U` gives `L_field(a) + Оё(L_field(b)) вҲ’ D` (crux lemma `AxisTwoPiRotS.rightGauge_conj_ringFieldHamiltonian` via `Gauge.lean:56`), so the two sources of sign вҖ” the gauge's `вҲ’ЕңВі` on the right and `physFieldOf`'s `вҲ’b(r z)` вҖ” **cancel** (no new sign lemma needed, purely definitional from the split). Staggered-field sanity check (`physFieldOf_self`): the uniform staggered field `h_x = (вҲ’1)ЛЈ hвӮҖ` decomposes as `b_x = a_x`, leaving `W(a,a)` on the diagonal (gauge-invariant, hence trace-equal identification). Nonnegativity (`ringTwoFieldWeight_self_trace_re_nonneg`): the diagonal weight `W(a,a)` is an RP trace weight (via `ringDLSFieldWeightSym_rpTraceWeight`), so by the RP cone property applied to `A = 1` (`SupportedOnLeftS.one`), `0 вүӨ Re Tr W(a,a)`. Identification: `Z_ОІ(physFieldOf a b) = Re Tr W(a,b)` (gauge invariance of trace + matrix exponential conjugation). One reflection step: setting `b_x := вҲ’h(r x)` and applying the identification three times вҖ” at `(h,b)`, `(h,h)`, `(b,b)` вҖ” combined with `ringTwoFieldWeight_reflection_cauchySchwarz` (PR #4982), yields `Z_ОІ(h)ВІ вүӨ Z_ОІ(physFieldOf n h h)В·Z_ОІ(physFieldOf n b b)`, where `physFieldOf n h h` and `physFieldOf n b b` are exactly the reflected copies `h_L` and `h_R` from (4.1.50). This is the **finite-ОІ partition-function version of (4.1.51)**; the `ОІ вҶ’ вҲһ` limit gives the ground-state reflection bound (PR7d/e, out of scope here). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionFieldPartition.lean` |
| `ringFieldPartitionRe_translate` / `ringFieldPartitionRe_neg` / `ringFieldPartitionRe_pos` | **RP capstone layer 7d-i** (`RingReflectionFieldPartitionSymmetry.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1 eqs. (4.1.49)вҖ“(4.1.52), pp. 85вҖ“86, field partition symmetries / PR #4984): the **three structural symmetries of the physical field partition function** `Z_ОІ(h) = ringFieldPartitionRe n N ОІ h` consumed by the `вҲ’log Z` symmetrisation step (PR7d-ii/iii). **(A) Translation invariance** `ringFieldPartitionRe_translate`: `Z_ОІ(h) = Z_ОІ(h вҲҳ finRotate)` вҖ” the ring Hamiltonian is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_heisenbergHamiltonianS`), so conjugation by the unitary ring translation carries `ДӨ_field(hвҲҳfinRotate)` to `ДӨ_field(h)` and the trace is invariant. **(B) Spin-flip invariance** `ringFieldPartitionRe_neg`: `Z_ОІ(вҲ’h) = Z_ОІ(h)` вҖ” a global axis-2 ПҖ-rotation `fullGauge` (the `AxisTwoPiRotS.U` on every site) preserves the Heisenberg dot product (`fullGauge_conj_spinSDot`) while flipping the field `ОЈ_z h_z ЕңВі_z вҶҰ ОЈ_z (вҲ’h_z) ЕңВі_z`, so `Z_ОІ(вҲ’h) = Z_ОІ(h)` by trace invariance. **(C) Strict positivity** `ringFieldPartitionRe_pos`: `0 < Z_ОІ(h)` вҖ” the field Hamiltonian is Hermitian (`ringFieldHamiltonian_isHermitian`), so `exp(вҲ’ОІВ·ДӨ_field(h))` is positive definite (`Matrix.posDef_exp_of_isHermitian`), hence its trace is strictly positive; this is the genuine domain condition of the `вҲ’log Z` step. The period-2 staggered relabel `P h z = (вҲ’1)^z h z` (PR7d-ii) has period 2, so a single one-step shift produces a global sign: both **A and B are required for cyclicity** of the averaged potential in the classical chessboard lemma (Lemma 4.5, Tasaki pp. 87вҖ“88). | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionFieldPartitionSymmetry.lean` |
| `Matrix.posDef_exp_of_isHermitian` | **Math helper** (generic finite-dim linear algebra, `Math/PosSemidef/ExpPosDef.lean`): the exponential of a Hermitian complex matrix is positive definite. For Hermitian `A`, `exp A` is positive definite: writing `M = exp(A/2)` (Hermitian + invertible), the split `exp A = Mбҙҙ В· M` (via the commuting-exponent addition law `exp(A/2 + A/2) = MВ·M`) is positive definite by `Matrix.PosDef.conjTranspose_mul_self`. This avoids the continuous-functional-calculus route (which causes deterministic typeclass-resolution timeouts). **Load-bearing strict positivity** behind `Z_ОІ(h) = Tr exp(вҲ’ОІH) > 0` for Hermitian Hamiltonians (consumed by `ringFieldPartitionRe_pos` in the `вҲ’log Z` step) | `Math/PosSemidef/ExpPosDef.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_ringLeftHamiltonian` | **RP infra layer 19** (`RingReflectionRightBondSum.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflected left bond Hamiltonian is the right bond Hamiltonian. Applying `Оё` to the left bond sum reflects each left bond `(x,x+1)` to a right bond `(r x, r(x+1)) = (2nвҲ’1вҲ’x, 2nвҲ’2вҲ’x)`: `Оё(ringLeftHamiltonian) = вҲ‘_x [if x+1 < n then Ең_{r x}В·Ең_{r(x+1)} else 0]` (via `ringReflectionThetaS_sum` + `ringReflectionThetaS_spinSDot`). This is the right part `Оё(H_L)` of the ring DLS decomposition | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRightBondSum.lean` |
| `ringLeftHamiltonian_eq_leftBondSum` | **RP infra layer 18** (`RingReflectionLeftBondSum.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the left-half bond Hamiltonian as a sum over left bonds. Collapsing the left coupling's double sum, `ringLeftHamiltonian n N = вҲ‘_x [if x+1 < n then Ең_x В· Ең_{x+1} else 0]` вҖ” only the bonds `(x, x+1)` entirely inside `{0,вҖҰ,nвҲ’1}` survive (helpers `ringLeftCoupling_succ_of_lt`, `ringLeftCoupling_eq_zero_of_ne`, `ringLeftCoupling_eq_zero_of_not_lt`). The left part of the ring DLS decomposition over its actual bonds | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionLeftBondSum.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_eq_bondSum` | **RP infra layer 17** (`RingReflectionBondSum.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian as a sum over nearest-neighbor bonds. The directed coupling `ringCoupling (2n)` is `1` exactly on successor pairs `(x, x+1 mod 2n)`, so the double sum collapses (`Finset.sum_eq_single`) to `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) N = вҲ‘_x Ең_x В· Ең_{ringBondSucc x}` (`ringBondSucc x = x+1 mod 2n`). This bond form is the starting point for the left/right/crossing bond split | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSum.lean` |
| `ringLeftHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft` | **RP infra layer 16** (`RingReflectionLeftHamiltonian.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the left part `H_L` of the ring DLS decomposition is left-supported. `SupportedOnLeftS.sum` (left-supportedness closed under finite sums); `heisenbergHamiltonianS_supportedOnLeft` (a Heisenberg Hamiltonian whose coupling `J x y` vanishes unless both sites are in the left half is left-supported, being `вҲ‘_{x,y} J x y В· Ең_xВ·Ең_y` with each surviving term left-supported); `ringLeftCoupling` (the ring nearest-neighbor coupling restricted to left-half sites); and `ringLeftHamiltonian = heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringLeftCoupling n) N`, the concrete `H_L` that feeds `ringCrossingRPDecomposition`. The remaining bond-split match to the gauged ring Hamiltonian and the unitary ungauge are the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionLeftHamiltonian.lean` |
| `ring_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` (ring instance) | **RP infra layer 15** (`RingReflectionRingInstance.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the DLS decomposition of the gauged ring antiferromagnet. The two crossing bonds of `Fin (2n)` are `(nвҲ’1,n)` and `(2nвҲ’1,0)`, reflected from left sites `nвҲ’1` and `0`; `ringCrossingRPDecomposition` packages (for an arbitrary left-supported `H_L`) the `RPDecomposition` whose crossing bonds are the single-site spin operators at sites `0` and `nвҲ’1`, with `ringCrossingRPDecomposition_interaction` identifying its interaction as `crossBondInteractionS 0 + crossBondInteractionS (nвҲ’1)` and `ringCrossingRPDecomposition_toHamiltonian` giving `toHamiltonian = H_L + Оё(H_L) вҲ’ (crossing)`. Then `ring_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` concludes (via the Gibbs capstone) that the gauged ring Gibbs weight `exp(вҲ’ОІВ·(H_L+Оё(H_L)вҲ’crossing))` is a reflection-positive trace weight (`ОІ вүҘ 0`). Also `spinSDot_supportedOnLeft` (a left-half bond is left-supported). The concrete left-bond `H_L` and the unitary equivalence to `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n))` are the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRingInstance.lean` |
| `RPDecomposition.gibbs_rpTraceWeight` (**Gibbs RP capstone**) | **RP infra layer 14** (`RingReflectionGibbsCapstone.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): **full Gibbs reflection positivity** вҖ” for a reflection-positive decomposition `H = H_L + Оё(H_L) вҲ’ D` (`RPDecomposition`) and `ОІ вүҘ 0`, the Gibbs weight `exp(вҲ’ОІВ·H)` is a reflection-positive trace weight. The DysonвҖ“LiebвҖ“Simon Trotter assembly: `e^{вҲ’ОІH} = e^{A+B}` (`A = вҲ’ОІВ·(H_L+ОёH_L)`, `B = ОІВ·D`), via the Lie product formula `e^{A+B} = lim_m (e^{A/m}В·e^{B/m})^m` (`LieProduct.lieProductFormula`); each factor is consumed by an accumulating RP trace weight вҖ” the kinetic factor `e^{A/m} = exp(Y+ОёY)` is cone-representable (`coneRep_exp_add` + `mul_coneRep_right`) and the interaction factor `e^{B/m}` is the exp of a cone-representable operator (`mul_exp_coneRep_right`) вҖ” then induction over the power and `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto`. Helpers: `realSmul_add_theta`, `RPDecomposition.interaction_coneRep`. This is the reflection positivity at the heart of the DLS / Shastry argument | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsCapstone.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_exp_coneRep_right` | **RP infra layer 13** (`RingReflectionMulExpConeRep.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): an RP trace weight absorbs the exponential of a cone-representable operator. If `M` is RP and `P` is `RPTraceConeRepS`, then `M В· exp P` is RP вҖ” the partial sums `вҲ‘_{k<m}(k!)вҒ»В№PбөҸ` are cone-representable (`pow`/`smul_nonneg`/`add`), so each `MВ·(partial sum)` is RP (`mul_coneRep_right`), and `MВ·exp P` is their limit (continuity of left multiplication + `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto`). This consumes the interaction Gibbs factor `e^{ОІD/m}` in the Trotter expansion | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionMulExpConeRep.lean` |
| `coneRep_exp_add` (kinetic factor) | **RP infra layer 12** (`RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the kinetic Gibbs factor is cone-representable. For left-supported `X`, `exp(X + Оё X) = exp X В· Оё(exp X) = Оё(exp X) В· exp X` (`ringReflectionThetaS_exp_add_eq`, via `exp_add_of_commute` since `X` and `Оё(X)` commute, `Оё(exp X) = exp(Оё X)`, and `mul_theta_comm`); the right-hand side is a single cone generator `Оё(L)В·L` (`L = exp X` left-supported), so `coneRep_exp_add` gives `RPTraceConeRepS (exp(X + Оё X))`. This is the kinetic building block (`e^{вҲ’ОІHвӮҖ/m}`) the Trotter expansion of `e^{вҲ’ОІH}` consumes | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_coneRep_right` (cone closure) | **RP infra layer 11** (`RingReflectionRPWeightCone.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflection-positive trace weights form a convex cone вҖ” `RPTraceWeightS.zero`/`one` (the identity is RP, the `ОІ=0` trace cone), `add`, `smul_nonneg`, `sum` (closed under finite sums) вҖ” and `RPTraceWeightS.mul_coneRep_right`: an RP trace weight times a cone-representable operator (`RPTraceConeRepS`) is again RP (since `MВ·вҲ‘cбөўОё(Cбөў)Cбөў = вҲ‘cбөў(MВ·Оё(Cбөў)Cбөў)`, each RP by the cone-generator closure, summed). This is the algebraic engine that accumulates reflection positivity factor-by-factor in the Trotter expansion of `e^{вҲ’ОІH}` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPWeightCone.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_weightGen_right` / `RPTraceWeightS.weightGen_mul_left` | **RP infra layer 10** (`RingReflectionGibbsRP.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): closure of reflection-positive trace weights under cone generators `Оё(L)В·L` (`L` left-supported). The naive "conjugation" closure `M вҶҰ LВ·MВ·Оё(L)` is **false** (the homomorphism order `Оё(LA)=Оё(L)Оё(A)` clashes with the test operator `B=AL`, and left-supported operators do not commute); the correct closure multiplies by a cone generator: `MВ·(Оё(L)В·L)` is RP (substitute the test operator `A вҶҰ LВ·A`, using `LВ·Оё(A)=Оё(A)В·L` and `Оё(L)В·Оё(A)=Оё(LВ·A)`) and `(Оё(L)В·L)В·M` is RP (substitute `A вҶҰ AВ·L`, via trace cyclicity). These are the kinetic-factor building blocks of the full DysonвҖ“LiebвҖ“Simon Gibbs reflection positivity | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsRP.lean` |
| `crossBondInteractionS` / `RPDecomposition` | **RP infra layer 9** (`RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean`, Tasaki В§4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the DLS decomposition data and the crossing-bond interaction. `crossBondInteractionS x = ОЈ_Оұ Ең_x^ОұВ·Оё(Ең_x^Оұ)` (left site `x`), with the **gauge identity** `crossBondInteractionS x = Ең_x^1 Ең_{r x}^1 вҲ’ Ең_x^2 Ең_{r x}^2 + Ең_x^3 Ең_{r x}^3` (the lone `вҲ’` on the `Ең^2` term is the sign flipped by the DLS/Marshall gauge вҖ” a ПҖ-rotation around axis 2 on the right half вҖ” making all three components contribute the same negative reflection-positive sign). Each `onSiteS x Ең^Оұ` (`x<n`) is left-supported; `crossBondInteractionS_exp_rpTraceWeight` shows `exp(tВ·crossBondInteractionS x)` (`tвүҘ0`) is a reflection-positive trace weight (via `mul_theta_comm` + the interaction-exponential cone). The abstract `RPDecomposition` structure (`H_L + Оё(H_L) вҲ’ ОЈ_b C_bВ·Оё(C_b)`, all `C_b` left-supported) packages the DLS form, with `interaction_exp_rpTraceWeight` giving the Gibbs interaction factor's reflection positivity | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean` |
| `no_long_range_order_1d` | **Corollary 4.3** (В§4.1, THEOREM; eq. (4.1.11)): absence of LRO in 1D on **even** rings. For the zero-field 1D AFM Heisenberg ring on **even** `L` sites (`Even L`, bipartite вҖ” faithful to Tasaki В§3.1/В§4.1.1 which define the lattice for even `L` only), the squared staggered order parameter per site vanishes in the thermodynamic limit `lim_{LвҶ‘вҲһ} вҹЁОҰ_GS\|(Г”_L^(3)/L)ВІ\|ОҰ_GSвҹ© = 0` (ОөвҖ“Оҙ form). Discharged (PR #5003) by feeding the documented Shastry susceptibility axiom `shastry_staggered_susceptibility_bound` (ПҮ(k*)вүӨCВ·L) into the conditional reduction `no_long_range_order_1d_of_susceptibility` for `N вүҘ 1`; the degenerate spin-0 case `N = 0` is unconditional (the staggered order operator vanishes). `#print axioms` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound, shastry_staggered_susceptibility_bound]` | `Quantum/SpinS/NoLongRangeOrder1D.lean` |
| `shastry_staggered_susceptibility_bound` | **Shastry susceptibility bound ПҮ(k*)вүӨCВ·L** (В§4.1, DOCUMENTED AXIOM; toward Corollary 4.3): for the zero-field 1D AFM Heisenberg ring on **even** `L вүҘ 2` sites (`Even L`, bipartite) there is a size-uniform `C вүҘ 0` with every normalized ground state admitting a potential `y` for `Г”ОҰ` (`(ДӨвҲ’EвӮҖ)y=Г”ОҰ`) of `O(L)` static staggered susceptibility `ReвҹЁy,Г”ОҰвҹ© вүӨ CВ·L` (physically `ПҮ(k*)=LВ·f_L^(-1)(k*)`). Tasaki does **not** prove this in the book вҖ” footnote 3 (p. 76) cites Shastry [58] / the rigorous formulation of TanakaвҖ“TakedaвҖ“Idogaki [63], and footnote 9 (p. 83) singles out the `f_L^(-1)(k*)` bound as the only "nontrivial part that requires some hard analysis". Per the project's explicit instruction this genuinely external hard-analysis estimate (massive-Green / inverse-Fourier `k*=ПҖ` control) based on Shastry J.Phys.A 25 L249 (1992) [58] and TanakaвҖ“TakedaвҖ“Idogaki JMMM 272вҖ“276 908 (2004) [63] is a documented axiom; it discharges `no_long_range_order_1d` (PR #5003) | `Quantum/SpinS/NoLongRangeOrder1D.lean` |
| `staggeredCasimirOpS` / `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` | **Theorem 4.4** (В§4.1, ShenвҖ“QiuвҖ“Tian, PROVED axiom-free; eqs. (4.1.12)вҖ“(4.1.13)): ferrimagnetic LRO on an asymmetric bipartite lattice. The SU(2)-invariant squared staggered operator `(Г”_Оӣ)ВІ = ОЈ_{x,y} Оө_x Оө_y Ең_xВ·Ең_y`, and the bound `SВІ(\|A\|вҲ’\|B\|)ВІ вүӨ вҹЁОҰ_GS\|(Г”_Оӣ)ВІ\|ОҰ_GSвҹ©` for any normalized ground state of the connected bipartite AFM model (same hypotheses as Thm 2.3). **PROVED axiom-free** (Issues #4604/#4617 CLOSED; book proof = chain (4.1.16): (4.1.15) cross-term + LiebвҖ“Mattis Thm 2.3 total spin + Casimir). PR1 (#4605) formalizes the **operator-algebra core** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROComponentAlgebra.lean`: `staggeredTransverseCasimirOpS` (transverse part of `(Г”_Оӣ)ВІ`), `(Г”_Оӣ)ВІ = transverse + (Г”_Оӣ^(3))ВІ`, the PSD drop `вҹЁtransverseвҹ© вүӨ вҹЁ(Г”_Оӣ)ВІвҹ©`, `(Ең_tot^(Оұ))ВІ = ОЈ_{x,y} Ең_x^(Оұ)Ең_y^(Оұ)`, the Casimir identity `ОЈ_{x,y}(Ең_x^(1)Ең_y^(1)+Ең_x^(2)Ең_y^(2)) = (Ең_tot)ВІ вҲ’ (Ең_tot^(3))ВІ`, and the M=0 reduction `вҹЁ(Ең_tot)ВІвҹ© = вҹЁtransverseвҹ©`. PR2 (#4606) adds the **cross-term inequality (4.1.15)** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCrossTermCore.lean` (transverse correlation + ladder positivity + per-pair sign) + `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCrossTerm.lean` (the summed cross-term inequality, split for build speed): the transverse correlation `вҹЁОҰ,T_xy ОҰвҹ©` (`T_xy = ВҪ(Ең_x^+Ең_y^- + Ең_x^-Ең_y^+)`), its non-positivity for cross-sublattice pairs on a Marshall-positive sector ground state (`marshallSignSВ·c`, `c>0`), and the summed dominance `вҹЁОҰ,(ОЈ_{x,y}T_xy)ОҰвҹ©.re вүӨ вҹЁОҰ,staggeredTransverseCasimirOpS ОҰвҹ©.re`. PR3 (#4607) adds the **total-spin value** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROTotalSpin.lean` (coupling-agnostic via a `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` hypothesis): `exists_centered_groundState_predictedCasimir_of_tasaki23` produces a centered (`ЕңВі_tot ОҰвҒ°=0`) Marshall ground state with `(Ең_tot)ВІ ОҰвҒ° = S_tot(S_tot+1)В·ОҰвҒ°` (`S_tot=(\|A\|вҲ’\|B\|)N/2`), reusing `tasaki23_pf_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_sector` + the extremal-Casimir machinery. PR4 (#4608) proves the **capstone** `ferrimagnetic_lro_completeBipartite_centered` in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCapstone.lean`: assembling chain (4.1.16) on the centered ground state, `(N/2)ВІ(\|A\|вҲ’\|B\|)ВІВ·вҹЁОҰвҒ°,ОҰвҒ°вҹ©.re вүӨ вҹЁОҰвҒ°,(Г”_Оӣ)ВІОҰвҒ°вҹ©.re` (existence form, coupling-agnostic via `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3`; sorry-free, axiom-clean, independent of the `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` axiom; that axiom is now itself proved in UniversalFinal). For build speed the proof's private Rayleigh-ratio helpers and the per-sector oriented bound `staggeredCasimir_weightComponent_bound_oriented` (now module-public: `S_totВІВ·вҖ–ОҰ_MвҖ–ВІ вүӨ вҹЁОҰ_M,(Г”_Оӣ)ВІОҰ_Mвҹ©.re` for each magnetization weight component `ОҰ_M` of a ground state) live in the companion `вҖҰUniversalFinalCore.lean`, with the sum assembly + the public `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` kept in `вҖҰUniversalFinal.lean`. | `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROUniversalFinal.lean` + `вҖҰUniversalFinalCore.lean` + `FerrimagneticLRO.lean` + `вҖҰComponentAlgebra.lean` + `вҖҰCrossTerm.lean` + `вҖҰTotalSpin.lean` + `вҖҰTotalSpinCore.lean` + `вҖҰCapstone.lean` + `вҖҰUniversal.lean` |
| `raiseLowerReachableS_of_connected` | **Connected-graph spin-config reachability** (В§2.5, PROVED; Issue #4609, prereq for the connected MarshallвҖ“LiebвҖ“Mattis extension / Thm 4.4 axiom): on a connected graph `G`, any two equal-magnetization configs are raise/lower reachable вҖ” `G.Connected вҶ’ magSumS Пғ = magSumS Пғ' вҶ’ RaiseLowerReachableS G Пғ Пғ'`. Discharged axiom-free by strong induction on the `configDistS` mismatch (surplus/deficit sites + connected-graph walk) with overflow-safe single-quantum path transport (`transportOne`; at each edge push the quantum forward when the next vertex has room, else recurse first to make room). Generalizes the complete-bipartite `raiseLowerReachableS_bipartiteCompleteGraph` to arbitrary connected graphs вҖ” the combinatorial core of extending Theorem 2.3 to connected couplings (PR #4610). **PR2 (#4611)** lifts this to the dressed-matrix per-sector PerronвҖ“Frobenius positivity for a general connected bipartite `G` in `Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedDressedPF.lean`: `exists_matrixPow_pos_of_magConfigS_connected` (`вҲғ k, 0 < (shiftedDressedSReMatrixOnMagSector A J N c M)^k Пғ' Пғ`) from `G.Connected` + `hGbip` + edge-positivity `hJ_pos_G : G.Adj x y вҶ’ 0 < (J x y).re` (not complete-bipartite positivity), via the graph-agnostic edge-local witness `neg_dressedHeisenbergSReMatrix_apply_pos_of_raiseLowerStepS_witness` + the connected reachability вҖ” generalizing `exists_matrixPow_pos_of_magConfigS_bipartite`. **PR3 (#4612)** assembles **Theorem 2.3 itself for connected couplings**: `isIrreducible_shiftedDressedSReMatrixOnMagSector_connected` (ConnectedSectorIrreducible.lean) + 20 `_of_irreducible` chain variants parameterizing the PF-consuming MarshallвҖ“LiebвҖ“Mattis chain by the irreducibility result (graph-agnostic) + the capstone `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_data_of_connected` (ConnectedTheorem23Core.lean, ConnectedTheorem23.lean): the per-magnetization-sector Marshall-positive ground states + global minimality of Theorem 2.3 hold for ANY connected bipartite `J` (`G.Connected` + `hGbip` + edge-positivity `hJ_pos_G`), not just complete-bipartite вҖ” the `hOutside`/lower-bound side reuses `tasaki23_general_hOutside`/`tasaki23_eigenvalue_ge_common` (which need only `hJ_nn`). This is the connected-coupling extension of MarshallвҖ“LiebвҖ“Mattis (LiebвҖ“Mattis 1962). **PR4 (#4613)** assembles the **connected-coupling ferrimagnetic LRO bound (existence form)** `ferrimagnetic_lro_connected_centered` (ConnectedFerrimagneticLRO.lean): for any connected bipartite AFM coupling (`G.Connected` + `hGbip` + `hJ_pos_G`), there is a centered ground state `ОҰвҒ°` with `(N/2)ВІ(\|A\|вҲ’\|B\|)ВІВ·вҹЁОҰвҒ°,ОҰвҒ°вҹ©.re вүӨ вҹЁОҰвҒ°,(Г”_Оӣ)ВІОҰвҒ°вҹ©.re` вҖ” Tasaki's chain (4.1.16) for the genuine connected (non-complete) coupling, built from the connected Theorem 2.3 data + connected irreducibility Casimir value + the PR1/PR2 operator-algebra (all graph-agnostic). **PR5 (#4614)** proves the **`SU(2)` invariance of `(Г”_Оӣ)ВІ`** in `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredCasimirSU2Invariance.lean`: `staggeredCasimirOpS_commute_totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}` + `вҖҰOpPlus`/`вҖҰOpMinus` (the squared staggered order operator commutes with every total-spin operator), immediate from the per-pair `spinSDot_commutator_totalSpinSOp*` vanishing вҖ” ingredient (a) of the universal-form transfer. **PR6 (#4615)** proves the **core of ingredient (b)** in `Quantum/SpinS/SU2ExpectationLadderInvariant.lean`: `su2_expectationRatioRe_ladder_invariant` вҖ” for an SU(2)-invariant operator `O` (commuting with `Ең^Вұ_tot`) and a joint `ЕңВі_tot`/Casimir eigenvector `v`, the real expectation ratio `вҹЁw,Owвҹ©.re/вҹЁw,wвҹ©.re` is unchanged under lowering `w вҶҰ ЕңвҒ»_tot v` (both numerator and denominator scale by the same `ЕңвҒәЕңвҒ» = Casimir вҲ’ (ЕңВі)ВІ + ЕңВі` eigenvalue). This is the ladder step that makes `(Г”_Оӣ)ВІ`'s expectation constant across the spin-`S_tot` multiplet; **PR7 (#4616)** iterates it to `(ЕңвҒ»_tot)^k` (`su2_expectationRatioRe_ladder_iterate_invariant`, `Quantum/SpinS/SU2ExpectationLadderIterated.lean`), so every member of the lowering tower shares the centered ratio. The **stated** `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` axiom (universal over *all* ground states) now remains pending only the final assembly of ingredient (b): the ground-state weight decomposition + the **ground-eigenspace classification** (any global-min eigenvector's nonzero `ЕңВі`-weight components lie in the admissible sectors вҖ” needs strict energy separation, the hard remaining gate) (#4604). **PR8 (#4618)** adds step 1 of that dimension bound: `heisenbergHamiltonianSMatrixOnMagSector_finrank_le_one_of_marshall_positive_connected` (`Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedSectorFinrankLeOne.lean`) вҖ” connected-coupling per-sector ground-state uniqueness (`finrank вүӨ 1`), the connected-irreducibility mirror of the complete-graph lemma (Issue #4617). **PR9 (#4619)** adds step 3: `weightPreserving_expectation_eq_sum_sector` (`Quantum/SpinS/WeightPreservingExpectationSum.lean`) вҖ” for any operator commuting with `ЕңВі_tot`, `вҹЁОҰ,OОҰвҹ© = ОЈ_M вҹЁОҰ_M,OОҰ_Mвҹ©` over the `ЕңВі`-weight sector components (cross terms vanish by `ЕңВі_tot`-eigenvalue orthogonality; no Hermiticity of `O` needed), the block-diagonal decomposition for `(Г”_Оӣ)ВІ`'s expectation. **PR10 (#4620)** clears the hard gate (step 2): `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_of_connected` (`Quantum/SpinS/StrictHOutsideFerrimagnetic.lean`; for build speed the per-sector ferrimagnetic foundation `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_ferrimagnetic` and its Casimir-obstruction helpers live in the companion `вҖҰStrictHOutsideFerrimagneticCore.lean`) вҖ” **strict** energy separation, `Ој < ОјM` for any non-admissible sector `M вҲү [minВ·N, maxВ·N]` (the connected ferrimagnetic generalization of the balanced `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_of_card_eq_zero_casimir_ladder_obstruction`, via the Casimir equality-obstruction: ladder a hypothetical non-admissible Ој-eigenvector to the admissible band edge, pull back the predicted Casimir `S_tot(S_tot+1)` through the ladder, contradict the abs Casimir lower bound `|centerвҲ’M|(|centerвҲ’M|+1) > S_tot(S_tot+1)`). This is the strict LiebвҖ“Mattis level ordering for the connected AFM Heisenberg вҖ” the last gate; only the final weight-decomposition assembly remains to discharge `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` (#4617). **PR11 (#4621)** adds the universal-form assembly infra in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROUniversal.lean`: `chain_bound_marshall_sector` (Tasaki's chain (4.1.16) at an arbitrary-weight Marshall sector vector: `(Оі вҲ’ mВІ)вҖ–wвҖ–ВІ вүӨ вҹЁw,(Г”_Оӣ)ВІwвҹ©.re`), `star_dotProduct_self_eq_sum_sector` (`вҖ–ОҰвҖ–ВІ = ОЈ_M вҖ–ОҰ_MвҖ–ВІ`), `heisenbergHamiltonianS_magSectorProjection_eigen` (a weight component of an H-eigenvector is an H-eigenvector), the diagonal-shift `c` existence, and the global-flip / weight-commute helpers. The remaining step to remove the axiom is the SU(2) Rayleigh-ratio constancy across the spin-`S_tot` multiplet (transfer the per-sector bound from a near-central admissible sector to all sectors via the merged iterated ladder-invariance) (#4617) | `Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedRaiseLower.lean` + `вҖҰConnectedDressedPF.lean` + `вҖҰConnectedSectorIrreducible.lean` + `вҖҰConnectedTheorem23Core.lean` + `вҖҰConnectedTheorem23.lean` + `вҖҰConnectedFerrimagneticLRO.lean` + `вҖҰStaggeredCasimirSU2Invariance.lean` + `вҖҰSU2ExpectationLadderInvariant.lean` + `вҖҰSU2ExpectationLadderIterated.lean` + `вҖҰConnectedSectorFinrankLeOne.lean` + `вҖҰWeightPreservingExpectationSum.lean` + `вҖҰStrictHOutsideFerrimagnetic.lean` + `вҖҰStrictHOutsideFerrimagneticCore.lean` + `вҖҰFerrimagneticLROUniversal.lean` |
| `reflectionPositivity_averaging` | **Lemma 4.5** (В§4.1.2, PROVED; eqs. (4.1.55)вҖ“(4.1.57)): the abstract reflection-positivity averaging inequality used in the proof of Thm 4.1. For `F : (Fin 2n вҶ’ V) вҶ’ в„қ` (arbitrary `V`) invariant under the cyclic shift and satisfying the reflection bound `F f вүҘ ВҪ(F(reflectLeft f)+F(reflectRight f))`, one has `(1/2n)В·ОЈ_j F(jвҶҰf j) вүӨ F f`. Discharged axiom-free via Tasaki's `G_min=0` collapse argument: minimise `G f = F f вҲ’ (2n)вҒ»В№ОЈ_j F(const f_j)` over the finite carrier `{fвҲҳПғ}`; at a minimiser the reflection bound is an equality, and a leading-run doubling collapses the minimiser to a constant string where `G = 0` (PR #4598) | `Math/ReflectionPositivityAveraging.lean` |
| `HypercubicTorus` / `torusNNCoupling` / `staggeredRaisingOpS` / `tower_lowLying_energy_bound` | **Theorem 4.6** (В§4.2.2, Anderson's tower, **now PROVED вҖ” axiom discharged**, PR #4769/#4773; eqs. (4.2.2)вҖ“(4.2.4)): low-lying tower states. Builds the d-dim hypercubic torus model `Fin d вҶ’ ZMod L` (`L^d` sites, NN coupling, parity sublattice) + staggered raising/lowering order operators `Г”_L^Вұ = ОЈ_x Оө_x Ең_x^Вұ`. The two-sided tower states `towerState M = (Г”_L^{sgn M})^{\|M\|} ОҰ_GS` (`M : в„Ө`, raising for MвүҘ0 / lowering for M<0) satisfy `вҹЁПҲ_M,ДӨПҲ_Mвҹ©/вҹЁПҲ_M,ПҲ_Mвҹ© вүӨ E_GS + CвӮӮMВІ/L^d` for `\|M\| вүӨ CвӮҒL^{d/2}` (Rayleigh-ratio form, scale-invariant). **Conditional on LRO** (`qвӮҖ>0` premise: `вҹЁ(Г”_L/L^d)ВІвҹ© = вҹЁГ”ВІвҹ©/(вҹЁОҰ,ОҰвҹ©В·(L^d)ВІ) вүҘ qвӮҖ`, eq. 4.1.7), so vacuous in d=1 per Cor 4.3. **Proof** (axiom-clean, `#print axioms` = propext/Classical.choice/Quot.sound): the variational double-commutator bound (вҳ…, `tower_numerator_double_commutator_le`) reduces the gap to `вҹЁОҰ,[(ГҙвҒ»)^M,[ДӨ,(ГҙвҒә)^M]]ОҰвҹ©`; this numerator is bounded `вүӨ MВІ(A/VВ·P_M + B MВІ/VВІВ·P_M)` via Lemma R2 (split-independent renormalized commutator estimate `r2_split_independent`) + locality decay of `dМӮ=[ГҙвҒә,[ДӨ,ГҙвҒ»]]` and `[ДӨ,ГҙвҒә]` + moment-factorвҶ’`P_M` conversions; the denominator `вҖ–(ГҙВұ)^M ОҰвҖ–ВІ вүҘ ВҪP_M` (Lemma R1, balanced-word `orderWord_balanced_re_close`); the LRO recursion `2qвӮҖP_n вүӨ P_{n+1}` (`phatMoment_succ_two_q0_le`) needs the **total-spin-singlet** premise `ЕңВіОҰ=ЕңВ№ОҰ=0` (added to `IsAndersonTowerConstants`; faithful вҖ” the bipartite AFM GS is the unique singlet by MLM Thm 2.3). `M<0` via Hermitian conjugation; constants `CвӮҒ=min(вҲҡ(qвӮҖ/6N),вҲҡ(2^d)вҲҡ(2qвӮҖ)/16N)`, `CвӮӮ=288dNвҒҙ/qвӮҖ+576CвӮҒВІdNВі(1+1/вҲҡ(2qвӮҖ))/qвӮҖ`; `N=0` vacuous (order op = 0) | `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTower.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTowerTheorem46.lean` |
| `tower_lowLying_eigenstates` | **Corollary 4.7** (В§4.2.2, **now PROVED вҖ” axiom discharged**, PR #4775; eq. (4.2.7)): the tower of low-lying energy **eigenstates**. For a total-spin-singlet GS and each `M вү  0` with `\|M\| вүӨ CвӮҒL^{d/2}` (nonzero tower state), вҲғ an ДӨ-eigenstate `ОЁ_M` in the `Ең_tot^(3)` sector `M` with `E_GS < E_M вүӨ E_GS + CвӮӮMВІ/L^d`. **Proof** (axiom-clean): `ОЁ_M` = the minimum-energy eigenstate of `ДӨ` restricted to the magnetization sector of `towerState M ОҰ` (`heisenbergHamiltonianS_magSector_min_eigenvector`, lifting the restricted-Hermitian min eigenvector via `magSectorEmbedding`); its energy `вүӨ Rayleigh(towerState)` (`tower_sectorMin_mul_le`, variational + norm/energy embedding bridges) `вүӨ E_GS + CвӮӮMВІ/L^d` (Theorem 4.6); strict gap from the **ground-sector-exclusion** premise (every ground eigenstate is a singlet) since `ОЁ_M` sits in sector `M вү  0`. Distinct `M` вҶ’ distinct sectors вҶ’ `O(L^{d/2})` distinct low-lying eigenstates (Anderson tower). Conditional on LRO (vacuous in d=1) | `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTowerEigenstates.lean` |

(4.2.12)вҖ“(4.2.15)/(4.2.56)/footnote 21): the Tanaka state exhibits **full SU(2) symmetry breaking** in the (1,0,0) direction. The former axiom is now a **proved theorem** for the **explicit** slowly-diverging sequence `M(L) := вҢҠL^{d/4}вҢӢ` (`Tendsto M atTop atTop`, with `M+1 вүӨ CвӮҒL^{d/2}` and `MВІ/V вҶ’ 0` both eventually). For that `M`, the per-site staggered moments (`tanakaOrderMean1/2/3`, `tanakaOrderSecond1/2/3`, Rayleigh-ratio via `expectationRatioRe`) satisfy `liminfвҹЁГ”^(1)/L^dвҹ©вүҘmStar`, `liminfвҹЁ(Г”^(1)/L^d)ВІвҹ©вүҘmStarВІ` (liminf per footnote 21, from `sqrt_q0_le_tanakaOrderMean1`/`q0_le_tanakaOrderSecond1` with `mStar = вҲҡqвӮҖ`), `вҹЁГ”^(Оұ)/L^dвҹ©=0` (Оұ=2,3, exactly, `tanakaOrderMean2/3_eq_zero`), `limвҹЁ(Г”^(Оұ)/L^d)ВІвҹ©=0` (Оұ=2,3, from `tanakaOrderSecond2_le` + the clean fluctuation bound `deltaFluctBound_le_clean` and the M(L) squeeze, with axis-3 reduced to axis-2 via `tanakaOrderSecond3_eq_tanakaOrderSecond2`, eq. 4.2.56 вҖ” `Ең_tot^(1) Оһ = 0` since `Г”^(1)` commutes with `Ең_tot^(1)`). The GS family is a total-spin singlet (`Ең_tot^(3)ОҰ=0`, `Ең_tot^(1)ОҰ=0`, eq. 4.1.7) that is axis-1 reversal invariant (`ОҳОҰ=ОҰ`, `Оҳ=manyBodyReversalS`); `mStar = вҲҡqвӮҖ > 0` existential (eq. 4.2.9 double limit not constructed); same `CвӮҒ` as Thm 4.6. | `sqrt_q0_le_tanakaOrderMean1` / `q0_le_tanakaOrderSecond1` / `staggeredOrderOp1S_isHermitian` / `orderOp1_evenMoment_ratio_ge_q0` / `tanakaSSBState_vecNormSqRe_eq_one` | **Theorem 4.9 lower bounds** (В§4.2.2, Tasaki Theorem 4.9 discharge PR3, #4970; eqs. (4.2.12)/(4.2.13)/(4.2.35)/(4.2.45)вҖ“(4.2.47), footnote 21): the Tanaka state order-parameter lower bounds at every finite volume. **(4.2.12)**: `вҲҡqвӮҖ вүӨ вҹЁОһ| Г”_L^(1)/L^d |Оһвҹ©` (axis-1 per-site staggered moment, eq. 4.2.12 `sqrt_q0_le_tanakaOrderMean1`); **(4.2.13)**: `qвӮҖ вүӨ вҹЁОһ| (Г”_L^(1)/L^d)ВІ |Оһвҹ©` (squared moment, eq. 4.2.13 `q0_le_tanakaOrderSecond1`). **Proof mechanism**: log-convexity of even bare moments `B_k := вҖ–(Г”_L^(1))^k ОҰвҖ–ВІ` (CauchyвҖ“Schwarz, eq. 4.2.35) yields non-decreasing ratio `R_k := B_{k+1}/(B_k VВІ)` via `real_logConvex_cross`; base case `R_0 вүҘ qвӮҖ` from charge selection `вҹЁ(Г”^(1))ВІвҹ© = вҹЁpМӮвҹ© VВІ/2` combined with proved long-range-order bridge `вҹЁpМӮвҹ© вүҘ 2qвӮҖ вҖ–ОҰвҖ–ВІ` (`phatMoment_succ_two_q0_le`); hence `R_M вүҘ qвӮҖ` via `orderOp1_evenMoment_ratio_ge_q0`; the Оһ sandwich `tanakaSSBState_dotProduct_mulVec_re_eq` with parity structure (odd-moment diagonals vanish, squared operator commutes with parity so cross-terms vanish) yields both bounds. Infrastructure: Hermiticity `staggeredOrderOp1S_isHermitian`, unit normalization `tanakaSSBState_vecNormSqRe_eq_one`. These bounds hold at every finite volume, so their `liminf` (footnote 21 form) is `вүҘ` the same; the existential slowly-diverging M and full SU(2)-breaking limit remain axiomatized in Theorem 4.9. Unlike Theorem 4.8 (eqs. 4.2.34вҖ“4.2.44), no central-binomial cancellation neededвҖ”only ratio monotonicity. | `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTowerTanakaLowerBounds.lean` |
| `manyBodyReversalS_conj_staggeredOrderOp1S`, `manyBodyReversalS_conj_staggeredOrderOp2S`, `manyBodyReversalS_conj_staggeredOrderOpS`, `manyBodyReversalS_commute_staggeredOrderOp1S`, `manyBodyReversalS_mulVec_tanakaTowerTerm`, `manyBodyReversalS_mulVec_tanakaSSBState`, `tanakaOrderMean2_eq_zero`, `tanakaOrderMean3_eq_zero`, `tanakaSSBState_dotProduct_mulVec_re_eq`, `tanakaTowerTerm_cross_charge_conserving_eq_zero`, `manyBodyReversalS_conjTranspose` | **Theorem 4.9 machinery** (В§4.2.2, Tasaki Theorem 4.9 discharge PR2, #4969; eqs. (4.2.14), (4.2.45)вҖ“(4.2.49), (4.2.69)): the axis-1 spin reversal `Оҳ = manyBodyReversalS` conjugation infrastructure. **Conjugation by `Оҳ`**: `Оҳ Г”^(1) Оҳ = Г”^(1)` (axis-1 fix), `Оҳ Г”^(2) Оҳ = вҲ’Г”^(2)`, `Оҳ Г”^(3) Оҳ = вҲ’Г”^(3)` (transverse reversal; `staggeredOrderOp1/2/3S`); `Оҳ` commutes with the axis-1 operator and fixes the Tanaka state `Оҳ Оһ = Оһ` when `Оҳ ОҰ = ОҰ`. **Symmetry of `Оҳ`**: real symmetric involution `Оҳбҙҙ = Оҳ` (`manyBodyReversalS_conjTranspose`). **Transverse moment vanishing** (Tasaki eq. 4.2.14): anti-invariance under a symmetric involution forces `вҹЁОһ| Г”^(Оұ) |Оһвҹ© = 0` for `Оұ = 2, 3` (proven for Оұ=2,3 via `tanakaOrderMean2/3_eq_zero`). **General Оһ sandwich expansion** (Tasaki eqs. 4.2.45/4.2.49): for Hermitian `O`, `вҹЁОһ| O |Оһвҹ©.re` decomposes into normalized tower-term expectations (`tanakaSSBState_dotProduct_mulVec_re_eq`). **Charge-parity cross-term vanishing** (Tasaki axis-3 analogue of eq. 4.2.69): for a charge-conserving operator `O` commuting with the parity `Гӣ = exp(iПҖ Ең_tot^(3))`, the two adjacent tower terms `(Г”^(1))^M ОҰ` and `(Г”^(1))^{M+1} ОҰ` decouple (`tanakaTowerTerm_cross_charge_conserving_eq_zero`) because they are `Гӣ`-eigenstates with distinct eigenvalues | `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTowerTanakaMoments.lean` |
| `orderWord_balanced_re_close_fine` | **Theorem 4.9 axis-2 decay вҖ” fine denominator closeness** (В§4.2.2, Tasaki Theorem 4.9 discharge PR4, #4971; eqs. (4.2.34)/(4.2.42)): the precision `O(1/V)` two-sided bound on balanced-word denominator. For a singlet `ОҰ` with size `3N(m+1)ВІ вүӨ 2qвӮҖV`, the summed-density even power `D_{m+1} = вҹЁОҰ, (ГҙвҒә + ГҙвҒ»)^{2(m+1)} ОҰвҹ©` is within the fine band `C(2(m+1),m+1) В· (m+1)ВІ (N/V) (3/2 P_m)` of `C(2(m+1),m+1) В· P_{m+1}`. Proof: expand into the `2^{2(m+1)}` order words, drop unbalanced words via singlet charge-selection, leaving `C(2(m+1),m+1)` balanced words, each pinched by the swap-chain kernel `orderWord_balanced_re_close_step` **(retaining the `(m+1)ВІ N/V` prefactorвҖ”not collapsing to crude `ВҪ P`)**; sum per-word deviations. This refinement (vs crude `orderWord_balanced_re_close`) resolves the central-binomial cancellation in axis-2 decay (Theorem 4.9 PR4) | `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTowerEnergyBoundR1.lean` |
| `orderSum_pow_two_denom_close` / `staggeredPhatS_manyBodyOperatorNormS_le` / `phatMoment_succ_le_normSq` / `orderSum_pow_phat_insert_close` / `tanakaOrderSecond2_eq_half_sum` / `tanaka_delta_eq` / `tanaka_delta_le` / `tanakaOrderSecond2_le` | **Theorem 4.9 axis-2 transverse fluctuation decay** (В§4.2.2, Tasaki Theorem 4.9 discharge PR4, #4971; eqs. (4.2.15)/(4.2.33)/(4.2.34)/(4.2.49)вҖ“(4.2.55)): the Tanaka state vanishes in the **axis-2 direction** (transverse to axis-1 SSB). The mechanism (the tower `u_k` is built from the axis-1 operator `Г”_L^{(1)} = (V/2) Гғ`, `Гғ = ГҙвҒә + ГҙвҒ»`, while the measured transverse observable is `(Гҙ^{(2)})ВІ` with `Гҙ^{(2)} = (2i)вҒ»В№(ГҙвҒә вҲ’ ГҙвҒ»)`): per-site transverse fluctuation `Оҙ_k := вҹЁu_k| (Гҙ^{(2)})ВІ |u_kвҹ© = Q_k вҲ’ R_k` with `Q_k = E_k/D_k` (single pМӮ-insertion, numerator `E_k = вҹЁОҰ, Гғ^k pМӮ Гғ^k ОҰвҹ©`), `R_k = D_{k+1}/(4D_k)` (ratio of denominators), `D_k = вҹЁОҰ, Гғ^{2k} ОҰвҹ©`. **(F1) Two-sided denominator closeness** (`orderSum_pow_two_denom_close`, eq. 4.2.42): `|D_{m+1} вҲ’ C(2(m+1),m+1) P_{m+1}| вүӨ C(2(m+1),m+1) В· (m+1)ВІ (N/V) (3/2 P_m)` via balanced-word expansion + fine bound `orderWord_balanced_re_close_fine` (eq. 4.2.34). **(F2) Numerator closeness with pМӮ insertion** (`orderSum_pow_phat_insert_close`, eq. 4.2.50): `|E_k вҲ’ C(2k,k) P_{k+1}| вүӨ ...` via length-`2(k+1)` balanced-word expansion with central pМӮ + Vandermonde counting. **(Gap2) Rayleigh power ratio** (`staggeredPhatS_manyBodyOperatorNormS_le`, `phatMoment_succ_le_normSq`): `P_{k+1} вүӨ NВІ P_k` and `вҖ–pМӮ u_kвҖ–ВІ вүӨ P_{k+1}`. **(F3) Per-site fluctuation decomposition** (`tanakaOrderSecond2_eq_half_sum`): `second2 = ВҪ(Оҙ_M + Оҙ_{M+1})` (tower-term average). **(F4) Оҙ_k bound and capstone** (`tanaka_delta_eq`, `tanaka_delta_le`, capstone `tanakaOrderSecond2_le`): Оҙ_k вүӨ P_{k+1}/(D_kВ·2k+2) вүӨ NВІ/(2k+2) + O(1/V) from Pascal ratio `(k+1) C(2k+2,k+1) = 2(2k+1) C(2k,k)` вҹ№ second2 вүӨ Оө as LвҶ’вҲһ. **Lemmas**: `staggeredPhatS_manyBodyOperatorNormS_le` (pМӮ norm), `phatMoment_succ_le_normSq` (ratio). **Status**: the section tip `tanakaOrderSecond2_le` is a **proved theorem** (all of F1вҖ“Gap2вҖ“F3вҖ“F4 discharged axiom-free); the overall Theorem 4.9 (`tanakaSSB_full_symmetry_breaking`) is now itself a **proved theorem**, discharged in PR5 (#4972) by assembling this finite-`L` bound with the explicit tower sequence `M(L) = вҢҠL^{d/4}вҢӢ` (see the Theorem 4.9 capstone row) | `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTowerEnergyBound.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/AndersonTowerTanakaFluctuation.lean` |

(4.3.4) / В§A.7, Issue #4644; AXIOM-FREE): the finite-box AFM Heisenberg Hamiltonian `ДӨ_{Оӣ_n} = ВҪ ОЈ_{(x,y) bond in Оӣ_n} Ең_xВ·Ең_y` of an `InfiniteSpinSystem`, as an element of the abstract quasi-local C*-algebra `A`. `boxOrderedBondPairs d n` is the ordered adjacent vertex pairs of the induced box graph (`mem_boxOrderedBondPairs`, `swap_mem_boxOrderedBondPairs`); the ВҪ compensates the ordered double sum (no `Ең_xВ·Ең_y=Ең_yВ·Ең_x` symmetry is assumed). `spinDot_mem_localSubalgebra_of_mem` (both endpoints in Оӣ вҹ№ bond op in A_Оӣ) gives `boxLocalHamiltonian_mem_boxLocalSubalgebra` (`ДӨ_{Оӣ_n}вҲҲA_{Оӣ_n}`) and `boxLocalHamiltonian_mem_localAlg` (`ДӨ_{Оӣ_n}вҲҲA_loc`). (4.4.1)вҖ“(4.4.6)): the 1D Heisenberg model is **disordered at any nonzero temperature**. New finite-temperature framework: Gibbs operator `thermalGibbsOpS ОІ H = e^{вҲ’ОІH}`, partition `thermalPartitionFnS`, real canonical average `thermalAverageReS ОІ H A = Re Tr[A e^{вҲ’ОІH}]/Re Tr[e^{вҲ’ОІH}]` (eq. 4.4.3); field Hamiltonians `heisenbergFieldHamiltonianS ferro` covering ferro (4.4.1, uniform field) and AFM (4.4.2, staggered field); torus embedding `torusEmbed`, `в„“В№` distance `intL1Dist`, even-side sequence `evenSide n=2(n+1)`. **(4.4.5)** `tasaki_4_22_magnetization_vanishes`: `lim_{hвҶ“0} lim_{LвҶ‘вҲһ} вҹЁЕң_x^(3)вҹ©_{ОІ,h}^L=0` (no SSB; order of limits essential), stated soundly per footnote 41 (ОөвҖ“Оҙ with inner liminf-subsequence `вҲғnвӮҖ вҲҖnвүҘnвӮҖ вҲғmвүҘn, |mag|<Оө`). **(4.4.6)** `tasaki_4_22_exponential_clustering`: вҲғ Оҫ(ОІ),C(ОІ)вҲҲ(0,вҲһ) with `|вҹЁЕң_x^(Оұ)Ең_y^(Оұ)вҹ©_{ОІ,0}^вҲһ| вүӨ C exp(вҲ’|xвҲ’y|/Оҫ)`, bounding the concrete `liminf` correlation `infiniteVolSpinCorrLiminf` | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergEquilibrium.lean` |

Reuses the Theorem 4.22 finite-temperature framework at general `d` (`hd : 2 вүӨ d`): there exists `ОІвӮҖ вҲҲ (0,вҲһ)` (depending only on `d` and `S`, **not** on the ferro/AFM flag вҖ” `ferro` is universally quantified under `вҲғ ОІвӮҖ`) such that for every `ОІ вҲҲ [0, ОІвӮҖ]`, **(4.4.7)** the magnetization vanishes `lim_{hвҶ“0} lim_{LвҶ‘вҲһ} вҹЁЕң_x^(3)вҹ©_{ОІ,h}^L = 0` (same ОөвҖ“Оҙ / liminf-subsequence form) and **(4.4.8)** вҲғ Оҫ(ОІ),C(ОІ)вҲҲ(0,вҲһ) with `|вҹЁЕң_x^(Оұ)Ең_y^(Оұ)вҹ©_{ОІ,0}^вҲһ| вүӨ C exp(вҲ’|xвҲ’y|/Оҫ)`. New `generalizedFieldHamiltonianS J Оҫ` (eq. 4.4.21): `J ОЈ ЕңВ·Ең вҲ’ h ОЈ_x ОЈ_Оұ (Оҫ_x^Оұ) Ең_x^(Оұ)` (field `h_x = h Оҫ_x`) with `J вҲҲ {вҲ’1,+1}` and an **arbitrary fixed field-direction family** `Оҫ` (`\|Оҫ_x\| вүӨ 1`), generalizing the uniform/staggered fields; `finiteVolMagnetizationGenS` is its finite-volume Gibbs magnetization `вҹЁЕң_x^(Оұ)вҹ©_{ОІ,h}^L`. For `d=2`, any `ОІ вҲҲ [0,вҲһ)`, every `J=Вұ1`, every bounded `Оҫ`, every component `Оұ=1,2,3`, and every `x вҲҲ в„ӨВІ`: `lim_{hвҶ“0} lim_{LвҶ‘вҲһ} вҹЁЕң_x^(Оұ)вҹ©_{ОІ,h}^L = 0` (4.4.22). Per footnote 48 the inner limit is a **limsup**, so the sound form is **eventual** (`вҲғnвӮҖ вҲҖnвүҘnвӮҖ`, all large even volumes) вҖ” stronger than the liminf-subsequence form of 4.22/4.23. (4.4.23)вҖ“(4.4.24)): the **McBryanвҖ“Spencer / KomaвҖ“Tasaki power-law bound** вҖ” in `d=2` at `h=0` the finite-volume two-point correlation `finiteVolSpinCorrS` decays at least as a power law: there is an `L`-independent positive **decreasing** exponent `О·(ОІ)` with `\|вҹЁЕң_x^(Оұ)Ең_y^(Оұ)вҹ©_{ОІ,0}^L\| вүӨ 2SВІ \|xвҲ’y\|^{вҲ’О·(ОІ)}` (`Real.rpow`) for every `Оұ=1,2,3`, `ОІвҲҲ[0,вҲһ)`, and every pair of **distinct** sites with `0 < \|xвҲ’y\| < L/2`. The distinctness `0<\|xвҲ’y\|` is **required for soundness** (`Real.rpow 0 (вҲ’О·)=0` in Lean, so the bound would be false at `x=y` where the self-correlation is positive). `О·(ОІ) вүғ (16CSВІОІ)^{вҲ’1}` for large `ОІ`; the same `О·` is bound before `ferro` (works for both signs). Weaker than the conjectured exponential decay but already excludes 2D long-range order | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergEquilibrium.lean` |
| `theorem_4_26_staggered_lro` | **Theorem 4.26** (В§4.4.4, AXIOM; eq. (4.4.52)): the **DysonвҖ“LiebвҖ“Simon theorem** вҖ” genuine NГ©el long-range order at sufficiently low temperature in `d вүҘ 3`. New per-axis staggered order operator `staggeredOrderOpAxisS Оұ A N` (`Оұ=0/1/2 вҶ’ Г”^(1)/Г”^(2)/Г”^(3)`). For the AFM Heisenberg model with `d вүҘ 3` (`hd : 3 вүӨ d`) and any `S = N/2` with `N вүҘ 1` (`[NeZero N]`): there exist `ОІвӮҖ вҲҲ (0,вҲһ)` and `q(ОІ)` with `q(ОІ) > 0` for `ОІ > ОІвӮҖ` such that `вҹЁ(Г”_L^(Оұ)/L^d)ВІвҹ©_{ОІ,0}^L = вҹЁ(Г”_L^(Оұ))ВІвҹ©_{ОІ,0}^L / (L^d)ВІ вүҘ q(ОІ)` for every `Оұ=1,2,3`, every `ОІ > ОІвӮҖ`, and all sufficiently large even volumes (the squared operator `Г”_L^(Оұ)/L^d` gives the `(L^d)ВІ` normalization вҖ” the intensive LRO density, as in В§4.1/В§4.2). `[NeZero N]` is **essential for soundness** (at `N=0` the order operator vanishes, contradicting `q(ОІ)>0`). Proof: reflection positivity (DLS; `d=3,S=1/2` via KennedyвҖ“LiebвҖ“Shastry). The ferromagnetic LRO is only conjectured (RP fails) | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergEquilibrium.lean` |
| `theorem_4_27_griffiths_koma_tasaki_ssb` | **Theorem 4.27** (В§4.4.4, AXIOM; eq. (4.4.53)): the **Griffiths / KomaвҖ“Tasaki theorem** вҖ” under the same conditions as Theorem 4.26 (AFM, `d вүҘ 3`, `N вүҘ 1` `[NeZero N]`), low-temperature LRO is accompanied by genuine SSB. With the staggered field (eq. 4.4.2), `lim_{hвҶ“0} lim_{LвҶ‘вҲһ} вҹЁГ”_L^(3)вҹ©_{ОІ,h}^L / L^d вүҘ вҲҡ(3 q(ОІ))` for `ОІ > ОІвӮҖ`. The axiom bundles the Theorem 4.26 LRO bound (4.4.52) and the SSB bound (4.4.53) under one **shared `вҲғОІвӮҖ вҲғq`**, making the "same q" dependence explicit; the `вҲҡ3` has the same origin as Theorem 4.11. Double limit in ОөвҖ“Оҙ form (outer `hвҶ“0`, inner liminf as eventual lower bound). Proof: KomaвҖ“Tasaki (extending Griffiths) | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergEquilibrium.lean` |

### BoseвҖ“Einstein condensation of hard-core bosons (Tasaki В§5.1вҖ“В§5.2)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `xyHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_5_1_xy_odlro_half_filling` | **Theorem 5.1** (В§5.1вҖ“В§5.2, AXIOM; eqs. (5.1.1)вҖ“(5.2.5)): **off-diagonal long-range order (ODLRO) of hard-core bosons at half filling**. Hard-core bosons (`uвҶ‘вҲһ` limit of the bosonic Hubbard model, eq. 5.1.4) are **equivalent to the S=1/2 XY model** (eq. 5.1.5) via the identification `Гў_xвҖ вҶ”(вҲ’1)^x Ең_x^+`, `nМӮ_xвҶ”Ең_x^(3)+ВҪ`, `NМӮвҶ”Ең_tot^(3)+L^d/2` (eqs. 5.1.6вҖ“5.1.7); half filling `ПҒ=ВҪ` вҶ” the `Ең_tot^(3)=0` sector. `xyHamiltonianS d L` = the XY Hamiltonian (XXZ at О»=D=0, N=1). The bosonic order operators `Г”_L^(1,2)` (eqs. 5.2.2/5.2.4) are the staggered XY-plane spin order operators. For `d вүҘ 2` (`hd : 2 вүӨ d`) and half filling, ODLRO holds with `qвӮҖ > 0` (depending only on `d`): every ground state `ОҰ_GS` (eigenvector + min-energy, `ОҰвү 0`, `Ең_tot^(3) ОҰ=0`) of `xyHamiltonianS` satisfies `вҹЁ(Г”_L^(Оұ))ВІвҹ©/вҹЁОҰ,ОҰвҹ©/(L^d)ВІ вүҘ qвӮҖ` for Оұ=1,2 (`Оұ:Fin 3, Оұвү 2`) and all large even `L` (eq. 5.2.5; the squared operator `Г”_L^(Оұ)/L^d` gives the `(L^d)ВІ` denominator, the intensive ODLRO density). Half filling is essential; the ground state shows LRO **without** SSB (eq. 5.2.8). Proof: reflection positivity (KennedyвҖ“LiebвҖ“Shastry, KuboвҖ“Kishi) | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |
| `xyChemicalPotentialHamiltonianS` / `IsBECTowerConstants` / `tasaki_5_2_bec_tower` | **Theorem 5.2** (В§5.3, AXIOM; eqs. (5.3.2)вҖ“(5.3.4)): **low-lying tower states of hard-core bosons**. The chemical-potential boson Hamiltonian `ДӨ_Ој = ДӨ вҲ’ Ој NМӮ` (spin form `2В·xyHamiltonianS вҲ’ ОјВ·totalSpinSOp3`, since `ДӨ = 2 ДӨ_XY` and `NМӮ вҶ” Ең_tot^(3)+L^d/2`, eq. 5.3.2; the factor 2 keeps the documented `Ој` equal to Tasaki's chemical potential, half filling `Ој=0`). Given a ground state `ОҰ_GS` of `ДӨ_Ој` exhibiting ODLRO with `qвӮҖ > 0` (Theorem 5.1), there are `CвӮҒ,CвӮӮ > 0` (depending on `d`, the density selected by `Ој`, and `qвӮҖ` вҖ” `Ој` is a parameter of `IsBECTowerConstants`, so the constants are per-density) such that the bosonic tower states `О“_M = (Г”_L^{sgn M})^{\|M\|} ОҰ_GS` (`\|M\| вүӨ CвӮҒL^{d/2}`) are **nonvanishing and** low-lying вҖ” both stated as conclusions (faithful to 5.2, which asserts `О“_M` is nonvanishing) вҖ” with the **cubic** energy increment `towerState вү  0 вҲ§ вҹЁО“_M,ДӨ_Ој О“_Mвҹ©/вҹЁО“_M,О“_Mвҹ© вүӨ EвӮҖ + CвӮӮ\|M\|Ві/L^d` (eq. 5.3.4; cubic, vs the quadratic `MВІ` of the Anderson-tower Theorem 4.6). Conditional on ODLRO (`qвӮҖ>0`); PМӮ_hc = identity for spin-ВҪ. Proof: KomaвҖ“Tasaki | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |
| `becCoherentState` / `IsBECCoherentSSBConstants` / `tasaki_5_3_bec_u1_ssb` | **Theorem 5.3** (В§5.3, AXIOM; eqs. (5.3.5)вҖ“(5.3.8)): **U(1) symmetry-breaking states of hard-core bosons** вҖ” the BEC counterpart of the Tanaka Theorem 4.9. The phase-`Оё` **coherent state** `Оһ_Оё = (2M_max+1)^{вҲ’1/2} ОЈ_{M=вҲ’M_max}^{M_max} e^{вҲ’iMОё} О“_M` (eq. 5.3.5; `О“_M` = normalized tower state), with a slow window `M_max(L)` (`IsSlowBECWindow`: monotone, вҶ’ вҲһ, `вүӨ CвӮҒL^{d/2}` eventually). For every `Оё` and realizing ground-state family (eventual GS of `ДӨ_Ој` with ODLRO `qвӮҖ`, nonvanishing tower states over `\|M\| вүӨ CвӮҒL^{d/2}`), **there exists a sufficiently slowly diverging** `M_max` (existential, matching Tasaki's "if `M_max` diverges not too rapidly" and Theorem 4.9's `вҲғ M`) for which `Оһ_Оё` fully breaks U(1): **(5.3.7)** `вҹЁГ”_L^(1)вҹ©/L^d вҶ’ m\*cosОё`, `вҹЁГ”_L^(2)вҹ©/L^d вҶ’ m\*sinОё`; **(5.3.8)** `вҹЁ(Г”_L^(Оұ))ВІвҹ©/(L^d)ВІ вҶ’ (m\*cosОё)ВІ/(m\*sinОё)ВІ`; **(5.3.6)** the complex moments (`expectationRatioComplex`) `вҹЁГ”_L^Вұвҹ©/L^d вҶ’ m\*e^{ВұiОё}`. The order parameter obeys `m\* вүҘ вҲҡ(2qвӮҖ)` (U(1) `вҲҡ2`, vs SU(2) `вҲҡ3` of Theorem 4.11). Limits in the sound eventual-`Оө` form (footnote 9). Conditional on ODLRO; `Ој` parametrizes density. Proof: KomaвҖ“Tasaki | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |
| `CoupledSite` / `coupledHamiltonian` / `coupledCrossCorrelation` / `tasaki_5_4_coupled_bec_ssb` | **Theorem 5.4** (В§5.5, AXIOM; eqs. (5.5.1)вҖ“(5.5.6)): **symmetry breaking in coupled BEC**. The coupled two-species lattice `CoupledSite = HypercubicTorus Г— Bool` (`(x,false)=a`, `(x,true)=b`); total Hamiltonian `ДӨ_tot^Оө = ДӨ_a + ДӨ_b + Оө ДӨ_tunnel` (`coupledHamiltonian`, eq. 5.5.1): the two uncoupled boson copies `ДӨ_a+ДӨ_b = 2(ДӨ_XY,a+ДӨ_XY,b)` (the boson factor `ДӨ=2ДӨ_XY`, consistent with `xyChemicalPotentialHamiltonianS`, so `Оө` is the textbook tunneling strength; via `sameSpeciesNNCoupling`) + the tunneling term `tunnelHamiltonian ПҶ = вҲ’ОЈ_x (e^{iПҶ} Ең^+_a Ең^вҲ’_b + e^{вҲ’iПҶ} Ең^вҲ’_a Ең^+_b)` (eq. 5.5.3). Total `2N` particles = doubled half filling `Ең_tot^(3)=0`. Assuming single-system ODLRO (`hODLRO`: the uncoupled XY ground states satisfy the Theorem 5.1 bound with `qвӮҖ`, tying `qвӮҖ` to genuine ODLRO), the unique coupled ground state `ОҰ^Оө` develops a definite relative U(1) phase: вҲғ `mМғ вүҘ вҲҡ(2qвӮҖ)` with `lim_{ОөвҶ“0} lim_{LвҶ‘вҲһ} вҹЁОҰ^Оө, ГўвҖ _{(x,a)}Гў_{(x,b)} ОҰ^Оөвҹ©/вҹЁОҰ^Оө,ОҰ^Оөвҹ© = mМғВІe^{вҲ’iПҶ}` (eq. 5.5.5; `coupledCrossCorrelation = Ең^+_{(x,a)}Ең^вҲ’_{(x,b)}`) **and** the conjugate `вҹЁОҰ^Оө, Гў_{(x,a)}ГўвҖ _{(x,b)} ОҰ^Оөвҹ©/вҹЁВ·вҹ© = mМғВІe^{+iПҶ}` (eq. 5.5.6; `coupledCrossCorrelationConj = Ең^вҲ’_{(x,a)}Ең^+_{(x,b)}`). Double limit in eventual-`Оө'` form (outer `ОөвҶ“0`, inner `LвҶ‘вҲһ`); GS family given (unique by MLM). The two condensates are coherently coupled (entangled). Proof: KomaвҖ“Tasaki. **Completes Tasaki Chapter 5** | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |

### Antiferromagnetic Heisenberg chains and the Haldane conjecture (Tasaki В§6.1)

(6.1.1)вҖ“(6.1.3)): the 1D AFM Heisenberg chain `ДӨ = ОЈ_x Ең_xВ·Ең_{x+1}` (PBC, `ringCoupling`, eq. 6.1.1, spin `S=N/2`). The spectral-gap dichotomy is captured by `realSpectrum` (real eigenvalues), `IsGroundEnergy`, `IsPositiveSpectralGap` (first excited вҲ’ ground), `GapOfChain`, `IsGapless` (gap вҶ’ 0 as `LвҶ‘вҲһ`, the `O(1/L)` half-integer case), `IsGapped` (вҲғ uniform Haldane gap `О”>0`). `haldaneConjecture N := (Odd N вҶ’ IsGapless N) вҲ§ (2 вүӨ N вҶ’ Even N вҶ’ IsGapped N)`: half-odd-integer `S` (`N` odd) gapless, integer `S` (`N` even вүҘ 2) gapped. A **conjecture** recorded as a `Prop` only (never proven/asserted); the correlation-decay clauses (HOI3)/(I3) are deferred. (6.2.1)вҖ“(6.2.19)): for a **half-odd-integer** spin chain the gap is `O(1/L)`. The **twist operator** `Гӣ_LSM = exp[вҲ’i ОЈ_x Оё_x Ең_x^(3)]` (`Оё_x = (2ПҖ/L)x`, eq. 6.2.2/6.2.3, via `NormedSpace.exp`) and the **trial state** `ОҰ_LSM = Гӣ_LSM ОҰ_GS` (eq. 6.2.4) are defined; **Lemma 6.1** (`lsm_energy_bound`, eq. 6.2.5; **PROVED**, axiom-free, in `LiebSchultzMattisProof.lean`): `вҹЁОҰ_LSM,ДӨОҰ_LSMвҹ©/вҹЁОҰ_LSM,ОҰ_LSMвҹ© вҲ’ E_GS вүӨ 8ПҖВІSВІ/L` for any `S` вҖ” via the per-bond symmetrised twist identity `ГӣвҖ (Ең_xВ·Ең_y)Гӣ + Гӣ(Ең_xВ·Ең_y)ГӣвҖ  вҲ’ 2(Ең_xВ·Ең_y) = (2cos(Оё_xвҲ’Оё_y)вҲ’2)В·XY_{xy}` (the longitudinal `ЕңВіЕңВі` part and the imaginary current cancel in the `ВұОё` average), `cos(Оё_xвҲ’Оё_y)=cos(2ПҖ/L)` on every bond, `|вҹЁXY_bвҹ©.re| вүӨ 2SВІвҖ–ОҰвҖ–ВІ`, and `1вҲ’cos(2ПҖ/L) вүӨ (2ПҖ/L)ВІ/2` summed over the `L` bonds; **Lemma 6.2** (`lsm_ground_twist_orthogonal`, eq. 6.2.18; **PROVED**, axiom-free, in `LiebSchultzMattisOrthogonality.lean`): for `N` odd (half-odd-integer `S`) and the unique GS (`Ең_tot^(3)=0`, `huniq` uniqueness), `вҹЁОҰ_GS,ОҰ_LSMвҹ©=0` вҖ” via the cyclic **translation operator** `TМӮ` (config-shift permutation): `TМӮ` commutes with `ДӨ` so the unique GS is a translation eigenvector `TМӮОҰ_GS=cОҰ_GS` (`|c|=1`), and the diagonal transformation law `TМӮвҖ Гӣ_LSM TМӮ = (вҲ’1)^{2S} Гӣ_LSM e^{i(2ПҖ/L)Ең_tot^(3)}` (eq. 6.2.17, reduced to a per-config `lsmPhase` identity with a `2ПҖ` boundary wrap giving `(вҲ’1)^N`) forces `вҹЁОҰ_GS,ОҰ_LSMвҹ©=(вҲ’1)^NвҹЁОҰ_GS,ОҰ_LSMвҹ©`, vanishing for `N` odd; **Theorem 6.3** (`lieb_schultz_mattis_affleck_lieb`, eq. 6.2.19; **PROVED**, axiom-free, in `LiebSchultzMattisRingGap.lean`): for `N` odd and even `L`, вҲғ a positive spectral gap (`IsPositiveSpectralGap` from В§6.1) `вүӨ 8ПҖВІSВІ/L` вҖ” so a half-odd-integer chain cannot have both a unique GS and a gap (the rigorous half of the Haldane conjecture). `S=N/2`. **The axiom is discharged**: ground-state uniqueness of the ring (connected-graph MarshallвҖ“LiebвҖ“Mattis, `ringSym_ground_uniqueness` + `afm_ring_ground_state_data` over `LiebSchultzMattisRing{Uniqueness,GroundUnique,EigenTransfer,MLMUnique,GroundData}.lean`) gives a one-dimensional `Ең_totВі=0` ground line; the generic CourantвҖ“Fischer second-eigenvalue bound `hermitian_second_eigenvalue_variational` (`HermitianSecondEigenvalue.lean`) with the existence of an eigenvalue above the ground line (`hermitian_exists_eigenvalue_gt`, `HermitianGapExists.lean`) defines `EвӮҒ`, and Lemma 6.1 (trial energy) + Lemma 6.2 (orthogonality) bound `EвӮҒ вүӨ EвӮҖ+8ПҖВІSВІ/L` | `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattis.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisRingGap.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisProof.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisOrthogonality.lean` |
