(6.3.7)вҖ“(6.3.9)): the **S=1 chain on the hidden-AFM subspace `H_HAF`**. **`tasaki_prop_6_5_hhaf_spin_one` is now a proved theorem** (formerly a documented axiom; `#print axioms` вҶ’ `propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound`): the final assembly `tasaki_prop_6_5_hhaf_spin_one` bundles, for even `L>0`, a ground state `ОҰ = hhafSubspaceEmbedding` of the balanced PerronвҖ“Frobenius eigenvector with (i) projection-fixedness + nonzero + eigenvector at `E = hhafMinEnergy`, (ii) minimality over `hhafRealSpectrum`, (iii) **uniqueness** (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_ground_finrank_le_one`: the restricted ground eigenspace is one-dimensional вҖ” at `E < вҲ’2` the single-`Вұ` block is vacated since its eigenvalues are `вүҘ вҲ’2`, and the balanced block contributes the 1-D PF ground state via the injective restriction `R` into the balanced block eigenspace), lifted to the operator via `hhafSubspaceEmbedding_of_projFixed` + `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_of_projFixed_eig` + `finrank_le_one_iff`, (iv) the positive gap (`exists_hhaf_positive_gap`), and (v) exponential decay (`hhaf_correlation_exp_decay_exists`). For the spin-1 chain (`N=2`, configs `Fin L вҶ’ Fin 3`, `Пғ_x=0/1/2 вҶҰ +1/0/вҲ’1`), a config has **complete hidden AFM order** (`IsHiddenAFMConfig`, via `IsPM`/`InCyclicOpen`/`IsNextPM`) when the nonzero (`Вұ`) spins strictly alternate around the ring with arbitrary `0`-spins between (eq. 6.3.9). `H_HAF` = span of those basis states; `hhafProjection` (diagonal projection), `hhafRestrictedChainHamiltonianS = P_HAFВ·ДӨВ·P_HAF`, `hhafRealSpectrum` (eigenvalues with `H_HAF` eigenvectors). For even `L>0`, the restricted chain has a **unique ground state**, a **finite gap** (`gap>0` in the `H_HAF`-restricted spectrum), and **exponentially decaying** correlation `\|chainCorrelation\| вүӨ C e^{вҲ’d(x,y)/Оҫ}` (`ringDist`) вҖ” so the Haldane conjecture for `S=1` holds rigorously within this artificial subspace. Proof: path-integral / 2D classical stat-mech. **Spectral foundations now proved** (infrastructure for the now-proved theorem, Issue #4718): `hhafProjection_isHermitian` (P_HAF Hermitian), `hhafProjection_mul_self` (P_HAF idempotent), `afmHeisenbergChainHamiltonianS_isHermitian` (defined in `HaldaneConjecture.lean`), `hhafRestrictedChainHamiltonianS_isHermitian` (compressed `P_HAFВ·ДӨВ·P_HAF` Hermitian). **Ground-energy existence now proved**: `hhafConfig` (hidden-AFM config subtype), `hhafRestrictedMatrix` (H_HAFГ—H_HAF submatrix, Hermitian), `hhafSubspaceEmbedding` (zero-extension) with the compressionвҖ“embedding intertwining `P_HAFВ·ДӨВ·P_HAF вҲҳ emb = emb вҲҳ restMat`, and `hhafMinEnergy`/`hhafMinEnergy_mem_realSpectrum` (the minimal restricted eigenvalue is a genuine `hhafRealSpectrum` member with a nonzero H_HAF ground state), and `exists_hhaf_min_real_eigenvalue` (it is `вүӨ` every restricted eigenvalue, so a genuine restricted **ground energy** with minimality). **Per-L exp-decay** proved (`exp_decay_envelope_of_finite`/`hhaf_correlation_exp_decay_exists`): on the finite ring any state's correlation admits `|вҹЁЕң_xВ·Ең_yвҹ©| вүӨ C e^{вҲ’d/Оҫ}` (Оҫ=1, C=ОЈ|corr|e^{d}) вҖ” the decay clause of Prop 6.5 (now all clauses proved). **Max-energy infra** added: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eigenvalue_mem_realSpectrum` (every restricted-matrix eigenvalue embeds into `hhafRealSpectrum`), `hhafMaxEnergy` + `hhafMaxEnergy_mem_realSpectrum`; **spectral completeness/finiteness** (`hhafRealSpectrum_restrict` reverse bridge, `hhafRealSpectrum_subset_eigenvalue_range` via mathlib `spectrum_real_eq_range_eigenvalues`, `hhafRealSpectrum_finite`) вҖ” the restricted spectrum IS the finite restricted-matrix eigenvalue set. **Diagonal bounds**: new general `HermitianVariationalUpperBound` (max-Rayleigh) + `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag`/`diag_le_hhafMaxEnergy` give `hhafMinEnergy вүӨ (restMat Пғ Пғ).re вүӨ hhafMaxEnergy` via Rayleigh at the basis vector (toward non-scalarity for the gap). **Non-scalarity PROVED**: `hhafMinEnergy_lt_hhafMaxEnergy` (even LвүҘ2) via all-zero config (diag=0) and domain-wall config Пғ=[0,2,1,вҖҰ,1] (HAF, diag<0); general formula `hhaf_diag_eq_succ_sum` (diag = ОЈ_x (1-Пғx)(1-Пғ_{x+1})). **Positive gap PROVED**: `exists_hhaf_positive_gap` (even L>0) вҖ” first-excited eigenvalue EвӮҒ = (eigenvalues image filtered above min).min with EвӮҒ вҲҲ spectrum, EвӮҒ > minEig (gap>0), and minimal among spectrum elements above minEig (via completeness). These foundations feed the now-discharged uniqueness clause. **PF real-form foundation** started: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_im_zero` (entries real), `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal` (real symmetric form) + `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal_ofReal`/`_isSymm` вҖ” the entry point for the Perron-Frobenius uniqueness route (Marshall gauge (now built: `ringSublattice`, `hhafDressedMatrix`, `hhafDressedMatrix_eq` = Оө(Пғ)Оө(П„)В·M_real via the dressed-Heisenberg machinery) + **symmetrized-coupling infra** for the off-diagonal sign step (`heisenbergHamiltonianS_coupling_swap`: the Heisenberg Hamiltonian is invariant under transposing the coupling, via `spinSDot_comm`; `ringCouplingSym` = `ringCoupling + ringCouplingбөҖ` with `_symm`/`_im_zero`/`_re_nonneg`; `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCouplingSym`: `H(ringCouplingSym) = 2В·H(ringCoupling)`, so the directed ring Hamiltonian equals ВҪ the symmetric-coupling one вҖ” letting the symmetric-coupling off-diagonal nonpositivity lemma apply) вҖ” **off-diagonal nonpositivity now PROVED**: `ringCouplingSym_bipartite` (even ring `L` is bipartite w.r.t. `ringSublattice`: for even `L`, `(a+1)%L` flips parity, so equal-parity sites are never adjacent вҖ” via `Nat.mod_mod_of_dvd`), `dressedHeisenbergS_ringCoupling_re_eq_half` (dressed directed = ВҪ dressed symmetric), and `hhafDressedMatrix_offdiag_nonpos` (even `L`: `hhafDressedMatrix Пғ П„ вүӨ 0` for `Пғ вү  П„`, via the symmetric-coupling nonpositivity lemma) вҖ” the diagonal `Вұ1` Marshall gauge makes the AFM off-diagonals sign-definite. **Symmetry + nonneg shift now PROVED**: `hhafDressedMatrix_isSymm` (the dressed matrix is symmetric), `hhafDressedMatrix_diag_eq` (diagonal = real restricted diagonal, since the Marshall sign squares to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix_diag_le_hhafMaxEnergy` (diagonal вүӨ max energy), and `hhafShifted_entry_nonneg` (for `c вүҘ hhafMaxEnergy` and even `L`, the shifted matrix `cВ·I вҲ’ M` has nonnegative entries вҖ” the nonnegativity hypothesis of `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric`). **Irreducibility started** (Issue #4732, GГіmez-Santos kink ergodicity): `hhafShifted_pos_of_ladderStep` вҖ” two hidden-AFM configs differing by one adjacent raise/lower (ladder) move on a bipartite bond have a strictly positive shifted entry `(cВ·I вҲ’ M) П„ Пғ > 0` (reusing `dressedHeisenbergS_apply_re_neg_of_raiseLowerStepS_witness` on `ringCouplingSym` + the ВҪ scaling), the single-edge positivity for the PerronвҖ“Frobenius reachability argument. **Ring bond graph** added: `hhafRingGraph` (cyclic nearest-neighbour graph on `Fin L`), with `hhafRingGraph_adj_sublattice_ne` (bipartite w.r.t. `ringSublattice` for even `L`) and `ringCouplingSym_re_pos_of_ringGraph_adj` (the symmetrized coupling is strictly positive on every edge) вҖ” the bond-graph data feeding the per-step positivity. **HAF reachability + walkвҶ’power** added: `RaiseLowerStepSHhaf` / `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf` (single HAF-preserving ladder move / its reflexive-transitive closure), `hhafShifted_pos_of_stepHhaf` (each step gives a strictly positive shifted entry), and `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable` (HAF-reachability вҮ’ some power `(cВ·I вҲ’ M)^k` is strictly positive вҖ” the walkвҶ’power bridge, mirroring the magnetization-sector version). **Kink-reduction setup** added: `hhafCanonical` (the all-`0`-spin configuration, base case), `pmCount` (number of `Вұ` spins, the induction measure), `pmCount_eq_zero_iff` (no `Вұ` spins вҹә canonical). **Slide move** added: `slidePM` (move the spin at `a` onto a cyclic neighbour `b`, leaving a `0` at `a`), with `slidePM_apply` (its pointwise values) and `slidePM_isRaiseLowerStep` (it is a single raise/lower ladder step on the ring-graph bond `{a, b}` when `a` carries a `Вұ` spin and `b` carries a `0`). **Slide preserves hidden-AFM order PROVEN** (the kink-ergodicity core): `slidePM_isHiddenAFM` вҖ” sliding a `Вұ` spin onto its `0`-carrying cyclic successor keeps the strict `+,вҲ’,вҖҰ` alternation, proved by transferring each `IsNextPM` pair of the moved configuration back to one of `Пғ` (relabelling `b вҶҰ a`) via the open-arc decomposition lemmas `inCyclicOpen_succ_left_imp` / `inCyclicOpen_succ_right_imp` / `notInCyclicOpen_succ` (each an `omega` calculation after splitting the cyclic wrap). **Slide lifted to a subtype step** (`slidePM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf`) and the **annihilation move** added: `annihPM` (set adjacent `a, b` both to `0`), `annihPM_apply`, and `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStep` (it is a single ladder step when `a, b` carry opposite `Вұ` spins). **Annihilation preserves hidden-AFM order PROVEN**: `annihPM_isHiddenAFM` вҖ” removing an adjacent opposite-sign `+,вҲ’` pair keeps the alternation (each `IsNextPM` pair of the annihilated config either avoids the removed pair, giving an `IsNextPM` pair of `Пғ`, or straddles it, where the chain `x, a, b, y` forces opposite endpoint signs), via the sub-arc lemmas `inCyclicOpen_succ_iff_mem` / `inCyclicOpen_sub_left` / `inCyclicOpen_sub_right` / `notInCyclicOpen_succ_right` / `notInCyclicOpen_pred_left`. **Move-count behaviour** added: `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf` (annihilation as a subtype step), `slidePM_pmCount_card` (sliding preserves the `Вұ` count), `annihPM_pmCount_card_lt` (annihilation strictly decreases it). **CONNECTIVITY PROVEN** (the kink-ergodicity theorem): the reduction measure `hhafS = ОЈ_{Вұ spins}(L вҲ’ x)` strictly decreases under a slide (`slidePM_hhafS_lt`, non-wrapping successor) and an annihilation (`annihPM_hhafS_lt`); `hhaf_single_step` produces, for any balanced (even-`pmCount`) non-canonical config, a single HAF ladder move to another balanced config of smaller measure (first `Вұ` spin at `p вүӨ LвҲ’2`, sliding past a `0` or annihilating an adjacent opposite `Вұ`); `hhaf_reachable_to_canonical` / `hhaf_reachable_canonical` (strong induction on `hhafS`) then show **every balanced hidden-AFM configuration is HAF-reachable from the canonical all-`0` configuration** (with `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf_symm` for the reverse). **Design note**: the *full* HAF matrix is reducible вҖ” a single-`Вұ` config (`pmCount = 1`, magnetization `Вұ1`) is vacuously hidden-AFM but unreachable from `canonical` (the moves conserve magnetization), so irreducibility holds only within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector; this is the sector containing the antiferromagnetic ground state. **Balanced-sector block irreducibility PROVEN**: `hhafConfig0` (the even-`pmCount` subtype) with `hhafShiftedMatrix0` (the `cВ·I вҲ’ M` block), `hhaf_reachable_to_canonical0` / `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf0_symm` (balanced-sector connectivity lifted to the subtype), `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable0` (subtype walkвҶ’power), and `hhafShiftedMatrix0_isIrreducible` вҖ” for even `L` and shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`, the balanced-sector shifted matrix is PerronвҖ“Frobenius irreducible (`isIrreducible_iff_exists_pow_pos`: nonnegative + entrywise-positive power, with the diagonal handled by the strict shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`). **Balanced-sector unique ground state PROVEN**: `hhafDressedMatrix0` (the dressed block on `hhafConfig0`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_isSymm` (submatrix of a symmetric matrix), `hhafShiftedMatrix0_eq` (`hhafShiftedMatrix0 = cВ·I вҲ’ MвӮҖ`, the identity restricting since `Subtype.val` is injective), and `hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_finrank_le_one` вҖ” applying `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` to the balanced block yields a strictly positive lowest eigenvector with a **one-dimensional** ground eigenspace: the unique ground state of the AFM chain *within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector* of `H_HAF`. **Marshall-gauge transfer DONE**: `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0` (undressed real balanced block), `hhafMarshallDiag0` (the `Вұ1` Marshall sign diagonal, squaring to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_eq_conj` (`MвӮҖ = ОҳВ·M_realвӮҖВ·Оҳ`), and `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0_ground_finrank_le_one` вҖ” transferring the **full PerronвҖ“Frobenius data** (nonzero lowest eigenvector `Оҳv`, ground minimality, and `finrank вүӨ 1`) through the Marshall similarity (the generic `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq`, now generalised from `в„Ӯ` to any field), so the **undressed** real balanced block also has a one-dimensional ground eigenspace with an explicit minimal eigenvector. Remaining for the *global* ground-state uniqueness: the inter-sector energy ordering (balanced sector strictly lowest, a MarshallвҖ“LiebвҖ“Mattis statement within `H_HAF`). **NГ©el witness** added (toward that ordering): `hhafNeel` (the maximally-AFM configuration `+1` on even sites, `вҲ’1` on odd), `hhafNeel_isHiddenAFM` (it is hidden-AFM on an even ring вҖ” all sites are `Вұ`, every `IsNextPM` pair is cyclically adjacent hence opposite parity, via `inCyclicOpen_succ_mem_of_ne`), `hhafNeel_pmCount` (`Вұ`-count `= L`), and `hhafNeelConfig0` (the NГ©el configuration as a balanced charge-`0` configuration). **Ground energy вүӨ вҲ’L PROVEN**: `hhafNeel_diag` (the NГ©el diagonal energy is `вҲ’L`, every nearest-neighbour bond antiferromagnetic `(+1)(вҲ’1) = вҲ’1`) and `hhafMinEnergy_le_neg_L` (the global restricted ground energy `hhafMinEnergy вүӨ вҲ’L`, via the Rayleigh quotient `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag` at the NГ©el state) вҖ” the first half of the inter-sector ordering (the balanced sector reaches energy `вҲ’L`; the remaining half bounds the magnetization-`Вұ1` single-`Вұ` sectors above this). Toward that bound: `hhaf_diag_eq_zero_of_pmCount_one` вҖ” a single-`Вұ` configuration has **vanishing diagonal energy** (every nearest-neighbour bond has a `0`-spin endpoint since only one site is `Вұ`), the Gershgorin-disc centre for the magnetization-`Вұ1` sectors. `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_raising_lowering` / `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_lowering_raising` вҖ” the spin-`1` ladder off-diagonal amplitude `|вҹЁПғ'|Ең_xВ·Ең_y|Пғвҹ©| вүӨ 1` (each `вҲҡ` factor `вүӨ вҲҡ2` for `N = 2`), the Gershgorin-disc radius ingredient. `hhafDressedMatrix_abs_le_ringCouplingSym` вҖ” for two hidden-AFM configs related by a single ladder move on a bond `{x, y}`, the dressed off-diagonal entry satisfies `|M Пғ П„| вүӨ (ringCouplingSym x y).re` (the Marshall signs are `Вұ1`, the Heisenberg element is `ringCouplingSym В· spinSDot`, and the ladder amplitude is `вүӨ 1`). **Magnetization-`Вұ1` sector min `вүҘ вҲ’2` PROVEN** (codex insight: the cyclic `IsHiddenAFMConfig` forbids odd `pmCount > 1`, so the only HAF sectors are balanced `Sz=0` (even `pmCount`) and single-`Вұ` `Sz=Вұ1` (`pmCount = 1`) вҖ” there are **no higher-`Sz` sectors**, dissolving the inter-sector LiebвҖ“Mattis wall): `hhafConfigPM1` (the `pmCount = 1` subtype) with `hhafDressedMatrixPM1` (dressed block), `hhafPM1Site` (the unique `Вұ` site, computed via `Finset.min'` so `Finset.image` reductions stay light), `hhafPM1_coupling_imp` (a nonzero off-diagonal forces `П„` to be the slide `slidePM` of `Пғ`'s `Вұ` spin to its new site, sharing the `Вұ` value by magnetization conservation `magEigenvalueS_eq_iff`), `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_offdiag_le` (per-entry bound `вүӨ ringCouplingSym`), `hhafPM1Site_injOn_support` (the `Вұ`-site map is injective on the coupling support), `ringCouplingSym_re_row_sum` (`вҲ‘_t (ringCouplingSym s t).re = 2`, the two incident directed ring bonds), the generic `sum_comp_le_sum_univ_of_injOn` (injective-on-support reindex avoiding a heavy concrete `Finset.univ`), and `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_rowSum_le_two` вҹ№ `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` (Gershgorin `eigenvalue_mem_ball`, diagonal `0` + row sum `вүӨ 2`: every single-`Вұ` eigenvalue `вүҘ вҲ’2`) вҖ” the single-kink tight-binding lower bound placing the `Sz = Вұ1` sectors above the balanced ground energy (`вүӨ вҲ’L вүӨ вҲ’2`). **Balanced ground energy `< вҲ’L` STRICT PROVEN** (`hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_lt_neg_L`): the PerronвҖ“Frobenius lowest eigenvector `v > 0` paired with the NГ©el row gives `ОјВ·v(NГ©el) = вҲ’LВ·v(NГ©el) + ОЈ_{П„вү NГ©el} MвӮҖ(NГ©el,П„) v(П„)`; every off-diagonal term is `вүӨ 0` (`hhafDressedMatrix0_offdiag_nonpos`) and the annihilation neighbour (`hhafNeel_step_neighbor`, annihilating the adjacent opposite-`Вұ` pair at sites `0,1`) is strictly `< 0`, so via the generic row inequality `row_sum_mul_lt_diag_mul_of_offdiag_nonpos_exists_neg` and `hhafDressedMatrix0_Neel_diag` (`MвӮҖ(NГ©el,NГ©el) = вҲ’L`) we get `ОјВ·v(NГ©el) < вҲ’LВ·v(NГ©el)`, hence `Ој < вҲ’L`. Since `вҲ’L вүӨ вҲ’2`, the balanced sector lies **strictly** below the magnetization-`Вұ1` sectors with **no `L=2` tie** вҖ” the inter-sector ordering is complete. **Sector classification PROVEN** (`hhaf_pmCount_eq_one_or_even`): every hidden-AFM configuration has `pmCount = 1` or even `pmCount` вҖ” an odd count `вүҘ 3` is impossible. Proof: the `Вұ` sites read in increasing index order are pairwise `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_consecutive` вҖ” no `Вұ` spin lies strictly between index-adjacent `Вұ` sites, via `orderEmbOfFin`), so their signs strictly alternate (`hhaf_pm_alternates`: the `i`-th `Вұ` value is the `0`-th when `i` is even and its complement `2 - В·` when odd, `pm_flip`); and the maximal/minimal `Вұ` sites are also `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_wrap` вҖ” the cyclic wrap arc holds no `Вұ` spin), which would force the first and last `Вұ` signs to *differ* while the linear alternation makes them *equal* at odd count вҖ” contradiction. The magnetization corollaries `hhaf_magSumS_eq_L_of_even` (even `pmCount вҹ№ magSumS = L`, via `magSumS`-invariance under HAF ladder moves `magSumS_eq_of_hhafReachable` + reachability from canonical) and `hhaf_magSumS_ne_L_of_pmCount_one` (single-`Вұ` вҹ№ `magSumS = L Вұ 1 вү  L`) identify the sectors: balanced `= Sz 0`, single-`Вұ` `= Sz Вұ1`, with no higher sectors вҖ” the cross-sector vanishing (magnetization conservation) needed to split a ground eigenvector into balanced + single-`Вұ` blocks. **Magnetization-block structure of the restricted matrix PROVEN**: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eq_zero_of_magSumS_ne` (entries between different-`magSumS` configurations vanish вҖ” magnetization conservation); the generic slice `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice P` (submatrix on `{Пғ // P Пғ}`) with `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice_mulVec_of_full_eigen` (a full eigenvector restricted to a block-closed `P`-slice is a `P`-slice eigenvector with the same eigenvalue); and the two block-closures `hhafRestrictedMatrix_pmCount_one_block_closed` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_even_block_closed` (via the classification: a single-`Вұ` config couples only to single-`Вұ`, an even config only to even) вҖ” the block-diagonal decomposition into the balanced and single-`Вұ` blocks, the structural input for the ground-eigenspace `finrank вүӨ 1`. **Per-block spectral bounds PROVEN**: the complex blocks are the real blocks cast to `в„Ӯ` (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eq_map` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eq_map`, entries real); `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eigenspace_finrank_le_one` вҖ” the **complex balanced block ground eigenspace is one-dimensional** (realвҶ’complex bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real` from the real balanced PerronвҖ“Frobenius `finrank вүӨ 1`); and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` вҖ” the **complex single-`Вұ` block has all eigenvalues `вүҘ вҲ’2`** (an eigenvalue is real, its re/im part is a real-block eigenvector, transferred to a dressed-block eigenvector via the Marshall conjugation `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eq_conj` where the Gershgorin bound `вүҘ вҲ’2` applies). These are the per-block spectral inputs: at the ground energy `E < вҲ’2` the single-`Вұ` block is vacated and the balanced block contributes the unique ground state. **Reverse block restriction PROVEN**: `hhafSliceExtend P` (zero-extension of a `P`-slice vector to all of `hhafConfig`) with `hhafSliceExtend_ne_zero` and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_hhafSliceExtend` (if there is no coupling *into* a `P`-slice from its complement, the zero-extension of a `P`-slice eigenvector is a full eigenvector at the same eigenvalue) вҖ” the reverse counterpart of the forward slice restriction, used to lift the balanced PerronвҖ“Frobenius eigenvector to a full restricted-matrix eigenvector (so the balanced ground energy is in the restricted spectrum). (The PerronвҖ“Frobenius uniqueness development вҖ” kink moves through the Marshall transfer, the magnetization-`Вұ1` Gershgorin bound, and the strict balanced ground energy вҖ” lives in the companion `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean`, with its combinatorial kink-move/balanced-sector core split off into `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` for build speed.) | `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrder.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` |

### The AKLT model (Tasaki В§7.1)

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `akltHamiltonianS` / `aklt_theorem_7_1` | **В§7.1.1 Theorem 7.1** (the AKLT main theorem; AXIOM; eqs. (7.1.1)вҖ“(7.1.2)): the AffleckвҖ“KennedyвҖ“LiebвҖ“Tasaki `S=1` chain. `akltHamiltonianS L = ОЈ_x {Ең_xВ·Ең_{x+1} + в…“(Ең_xВ·Ең_{x+1})ВІ}` (eq. 7.1.1, PBC ring, SU(2)-invariant; the biquadratic term puts it outside MarshallвҖ“LiebвҖ“Mattis). For all sufficiently large chains (`L = n+1`, any parity), `aklt_theorem_7_1` gives: a **unique ground state** `ОҰ` (= the valence-bond-solid VBS state; nonzero eigenvector at `EвӮҖ`, every ground eigenvector вҲқ ОҰ); a **spectral gap** `вүҘ О”EвӮҖ` for a positive `L`-independent `О”EвӮҖ` (`IsPositiveSpectralGap`); and the **exact correlation** `вҹЁОҰ,Ең_xВ·Ең_y ОҰвҹ©/вҹЁОҰ,ОҰвҹ© вҶ’ 4(вҲ’3)^{вҲ’\|xвҲ’y\|}` (eq. 7.1.2, sign-alternating exponential decay) for fixed sites with `\|xвҲ’y\| вүҘ 1`, as `LвҶ‘вҲһ` (`chainSite` embedding, eventual-Оө form). `О”EвӮҖ` and `ОҰ` quantified outermost. Proof: explicit VBS / matrix-product representation (AKLT 1987) | `Quantum/SpinS/AKLT.lean` |
| `IsAKLTChainDynamics` / `aklt_theorem_7_2` | **В§7.1.3 Theorem 7.2** (Matsui; AXIOM, operator-algebraic): the AKLT model on the **infinite chain** has a **unique gapped ground state**. On the quasi-local C*-algebra, a state `Пү` is a ground state iff `IsGroundState Пү Оҙ` (Def A.25: `Пү(ГӮвҖ [ДӨ,ГӮ])вүҘ0`) and has a nonzero gap iff `HasNonzeroGap Пү Оҙ Оі` (Def A.27, `Оі>0`). For the 1D `InfiniteSpinSystem` with the AKLT dynamics `Оҙ` (tied by the uninterpreted marker `IsAKLTChainDynamics`), `aklt_theorem_7_2` gives `вҲғ Пү`, `IsState Пү`, `IsGroundState Пү Оҙ`, **unique among states** (`вҲҖПү', IsState Пү' вҶ’ IsGroundState Пү' Оҙ вҶ’ Пү'=Пү`), and `вҲғОі, HasNonzeroGap Пү Оҙ Оі`. The unique GS is the `LвҶ‘вҲһ` limit of the VBS state. Proof: Matsui (uniqueness) on AKLT; documented operator-algebra axiom (per the C*-algebra policy) | `Quantum/SpinS/AKLTInfiniteChain.lean` |
| `IsAKLTPerturbation` / `perturbedAKLTHamiltonianS` / `aklt_theorem_7_3` | **В§7.1.3 Theorem 7.3** (Yarotsky stability; AXIOM; eq. (7.1.4)): the AKLT gap is **stable** under small local perturbations. `perturbedAKLTHamiltonianS L Оө v = akltHamiltonianS L + Оө ОЈ_x vМӮ_x` (eq. 7.1.4); `IsAKLTPerturbation L r vвӮҖ v` bundles: each `vМӮ_x` Hermitian, `r`-local (`IsLocalRangeR`), bounded (`manyBodyOperatorNormS вүӨ vвӮҖ`), and translation-covariant (`IsTranslationCovariant`, an uninterpreted marker for `vМӮ_x = TМӮ^x vМӮ_o TМӮ^{вҲ’x}`). For range `r` bounded by `vвӮҖ`, вҲғ `ОөвӮҖ>0` such that for `|Оө|<ОөвӮҖ` there are `О”E,C,Оҫ>0` **L-independent** with: for any `L вүҘ 3` and any such `v`, the perturbed AKLT chain has a **unique ground state** (`IsUniqueChainGroundState`), a **gap вүҘ О”E** (`IsPositiveSpectralGap`), and **exponential decay of the connected correlation** `|connectedChainCorrelation| вүӨ C e^{вҲ’d(x,y)/Оҫ}` (`ringDist`). The **connected/truncated** correlation `вҹЁЕң_xВ·Ең_yвҹ© вҲ’ ОЈ_ОұвҹЁЕң_x^(Оұ)вҹ©вҹЁЕң_y^(Оұ)вҹ©` is used since a symmetry-breaking perturbation can give nonzero one-point functions (the raw correlation need not decay); `L вүҘ 3` excludes the degenerate small rings. The gapful AKLT phase is stable. Proof: cluster expansion (Yarotsky) | `Quantum/SpinS/AKLTStability.lean` |
| `bondSpin2ProjectionS` / `aklt_bond_term_eq_bondSpin2Projection` / `tasaki_lemma_7_4` | **В§7.1.3 Lemma 7.4** (local VBS ground-state characterization; eqs. (7.1.5), (7.1.19)вҖ“(7.1.20)): the bond projection onto total spin 2 and the singlet form. `bondSpin2ProjectionS x y = ВҪ(Ең_xВ·Ең_y) + в…ҷ(Ең_xВ·Ең_y)ВІ + в…“В·1` is the projection `PМӮвӮӮ[Ең_x+Ең_y]` onto the bond total-spin-2 subspace. `aklt_bond_term_eq_bondSpin2Projection` (**PROVED**, eq. 7.1.5) records the affine identity `Ең_xВ·Ең_y + в…“(Ең_xВ·Ең_y)ВІ = 2 PМӮвӮӮ[Ең_x+Ең_y] вҲ’ в…”`, so the AKLT local term penalizes only the spin-2 component of each bond. `tasaki_lemma_7_4` (**AXIOM**, eqs. 7.1.19вҖ“7.1.20): for `1 < L` (so the bond `{x, ringSucc x}` is genuinely two-site), a state `ОҰ` is annihilated by the bond projection, `PМӮвӮӮ[Ең_x+Ең_{x+1}] ОҰ = 0`, **iff** `ОҰ` has the valence-bond-solid singlet-tensor form on that bond (`IsVBSGroundForm`, an uninterpreted marker for the duplicated-`S=ВҪ` / Schwinger-boson singlet construction not yet formalized). The AKLT ground space = states annihilated by every bond projection = the VBS state (KennedyвҖ“LiebвҖ“Tasaki). Proof: spin-`{0,1,2}` decomposition + duplicated-chain singlet construction (KLT 1988) | `Quantum/SpinS/AKLTBondProjection.lean` |
| `spinSStringPhaseS1` / `stringOperatorS` / `stringCorrelationS` / `aklt_string_order_7_2_8` | **В§7.2.1 hidden antiferromagnetic order / string order parameter** (den NijsвҖ“Rommelse; eqs. (7.2.6)вҖ“(7.2.8)): the AKLT VBS ground state has positive string order. `spinSStringPhaseS1 = diag(вҲ’1,1,вҲ’1)` is the single-site string phase `exp(iПҖЕң^z)` for `S=1` (entry `(вҲ’1)^{k+1}`). `stringOperatorS x y` is the diagonal string operator `exp(iПҖ ОЈ_{x<z<y} Ең_z^{(3)})` (phase `вҲҸ_{x<z<y}(вҲ’1)^{Пғ_z+1}`). `stringCorrelationS x y ОҰ = вҲ’вҹЁОҰ\|Ең_x^{(3)} exp(iПҖОЈЕң^{(3)}) Ең_y^{(3)}\|ОҰвҹ©` (= `S^{(3)}_{x,y}`, eq. 7.2.6, with the leading minus; the raw expectation is negative, вҶ’ `вҲ’4/9` for VBS, so the minus makes it positive, and `O_string^{(3)} = lim lim S^{(3)}_{x,y}`). `aklt_string_order_7_2_8` (**AXIOM**, eq. 7.2.8): for the AKLT VBS ground-state family (`IsAKLTVBSGroundStateFamily`, an uninterpreted marker), the den NijsвҖ“Rommelse string order parameter `O_string^{(3)}(ОҰ_VBS) = lim_{dвҶ‘вҲһ} lim_{LвҶ‘вҲһ} stringCorrelationS = 4/9 > 0` (explicit double-Оө form over ordered linear windows `0 < x < y < L` with separation `d = yвҲ’x`, inner `LвҶ‘вҲһ` first), so the VBS state has hidden AFM order in all three directions (SU(2)) while NГ©el order vanishes вҖ” the hallmark of the Haldane phase. Proof: VBS / matrix-product computation | `Quantum/SpinS/AKLTStringOrder.lean` |
| `mpsTransferMatrix` / `IsInjectiveMPS` / `mps_theorem_7_5` / `mps_theorem_7_6` | **В§7.2.2 Matrix product representation** (Theorems 7.5вҖ“7.6; eqs. (7.2.36), (7.2.41)вҖ“(7.2.42)): the AKLT VBS state as an injective matrix product state. `MPSMatrices D N = Fin(N+1)вҶ’DГ—D` matrices `A^Пғ`; `mpsTransferMatrix A` is the DВІГ—DВІ transfer matrix `Гғ_{(Оұ,ОІ),(Оұ',ОІ')} = ОЈ_Пғ conj(A^Пғ_{Оұ,Оұ'}) A^Пғ_{ОІ,ОІ'}` (eq. 7.2.42); `orderedProd A Пғs` the ordered product `A^{Пғ_1}вӢҜA^{Пғ_в„“}`; `mpsProductsSpanAt A в„“` (products span all DГ—D matrices); `IsMPSNormalized A О»` (eq. 7.2.41, `ОЈ_Пғ A^Пғ(A^Пғ)вҖ  = О»I`, `О»>0`); `HasPrimitiveTransferSpectrum A О»` (**concrete**: `О» вҲҲ spectrum`, eigenspace `finrank = 1`, every other eigenvalue `вҖ–ОјвҖ– < О»`); `IsInjectiveMPS` bundles normalization + (i)+(ii)+(iii). `mps_theorem_7_5` (**AXIOM**): for normalized `A` (`0<D`), the three conditions (i) `MPSSpansEventually` вҶ” (ii) `MPSSpansForAllLarge` вҶ” (iii) `HasPrimitiveTransferSpectrum` are equivalent (PerronвҖ“Frobenius for the CP transfer map). `mps_theorem_7_6` (**AXIOM**, FannesвҖ“NachtergaeleвҖ“Werner, unproven in book): two injective collections generating the same MPS (`GeneratesSameMPS` marker) are related by a **pure unitary gauge** `A^Пғ = U B^Пғ UвҖ ` (`U` unitary, eq. 7.2.44; no extra scalar вҖ” a rescaling would change the length-`L` amplitudes by its `L`-th power) вҖ” the MPS representation is essentially unique, underlying SPT-phase classification (В§8.3.4). Proof: PerronвҖ“Frobenius / FNW | `Quantum/SpinS/AKLTMatrixProduct.lean` |
| `bondMaxSpinProjectionS` / `regularGraphAKLTHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_7_7` | **В§7.3.2 AKLT model on a general graph** (Theorem 7.7; eqs. (7.3.6)вҖ“(7.3.9)): the graph-centric generalized AKLT model and hexagonal-lattice correlation decay. `bondCasimirS x y N = (Ең_x+Ең_y)ВІ = 2S(S+1)В·1 + 2Ең_xВ·Ең_y`; `bondMaxSpinProjectionS x y N` = the **concrete** Lagrange/Casimir projector `вҲҸ_{j=0}^{NвҲ’1}(ДҲвҲ’j(j+1))/(N(N+1)вҲ’j(j+1))` onto the maximal bond total spin `J=N`; `regularGraphAKLTHamiltonianS G N = ВҪ ОЈ_{x,y} [G.Adj x y] PМӮ_N[Ең_x+Ең_y]` = the **regular (uniform-spin) specialization** of eq. (7.3.7) (single global `S=N/2`, each bond вҶ’ `J=N`; equals eq. 7.3.7 exactly on an `N`-regular graph вҖ” site-dependent spins `SМ„_x=deg(x)/2` are not expressible in `ManyBodyOpS Оӣ N`). On the 3-regular hexagonal lattice (`N=3`) it is `ОЈ_bonds PМӮ_3` (eq. 7.3.8), the setting of Theorem 7.7. `tasaki_theorem_7_7` (**AXIOM**): for the hexagonal lattice (`IsHexagonalLatticeAKLT` marker) with VBS GS `ОҰ` (`IsGeneralGraphVBSGroundState` marker), вҲғ `C,Оҫ>0` size-independent with sign-alternating exponential decay `0 вүӨ (вҲ’1)^{D(x,y)}вҹЁЕң_xВ·Ең_yвҹ© вүӨ C e^{вҲ’D/Оҫ}` (`D = G.dist`, eq. 7.3.9) and the translation-invariant infinite-volume GS is unique (`HasUniqueInfiniteVolumeVBSGroundState`). The hexagonal restriction is essential (general-graph decay can fail). Proof: explicit VBS analysis (AKLT, KLT) | `Quantum/SpinS/GeneralAKLT.lean` |
| `graphStateHamiltonianS` / `brStabilizer` / `tasaki_theorem_7_8` | **В§7.3.3 BriegelвҖ“Raussendorf (cluster / graph) state** (Theorem 7.8; eqs. (7.3.29)вҖ“(7.3.37)): the `S=1/2` stabilizer state, the qubit analogue of the VBS. `pauliXS x = 2Ең_x^{(1)} = Пғ_x^x`, `pauliZS x = 2Ең_x^{(3)} = Пғ_x^z` (qubits, `N=1`, on `Fin L`); `neighborZProduct G x` = the diagonal `вҲҸ_{yвҲјx} Пғ_y^z` (acting by `вҲҸ_{yвҲјx}(вҲ’1)^{Пғ_y}`); `brStabilizer G x = Пғ_x^x вҲҸ_{yвҲјx} Пғ_y^z = KМӮ_x` (stabilizer, `KМӮ_xВІ=1`, eigenvalues `Вұ1`); `graphStateHamiltonianS G = вҲ’ОЈ_x KМӮ_x` (eq. 7.3.30). `tasaki_theorem_7_8` (**AXIOM**, `0 < L`): the cluster state `ОҰ` (`IsClusterState` marker) is the **unique** ground state with **E_GS = вҲ’N** (`N = |Оӣ| = L`, `IsGroundEnergy`), gap above it **exactly `2`** (`IsPositiveSpectralGap вҖҰ 2`; `0 < L` excludes the 1-dim empty lattice), and every ground-energy eigenvector вҲқ `ОҰ`. Proof: map `ДӨ_BR` to the trivial paramagnet `вҲ’ОЈ Пғ_x^x` via the controlled-`Z` unitary `Гӣ_C` (`Гӣ_CВІ=1`) | `Quantum/SpinS/ClusterState.lean` |
| `anisotropicChainHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_8_1` | **В§8.1.1 Large-`D` phase of the anisotropic `S=1` chain** (Theorem 8.1; eqs. (8.1.1)вҖ“(8.1.3)): the topological phase transition. `anisotropicChainHamiltonianS L D = heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling L) 2 + D ОЈ_x (Ең_x^{(3)})ВІ` (eq. 8.1.1, `ДӨ_D = ОЈ_x[Ең_xВ·Ең_{x+1} + D(Ең_x^{(3)})ВІ]`, PBC ring, crystal-field anisotropy); `spinSSiteComponentS Оұ x` selects `Ең_x^{(Оұ)}` (`Оұ : Fin 3`). `tasaki_theorem_8_1` (**AXIOM**): вҲғ `DвӮҖ>0` s.t. for `D вүҘ DвӮҖ` there are `L`-independent `О”EвӮҖ,C,Оҫ>0` with, for every even `LвүҘ2`, a **unique ground state** (`IsUniqueChainGroundState`), a **gap вүҘ О”EвӮҖ** (`IsPositiveSpectralGap`), and **exponential decay** `|вҹЁЕң_x^{(Оұ)}Ең_y^{(Оұ)}вҹ©| вүӨ C e^{вҲ’d(x,y)/Оҫ}` for all three `Оұ` (`d = ringDist`) вҖ” the large-`D` phase is unique, disordered, gapped. Raw = connected here (disordered symmetric GS, one-point fns vanish). Proof: cluster-expansion perturbation theory (large `D`, book `DвӮҖвүҲ28`; phase to `D_cвүҲ1`) | `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicLargeD.lean` |
| `openAnisotropicChainHamiltonianS` / `HasStringLRO` / `tasaki_theorem_8_2` | **В§8.1.2вҖ“В§8.1.3 Hidden order forces edge states** (Theorem 8.2, KomaвҖ“Tasaki; eqs. (8.1.9)вҖ“(8.1.11)): in the anisotropic chain, hidden antiferromagnetic order (positive den NijsвҖ“Rommelse string order `O_string^{(Оұ)}(D)`, В§7.2.1) distinguishes the Haldane phase (`0вүӨD<D_c`) from the large-`D` phase, and forces low-lying edge states. `openAnisotropicChainHamiltonianS L D` is the **open-boundary** anisotropic chain (`openAnisotropicChainCoupling`, no wrap-around вҖ” the free ends carry the `S=1/2` edge spins). `HasStringLRO L D ОҰ q` (marker) is the hidden-order bound (8.1.10) `вҹЁОҰ\|(Г”_string^{(Оұ)}/L)ВІ\|ОҰвҹ© вүҘ q_Оұ` (`q_Оұ>0`). `tasaki_theorem_8_2` (**AXIOM**): for fixed `D, q` there are **L-independent** `C_ОҪ>0` such that for every `L>0`, whenever `ОҰ` is the **unique** ground state (`IsUniqueChainGroundState`) of `ДӨ_D^open` at `EвӮҖ` with `HasStringLRO`, there are **three linearly independent excited states** `ОЁ_ОҪ` (`ОҪ:Fin 3`, `LinearIndependent в„Ӯ ОЁ`) with `ДӨ_D^open ОЁ_ОҪ = E_ОҪ ОЁ_ОҪ` and `EвӮҖ < E_ОҪ вүӨ EвӮҖ + C_ОҪ/L` вҖ” hidden order вҹ№ near four-fold degeneracy (free `S=1/2` edge spins). `C_ОҪ` quantified outside `вҲҖL` (genuinely length-uniform). Proof: HorschвҖ“von der Linden / KomaвҖ“Tasaki variational argument (as Theorem 3.1) | `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicEdgeStates.lean` |
| `lambdaDChainHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_8_3` | **В§8.2.1 О»-D model: NГ©el вүӨ string order** (Theorem 8.3, KennedyвҖ“Tasaki; eqs. (8.2.1)вҖ“(8.2.4)): the two-parameter Haldane-phase model. `lambdaDChainHamiltonianS L О» D = ОЈ_{x,y} [openAnisotropicChainCoupling] В· spinSDotXXZ x y О» 2 + D ОЈ_x (Ең_x^{(3)})ВІ` (eq. 8.2.1, `ДӨ_{О»,D}` = open XXZ chain with Ising anisotropy `О»` + crystal field `D`; `О»=1` вҮ’ open-boundary version of the anisotropic model 8.1.1, agreeing in the bulk). `neelOrderParameterS`/`stringOrderParameterS` (markers, `в„қвҶ’в„қвҶ’Fin 3вҶ’в„қ`) are the thermodynamic double-limit NГ©el (8.2.2) and den NijsвҖ“Rommelse string (8.2.3) order parameters of the GS. `tasaki_theorem_8_3` (**AXIOM**, eq. 8.2.4): for `О»вүҘ0` and any `D`, `O_NГ©el^{(Оұ)} вүӨ O_string^{(Оұ)}` for all `Оұ` вҖ” nonzero NГ©el order (Ising AF phase) forces nonzero hidden order. `tasaki_neel_transverse_eq_nonneg` (`О»вүҘ0`): `O_NГ©el^{(1)} = O_NГ©el^{(2)} вүҘ 0`. Proof: path-integral representation (В§6.3) | `Quantum/SpinS/LambdaDModel.lean` |
| `ktUnitaryS` / `piRotationS` / `IsZ2Z2Invariant` / `tasaki_prop_8_4` | **В§8.2.2вҖ“В§8.2.3 KennedyвҖ“Tasaki transformation + Proposition 8.4** (PollmannвҖ“TurnerвҖ“BergвҖ“Oshikawa; eqs. (8.2.5)вҖ“(8.2.7)): the nonlocal unitary realizing hidden ZвӮӮГ—ZвӮӮ symmetry breaking. `ktUnitaryS L` (marker) is the KennedyвҖ“Tasaki unitary `Гӣ_KT = вҲҸ_{u<v} exp(iПҖ Ең_u^{(3)} Ең_v^{(1)})` (eq. 8.2.5), with `ktUnitaryS_sq` (`Гӣ_KTВІ=1`) and `ktUnitaryS_selfAdjoint` (`Гӣ_KT=Гӣ_KTвҖ `) вҖ” a self-adjoint involution. `piRotationS L Оұ = вҲҸ_x exp(iПҖ Ең_x^{(Оұ)})` (**concrete**, on-site matrix exponentials) is the ПҖ-rotation about axis `Оұ`; `IsZ2Z2Invariant H` = `(Гӣ_ПҖ^{(Оұ)})вҖ  H Гӣ_ПҖ^{(Оұ)} = H` for all `Оұ` (commutes with all three ПҖ-rotations). `HasShortRangeInteraction`/`HasSomeShortRangeInteraction` (markers) capture range-`r` locality. `tasaki_prop_8_4` (**AXIOM**): for a short-range open-chain `ДӨ`, `Гӣ_KT ДӨ Гӣ_KT` is again short-range **iff** `ДӨ` is ZвӮӮГ—ZвӮӮ invariant вҖ” the hidden-symmetry-breaking picture is effective exactly when `ДӨ` has ZвӮӮГ—ZвӮӮ symmetry | `Quantum/SpinS/KennedyTasakiTransformation.lean` |
| `guWenHamiltonianS` / `IsSPTPhase` / `tasaki_oshikawa_8_3_3` | **В§8.3.1вҖ“В§8.3.2 Symmetry protected topological (SPT) phase** (GuвҖ“Wen / PollmannвҖ“TurnerвҖ“BergвҖ“Oshikawa; eqs. (8.3.1)вҖ“(8.3.4)): the Haldane phase as an SPT phase. `guWenHamiltonianS L D B = anisotropicChainHamiltonianS L D + B ОЈ_x Ең_x^{(1)}` (eq. 8.3.4, S=1 chain + magnetic field B in 1-dir, breaks ZвӮӮГ—ZвӮӮвҶ’ZвӮӮ; B=0 вҮ’ anisotropic 8.1.1). `tasaki_oshikawa_8_3_3` (**AXIOM**, eq. 8.3.3): the spin-`S` VBS string order `O_string^{(Оұ)}` is `>0` for odd `S`, `=0` for even `S` (even/odd-`S` distinction); `tasaki_vbs_edge_degeneracy` (**AXIOM**): the spin-`S` AKLT open chain has `(S+1)ВІ`-fold edge degeneracy. **SPT definition** (`def : Prop`, NOT axiom): `HamiltonianPath` (continuous `в„қвҶ’` Hamiltonian, each short-range gapped-unique via `IsShortRangeGappedUniqueGS` marker); `ContinuouslyConnected`/`SymmetryConnected sym` (path with all `ДӨ_s` having the symmetry `sym`); `IsTrivialPhase sym H` = symmetry-connected to a product state (`IsProductStateHamiltonian`); `IsSPTPhase sym H` = short-range gapped-unique + `sym H` + В¬trivial. The Haldane phase is the prototypical SPT phase protected by ZвӮӮГ—ZвӮӮ (`IsZ2Z2Invariant`) | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTPhase.lean` |
| `IsTimeReversalInvariant` / `IsBondInversionInvariant` / `vbsInversionParityS` / `tasaki_spt_classification` | **В§8.3.2вҖ“В§8.3.3 Protecting symmetries + topological indices for SPT** (PollmannвҖ“TurnerвҖ“BergвҖ“Oshikawa; eqs. (8.3.6)вҖ“(8.3.10)): the Haldane phase is protected by any of three symmetries вҖ” (S1) ZвӮӮГ—ZвӮӮ (`IsZ2Z2Invariant`), (S2) time-reversal (`IsTimeReversalInvariant` marker), (S3) bond-centered inversion (`IsBondInversionInvariant` marker). `vbsInversionParityS L S` (marker, в„Ө) + `tasaki_vbs_inversion_parity` (**AXIOM**): `Гӣ_inv|ОҰ_VBS^Sвҹ© = (вҲ’1)^{LВ·S}|ОҰ_VBS^Sвҹ©` вҖ” odd `LВ·S` вҹ№ odd parity вҹ№ ZвӮӮ obstruction to connecting to the trivial state. `IsSpinSVBSNontrivialSPT S` (marker) + `tasaki_spt_classification` (**AXIOM**): the spin-`S` VBS is a nontrivial SPT phase **iff `S` is odd** (even `S` вҮ’ trivial). `entanglementEntropyS` (marker, eqs. 8.3.7вҖ“8.3.8): the bipartite entanglement entropy `вҲ’ОЈ p_j log p_j` from the Schmidt decomposition. В§8.3.3 is heuristic; precise indices come in В§8.3.4 (MPS) / В§8.3.6 (Ogata) | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTTopologicalIndex.lean` |
| `IsTrivialProjectiveRep` / `tasaki_theorem_8_7` / `tasaki_corollary_8_5` | **В§8.3.4 Matrix-product SPT index** (Theorem 8.7 Tachikawa + Corollary 8.5; eqs. (8.3.42)вҖ“(8.3.47)): the precise MPS invariant. A protecting symmetry `G` acts on the bond space by a projective representation with phase function (2-cocycle) `ПҶ : GвҶ’GвҶ’в„қ` (`IsProjectiveRep` marker); it is **trivial** (`IsTrivialProjectiveRep` marker) iff `ПҶ` is a coboundary (eq. 8.3.43) вҖ” the cohomology class is the SPT index. `SymmetricInjectiveMPSExists G ПҶ` (marker): an injective MPS invariant up to phase under `VМӮ(g)`. `tasaki_theorem_8_7` (**AXIOM**): symmetric injective MPS вҹ№ trivial projective rep. For half-odd-integer spin (`N` odd), `z2z2Spin_nontrivial_of_odd` (**AXIOM**, eq. 2.1.31): the ZвӮӮГ—ZвӮӮ rep is nontrivial. `tasaki_corollary_8_5` (**PROVED**, contrapositive of Thm 8.7): for `N` odd there is **no** ZвӮӮГ—ZвӮӮ-invariant injective MPS вҖ” the matrix-product LiebвҖ“SchultzвҖ“Mattis no-go | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTMatrixProductIndex.lean` |
| `tasaki_theorem_8_6` | **В§8.3.5 Discrete-symmetry LiebвҖ“SchultzвҖ“Mattis theorem** (Theorem 8.6, OgataвҖ“Tasaki): the general no-go without continuous symmetry. For **half-odd-integer** spin (`S=N/2`, `N` odd) with a short-ranged translation-invariant Hamiltonian (`HasShortRangeHamiltonianS`/`IsTranslationInvariantS` markers) having **either** ZвӮӮГ—ZвӮӮ **or** time-reversal symmetry (`IsZ2Z2SymmetricS вҲЁ IsTimeReversalSymmetricS`), the ground state can **never** be both unique and gapped: `tasaki_theorem_8_6` (**AXIOM**) `вҹ№ В¬HasUniqueGappedGroundStateS H`. Unlike the original LSM (В§6.2, continuous U(1)), this needs only a discrete symmetry; proved rigorously by OgataвҖ“Tasaki via the Cuntz algebra / split property | `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisDiscrete.lean` |
| `sptInterpolatingHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_8_8` | **В§8.3.6 Rigorous SPT phase transition** (Theorem 8.8, Tasaki 2018 index theorem; eq. (8.3.55)): the existence of a topological phase transition. `sptInterpolatingHamiltonianS L s = sВ·akltHamiltonianS L + (1вҲ’s)В·ОЈ_x (Ең_x^{(3)})ВІ` (eq. 8.3.55) linearly connects the trivial Hamiltonian (8.1.2, `s=0`, large-`D` phase) to the AKLT/VBS Hamiltonian (7.1.1, `s=1`, Haldane phase), keeping all three protecting symmetries. `tasaki_theorem_8_8` (**AXIOM**): the model's infinite-volume limit undergoes a phase transition вҖ” вҲғ intermediate `sвҲҲ(0,1)` at which the family `LвҶҰДӨ_s` is **either** gapless (`IsGaplessInInfiniteVolume`), **or** has multiple ground states (`HasMultipleGroundStatesInInfiniteVolume`), **or** has a local-expectation discontinuity (`HasLocalExpectationDiscontinuityInInfiniteVolume`) вҖ” markers on the `LвҶ‘вҲһ` family. Proving topological phase transitions resists standard methods; Tasaki settled it via an index theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTPhaseTransition.lean` |
| `toricCodeHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_8_9` | **В§8.4 Topological order: Kitaev's toric code** (Theorem 8.9, BravyiвҖ“HastingsвҖ“Michalakis; eqs. (8.4.1), (8.4.19)): a gapped phase whose degeneracy is protected by topology, not symmetry. Qubits live on the edges `ToricEdge L = (ZMod L Г— ZMod L) Г— Fin 2` of an `LГ—L` torus (`2LВІ` edges); `starEdges`/`plaquetteEdges` are the 4-edge stars `A_v`/plaquettes `B_p`; `sigmaZS`/`sigmaXS` = `Пғ^z`/`Пғ^x`. `toricCodeHamiltonianS L = вҲ’ОЈ_v вҲҸ_{A_v} Пғ^z вҲ’ ОЈ_p вҲҸ_{B_p} Пғ^x` (eq. 8.4.1, commuting stabilizers, gap вүҘ 2, 4-fold degenerate on the torus). `perturbedToricHamiltonianS L Оө V = ДӨ_tc + Оө ОЈ_x V_x` (eq. 8.4.19). `tasaki_theorem_8_9` (**AXIOM**): for range-`r` perturbations there are **L-independent** `ОөвӮҖ,О”>0` such that for `|Оө|вүӨОөвӮҖ`, all `LвүҘ2` (nontrivial torus), and any local perturbation (`IsToricPerturbation`), the perturbed Hamiltonian has **four near-degenerate ground states** separated by `О”` (`HasFourNearlyDegenerateGroundStates`) вҖ” topological 4-fold degeneracy stable under arbitrary perturbation, no symmetry needed. Lattice + stabilizers + both Hamiltonians concrete | `Quantum/SpinS/ToricCode.lean` |

(2.4.9), p. 33, up to normalisation). Proof via Nat induction using the Cartan ladder `[Еңtot^(3), Еңtot^-] = -Еңtot^-` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `mulVec_preserves_eigenvalue_of_commute` | generic abstract pattern: for any `A B : ManyBodyOp Оӣ` with `Commute A B`, if `A В· v = О» В· v` then `A В· (B В· v) = О» В· (B В· v)` вҖ” the backbone of all commutator-based eigenvalue propagation | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_all_down` | dual ladder: for any `k : в„•`, `Еңtot^(3) В· (Еңtot^+)^k В· |вҶ“..вҶ“вҹ© = (-|Оӣ|/2 + k) В· (Еңtot^+)^k В· |вҶ“..вҶ“вҹ©` вҖ” same Tasaki В§2.4 (2.4.9) ladder parameterised from the lowest weight `M = -Smax`, raised by `Еңtot^+`. | `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_all_up` / `_all_down` | constant-config Еңtot^(3) eigenvalue: `Еңtot^(3) В· |s..sвҹ© = (|Оӣ| В· spinHalfSign s) В· |s..sвҹ©`; `s = 0` gives eigenvalue `|Оӣ|/2 = Smax`, `s = 1` gives `-|Оӣ|/2 = -Smax` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `Еңtot^- В· |вҶ“..вҶ“вҹ© = 0`: lowering annihilates the bottom of the ladder | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `Еңtot^+ В· |вҶ‘..вҶ‘вҹ© = 0`: raising annihilates the top of the ladder | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{Minus,Plus}_pow_basisVec_all_{up,down}_mem_magnetizationSubspace` | Submodule-form: `(Еңtot^-)^k В· |вҶ‘..вҶ‘вҹ© вҲҲ H_{Smax - k}` and `(Еңtot^+)^k В· |вҶ“..вҶ“вҹ© вҲҲ H_{-Smax + k}` вҖ” Tasaki В§2.4 eq. (2.4.9) ladder iterates explicitly placed in the magnetisation sectors of Tasaki eq. (2.2.10) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `basisVec_{upDown,basisSwap_upDown}_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | two-site antiparallel states `|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ©`, `|вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©` lie in `H_0` (Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.2), p. 37, NГ©el state for the spin-1/2 Fin 2 instance) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `singlet_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` / `triplet_zero_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | singlet `|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© - |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©` and triplet-`m=0` state `|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© + |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©` lie in `H_0` (Tasaki В§A.3 decomposition at the `M = 0` sector) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `neelChainConfig` / `neelChainState` | Tasaki В§2.5 eq. (2.5.2) NГ©el state at `S = 1/2` on the parity-coloured chain `Fin (2 * K)`: `Пғ(i) = вҶ‘` if `i.val` even, `вҶ“` if odd | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelChainConfig_magnetization_zero` / `neelChainState_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | the NГ©el chain configuration has total magnetisation `0`, so the corresponding basis state lies in the `Ең_tot^(3) = 0` eigenspace `H_0` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_neelChainState_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | for any coupling `J`, `H В· |ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ©` again lies in `H_0` вҖ” immediate corollary of SU(2) invariance applied to the NГ©el state. The NГ©el state is *not* an H-eigenstate (Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.3)), but it cannot leak into other magnetisation sectors | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelChainState_adjacent` | Tasaki В§2.5 eq. (2.5.3) per-bond action: for every adjacent pair `(i, i+1)` of the chain `Fin (2 * K)`, `Ең_вҹЁiвҹ© В· Ең_вҹЁi+1вҹ© В· |ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ© = (1/2) В· |swap_{i,i+1} ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ© - (1/4) В· |ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ©` (antiparallel case, parity-derived) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelChainState_wrap` | wrap-around bond `(2K + 1, 0)` action on the periodic chain `Fin (2 * (K + 1))`: same `(1/2) swap - (1/4) NГ©el` decomposition as the open-bond case (parities `1` and `0` differ since the cycle length is even). Together with the adjacent lemma, covers every bond of the periodic chain | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_openChainCoupling_one_mulVec_neelChainState_one` | `K = 1` instance: `H_open(N=1, J) В· |ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ© = -J В· |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ© + (J/2) В· |ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ©`. Lifts the per-bond `spinHalfDot` calculation through `H_open(N=1, J) = -2J В· spinHalfDot 0 1`. The non-eigenstate character of the NГ©el state is plain | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelChainConfig_one_eq_upDown` / `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelChainState_one` | bridges the `K = 1` NГ©el chain configuration to the existing `upDown` config and computes `ОҳМӮ_tot (neelChainState 1) = -basisVec (basisSwap upDown 0 1)` (the per-down sign convention of `ОҳМӮ` flips the antiparallel pair) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelSquareConfig` / `neelSquareState` | 2D checkerboard NГ©el state on `Fin (2K) Г— Fin (2L)` (Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.2) bipartite case): `Пғ(i, j) = вҶ‘` if `(i.val + j.val) % 2 = 0`, `вҶ“` otherwise | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelSquareConfig_magnetization_zero` / `neelSquareState_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | the 2D NГ©el configuration has total magnetisation `0` and the corresponding state lies in the `Ең_tot^(3) = 0` eigenspace `H_0`. Proof: row-by-row column-sum vanishes (helper `sum_alternating_sign_offset` for the 1D parity sum with offset) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelSquareState_horizontal_adjacent` / `_vertical_adjacent` | Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.3) per-bond action on the 2D NГ©el state for the horizontal (`(i,j)~(i+1,j)`) and vertical (`(i,j)~(i,j+1)`) nearest-neighbour bonds: same `(1/2) В· |swapвҹ© - (1/4) В· |ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ©` decomposition as the 1D chain | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelSquareState_horizontal_wrap` / `_vertical_wrap` | wrap-around bond actions on the 2D torus NГ©el state: horizontal `((2K+1, j), (0, j))` on `Fin (2(K+1)) Г— Fin (2L)` and vertical `((i, 2L+1), (i, 0))` on `Fin (2K) Г— Fin (2(L+1))` are antiparallel (parities differ by an odd shift); both inherit the same `(1/2)В·|swapвҹ© - (1/4)В·|ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ©` decomposition. With `_horizontal_adjacent` / `_vertical_adjacent`, covers every nearest-neighbour bond of the 2D torus NГ©el state | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelCubicConfig` / `neelCubicState` / `neelCubicConfig_magnetization_zero` / `neelCubicState_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | 3D cubic checkerboard NГ©el state on `(Fin (2K) Г— Fin (2L)) Г— Fin (2M)`: `Пғ((i,j),k) = вҶ‘` if `(i+j+k) % 2 = 0`, magnetisation = 0, lies in `H_0` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelCubicState_x_adjacent` / `_y_adjacent` / `_z_adjacent` | Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.3) per-bond actions on the 3D cubic NГ©el state for the three nearest-neighbour bond axes (x, y, z): same `(1/2)В·|swapвҹ© - (1/4)В·|ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ©` decomposition | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelCubicState_x_wrap` / `_y_wrap` / `_z_wrap` | wrap-around bond actions on the 3D cubic-torus NГ©el state: each axis-wrap (`((2K+1, j), k) ~ ((0, j), k)`, `((i, 2L+1), k) ~ ((i, 0), k)`, `((i, j), 2M+1) ~ ((i, j), 0)`) is antiparallel (one coordinate shifts by an odd amount). All three axes inherit the same `(1/2)В·|swapвҹ© - (1/4)В·|ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ©` decomposition. With `_x_adjacent` / `_y_adjacent` / `_z_adjacent`, covers every nearest-neighbour bond of the 3D cubic torus NГ©el state | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelSquareState_one_one` | concrete `K = L = 1` (2Г—2 = 4-site) instance: `ОҳМӮ_tot (neelSquareState 1 1) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelSquareConfig 1 1))` (the equal up/down counts make `(-1)^|A| = 1`, so no overall sign) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelCubicState_one_one_one` | concrete `K = L = M = 1` (2Г—2Г—2 = 8-site) instance: `ОҳМӮ_tot (neelCubicState 1 1 1) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelCubicConfig 1 1 1))` (4 down spins after flipping вҶ’ `(-1)^4 = 1`, so no overall sign) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelChainState` | general-`K` 1D chain: `ОҳМӮ_tot (neelChainState K) = (-1)^K В· basisVec (flipConfig (neelChainConfig K))` (helper `prod_alternating_neg_one` collapses the per-site sign product). | `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelSquareState` | general-`K, L` 2D checkerboard: `ОҳМӮ_tot (neelSquareState K L) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelSquareConfig K L))` (no sign because `(-1)^(2KL) = 1`). | `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelCubicState` | general-`K, L, M` 3D cubic checkerboard: `ОҳМӮ_tot (neelCubicState K L M) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelCubicConfig K L M))` (no sign because `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`). | `basisVec_apply` / `basisVec_self` / `basisVec_of_ne` | foundational evaluation lemmas for the standard basis vectors: explicit `if`-form, diagonal `=1`, and off-diagonal `=0` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `sum_mul_basisVec` / `basisVec_sum_mul` | selector-sum identities `вҲ‘ П„, f П„ В· basisVec Пғ П„ = f Пғ` (and the symmetric form), the workhorses for inner-product computations on the spin Hilbert space | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec_inner` | basis-vector orthonormality `вҲ‘ П„, basisVec Пғ П„ В· basisVec ПҒ П„ = if ПҒ = Пғ then 1 else 0`. Real bilinear pairing (no complex conjugation needed since `basisVec` values are 0 or 1) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisSwap_ne_self` | `Пғ x вү  Пғ y вҶ’ basisSwap Пғ x y вү  Пғ` (the swap of an antiparallel pair changes the configuration). Useful for orthogonality computations on swapped states | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `neelChainState_norm_squared` / `neelSquareState_norm_squared` / `neelCubicState_norm_squared` | the 1D / 2D / 3D NГ©el states are normalized: `вҲ‘ П„, |ОҰ_NГ©el(П„)|ВІ = 1` (one-line consequence of `basisVec_inner`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_basisVec_basisSwap_adjacent_eq_zero` | the NГ©el chain state is orthogonal to the swapped basis vector at any adjacent (antiparallel) bond: `вҲ‘ П„, ОҰ_NГ©el(П„) В· basisVec(swap)(П„) = 0`. Direct consequence of `basisVec_inner` + `basisSwap_ne_self` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_spinHalfDot_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` | the per-adjacent-bond expectation `вҹЁОҰ_NГ©el, Ең_x В· Ең_y В· ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ© = -1/4` (Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.4) ingredient at S = 1/2). Combines `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelChainState_adjacent` (bond action) with the orthogonality + normВІ lemmas to compute `(1/2)В·0 - (1/4)В·1 = -1/4` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_basisVec_antiparallel` | generic lemma: for any antiparallel `(x, y)` configuration `Пғ`, `вҹЁbasisVec Пғ, Ең_x В· Ең_y В· basisVec Пғвҹ© = -1/4`. The 1-line foundation for every NГ©el-bond expectation | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_basisVec_parallel` | parallel companion: for `Пғ x = Пғ y` (and `x вү  y`), `вҹЁbasisVec Пғ, Ең_x В· Ең_y В· basisVec Пғвҹ© = +1/4`. Both basis vectors at the parallel pair are eigenvectors of `Ең_x В· Ең_y` (eigenvalue `+1/4`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_spinHalfDot_parallel_eq_one_quarter` | NГ©el chain same-sublattice (parallel) bond expectation `+1/4`: for any `x вү  y` with `x.val % 2 = y.val % 2` (e.g., `(0, 2)`, `(1, 3)`), `вҹЁОҰ_NГ©el, Ең_x В· Ең_y В· ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ© = +1/4` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `onSite_spinHalfOp3_mul_onSite_spinHalfOp3_mulVec_basisVec` | `(Ең^(3)_x В· Ең^(3)_y) В· basisVec Пғ = (spinHalfSign Пғ x В· spinHalfSign Пғ y) В· basisVec Пғ`: every basis vector is an eigenvector of the diagonal `Ең^zВ·Ең^z` correlator. | `inner_basisVec_szsz_basisVec` | generic `вҹЁbasisVec Пғ, Ең^(3)_x В· Ең^(3)_y В· basisVec Пғвҹ© = spinHalfSign Пғ x В· spinHalfSign Пғ y`. The diagonal-only spin-spin correlator on a basis state | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfSign_mul_antiparallel` | for antiparallel `s вү  t : Fin 2`, `spinHalfSign s В· spinHalfSign t = -(1/4)`. Made public in PR #332 to power the generic `inner_neelStateOf_szsz_neelStateOf_antiparallel = -(1/4)` NГ©el correlator | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_sub_szsz_basisVec_antiparallel` | generic off-diagonal correlator: for any antiparallel `(x, y)` configuration `Пғ`, `вҹЁbasisVec Пғ, (Ең_x В· Ең_y - Ең^(3)_x В· Ең^(3)_y) В· basisVec Пғвҹ© = 0`. | `neelChainState_inner_off_diagonal_correlator_adjacent_eq_zero` | the per-adjacent-bond off-diagonal correlator on the NГ©el chain vanishes: `вҹЁОҰ_NГ©el, (Ең_x В· Ең_y - Ең^(3)_x В· Ең^(3)_y) В· ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ© = 0`. Direct application of the generic helper | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_szsz_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` | per-adjacent-bond `Ең^(3)_x В· Ең^(3)_y` correlation on the NГ©el chain: `-1/4`. | `neelChainState_inner_szsz_wrap_eq_neg_one_quarter` | 1D NГ©el periodic chain: per-wrap-bond `Ең^(3)_x В· Ең^(3)_y` correlation `-1/4` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelSquareState_inner_szsz_{horizontal,vertical}_{adjacent,wrap}_eq_neg_one_quarter` | 2D NГ©el: per-bond `Ең^(3)В·Ең^(3)` correlation `-1/4` for every horizontal / vertical adjacent and wrap bond | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelCubicState_inner_szsz_{x,y,z}_{adjacent,wrap}_eq_neg_one_quarter` | 3D NГ©el: per-bond `Ең^(3)В·Ең^(3)` correlation `-1/4` for every x / y / z adjacent and wrap bond. Completes the `Ең^zВ·Ең^z` correlation coverage parity with the full `ЕңВ·Ең` family from #273 | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_spinHalfDot_wrap_eq_neg_one_quarter` | 1D wrap-bond expectation `-1/4` on the periodic NГ©el chain `Fin (2(K+1))` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelSquareState_inner_spinHalfDot_horizontal_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` / `_vertical_adjacent_` / `_horizontal_wrap_` / `_vertical_wrap_` | 2D NГ©el: per-bond expectation `-1/4` for every horizontal / vertical adjacent and wrap bond | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelCubicState_inner_spinHalfDot_{x,y,z}_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` / `_{x,y,z}_wrap_` | 3D NГ©el: per-bond expectation `-1/4` for every x / y / z adjacent and wrap bond. | `neelChainState_energy_expectation_K1` | `K = 1` (2-site) open-chain Heisenberg energy expectation `вҹЁОҰ_NГ©el, H_open В· ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ© = J/2`. Combines `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_eq` (`H = -2J В· spinHalfDot 0 1`) with the per-bond `-1/4` expectation, giving `-2J В· (-1/4) = J/2` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelConfigOf` / `neelStateOf` | generic graph-centric NГ©el state from a sublattice indicator `A : V вҶ’ Bool`: `neelConfigOf A x := if A x then вҶ‘ else вҶ“` and `neelStateOf A := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`. The chain / 2D / 3D `neelXyzConfig` / `neelXyzState` definitions are bridged via `_eq_neelConfigOf` / `_eq_neelStateOf`. Tasaki В§2.5 eq. (2.5.2) graph-centric form | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `Ең_x В· Ең_y` action on the canonical NГ©el state: for any `x вү  y` with `A x вү  A y`, `Ең_x В· Ең_y В· ОҰ_NГ©el(A) = (1/2) В· |swap_{x, y} ОҰ_NГ©el(A)вҹ© - (1/4) В· ОҰ_NГ©el(A)`. Tasaki В§2.5 eq. (2.5.3) graph-centric form. The chain / 2D / 3D `_adjacent` / `_wrap` bond actions are 1-line corollaries via the `_eq_neelStateOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_neelStateOf_spinHalfDot_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `Ең_x В· Ең_y` expectation on the canonical NГ©el state: for any `x вү  y` with `A x вү  A y`, `вҹЁОҰ_NГ©el(A), Ең_x В· Ең_y В· ОҰ_NГ©el(A)вҹ© = -(1/4)`. Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.4) ingredient (graph-centric form). The chain / 2D / 3D `_eq_neg_one_quarter` companions reduce to this via the `_eq_neelStateOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_neelStateOf_szsz_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `Ең^z_x В· Ең^z_y` correlation on the canonical NГ©el state: for any `A x вү  A y`, `вҹЁОҰ_NГ©el(A), Ең^z_x В· Ең^z_y В· ОҰ_NГ©el(A)вҹ© = -(1/4)`. Diagonal half of Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.4) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf` | generic graph-centric Marshall sign `вҲҸ_{x вҲҲ A} (-1)^(Пғ x)` for any finite vertex type `V`, sublattice indicator `A : V вҶ’ Bool`, and configuration `Пғ : V вҶ’ Fin 2`. Aligns with the project-wide graph-centric design (CLAUDE.local.md) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_const_zero` | for any sublattice indicator `A`, the all-up Marshall sign is `marshallSignOf A (const 0) = 1`. Generic counterpart of `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` etc.; those are now 1-line corollaries via the `_eq_marshallSignOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig` / `marshallSignChainConfig_neelChainConfig` | the Marshall sign `(-1)^(N_A^вҶ“)` for spin-1/2 configurations on the parity-coloured chain `Fin (2K)`, encoded as `вҲҸ_{x even} (-1)^(Пғ x)`; specialisation to the NГ©el configuration gives sign `+1` (no down spins on sublattice `A`). Foundational definition for the Marshall basis change underpinning the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki В§2.5). **Deprecated** as of 2026-04-22 in favour of the generic `marshallSignOf` (the chain / 2D / 3D Marshall sign defs are kept for backward compatibility but new code should prefer the generic form) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_eq_marshallSignOf` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the chain / 2D / 3D parity-coloured Marshall signs are precisely `marshallSignOf` instantiated at the corresponding parity colouring | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig` / `marshallSignSquareConfig_neelSquareConfig` | 2D analogue: Marshall sign `вҲҸ_{(i,j) with i+j even} (-1)^(Пғ (i,j))` on `Fin (2K) Г— Fin (2L)`; equals `+1` on the 2D checkerboard NГ©el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_neelCubicConfig` | 3D analogue: Marshall sign `вҲҸ_{((i,j),k) with i+j+k even} (-1)^(Пғ ((i,j),k))` on `(Fin (2K) Г— Fin (2L)) Г— Fin (2M)`; equals `+1` on the 3D cubic checkerboard NГ©el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down chain configurations: `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 0) = 1` and `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 1) = (-1)^K` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 2D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down checkerboard: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `2KL` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(2KL) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 3D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down cubic: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `4KLM` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig` | Marshall sign under the global spin-flip on the chain: `marshallSignChainConfig K (flipConfig Пғ) = (-1)^K В· marshallSignChainConfig K Пғ`. Each of the K even-indexed sites contributes `-1`. Proof: `Finset.prod_mul_distrib` + helper `prod_alternating_neg_one` collapses the contributing factor product to `(-1)^K`, with the per-site identity `(-1)^((1-s).val) = (-1)В·(-1)^(s.val)` closed by `fin_cases` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_flipConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_flipConfig` | 2D / 3D Marshall sign invariant under the global spin-flip (the contributing factor product `(-1)^(2KL)` resp. `(-1)^(4KLM)` equals `+1` for all K, L, M) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState` / `_neelChainConfig` | Marshall-rotated chain basis state `marshallSignChainConfig K Пғ вҖў basisVec Пғ`; specialisation at the NГ©el configuration coincides with `neelChainState K` (since the Marshall sign of the NГ©el state is `+1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSquareState` / `_neelSquareConfig` | 2D Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelSquareState K L` at the NГ©el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallCubicState` / `_neelCubicConfig` | 3D cubic Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelCubicState K L M` at the NГ©el configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig_neelChainConfig` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | Marshall sign on the *flipped* NГ©el configuration: `(-1)^K` (1D), `+1` (2D), `+1` (3D) вҖ” direct compositions of `_flipConfig` and `_neelChainConfig` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState_flipConfig_eq_timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the Marshall-rotated *flipped* NГ©el state coincides with the time-reversed NГ©el state in 1D, 2D, 3D вҖ” both sides equal the same explicit `(-1)^K` (1D) or `+1` (2D, 3D) scaled basis vector. Establishes a direct bridge between the Marshall basis change (Tasaki В§2.5 / Marshall-Lieb-Mattis) and the time-reversal operator (Tasaki В§2.3) on the NГ©el ground-state ansatz | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis A Пғ` | **Marshall-dressed standard basis state** `:= marshallSignOf A Пғ вҖў basisVec Пғ` on a generic finite vertex type `V` with sublattice indicator `A : V вҶ’ Bool` (Tasaki В§2.5 eq. (2.5.8), p. 41). The dressing produces a basis in which the spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a connected bipartite graph has all off-diagonal matrix elements `вүӨ 0` (Marshall sign trick), the input to the PerronвҖ“Frobenius proof of the MLM theorem | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_self` / `_of_ne` / `_apply` | pointwise rules: `ОЁМғ^Пғ Пғ = marshallSignOf A Пғ`; `ОЁМғ^Пғ П„ = 0` for `П„ вү  Пғ`; explicit `ОЁМғ^Пғ П„ = marshallSignOf A Пғ В· basisVec Пғ П„` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one` | each factor of `marshallSignOf` is `Вұ1`, so the sign squares to `1`: `(marshallSignOf A Пғ)ВІ = 1` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_inner` | orthonormality of the Marshall-dressed basis under the real bilinear pairing: `ОЈ_П„ ОЁМғ^Пғ П„ В· ОЁМғ^ПҒ П„ = if ПҒ = Пғ then 1 else 0` (combines `basisVec_inner` with `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one`) | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_mem_magnetizationSubspace` / `_zero` | the dressed basis state lies in the same magnetisation-`M` subspace `H_M = H_{ПғМ„/2}` as the underlying `basisVec Пғ` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.10)); the `_zero` specialisation places it in `H_0` when `ОЈ_x Пғ_x = 0` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | every matrix entry of the two-site spin product `Ең_x В· Ең_y` is **real**: `((spinHalfDot x y) Пғ Пғ').im = 0` for all `x, y, Пғ, Пғ'`. Case analysis on `x = y` / parallel / antiparallel via the existing `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_{parallel,antiparallel}` action lemmas. **Property (i) ingredient** for the MarshallвҖ“LiebвҖ“Mattis theorem (Tasaki В§2.5, p. 41) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_im_eq_zero` | for **real** coupling `J : Оӣ вҶ’ Оӣ вҶ’ в„Ӯ` (`(J x y).im = 0` for all `x, y`), every matrix entry of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ОЈ_{x,y} J(x,y) В· spinHalfDot x y` is real: `((heisenbergHamiltonian J) Пғ Пғ').im = 0`. в„қ-linearity + `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_im_eq_zero` | the Marshall sign `marshallSignOf A Пғ` is **real**: `(marshallSignOf A Пғ).im = 0`. Each factor of the product is `Вұ1 вҲҲ в„қ` (either `1` or `(-1 : в„Ӯ)^(Пғ x : в„•)` with `(Пғ x : в„•) вҲҲ {0, 1}`); products of reals are real | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_im_eq_zero` | **MLM Property (i)**: for real coupling `J`, the dressed Heisenberg bilinear pairing `ОЈ_П„ \|ОЁМғ^Пғвҹ© П„ В· (H В· \|ОЁМғ^{Пғ'}вҹ©) П„` is real (Tasaki В§2.5, p. 41 in the proof of Theorem 2.2). Reduces to `marshallSignOf A Пғ В· marshallSignOf A Пғ' В· H Пғ Пғ'` (each factor real) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | for any operator `M`, the dressed bilinear pairing factorises: `ОЈ_П„ \|ОЁМғ^Пғвҹ© П„ В· (M В· \|ОЁМғ^{Пғ'}вҹ©) П„ = marshallSignOf A Пғ В· marshallSignOf A Пғ' В· M Пғ Пғ'`. Generalises the inner-product computation used in Property (i) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_mul_marshallSignOf_basisSwap_of_bipartite_antiparallel` | **Marshall sign relation**: for a bond `{x, y}` crossing the bipartition (`A x вү  A y`) with `Пғ` antiparallel at `{x, y}` (`Пғ x вү  Пғ y`), `marshallSignOf A Пғ * marshallSignOf A (basisSwap Пғ x y) = -1`. The combined product over `Оӣ` of pairwise factors collapses: outside `{x, y}` each pairwise factor is `(Вұ1)ВІ = 1`; at the unique site in `A вҲ© {x, y}` the pair contributes `(-1)^(Пғ x + Пғ y) = -1` since `Пғ x вү  Пғ y`; the other site of `{x, y}` lies outside `A` and contributes `1` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos` | per-bond non-positivity: for `Пғ вү  Пғ'` and any bond `(x, y)` with real non-negative `J(x, y)` supported on bipartite bonds, the contribution `marshallSignOf A Пғ В· marshallSignOf A Пғ' В· J(x,y) В· (spinHalfDot x y) Пғ Пғ'` to the dressed off-diagonal element has non-positive real part. Case analysis on `(spinHalfDot x y) Пғ Пғ'` (zero off-diagonal except at `Пғ = basisSwap Пғ' x y`, antiparallel Пғ', `x вү  y`) combined with the Marshall sign relation | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_re_nonpos_of_ne` | **MLM Property (ii)** (Tasaki В§2.5, p. 41): for real non-negative `J` supported on bipartite bonds and `Пғ вү  Пғ'`, the dressed off-diagonal Heisenberg pairing `ОЈ_П„ \|ОЁМғ^Пғвҹ© П„ В· (H В· \|ОЁМғ^{Пғ'}вҹ©) П„` has non-positive real part. Sum bond-by-bond using `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos`. The **Marshall sign trick** at the heart of the MarshallвҖ“LiebвҖ“Mattis Theorem 2.2 proof | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `SwapStep`, `SwapReachable` | one-step swap relation `Пғ вҶҰ basisSwap Пғ x y` along a graph edge `(x, y)` with `Пғ x вү  Пғ y`; reflexive transitive closure for multi-step reachability | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_walk_of_ne` | for any `G`-walk from `x` to `y` and `Пғ x вү  Пғ y`, `SwapReachable G Пғ (basisSwap Пғ x y)`. Walk induction with case analysis on `Пғ z` at intermediate vertex (Tasaki p. 41 "Proof of Property (iii)" Lemma) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_reachable_of_ne` / `_preconnected_` | for any `x, y` reachable in `G` (or any `x, y` if `G` preconnected) with `Пғ x вү  Пғ y`, the swap is reachable. **MLM Property (iii) ingredient** (Tasaki В§2.5 p. 41) вҖ” combined with iteration over the magnetisation-difference, gives PerronвҖ“Frobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `HвӮҖIndex Оӣ` | index type `{Пғ : Оӣ вҶ’ Fin 2 // magnetization Оӣ Пғ = 0}` for the zero-magnetisation subspace `H_0`; `Fintype` and `DecidableEq` instances | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0` | real-valued matrix on `HвӮҖIndex Оӣ` with entries `Re (marshallSignOf A Пғ В· marshallSignOf A П„ В· (H_J)_{Пғ,П„})` вҖ” the matrix to which Tasaki's PerronвҖ“Frobenius proof of MLM applies | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_isSymm` | the matrix is **symmetric** for real symmetric `J` (Hermiticity of Heisenberg + realness of entries) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_offdiag_nonpos` | **off-diagonal entries are non-positive** for real non-negative bipartite `J` and distinct `Пғ вү  П„`, packaged from PR Оұ-3's Property (ii) via `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `magnetization_basisSwap` | `basisSwap Пғ x y` preserves total magnetisation. Proof uses the identification `basisSwap Пғ x y = Пғ вҲҳ Equiv.swap x y` (the swap is a permutation of `Оӣ`); the magnetisation `вҲ‘_z spinSign(Пғ z)` is invariant under such reindexing (`Equiv.sum_comp`). Key ingredient for Tasaki В§2.5 p. 42 Proposition (equal-magnetisation reachability) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetization.lean` |
| `disagreementSet` / `configDist` | the set / count of sites where `Пғ` and `Пғ'` disagree; `configDist_eq_zero_iff` characterises configuration equality | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization` | for `Пғ вү  Пғ'` with equal magnetisation, there exist sites `x` (with `Пғ x = 0, Пғ' x = 1`) and `y` (with `Пғ y = 1, Пғ' y = 0`). Pigeonhole/cardinality argument: the `(0, 1)`-disagreement and `(1, 0)`-disagreement sets have equal cardinality from magnetisation equality, and the disagreement set is non-empty for `Пғ вү  Пғ'` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `configDist_basisSwap_lt` | swapping at sites `x вҲҲ D01`, `y вҲҲ D10` strictly decreases the configuration distance to `Пғ'`. The disagreement set strictly shrinks (`x` newly agrees with `Пғ'` after swap) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_eq_magnetization` | **Tasaki В§2.5 p. 42 Proposition**: any two configurations `Пғ`, `Пғ'` with the same total magnetisation are connected by a chain of single-edge bond swaps, on a connected graph. Strong induction on `configDist`, reducing by `вүҘ 2` per step via the swap pair from `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization`. **Final ingredient** for PerronвҖ“Frobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted` | the shifted matrix `B := cВ·I вҲ’ M` on `HвӮҖIndex Оӣ`. Used as input to PerronвҖ“Frobenius: `B` is symmetric, has non-negative off-diagonal (sign flip of `M`'s non-positive off-diagonal), and non-negative diagonal when `c вүҘ M Пғ Пғ` for all `Пғ`. The maximum eigenvalue of `B` corresponds to the minimum eigenvalue of `M` (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` / `_nonneg` (`_offdiag_nonneg`, `_diag_nonneg`) | symmetry and (off-diagonal / full) non-negativity of `B` under the appropriate hypotheses on `J` and `c` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap` | the off-diagonal matrix entry `(spinHalfDot x y) Пғ (basisSwap Пғ x y) = 1/2` for `x вү  y` and antiparallel `Пғ_x вү  Пғ_y`. Building block for the explicit Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations needed for PerronвҖ“Frobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SpinDotSwapEntry.lean` |
| `basisSwap_basisSwap_ne_self_of_ne_bond` | combinatorial helper: for `x вү  y`, `Пғ_x вү  Пғ_y`, and `(u, v) вҲү {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the configuration `basisSwap (basisSwap Пғ x y) u v вү  Пғ`. Site analysis: `Пғ` and `Пғ' = basisSwap Пғ x y` differ at exactly `{x, y}`, so for the iterated swap to return to `Пғ`, the swap sites `{u, v}` must coincide with `{x, y}`. Used for off-bond vanishing in the Heisenberg matrix entry computation | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/HeisenbergSwapEntry.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap_off_bond_eq_zero` | for `Пғ' = basisSwap Пғ x y` (with `x вү  y`, `Пғ_x вү  Пғ_y`) and any `(u, v) вҲү {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the matrix entry `(spinHalfDot u v) Пғ Пғ' = 0`. Three cases: `u = v` (diagonal), `u вү  v` parallel Пғ' (constant action), `u вү  v` antiparallel + off-bond (combinatorial helper) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SpinDotOffBond.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_basisSwap` | the Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations: `(heisenbergHamiltonian J) Пғ (basisSwap Пғ x y) = (J x y + J y x) / 2`. Decomposes the double sum and uses Оұ-5e (active bond = 1/2) + Оұ-5g (off-bond = 0). For symmetric `J`, simplifies to `J x y` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/HeisenbergSwapValue.lean` |
| `dressed_pairing_basisSwap_eq` / `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_apply_basisSwap` | the dressed Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related H_0 configurations: complex-level value `-J(x, y)` (Marshall sign trick Г— Heisenberg formula Г— symmetric `J`), real-part value `-(J x y).re`. Combined with `J(x, y).re > 0` on graph edges gives strict negativity of M off-diagonal at swap pairs, hence strict positivity of `B = cВ·I вҲ’ M` вҖ” the input for PerronвҖ“Frobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/DressedSwapValue.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pos_of_basisSwap` | strict positivity `0 < B Пғ П„` on swap-related H_0 configurations with positive symmetric bipartite `J`. Combines the dressed matrix value `-J(x, y).re` (PR Оұ-5i) with the off-diagonal definition `B Пғ П„ = -M Пғ П„` (PR Оұ-5d). Single-step strict positivity for PerronвҖ“Frobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedSwap.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_path` | generic matrix-power positivity from a positive path: for non-negative matrix `B` and a path `p_0, ..., p_{k+1}` with `B(p_i, p_{i+1}) > 0` on every consecutive pair, `(B^(k+1))(p_0)(p_{k+1}) > 0`. Induction on `k` using `pow_succ` + `Finset.sum_pos'`. Used to lift single-step swap positivity (Оұ-5j) to multi-step matrix-power positivity for PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowPath.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_pow_pos_step` | one-step extension: if `(B^m) Пғ П„ > 0` and `B П„ ПҒ > 0` for non-negative `B`, then `(B^(m+1)) Пғ ПҒ > 0`. Inductive building block for ReflTransGen-style matrix-power lifting | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowExtend.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pow_pos_of_swapReachable` | for `Пғ : HвӮҖIndex Оӣ` and any `Оҫ` with `Relation.ReflTransGen (SwapStep G) Пғ.val Оҫ`, there exists `m` with `(B^m) Пғ вҹЁОҫ, h_magвҹ© > 0`. Induction on `ReflTransGen`: refl gives `m = 0`, tail extends by one swap using Оұ-5j (single-step swap positivity) and Оұ-5l (one-step matrix-power extension). Key bridge from combinatorial reachability to PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isIrreducible` | **`B = c В· I вҲ’ M` is irreducible** on `H_0` for connected bipartite `G` with positive symmetric real coupling supported on `G`-edges and shift constant `c > M Пғ Пғ` (strict). Cases on `Пғ = П„` (use diagonal positivity) vs `Пғ вү  П„` (use Оұ-5c reachability + Оұ-5m matrix-power lift). Final input for PerronвҖ“Frobenius application | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedIrreducible.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isHermitian` | the shifted matrix is Hermitian (= symmetric for real matrices). Wraps `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` (PR Оұ-5d) into the IsHermitian form needed by PerronвҖ“Frobenius | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **PerronвҖ“Frobenius applied to `B = c В· I вҲ’ M` on `H_0`**: existence of a strictly positive eigenvector `v` for some real eigenvalue `Ој`, and uniqueness up to positive scalar. Translating back to `M`, `v` is the eigenvector for the **minimum** eigenvalue (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg). This is the matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4): the H_0 ground-state expansion `ОЈ_Пғ c_Пғ \|ОЁМғ^Пғвҹ©` with `c_Пғ = v Пғ > 0` is unique up to positive scalar | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `bipartiteCoupling` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian` | the Tasaki В§2.5 p. 40 toy Hamiltonian setup: `bipartiteCoupling A x y := if A x вү  A y then 1 else 0` (the unnormalised bipartite coupling), and `heisenbergToyHamiltonian A := heisenbergHamiltonian (bipartiteCoupling A)`. Real symmetric, non-negative, supported on bipartite bonds, positive on inter-sublattice pairs. Hermitian. Used in subsequent PRs to derive `S_tot = 0` for the AFM Heisenberg ground state via inner-product comparison | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonian.lean` |
| `bipartiteGraphFromA` | the complete bipartite graph on `Оӣ` from sublattice indicator `A : Оӣ вҶ’ Bool`: vertices `x, y` are adjacent iff `A x вү  A y`.
