| `bipartiteGraphFromA_preconnected` | `bipartiteGraphFromA A` is preconnected when both sublattices are non-empty. Cases on `A x = A y` (length-2 path via opposite sublattice) vs `A x вү  A y` (direct edge). Provides the `G.Preconnected` hypothesis needed for MLM application to the toy Hamiltonian | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/BipartiteGraph.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_toy_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **Matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4) for the toy Hamiltonian**: the shifted toy matrix `B_toy = c В· I вҲ’ M_toy` (under both-sublattices-nonempty + diagonal-shift hypothesis) has a unique-up-to-positive-scalar strictly positive eigenvector. Specialises Оұ-5o to the toy via Оұ-6a + Оұ-6b | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyPF.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOp1/2/3` | sublattice spin operators `Ең_A^(Оұ) := ОЈ_{x вҲҲ A} onSite x Ең^(Оұ)` for `Оұ вҲҲ {1, 2, 3}`. Foundation for the Casimir identity `ДӨ_toy = (1/(2|Оӣ|))((Ең_tot)ВІ вҲ’ (Ең_A)ВІ вҲ’ (Ең_B)ВІ)` (Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp1/2/3` / `totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_sublattice_sum` | **Spin-`S` analogues**: `Ең_A^(Оұ) := ОЈ_{x : A x} onSiteS x (spinSOp_Оұ N)` for `Оұ вҲҲ {1, 2, 3}`, with decomposition `Ең_tot^(Оұ) = Ең_A^(Оұ) + Ең_В¬A^(Оұ)`. First step toward Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.3 (Оі-4, `|A| вү  |B|` case) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1042) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | Spin-`S` sublattice operator Hermiticity (Оі-4 step 2). Sum of Hermitian summands is Hermitian | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1043) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_isHermitian` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir `(Ең_A)ВІ := ОЈ_Оұ (Ең_A^(Оұ))ВІ` plus Hermiticity. Foundation for the Casimir identity in Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.11) at general spin-S (Оі-4 step 3) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1044) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute` | spin-`S` cross-sublattice same-axis commutativity `Commute (Ең_A^(Оұ)) (Ең_В¬A^(Оұ))` for Оұ вҲҲ {1, 2, 3}. Sites in A vs В¬A are distinct, so per-site operators commute via `onSiteS_commute_of_ne` (Оі-4 step 4) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1045) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpGeneric_cross_commute` / `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute_op{1,2,3}` | spin-`S` mixed-axes cross-sublattice commutativity: `Commute (Ең_A^(Оұ)) (Ең_В¬A^(ОІ))` for any Оұ, ОІ вҲҲ {1, 2, 3}. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_cross_commute` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir cross-commute: `Commute (Ең_A)ВІ (Ең_В¬A)ВІ`. | `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_commutator_sublatticeSpinSOp{2,3,1}` | spin-`S` sublattice SU(2) algebra: `[Ең_A^Оұ, Ең_A^ОІ] = i Оө^ОұОІОі Ең_A^Оі`. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commutator_sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir self-invariance: `[(Ең_A)ВІ, Ең_A^(Оұ)] = 0`, equivalently `Commute (Ең_A)ВІ Ең_A^(Оұ)`. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_complement` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir vs complement axis-Оұ: `Commute (Ең_A)ВІ Ең_В¬A^(Оұ)` for Оұ вҲҲ {1, 2, 3}. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir vs total spin axis-Оұ: `Commute (Ең_A)ВІ Ең_tot^(Оұ)` for Оұ вҲҲ {1, 2, 3}. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_totalSpinSSquared` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir vs total Casimir: `Commute (Ең_A)ВІ (Ең_tot)ВІ`. | `sublatticeSpinSDot` / `sublatticeSpinSDot_def` / `sublatticeSpinSDot_eq_sum_sum` / `sublatticeSpinSDot_complement_isHermitian` | spin-`S` cross-sublattice spin dot product `Ең_A В· Ең_B := ОЈ_Оұ Ең_A^(Оұ) Ең_B^(Оұ)`, definitional unfolding, bilinear expansion `Ең_A В· Ең_B = ОЈ_{x : A x} ОЈ_{y : B y} Ең_x В· Ең_y`, and Hermiticity for `B = В¬A` (each axis-Оұ summand is a product of two commuting Hermitian operators) (Оі-4 step 13) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` (PR #1054) |
| `sublatticeSpinSDot_complement_comm` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eq_sublatticeSpinSDot_sum` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eq_two_sublatticeSpinSDot` | spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian decomposes as oriented cross-sublattice spin dot products: `ДӨ_toy_S = Ең_A В· Ең_В¬A + Ең_В¬A В· Ең_A`, and via cross-sublattice symmetry the closed form `ДӨ_toy_S = 2 вҖў Ең_A В· Ең_В¬A`. | `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSSquared` / `_commute_sublatticeSpinSquaredS` / `_complement` | spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian commutes with the three Casimirs `(Ең_tot)ВІ`, `(Ең_A)ВІ`, `(Ең_В¬A)ВІ`. | `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_jointCasimirEigenvector` / `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_via_joint` | **spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian eigenvalue on simultaneous Casimir eigenvectors**: for `v` a joint eigenvector of `(Ең_tot)ВІ`, `(Ең_A)ВІ`, `(Ең_В¬A)ВІ` at eigenvalues `Оұ, ОІ_A, ОІ_B`, `ДӨ_toy_S В· v = (Оұ вҲ’ ОІ_A вҲ’ ОІ_B) вҖў v` and `вҹЁv, ДӨ_toy_S vвҹ© = (Оұ вҲ’ ОІ_A вҲ’ ОІ_B) В· вҹЁv, vвҹ©`. | `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_jointCasimirEigenspace_le_eigenspace` | **subspace-level form of PR #2774**: the meet of the three Casimir eigenspaces at `(Оұ, ОІ_A, ОІ_B)` is contained in the `ДӨ_toy_S`-eigenspace at `(Оұ вҲ’ ОІ_A вҲ’ ОІ_B)`, i.e. `eigenspace(Ең_totВІ)_Оұ вҠ“ eigenspace(Ең_AВІ)_ОІ_A вҠ“ eigenspace(Ең_В¬AВІ)_ОІ_B вүӨ eigenspace(ДӨ_toy_S)_{ОұвҲ’ОІ_AвҲ’ОІ_B}`. | `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_eq_total_minus_sublattice_casimirs` | **NГ©el toy-Hamiltonian expectation via Casimir decomposition**: `вҹЁОҰ_NГ©el \| ДӨ_toy_S \| ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ© = вҹЁОҰ_NГ©el \| (Ең_tot)ВІ \| ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ© вҲ’ s_A(s_A+1) В· вҹЁОҰ_NГ©el,ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ© вҲ’ s_B(s_B+1) В· вҹЁОҰ_NГ©el,ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ©`, where `s_A = \|A\|В·N/2, s_B = \|В¬A\|В·N/2`. Bridges PR #2774's joint-Casimir framework to the existing direct expectation `вҲ’\|A\|В·\|В¬A\|В·NВІ/2` (PR #1070); useful for subsequent Оі-4 variational comparisons against the predicted toy-Hamiltonian ground state (PR #2776, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelToyHamiltonianViaCasimir.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_apply_diag_neel_complement` / `neelStateOfS_complement_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation` | **complement-orientation NГ©el toy-Hamiltonian expectation**: `вҹЁОҰ_NГ©el(В¬A) \| ДӨ_toy_S(A) \| ОҰ_NГ©el(В¬A)вҹ© = вҲ’\|A\|В·\|В¬A\|В·NВІ/2`. Same value as the original-orientation expectation (PR #1093): the toy Hamiltonian is sublattice-swap invariant (via `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eq_two_sublatticeSpinSDot` + `sublatticeSpinSDot_complement_comm`), and the complement NГ©el basis configuration produces the same per-bond `m_x В· m_y = -NВІ/4` contribution. Completes the orientation-pair (`ОҰ_NГ©el(A)` for `\|A\| вүҘ \|В¬A\|`, `ОҰ_NГ©el(В¬A)` for `\|В¬A\| вүҘ \|A\|`) at the same toy-Hamiltonian value (PR #2777, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelToyComplementExpectation.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted` / `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_eq_simplified` / `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted` / `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_le_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eigenspace` | **predicted toy-Hamiltonian ground state for the bipartite \|A\| вүҘ \|В¬A\| case** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.3): definition `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N := (s_A вҲ’ s_B)(s_A вҲ’ s_B + 1) вҲ’ s_A(s_A+1) вҲ’ s_B(s_B+1)` with `s_A = \|A\|В·N/2, s_B = \|В¬A\|В·N/2`; simplification to `вҲ’\|A\|В·\|В¬A\|В·NВІ/2 вҲ’ \|В¬A\|В·N` via ring; definition `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` as the joint eigenspace at the three target eigenvalues (sublattice Casimirs at maximum, total Casimir at the triangle-inequality minimum); subspace inclusion `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted вүӨ eigenspace(ДӨ_toy_S)_{bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted}` via PR #2775's joint-Casimir-eigenspace inclusion. The minimality of `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted` as the actual `ДӨ_toy_S` ground-state eigenvalue (variational lower bound + sector non-emptiness) is the next Оі-4 milestone (PR #2778, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergy.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_eq_zero_of_cardNotA_zero` / `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_eq_balanced_form_of_card_eq` | **structural properties of `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted`**: (i) saturated edge case `\|В¬A\| = 0` вҶ’ energy 0 (trivial empty-cross-bond limit); (ii) balanced edge case `\|A\| = \|В¬A\|` вҶ’ energy collapses to `вҲ’2 s_A(s_A+1)`, the anti-ferromagnetic-saturation value (PR #2779, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyProperties.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_sub_complement` | **complement-orientation gap identity**: `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N вҲ’ bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted (В¬A) N = (\|A\| вҲ’ \|В¬A\|)В·N`. Quantifies the orientation-asymmetry of the signed-difference formula in PR #2778: the true Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction is `min(E(A), E(В¬A))`, achieved at the orientation `\|A\| вүҘ \|В¬A\|` (or the complement orientation). Direct ring computation from PR #2779's simplified form via `(В¬В¬A) = A` at the filter level (PR #2780, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm` / `_eq_predicted_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` / `_eq_complement_predicted_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` / `_complement` | **sublattice-swap symmetric predicted min energy**: `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm A N := вҲ’\|A\|В·\|В¬A\|В·NВІ/2 вҲ’ min(\|A\|, \|В¬A\|)В·N`, the true Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction independent of orientation. Bridges: equals `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N` when `\|В¬A\| вүӨ \|A\|`, equals `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted (В¬A) N` when `\|A\| вүӨ \|В¬A\|`. Sublattice-swap invariant: `Symm (В¬A) N = Symm A N` via `Nat.min_comm` + `(В¬В¬A) = A` (PR #2781, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergySymm.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_zero_mem_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_of_cardNotA_zero` | **non-emptiness of the predicted GS subspace in the saturated edge case**: for `\|В¬A\| = 0`, the all-up state `|Пғ_вҠӨвҹ©` is in `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N`. This is the first concrete realisation of PR #2778's predicted ground-state subspace. Three eigenvector witnesses: `(Ең_tot)ВІ В· \|Пғ_вҠӨвҹ© = s_A(s_A+1) В· \|Пғ_вҠӨвҹ©` (PR #878 with `\|V\| = \|A\|`), `(Ең_A)ВІ В· \|Пғ_вҠӨвҹ© = s_A(s_A+1) В· \|Пғ_вҠӨвҹ©` (PR #1067), `(Ең_В¬A)ВІ В· \|Пғ_вҠӨвҹ© = 0 В· \|Пғ_вҠӨвҹ©` (PR #1067 with `\|В¬A\| = 0` collapsing the eigenvalue formula) (PR #2782, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGroundStateSaturated.lean`) |
| `allAlignedStateS_last_mem_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_of_cardNotA_zero` | **all-down version of PR #2782**: for `\|В¬A\| = 0`, the all-down state `\|Пғ_вҠҘвҹ©` is also in `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N`. Mirror of PR #2782 using `_last` variants (`totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last_eigenvalue` PR #879, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` PR #1067-mirror). Together with PR #2782, gives two distinct elements of the predicted GS subspace (for `N вүҘ 1`, where `\|Пғ_вҠӨвҹ© вү  \|Пғ_вҠҘвҹ©`; for `N = 0` the spin-`0` case collapses to a 1-dim space) вҖ” and via the saturated-ferromagnet ladder, the entire (2 s_A + 1)-dim irrep is contained for any `N` (PR #2783, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGroundStateSaturatedLast.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_zero_of_card_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eq_zero_of_card_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_eq_totalSpinSOp{1,2,3}_of_cardNotA_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eq_totalSpinSSquared_of_cardNotA_zero` | **sublattice вҶ” total spin identities at the saturated edge case**: when `\|В¬A\| = 0`, the complement sublattice `В¬A` is empty, so `Ең_В¬A^(Оұ) = 0` and `Ең_В¬AВІ = 0`, and the `A`-sublattice operators coincide with the total operators: `Ең_A^(Оұ) = Ең_tot^(Оұ)` for `Оұ вҲҲ {1,2,3}` and `Ең_AВІ = Ең_totВІ`. Foundation for the dim-bound on the predicted GS subspace in the saturated case: `eigenspace(Ең_totВІ)_Оұ вҠҶ bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` whenever `\|В¬A\| = 0` and `Оұ = s_A(s_A+1)` (PR #2784, file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeEqTotalOfCardZero.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquaredEigenspace_le_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_of_cardNotA_zero` | **eigenspace bridge for the saturated case**: when `\|В¬A\| = 0`, `eigenspace((Ең_tot)ВІ)_{s_A(s_A+1)} вҠҶ bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N`. Proof: any `(Ең_tot)ВІ`-eigenvector at `s_A(s_A+1)` automatically satisfies all three joint Casimir eigenspace conditions, since (i) `(Ең_tot)ВІ` eigenvalue matches the predicted target `(s_AвҲ’0)(s_AвҲ’0+1)`, (ii) `Ең_AВІ = Ең_totВІ` (PR #2784) gives the `(Ең_A)ВІ` condition for free, (iii) `Ең_В¬AВІ = 0` trivially. Combined with PR #2768's `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace = span(ladderIterateUp)` and finrank `2 m_max + 1`, this yields the dimension lower bound `finrank(predicted GS) вүҘ 2 m_max + 1 = 2 s_A + 1` at the saturated edge case, matching the Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.3 prediction `2 S_tot + 1` (PR #2785, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGSEigenspaceBridge.lean`) |
| `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace_le_bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_of_cardNotA_zero` | **В§2.4 вҶ” Оі-4 bridge at saturated case**: when `\|В¬A\| = 0`, `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace J N вҠҶ bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N` for any choice of coupling `J`. Direct corollary of PR #2785's eigenspace bridge applied to the `(Ең_tot)ВІ`-eigenspace component of the saturated FM joint eigenspace, with the identification `m_max = s_A` (forced by `\|V\| = \|A\|`). With PR #2768 (joint = span ladder) and PR #2769 (finrank = 2 m_max + 1), this gives the dimension lower bound `finrank(predicted GS subspace) вүҘ 2 m_max + 1 = 2 s_A + 1` (PR #2786, file `Quantum/SpinS/SaturatedFMJointLePredictedGS.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_finrank_ge_succ_card_mul_N_of_cardNotA_zero` | **predicted GS subspace dim вүҘ 2 m_max + 1 at saturated case**: when `\|В¬A\| = 0`, `\|V\|В·N + 1 вүӨ finrank(bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted A N)`. Direct corollary of PR #2786's bridge + PR #2769's `saturatedFerromagnetJointEigenspace_finrank_eq` via `Submodule.finrank_mono`. Realises the Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.3 degeneracy `2 S_tot + 1` lower bound at the saturated edge case, where `S_tot = m_max = \|V\|В·S` (PR #2787, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGSFinrankSaturated.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_complement_finrank_ge_succ_card_mul_N_of_cardA_zero` | **opposite saturated case**: when `\|A\| = 0`, the **complement-orientation** predicted GS subspace `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted (В¬A) N` has `finrank вүҘ \|V\|В·N + 1`. Application of PR #2787 to the flipped sublattice, reducing `\|В¬В¬A\| = 0` to the original `\|A\| = 0` via filter congruence. Note: with `\|A\| = 0`, the original-orientation predicted GS subspace would target a different (Ең_tot)ВІ eigenvalue (signed-difference goes negative); the complement orientation gives the natural choice (PR #2788, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyGSFinrankOppositeSaturated.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_eigenspace_at_predicted_min_finrank_ge_succ_card_mul_N_of_cardNotA_zero` | **ДӨ_toy_S eigenspace finrank lower bound at saturated case**: when `\|В¬A\| = 0`, the `ДӨ_toy_S`-eigenspace at `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N` has finrank `вүҘ \|V\|В·N + 1`. Direct chain of PR #2775 (predicted GS вҠҶ ДӨ_toy_S eigenspace) and PR #2787 (predicted GS finrank lower bound) via `Submodule.finrank_mono`. Operator-level version of the Theorem 2.3 degeneracy `2 S_tot + 1` lower bound, applied directly to the `ДӨ_toy_S`-eigenspace (PR #2789, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyEigenspaceFinrankSaturated.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_complement_eigenspace_at_predicted_min_finrank_ge_succ_card_mul_N_of_cardA_zero` | **opposite saturated case ДӨ_toy_S(В¬A) eigenspace finrank lower bound**: when `\|A\| = 0`, the `ДӨ_toy_S(В¬A)`-eigenspace at `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted (В¬A) N` has finrank `вүҘ \|V\|В·N + 1`. Mirror of PR #2789 via PR #2788; since the toy Hamiltonian is sublattice-swap invariant (`ДӨ_toy_S(A) = ДӨ_toy_S(В¬A)`), this also applies to the original-orientation toy Hamiltonian (PR #2790, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyEigenspaceFinrankOppositeSaturated.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_nonpos` | **predicted toy-H min energy is non-positive (real part)**: `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N).re вүӨ 0` for any bipartite sublattice `A` and any `N`. Direct from PR #2779's simplified form `-\|A\|В·\|В¬A\|В·NВІ/2 - \|В¬A\|В·N`, both terms non-positive. Identifies z-axis Casimir with predicted S_tot squared; transverse Casimir expectation `(Ең_tot^(1))ВІ + (Ең_tot^(2))ВІ = \|Оӣ\|В·N/2` (bipartition-independent) by subtraction (PR #2904, file `Quantum/SpinS/NeelStateOfSTotalSpinSOp3SqReViaImbalanceNormSq.lean`) |

Real-part form of Оі-4 step 156 (spin-S). | `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp1_expectation_re_eq_zero` / `_Op2_` | **Linear transverse NГ©el expectations vanish**: `(<ОҰ_NГ©el\|Ең_tot^(1)\|ОҰ_NГ©el>).re = 0` and `(<ОҰ_NГ©el\|Ең_tot^(2)\|ОҰ_NГ©el>).re = 0`. Direct from Оі-4 step 241 (complex form `= 0`) + `Complex.zero_re`. Companion of the existing `_sub_complement` (gap identity). Together they give the affine sandwich: `predicted_min A = вҲ’\|A\|В·\|В¬A\|В·NВІ/2 вҲ’ \|В¬A\|В·N` and `predicted_min (В¬A) = вҲ’\|A\|В·\|В¬A\|В·NВІ/2 вҲ’ \|A\|В·N` (PR #2997, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAddComplement.lean`) |

Direct corollary of Hermiticity plus realness of matrix entries: for a Hermitian complex matrix with real entries, `conjTranspose = transpose` (Оі-4 step 42) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/Heisenberg.lean`, `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` (PR #1084) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus` / `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus` / `_eq_add` / `_eq_sub` | spin-`S` sublattice raising / lowering ladder operators `Ең_A^Вұ := ОЈ_{x : A x} onSiteS x (spinSOpPlus/Minus N)` with definitional unfoldings `Ең_A^Вұ = Ең_A^(1) Вұ i Ең_A^(2)`. Foundation for sublattice-level SU(2) representation theory analysis; mirror of `totalSpinSOpPlus/Minus` restricted to `A` (Оі-4 step 43) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1085) |
| `totalSpinSOpPlus_eq_sublattice_sum` / `totalSpinSOpMinus_eq_sublattice_sum` | spin-`S` total raising/lowering operators decompose across the bipartition: `Ең^Вұ_tot = Ең_A^Вұ + Ең_В¬A^Вұ`. Mirror of axis decompositions (Оі-4 step 44) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1086) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | spin-`S` sublattice ladder operators annihilate the appropriate extremal all-aligned state: `Ең_A^+ В· \|Пғ_вҠӨвҹ© = 0` and `Ең_A^- В· \|Пғ_вҠҘвҹ© = 0`. Direct from on-site annihilations summed over `A` (Оі-4 step 45) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1087) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp3_commutator_sublatticeSpinSOpPlus` / `_OpMinus` | spin-`S` sublattice Cartan relations `[Ең_A^(3), Ең_A^Вұ] = ВұЕң_A^Вұ`. Derived from the sublattice SU(2) algebra (PR #1048) and `Ең_A^Вұ = Ең_A^(1) Вұ i Ең_A^(2)`. Mirror of `totalSpinSOp3_commutator_totalSpinSOpPlus/Minus` for sublattices (Оі-4 step 46) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1088) |
| `totalSpinSOp3_commutator_sublatticeSpinSOpPlus` / `_OpMinus` | total Cartan relation for sublattice ladders: `[Ең_tot^(3), Ең_A^Вұ] = ВұЕң_A^Вұ`. Combines sublattice Cartan (PR #1088) with cross-sublattice commutativity (PR #1046): `[Ең_В¬A^(3), Ең_A^Вұ] = 0`. Consequence: `Ең_A^Вұ` shifts the total magnetisation by Вұ1 (Оі-4 step 47) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1089) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem` / `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem` | spin-`S` sublattice ladder operators shift `magSubspaceS` by Вұ1: `Ең_A^Вұ : magSubspaceS Оӣ N M вҶ’ magSubspaceS Оӣ N (M Вұ 1)`. Direct corollary of total Cartan relation (PR #1089) (Оі-4 step 48) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1090) |
| `sublatticeSpinSDot_self_eq_sublatticeSpinSquaredS` | `sublatticeSpinSDot N A A = sublatticeSpinSquaredS N A`. Definitional identity unifying the cross-sublattice dot product API (`Ең_A В· Ең_B`) with the sublattice Casimir `(Ең_A)ВІ` for the diagonal `B = A` case (Оі-4 step 49) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` (PR #1092) |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOpPlus` / `sublatticeSpinHalfOpMinus` / `_eq_add` / `_eq_sub` / `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_eq_sublattice_sum` / `_OpMinus_eq_sublattice_sum` | spin-`1/2` sublattice ladder operators (mirror back of Оі-4 steps 43, 44): `Ең_A^Вұ := ОЈ_{x : A x} onSite x spinHalfOpВұ`, with `Ең_A^Вұ = Ең_A^(1) Вұ i Ең_A^(2)` and `Ең^Вұ_tot = Ең_A^Вұ + Ең_В¬A^Вұ`. Brings spin-`1/2` to parity with spin-`S` (Оі-4 step 50) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1093) |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOp3_commutator_sublatticeSpinHalfOpPlus` / `_OpMinus` / `totalSpinHalfOp3_commutator_sublatticeSpinHalfOpPlus` / `_OpMinus` | spin-`1/2` sublattice + total Cartan relations: `[Ең_A^(3), Ең_A^Вұ] = ВұЕң_A^Вұ` and `[Ең_tot^(3), Ең_A^Вұ] = ВұЕң_A^Вұ`. Mirror back of Оі-4 steps 46/47 (Оі-4 step 51) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1094) |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` / `sublatticeSpinHalfOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | spin-`1/2` sublattice ladder operators shift `magnetizationSubspace` by Вұ1: `Ең_A^Вұ : H_M вҶ’ H_(M Вұ 1)`. Direct corollary of total Cartan (PR #1094). Mirror back of Оі-4 step 48 (Оі-4 step 52) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1095) |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOpPlus_mulVec_basisVec_zero` / `sublatticeSpinHalfOpMinus_mulVec_basisVec_one` | spin-`1/2` sublattice ladder operators annihilate extremal all-up / all-down basis state: `Ең_A^+ В· \|0...0вҹ© = 0` and `Ең_A^- В· \|1...1вҹ© = 0`. Direct from on-site annihilation summed over A. Mirror back of Оі-4 step 45 (Оі-4 step 53) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1096) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_conjTranspose` / `_OpMinus_conjTranspose` (spin-`S`) and `sublatticeSpinHalfOpPlus_conjTranspose` / `_OpMinus_conjTranspose` (spin-`1/2`) | sublattice ladder operators are Hermitian conjugates: `(Ең_A^+)вҖ  = Ең_A^-` and `(Ең_A^-)вҖ  = Ең_A^+`. Direct from `spinSOpPlus_conjTranspose = spinSOpMinus` summed over A. Mirror of `totalSpinSOpPlus_conjTranspose` for sublattices (Оі-4 step 54). Also unprivates `onSite_conjTranspose` and `onSiteS_conjTranspose` helpers | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpinLadder.lean`, `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1098) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_const_false` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_const_false` / `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_const_false` / `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_const_false` (and spin-`1/2` mirrors) | sublattice operators vanish on the empty sublattice (`A = const false`). Useful for boundary cases / degenerate bipartitions (Оі-4 step 55) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean`, `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1099) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_const_true` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_const_true` / `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_const_true` / `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_const_true` (and spin-`1/2` mirrors) | sublattice operators on full sublattice (`A = const true`) equal total operators: `Ең_(true) = Ең_tot`. Useful for boundary cases (Оі-4 step 56) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean`, `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1100) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_apply_im_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_apply_im_zero` | spin-`S` sublattice ladder operators have real matrix elements: `((Ең_A^Вұ) Пғ' Пғ).im = 0`. Direct from on-site realness summed over A. Useful for downstream realness arguments (Оі-4 step 57) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpinLadder.lean` (PR #1101) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_add_sublatticeSpinSOpMinus` / `_sub_*` (and spin-`1/2` mirrors) | reverse identities: `Ең_A^+ + Ең_A^- = 2 вҖў Ең_A^(1)` and `Ең_A^+ - Ең_A^- = 2i вҖў Ең_A^(2)`. Inverts the `_eq_add` / `_eq_sub` decompositions for converting between Cartesian and ladder presentations (Оі-4 step 58) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean`, `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1102) |
| `sublatticeSpinDot_self_eq_sublatticeSpinHalfSquared` | spin-`1/2` definitional identity: `sublatticeSpinDot A A = sublatticeSpinHalfSquared A`. Mirror back of Оі-4 step 49 (Оі-4 step 59) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` (PR #1105) |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOpPlus_apply_im_eq_zero` / `sublatticeSpinHalfOpMinus_apply_im_eq_zero` (and on-site / single-site lemmas) | spin-`1/2` sublattice ladder operators have real matrix elements. | `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_sq_eq_conjTranspose_mul` (and spin-`1/2` mirrors) | sublattice axis operators squared equal `(Ең_A^(Оұ))бҙҙ * Ең_A^(Оұ)`. Direct from Hermiticity. The operator identity underlying PSD: `Aбҙҙ A` is PSD for any A. Useful for setting up positivity arguments without `Matrix.PosSemidef` (Оі-4 step 61) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpin.lean`, `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1107) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp1_apply_im_zero` / `sublatticeSpinSOp3_apply_im_zero` | spin-`S` sublattice axis-1 and axis-3 operators have real matrix elements. Direct from `onSiteS_spinSOp{1,3}_apply_im_zero` summed over A (Оі-4 step 62) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeSpinLadder.lean` (PR #1108) |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOp1_apply_im_eq_zero` / `sublatticeSpinHalfOp3_apply_im_eq_zero` (and on-site / single-site) | spin-`1/2` sublattice axis-1 / axis-3 operators have real matrix elements. Mirror back of Оі-4 step 62 (Оі-4 step 63) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpin.lean` (PR #1109) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_basisVecS_zero_on` / `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_basisVecS_last_on` | spin-`S` sublattice ladder operators annihilate basis states with extreme A-values: `Ең_A^+ В· \|Пғвҹ© = 0` if Пғ\|_A = 0; `Ең_A^- В· \|Пғвҹ© = 0` if Пғ\|_A = Fin.last N. | `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_neelStateOfS` / `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_complement_mulVec_neelStateOfS` | spin-`S` NГ©el state annihilated by sublattice ladders: `Ең_A^+ В· \|ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ© = 0` (highest weight on A) and `Ең_В¬A^- В· \|ОҰ_NГ©elвҹ© = 0` (lowest weight on В¬A). Lifts the diagonal matrix element to the state expectation via `basisVecS_expectation_eq_diagonal` (spin-S) and `basisVec_expectation_eq_diagonal` (spin-1/2) (Оі-4 step 157) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` and `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelHeisenberg.lean` (spin-`1/2` moved in refactor #42, PR #2900) (PR #1204) |
| `neelStateOfS_expectation_spinSDot_of_same` / `neelStateOf_expectation_spinHalfDot_of_same` | per-pair FM same-sublattice NГ©el correlation: `<ОҰ_NГ©el \| Ең_x В· Ең_y \| ОҰ_NГ©el> = +NВІ/4` (spin-S) and `+1/4` (spin-1/2) for any same-sublattice pair `x вү  y` with `A x = A y`. Positive sign reflects parallel alignment within each sublattice. Companion to Оі-4 step 157; relies on the parallel-diagonal lemmas `spinSDot_apply_diag_neelConfigOfS_of_same` and `spinHalfDot_apply_diag_neelConfigOf_of_same` (the latter using a new `spinHalfDot_apply_diag_of_ne_parallel`) (Оі-4 step 158) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` and `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelHeisenberg.lean` (spin-`1/2` moved in refactor #42, PR #2900) (PR #1205) |
| `neelStateOfS_expectation_spinSDot_self` / `neelStateOf_expectation_spinHalfDot_self` | same-site (diagonal) per-pair NГ©el correlation: `<ОҰ_NГ©el \| Ең_x В· Ең_x \| ОҰ_NГ©el> = N(N+2)/4` (spin-S) and `3/4` (spin-1/2). Universal local Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)`, completing the cross/same/self trio of per-pair correlations. Spin-S applies `spinSDot_self_expectation_normalized` to `neelStateOfS_inner_self`; spin-1/2 uses `spinHalfDot_self` directly (Оі-4 step 159) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeelHeisenbergExpectation.lean` and `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelHeisenberg.lean` (spin-`1/2` moved in refactor #42, PR #2900) (PR #1206) |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_apply_diag_neel` / `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_diag_neel` and `neelStateOfS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_expectation` / `neelStateOf_heisenbergHamiltonian_expectation` | **Synthesis of the per-pair trio**: for arbitrary coupling `J`, the Heisenberg Hamiltonian's diagonal/state-expectation on the NГ©el state is `ОЈ_{x,y} J(x,y) В· v(x,y)` where `v(x,y) = N(N+2)/4` if `x = y` (3/4 spin-1/2), `+NВІ/4` (+1/4) if `x вү  y, A x = A y`, and `-NВІ/4` (-1/4) if `x вү  y, A x вү  A y`. For the bipartite AFM (J on cross-bonds only), the same/self contributions vanish, recovering the toy Hamiltonian NГ©el expectation (Оі-4 step 160) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` and `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelHeisenberg.lean` (spin-`1/2` moved in refactor #42, PR #2900) (PR #1207) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_expectation_re_pos` / `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_expectation_re_pos` | **Strict positivity in в„қ** of transverse Casimir on NГ©el: `0 < Re <ОҰ_NГ©el \| (Ең_tot^(1))ВІ + (Ең_tot^(2))ВІ \| ОҰ_NГ©el>` when `Оӣ` non-empty (and `0 < N` for spin-S). Combined with `<(Ең_tot^(3))ВІ>_NГ©el = MВІ` (Оі-4 step 155) yields the strict spread `<(Ең_tot)ВІ>_NГ©el > MВІ` вҖ” the NГ©el state is not concentrated in a single `S_tot` sector, foundational input for Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.3 (Оі-4 step 161) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` and `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelHeisenberg.lean` (spin-`1/2` moved in refactor #42, PR #2900) (PR #1208) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_re_gt_OpZ_sq` / `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_re_gt_OpZ_sq` | **Strict spread in в„қ** of total Casimir on NГ©el: `Re <ОҰ_NГ©el \| (Ең_tot^(3))ВІ \| ОҰ_NГ©el> < Re <ОҰ_NГ©el \| (Ең_tot)ВІ \| ОҰ_NГ©el>` when `Оӣ` non-empty (and `0 < N` for spin-S). | `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` `(Ең_tot^(3))ВІ` is sublattice-swap invariant on NГ©el**: `<ОҰ_NГ©el(В¬A)\|(Ең_tot^(3))ВІ\|ОҰ_NГ©el(В¬A)> = <ОҰ_NГ©el(A)\|(Ең_tot^(3))ВІ\|ОҰ_NГ©el(A)>`. Direct from Оі-4 step 204 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of Оі-4 step 235 (Оі-4 step 236) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1283) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(Ең^1)ВІ+(Ең^2)ВІ` is sublattice-swap invariant on NГ©el**: equal expectations on `ОҰ_NГ©el(A)` and `ОҰ_NГ©el(В¬A)`. Both reduce to `\|V\|В·N/2` via Оі-4 step 220 + 221 (Оі-4 step 237) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1284) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir invariance on NГ©el**: both expectations equal `\|Оӣ\|/2`. Direct from Оі-4 step 219 (Пғ-independent constant). Spin-`1/2` mirror of Оі-4 step 237 (Оі-4 step 238) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1285) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`S` linear `Ең_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on NГ©el**: `<ОҰ_NГ©el(В¬A)\|Ең_tot^(3)\|ОҰ_NГ©el(В¬A)> = вҲ’<ОҰ_NГ©el(A)\|Ең_tot^(3)\|ОҰ_NГ©el(A)>`. Direct from Оі-4 step 206 + 231 (Оі-4 step 239) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1286) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` linear `Ең_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on NГ©el**. Direct from Оі-4 step 207 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of Оі-4 step 239 (Оі-4 step 240) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1287) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`S` NГ©el transverse linear expectations vanish**: `<ОҰ_NГ©el\|Ең_tot^(1,2)\|ОҰ_NГ©el> = 0`. Trivial corollary of Оі-4 step 214 applied to `Пғ = neelConfigOfS A N` (Оі-4 step 241). Together with Оі-4 step 91 (`<Ең_tot^(3)> = magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS`), gives the complete per-axis profile `(0, 0, (\|A\|вҲ’\|В¬A\|)В·N/2)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1288) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`1/2` NГ©el transverse linear expectations vanish**: same as Оі-4 step 241 for spin-`1/2`, derived from Оі-4 step 215 (Оі-4 step 242) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1289) |

The single-cluster Problem 2.5.a modules below are imported from the
build root again after the 2026-05-30 orphan-module sweep had removed
the earlier implementation.  The restored concrete tip is
`Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean`, and the
minimum-eigenvalue bridge in `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianMin.lean`
keeps the abstract Hamiltonian, energy hygiene, concrete
dimer/trimer/quartet/pentamer eigenvalue formulas, and variational
upper/lower-bound consumers live in the default Lean build
(PR #4032; PR #4033; PR #4034; PR #4035; PR #4036).

| `singleClusterHamiltonianS` | **Single-cluster (star-graph) Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a, p. 38): `H = ОЈ_{j=1}^z Ең_0 В· Ең_j` on `Fin (z + 1)` with central vertex `0` and `z` leaves. Ground-state energy `вҲ’S(1 + zS)` (Оі-5 step 243) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1290) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` | **Hermiticity** of the single-cluster Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Sum of Hermitian `spinSDot 0 j N` over `j вҲҲ univ.erase 0` (Оі-5 step 244) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1291) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_zero_z` | **Edge case `z = 0`**: `singleClusterHamiltonianS 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = вҲ…` in `Fin 1`. Tasaki's formula `вҲ’S(1 + zS)` is intended for `z вүҘ 1` (Оі-5 step 245) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1292) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<ОҰ_вҠӨ\|H\|ОҰ_вҠӨ> = zВ·(N/2)ВІ` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian. Far above Tasaki's GS energy `вҲ’S(1+zS)` since all-up is in the maximum-`s_tot` Casimir sector, not the minimum (Оі-5 step 246) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1293) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allAligned_expectation` | **AllAligned-c expectation**: `<ОҰ_aligned(c)\|H\|ОҰ_aligned(c)> = zВ·(N/2 вҲ’ c.val)ВІ`. Generalises Оі-5 step 246 (the `c = 0` case). For `c = Fin.last N` (all-down) gives same `zВ·(N/2)ВІ` (Оі-5 step 247) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1294) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allDown_expectation` | **All-down expectation**: `<ОҰ_вҠҘ\|H\|ОҰ_вҠҘ> = zВ·(N/2)ВІ`. Direct specialisation of Оі-5 step 247 at `c = Fin.last N` (Оі-5 step 248) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1295) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Leaf-spin operators on `Fin (z+1)`**: `Ең_R^(Оұ) = ОЈ_{j вү  0} onSiteS j Ең^(Оұ)` for axes `Оұ = 1, 2, 3`. Sum over leaves of single-site `Ең^(Оұ)` (excludes central vertex `0`). Building blocks for the upcoming Casimir decomposition `H = (Ең_totВІ вҲ’ Ең_0ВІ вҲ’ Ең_RВІ)/2` (Оі-5 step 249) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1296) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eq_dot_leaves` | **`Ең_0 В· Ең_R` decomposition**: `H = ОЈ_Оұ onSiteS 0 (Ең^(Оұ)) В· leafSpinSOp_Оұ`. Direct distribution of left multiplication over the sum `ОЈ_j (A * B_j) = A * (ОЈ_j B_j)` via `Finset.mul_sum` (Оі-5 step 250) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1297) |
| `totalSpinSOp1/2/3_eq_onSite_zero_add_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Total = central + leaves** (per axis): `totalSpinSOp_Оұ (Fin (z+1)) N = onSiteS 0 (Ең^(Оұ)) + leafSpinSOp_Оұ z N`. Direct from `Finset.sum_erase_add` + `add_comm` (Оі-5 step 251) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1298) |
| `leafSpinSSquared` | **Leaf-spin Casimir**: `Ең_RВІ := (Ең_R^(1))ВІ + (Ең_R^(2))ВІ + (Ең_R^(3))ВІ`, total-spin-squared restricted to leaves of `Fin (z+1)` (Оі-5 step 252) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1299) |
| `onSiteS_zero_commute_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Center-leaf commutativity**: `Commute (onSiteS 0 Ең^(Оұ)) (leafSpinSOp_Оұ z N)`. Direct from `Commute.sum_right` + `onSiteS_commute_of_ne` (disjoint sites) (Оі-5 step 253) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1300) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir decomposition**: `2В·H = (Ең_tot)ВІ вҲ’ Ең_0ВІ вҲ’ Ең_RВІ` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian, where `Ең_0ВІ = spinSDot 0 0 N` and `Ең_RВІ = leafSpinSSquared z N`. Proof: expand `ОЈ_Оұ totalSpinSOp_ОұВІ = ОЈ_Оұ (onSite 0 + leaf_Оұ)ВІ` via Оі-5 step 251 + `(a+b)ВІ = aВІ + 2ab + bВІ` (using Оі-5 step 253 commutativity) + Оі-5 step 250 (cross term identification) + `noncomm_ring` (Оі-5 step 254) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1301) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_smul_eq_casimir_diff` | **в„Ӯ-smul Casimir form**: `(2 : в„Ӯ) вҖў H = (Ең_tot)ВІ вҲ’ Ең_0ВІ вҲ’ Ең_RВІ`. Direct corollary of Оі-5 step 254 via `two_mul + two_smul` (Оі-5 step 255) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1302) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation` | **Casimir decomposition expectation form**: `2 В· <v\|H\|v> = <v\|StotВІ\|v> вҲ’ <v\|S0ВІ\|v> вҲ’ <v\|SRВІ\|v>` for any `v`. Direct corollary of Оі-5 step 255 + linearity of `dotProduct + mulVec` over `smul` and subtraction (Оі-5 step 256) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1303) |
| `spinSDot_self_expectation_general` | **Single-site Casimir expectation**: `<v\|spinSDot x x N\|v> = (N(N+2)/4) В· <v\|v>` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix) + linearity. Used to evaluate the `S0ВІ` term in the Casimir decomposition (Оі-5 step 257) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1304) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation_simplified` | **Simplified Casimir expectation**: `2 В· <v\|H\|v> = <v\|StotВІ\|v> вҲ’ (N(N+2)/4)В·<v\|v> вҲ’ <v\|SRВІ\|v>`. Combines Оі-5 step 256 + 257; the `S0ВІ` term is now an explicit scalar multiple of the norm-squared (Оі-5 step 258) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1305) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_of_joint_casimir_eigenvec` | **H eigenvalue from joint Casimir eigenvalues**: if `StotВІВ·v = ОұВ·v` and `SRВІВ·v = ОІВ·v`, then `HВ·v = (Оұ вҲ’ N(N+2)/4 вҲ’ ОІ)/2 В· v`. Reduces the GS-energy problem to enumerating admissible `(Оұ, ОІ)` joint eigenvalues (Оі-5 step 259) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1306) |
| `spinSDot_self_mulVec_eq_smul` | **Single-site Casimir as scalar action**: `spinSDot x x N В· v = (N(N+2)/4) вҖў v` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix). Standalone helper extracted from Оі-5 step 259 (Оі-5 step 260) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1307) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3_zero_z` and `leafSpinSSquared_zero_z` | **Edge case `z=0`** (single vertex, no leaves): `leafSpinSOp_Оұ 0 N = 0` and `leafSpinSSquared 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = вҲ…` in `Fin 1` (Оі-5 step 261) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1308) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` | **`leafSpinSSquared` as double sum**: `leafSpinSSquared z N = ОЈ_{j,k вҲҲ univ.erase 0} spinSDot j k N`. Mirrors `totalSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` for the leaf-only Casimir (Оі-5 step 262) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1309) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<ОҰ_вҠӨ\|leafSpinSSquared z N\|ОҰ_вҠӨ> = (zN/2)В·(zN/2 + 1) = s_R(s_R+1)` where `s_R = zВ·(N/2)` is max leaf spin. Computed by rearranging Оі-5 step 254 (Casimir decomposition) and applying closed forms for `<StotВІ>`, `<S0ВІ>`, `<H>` (Оі-5 step 263) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1310) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **Eigenvector form on allUp**: `H В· \|ОҰ_вҠӨвҹ© = zВ·(N/2)ВІ В· \|ОҰ_вҠӨвҹ©`. Proof: each leaf-pair `spinSDot 0 j` at `j вү  0` gives `(NВІ/4)В·\|ОҰ_вҠӨвҹ©` (via `spinSDot_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_of_ne`); sum over `z` leaves (Оі-5 step 264) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1311) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **Eigenvector form** of `Ең_RВІ` on allUp: `Ең_RВІ В· \|ОҰ_вҠӨвҹ© = (zN/2)В·(zN/2+1) В· \|ОҰ_вҠӨвҹ©`. Witnesses that `\|ОҰ_вҠӨвҹ©` is in the maximum-leaf-spin Casimir sector `s_R = zN/2`. Combined with Оі-5 step 264 + existing `StotВІ` eigenvector identity, confirms `\|ОҰ_вҠӨвҹ©` is a joint eigenstate of `H, StotВІ, Ең_0ВІ, Ең_RВІ`. Proof: rearrange Оі-5 step 255 + apply (Оі-5 step 265) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1312) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | **Eigenvector form on allDown**: `H В· \|ОҰ_вҠҘвҹ© = zВ·(N/2)ВІ В· \|ОҰ_вҠҘвҹ©`. AllDown mirror of Оі-5 step 264; same eigenvalue (spin-flip symmetry) (Оі-5 step 266) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1313) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_last` | **Eigenvector form** of `Ең_RВІ` on allDown: `Ең_RВІ В· \|ОҰ_вҠҘвҹ© = (zN/2)В·(zN/2+1) В· \|ОҰ_вҠҘвҹ©`. AllDown mirror of Оі-5 step 265; both `\|ОҰ_вҠӨвҹ©` and `\|ОҰ_вҠҘвҹ©` lie in the maximum-leaf-spin Casimir sector `s_R = zN/2`, differing only by total `Ең_tot^(3)` magnetization (Оі-5 step 267) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1314) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_at_gs_casimir_sector` | **GS-sector eigenvalue specialization**: if `v` is a joint eigenvector of `Ең_totВІ` at `((zвҲ’1)N/2)((zвҲ’1)N/2+1)` and `Ең_RВІ` at `(zN/2)(zN/2+1)`, then `H В· v = вҲ’(N/2)В·(zN/2+1) В· v = вҲ’S(1+zS) В· v`. Specialization of Оі-5 step 259 to the ground-state sector predicted by Tasaki Problem 2.5.a (Оі-5 step 268) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1315) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_at_max_casimir_sector` | **Max-Casimir-sector eigenvalue specialization**: if `v` is a joint eigenvector of `Ең_totВІ` at `((z+1)N/2)((z+1)N/2+1)` and `Ең_RВІ` at `(zN/2)(zN/2+1)`, then `H В· v = zВ·(N/2)ВІ В· v = zSВІ В· v`. Maximum Casimir sector containing `\|ОҰ_вҠӨвҹ©, \|ОҰ_вҠҘвҹ©` (Оі-5 step 269) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1316) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_eq_gs_energy_smul` | **Predicted ground-state energy** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a target): `singleClusterGSEnergyS z N := вҲ’(N/2)В·(zN/2+1) = вҲ’S(1+zS)`, with named eigenvalue identity `H В· v = singleClusterGSEnergyS z N вҖў v` at the GS Casimir sector (Оі-5 step 270) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1317) |
| `singleClusterMaxEnergyS` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_eq_max_energy_smul` | **Maximum Casimir-sector energy**: `singleClusterMaxEnergyS z N := zВ·(N/2)ВІ = zSВІ`, with named eigenvalue identity `H В· v = singleClusterMaxEnergyS z N вҖў v` at the max Casimir sector containing `\|ОҰ_вҠӨвҹ©, \|ОҰ_вҠҘвҹ©` (Оі-5 step 271) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1318) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_le_zero` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_nonneg` | **Energy real-part sign hygiene**: `Re(GS) вүӨ 0` (AFM physical bound) and `0 вүӨ Re(Max)` (extremal aligned-state eigenvalue is non-negative) (Оі-5 step 272) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1319) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_le_singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re` | **Energy ordering**: `Re(GS) вүӨ Re(Max)`. Consistency check between Оі-5 steps 268 and 269 (Оі-5 step 273) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1320) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_im_zero` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_im_zero` | **Energies are real**: imaginary parts of both named single-cluster energies vanish (Оі-5 step 274) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1321) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_one_eq` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_one_eq` | **Dimer (z=1) closed forms**: `singleClusterGSEnergyS 1 N = вҲ’(N/2)(N/2+1) = вҲ’S(S+1)` (canonical singlet eigenvalue of `Ең_0В·Ең_1`); `singleClusterMaxEnergyS 1 N = (N/2)ВІ = SВІ` (canonical triplet eigenvalue) (Оі-5 step 275) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1322) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_zero_right` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_zero_right` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_zero_left` | **Trivial edge cases**: `GS z 0 = 0` (spin-0), `Max z 0 = 0` (spin-0), `Max 0 N = 0` (single-site, no leaves) (Оі-5 step 276) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1323) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_eq` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_eq` | **Explicit `.re` forms** as `в„қ` values: `Re(GS) = -(N/2)(zN/2+1)`, `Re(Max) = zВ·NВІ/4`. Useful for downstream real comparisons (Оі-5 step 278) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1325) |
| `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_sub_singleClusterGSEnergyS` | **GS-Max energy gap**: `Max вҲ’ GS = (N/2)(zN+1) = S(2zS+1)`. Closed-form difference between the two named eigenvalues (Оі-5 step 280) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1327) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_lt_singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_of_pos` | **Strict gap for `N вүҘ 1`**: `Re(GS) < Re(Max)` whenever spin is non-trivial (Оі-5 step 281) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1328) |
| `singleClusterGSEnergyS_re_neg_of_pos` / `singleClusterMaxEnergyS_re_pos_of_pos` | **Strict sign**: `Re(GS) < 0` for `N вүҘ 1`; `0 < Re(Max)` for `z вүҘ 1, N вүҘ 1`. Strengthens Оі-5 step 272 (Оі-5 step 283) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianEnergy.lean` (PR #1330) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_one` | **Single-leaf reduction**: `leafSpinSSquared 1 N = spinSDot 1 1 N` on `Fin 2`. Single-leaf leaf-Casimir collapses to single-site Casimir (Оі-5 step 285) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1332) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_one_mulVec` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_dimer` | **Dimer eigenvalue from StotВІ alone**: at z=1, `leafSpinSSquared` acts as the scalar `N(N+2)/4`, so any StotВІ eigenvector is auto a joint eigenvector with `H В· v = ((Оұ вҲ’ N(N+2)/2) / 2) вҖў v` (Оі-5 step 286) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1333) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_dimer_singlet` | **Dimer singlet attains GS energy**: for z=1, any `StotВІ = 0` eigenvector is an `H`-eigenvector at `singleClusterGSEnergyS 1 N`. Strongest concrete realisation of Tasaki Problem 2.5.a in the dimer case (Оі-5 step 287) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1334) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_dimer_top` | **Dimer top-spin attains Max energy**: for z=1, any `StotВІ = N(N+1)` eigenvector (`s_tot = 2S`) is an `H`-eigenvector at `singleClusterMaxEnergyS 1 N` (Оі-5 step 288) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1335) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_two` | **Trimer (z=2) leaf-Casimir decomposition**: `leafSpinSSquared 2 N = (N(N+2)/2) вҖў 1 + 2 вҖў spinSDot 1 2 N` on `Fin 3`. Two diagonal `spinSDot j j` terms collapse to `N(N+2)/4 вҖў 1` each; off-diagonal `spinSDot 1 2`, `spinSDot 2 1` are equal by `spinSDot_comm` (Оі-5 step 292) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1339) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_trimer` | **Trimer eigenvalue from `StotВІ` + leaf-leaf coupling**: for z=2, joint eigenvector of `StotВІ (=Оұ)` and `spinSDot 1 2 (=ОІ)` is an `H`-eigenvector at `(Оұ вҲ’ 3В·N(N+2)/4 вҲ’ 2ОІ)/2`. Specialisation of Оі-5 step 259 using Оі-5 step 292 (Оі-5 step 293) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1340) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_trimer_gs` | **Trimer GS-sector eigenvalue at GS energy**: for z=2, joint eigenvector of `StotВІВ·v=(N(N+2)/4)В·v` (`s_tot=N/2`) and `spinSDot 1 2В·v=(NВІ/4)В·v` (leaf triplet) is an `H`-eigenvector at `singleClusterGSEnergyS 2 N = -N(N+1)/2` (Оі-5 step 294) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1341) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_trimer_top` | **Trimer top-spin sector eigenvalue at Max energy**: for z=2, joint eigenvector at `s_tot=3N/2` (max) and leaf triplet `s_R=N` is an `H`-eigenvector at `singleClusterMaxEnergyS 2 N = NВІ/2` (Оі-5 step 295) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1342) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_trimer_leaf_singlet` | **Trimer leaf-singlet sector eigenvalue = 0**: for z=2, joint eigenvector at `s_tot=N/2` and leaf singlet `s_R=0` (`spinSDot 1 2В·v = -(N(N+2)/4)В·v`) gives `H В· v = 0`. Decoupling sector containing `0` from spin-1/2 trimer spectrum `{-1, 0, 1/2}` (Оі-5 step 296) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1343) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_three` | **Quartet (z=3) leaf-Casimir decomposition**: `leafSpinSSquared 3 N = (3В·N(N+2)/4) вҖў 1 + 2 вҖў (spinSDot 1 2 + spinSDot 1 3 + spinSDot 2 3)` on `Fin 4`. Three diagonal `spinSDot j j` terms collapse via `spinSDot_self`; six off-diagonal pair up via `spinSDot_comm` (Оі-5 step 303) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean` (PR #1350) |

This is the linear-algebra bridge for the next Problem 2.5.a joint total/leaf Casimir sector-decomposition step after magnetization extraction (Оі-5 step 332) | `Math/InvariantSubmoduleEigenvector.lean` (PR #4041) |
| `exists_joint_eigenvector_in_invariant_submodule` | **Joint invariant-submodule eigenvector bridge**: over an algebraically closed field, any non-zero finite-dimensional submodule invariant under two commuting endomorphisms contains a non-zero simultaneous eigenvector for both. This packages the next linear-algebra step for extracting total/leaf Casimir sector data inside the single-cluster magnetization/Hamiltonian component (Оі-5 step 333) | `Math/InvariantSubmoduleEigenvector.lean` (PR #4042) |

If each local Hamiltonian has a lower bound `Оө x`, then the Hermitian minimum eigenvalue of the sum is at least `ОЈ x, Оө x`. The local-cluster wrappers specialize this to the Problem 2.5.a star-cluster energies, yielding the abstract lower bound `E_GS вүҘ -ОЈ_{xвҲҲA} S(1 + degree(x) S)` with `S = N/2` once a graph Hamiltonian is decomposed into centered local cluster terms (Оі-6 step 339). The generic "a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian" helper has been extracted as `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` into `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` (PR #4341), replacing the per-file private copies in `Quantum/IsingChain.lean`, `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean`, and `Quantum/SpinS/TotalSpin.lean` | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianMinEigenvalueSumLower.lean` (PR #4048), `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` (PR #4341) |

| `graphLocalOutsideSite` / `graphLocalOutsideSite_fintype` / `graphLocalOutsideSite_decidableEq` / `graphLocalOutsideConfigExtend` / `graphLocalProductConfig` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv_apply_none` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv_apply_some` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv_apply_outside` / `graphLocalConfigEquiv_symm_apply` / `graphLocalProductConfig_outside` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_isHermitian` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_apply_of_outside_eq` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_apply_of_outside_ne` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_mulVec` / `matrix_mulVec_reindex_comp_symm` / `dotProduct_comp_equiv_symm` / `rayleighOnVec_reindex_comp_symm` / `dotProduct_product_re_eq_sum_blocks` / `rayleighOnVec_graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_rayleigh_lower` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_product_minEigenvalue_lower` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_rayleigh_lower` / `graphLocalClusterHamiltonianS_minEigenvalue_lower` | **Problem 2.5.b graph-local product lower-bound bridge**: reindexes a full graph configuration around `x` as an option-star configuration paired with an outside assignment. In these product coordinates the graph-local star is block diagonal in the outside coordinate, each diagonal block is the option-star Hamiltonian, and the Rayleigh numerator and squared norm decompose as sums over outside blocks. Reindex-invariance lemmas transfer the result back to the original same-Hilbert-space graph-local star. Therefore any common Rayleigh lower bound for the option-star blocks gives the same Hermitian minimum-eigenvalue lower bound for the original graph-local star (Оі-6 step 343) | `Quantum/SpinS/GraphLocalStarLowerBoundCore.lean` (product coordinates + product cluster Hamiltonian with Hermiticity / outside-block apply) + `Quantum/SpinS/GraphLocalStarLowerBound.lean` (product mulVec + reindexing + Rayleigh decomposition over outside blocks + lower bound, split for build speed) (PR #4053) |

The finite-sum wrapper then bounds a chosen family of graph-local stars, and the bipartite half-coupling theorem applies the bound directly to `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraphS G (1 / 2) N = ОЈ_{xвҲҲA} h_x` for one side of a bipartition (Оі-6 step 344) | `Quantum/SpinS/GraphLocalStarSumWrapper.lean` (PR #4054) |
| `tasaki25b_graphLocalCluster_sum_lower_bound_closed_form` / `tasaki25b_graphLocalCluster_sum_lower_bound_degree_closed_form` / `tasaki25b_heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraphS_half_lower_bound_closed_form` / `tasaki25b_heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraphS_half_lower_bound_degree_closed_form` | **Problem 2.5.b closed-form and degree wrappers**: rewrites the graph-local star and half-coupling graph-Hamiltonian lower bounds using the explicit Problem 2.5.a formula `Re E_GS(z) = -(N/2)(zN/2+1)`. The degree-spelled variants replace `(G.neighborFinset x).card` by `G.degree x`, matching Tasaki's graph-theoretic statement while preserving the necessary positive-degree hypothesis for centers on the chosen side (Оі-6 step 345) | `Quantum/SpinS/GraphLocalStarSumWrapper.lean` (PR #4055) |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_eq_sublattice_sum` | total spin decomposition: `Ең_tot^(Оұ) = Ең_A^(Оұ) + Ең_В¬A^(Оұ)` for `Оұ вҲҲ {1, 2, 3}`. Direct from the partition `Оӣ = A вҲӘ В¬A` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared` / `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_isHermitian` | sublattice spin Casimir: `(Ең_A)ВІ := ОЈ_Оұ (Ең_A^(Оұ))ВІ`. Hermitian (each `(Ең_A^(Оұ))ВІ` is the square of a Hermitian operator). Foundation for the Casimir identity `ДӨ_toy = (1/(2|Оӣ|))((Ең_tot)ВІ вҲ’ (Ең_A)ВІ вҲ’ (Ең_B)ВІ)` (Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOpGeneric_cross_commute` / `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute_op{1,2,3}` | mixed-axes cross-sublattice commutativity: `Commute (Ең_A^(Оұ)) (Ең_В¬A^(ОІ))` for any axes `Оұ, ОІ вҲҲ {1, 2, 3}`. Generic helper expresses this for arbitrary single-site operators `S, T`; the six mixed-axis specialisations follow as one-line corollaries | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_cross_commute` | the two sublattice Casimirs commute: `Commute (Ең_A)ВІ (Ең_В¬A)ВІ`. Each pairwise component `Commute ((Ең_A^(Оұ))ВІ) ((Ең_В¬A^(ОІ))ВІ)` follows from the mixed-axes cross-commute by chaining `Commute.mul_left` / `mul_right`. Sets up the joint eigenbasis of `(Ең_tot)ВІ`, `(Ең_A)ВІ`, `(Ең_В¬A)ВІ` for the toy-Hamiltonian eigenvalue analysis | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_commutator_sublatticeSpinHalfOp{2,3,1}` | **Sublattice SU(2) algebra**: `[Ең_A^(Оұ), Ең_A^(ОІ)] = i Оө^ОұОІОі Ең_A^(Оі)` for each cyclic axis triple. Generic helper `sublatticeSpin_commutator_general` lifts the single-site commutator `[SОұ, SОІ] = i вҖў SОі` to the sublattice sum (off-diagonal pairs vanish by `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne`; diagonal contributes `if A x then i вҖў onSite x SОі else 0`) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | **Sublattice Casimir self-invariance**: `Commute (Ең_A)ВІ (Ең_A^(Оұ))` for each axis. Standard SU(2) Casimir argument applied at the sublattice level: per-axis Leibniz rule `[XВІ, C] = X[X,C] + [X,C]X` combined with the sublattice SU(2) algebra. Together with cross-commute, gives `Commute (Ең_A)ВІ (Ең_tot^(Оұ))`, hence `Commute (Ең_A)ВІ (Ең_tot)ВІ` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_complement` / `_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` / `_totalSpinHalfSquared` | `(Ең_A)ВІ` commutes with each `Ең_В¬A^(Оұ)` (`Commute.mul_left` over the mixed-axes cross-commute), with each `Ең_tot^(Оұ) = Ең_A^(Оұ) + Ең_В¬A^(Оұ)`, and with `(Ең_tot)ВІ = ОЈ_Оұ (Ең_tot^(Оұ))ВІ`. Provides the **third pairwise commutativity** needed for the joint eigenbasis of `(Ең_tot)ВІ`, `(Ең_A)ВІ`, `(Ең_В¬A)ВІ` (the first two are Оұ-6r self-invariance and Оұ-6o inter-Casimir cross-commute) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinDot` / `sublatticeSpinDot_complement_isHermitian` | cross-sublattice spin dot product: `Ең_A В· Ең_B := ОЈ_Оұ Ең_A^(Оұ) Ең_B^(Оұ)`. `Ең_A В· Ең_В¬A` is Hermitian (each summand is the product of two commuting Hermitian operators). Bilinear expansion `sublatticeSpinDot_eq_sum_sum`: `Ең_A В· Ең_B = ОЈ_{x : A x} ОЈ_{y : B y} Ең_x В· Ең_y` connects the operator-level Casimir form to the bond-level Heisenberg expression in the toy Hamiltonian (Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.10)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_eq_sum_dot` / `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_all_up` / `_all_down` / `_of_const_on` | `(Ең_A)ВІ = ОЈ_{x вҲҲ A} ОЈ_{y вҲҲ A} Ең_x В· Ең_y` (specialisation `B = A` of the bilinear expansion), and the **maximum-spin Casimir eigenvalue on the all-aligned state**: `(Ең_A)ВІ В· \|s s вҖҰ sвҹ© = (\|A\|В·(\|A\|+2)/4) В· \|s s вҖҰ sвҹ©` for any `s : Fin 2`. Generalised form `_of_const_on`: any `\|Пғвҹ©` with `Пғ` **constant on `A`** is an eigenvector with eigenvalue `\|A\|В·(\|A\|+2)/4` (relevant for NГ©el-style states which are constant on each sublattice but not globally) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_sublatticeSpinDot_sum` | the MLM toy Hamiltonian decomposes as an oriented cross-sublattice spin dot product: `ДӨ_toy = Ең_A В· Ең_В¬A + Ең_В¬A В· Ең_A`. Bridges the bipartite-bond sum (Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.10)) to the operator-level Casimir form (Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.11)) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinDot_complement_comm` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_two_sublatticeSpinDot` | cross-sublattice symmetry: `Ең_A В· Ең_В¬A = Ең_В¬A В· Ең_A` (each axis pair commutes by `sublatticeSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}_cross_commute`), giving the closed form `ДӨ_toy = 2 вҖў Ең_A В· Ең_В¬A` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_eq_sublattice_casimir` / `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir identity** (Tasaki В§2.5 (2.5.11)): `(Ең_tot)ВІ = (Ең_A)ВІ + 2 вҖў (Ең_A В· Ең_В¬A) + (Ең_В¬A)ВІ` (per-axis `(a + b)ВІ = aВІ + 2ab + bВІ` via cross-commute), and the closed form (without `1/|Оӣ|`) `ДӨ_toy = (Ең_tot)ВІ вҲ’ (Ең_A)ВІ вҲ’ (Ең_В¬A)ВІ` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | the toy Hamiltonian commutes with `(Ең_tot)ВІ` (specialisation of `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared`). The standard fact used to project the toy ground state onto a fixed `(Ең_tot)ВІ` eigenspace, underpinning the `S_tot = 0` selection of the toy ground state | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_commute_sublatticeSpinHalfSquared` / `_complement` | the toy Hamiltonian commutes with `(Ең_A)ВІ` and with `(Ең_В¬A)ВІ`. Direct consequences of the closed form `ДӨ_toy = (Ең_tot)ВІ вҲ’ (Ең_A)ВІ вҲ’ (Ең_В¬A)ВІ` and the three pairwise Casimir commutativities (PRs Оұ-6o, Оұ-6s, self-commute trivially). Together with Оұ-6p, gives all four Casimir-style commutators of `ДӨ_toy`, the prerequisite for the joint eigenbasis analysis on which the `S_tot = 0` selection rests | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` / `_simplified` | explicit eigenvalue of `ДӨ_toy` on the all-aligned state: the Casimir-difference form `\|Оӣ\|В·(\|Оӣ\|+2)/4 вҲ’ \|A\|В·(\|A\|+2)/4 вҲ’ \|В¬A\|В·(\|В¬A\|+2)/4` algebraically simplifies via `\|Оӣ\| = \|A\| + \|В¬A\|` to the **product form** `\|A\|В·\|В¬A\|/2`. The eigenvalue is non-negative for any bipartite lattice and strictly positive when both sublattices are non-empty | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` |
| `sublatticeSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_neelStateOf` / `_complement_mulVec_neelStateOf` | sublattice Casimir eigenvalues on the **NГ©el state** `ОҰ_NГ©el(A) := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`: `(Ең_A)ВІ В· \|ОҰ_NГ©el(A)вҹ© = (\|A\|В·(\|A\|+2)/4) В· \|ОҰ_NГ©el(A)вҹ©` and `(Ең_В¬A)ВІ В· \|ОҰ_NГ©el(A)вҹ© = (\|В¬A\|В·(\|В¬A\|+2)/4) В· \|ОҰ_NГ©el(A)вҹ©`. Direct corollaries of `_of_const_on` since the NГ©el configuration is constant on each sublattice (`Пғ x = 0` on `A`, `Пғ x = 1` on `В¬A`); the NГ©el state is simultaneously a `(Ең_A)ВІ` and `(Ең_В¬A)ВІ` eigenvector at maximum-spin eigenvalues | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelCore.lean` |
| `mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_commute_of_mem` | generic preservation: any operator `A` with `Commute A (Еңtot^(3))` maps each magnetisation sector `H_M` to itself вҖ” operator-level form of Tasaki В§2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 block-diagonalisation | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | Casimir specialisation: `ЕңtotВІ` preserves every `H_M` (since `[ЕңtotВІ, Еңtot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `J : Оӣ вҶ’ Оӣ вҶ’ в„Ӯ` and `M : в„Ӯ`, `v вҲҲ H_M` implies `H В· v вҲҲ H_M` вҖ” the operator-level statement that any Heisenberg Hamiltonian block-diagonalises against Tasaki В§2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 magnetisation-sector decomposition (consequence of SU(2) invariance `[H, Еңtot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : в„Ӯ`, `v вҲҲ H_M` implies `Еңtot^- В· v вҲҲ H_{M-1}` вҖ” the standard SU(2) lowering ladder action via the Cartan relation `[Еңtot^(3), Еңtot^-] = -Еңtot^-` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : в„Ӯ`, `v вҲҲ H_M` implies `Еңtot^+ В· v вҲҲ H_{M+1}` вҖ” the SU(2) raising ladder action via `[Еңtot^(3), Еңtot^+] = +Еңtot^+` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_two_site` | for `Оӣ = Fin 2` and any `Оё`, the global rotation factors as `onSite 0 (Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё) * onSite 1 (Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё)` (general-Оё extension of Problem 2.2.b) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` | `onSite x (exp A) = exp (onSite x A)` вҖ” bridge between single-site and many-body matrix exponential. Local Frobenius normed structure + `LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional` + `NormedSpace.map_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_eq_exp` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.11): `Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё_tot = exp(-iОё Ең_tot^(Оұ))`. Composes `spinHalfRot{Оұ}_eq_exp` (single site), `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` (per-site bridge), and `Matrix.exp_sum_of_commute` (commuting-summand exp = noncommProd of exps) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_commute_of_commute` | Tasaki В§2.2 (2.2.12) вҶ’ (2.2.13): `Commute A (Ең_tot^(Оұ)) вҶ’ Commute A (Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё_tot)`. Generic operator version, follows from `Commute.exp_right` after rewriting `Гӣ` via `_eq_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}_exp_commute_of_commute` | ladder version: `Commute A (Ең^Вұ_tot) вҶ’ Commute A (exp(c вҖў Ең^Вұ_tot))` for any `c : в„Ӯ` (useful for U(1) symmetry) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conjTranspose_mul_self` | `(Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё_tot)бҙҙ * Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё_tot = 1` (unitarity). Derived from `exp_mem_unitary_of_mem_skewAdjoint` after recognizing `-iОё Ең_tot^(Оұ)` as skew-adjoint | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_eq_self_of_commute` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.13) finite form: `Commute A (Ең_tot^(Оұ)) вҶ’ (Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё_tot)бҙҙ * A * Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё_tot = A`. Combines `_commute_of_commute` with unitarity | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `IsInMagnetizationSubspace` | predicate for the magnetization-`M` eigenspace `H_M` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.9)/(2.2.10)) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace M` | the magnetization-`M` eigenspace as a `Submodule в„Ӯ ((Оӣ вҶ’ Fin 2) вҶ’ в„Ӯ)` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `basisVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace` | `|Пғвҹ© вҲҲ H_{|Пғ|/2}` вҖ” basis states lie in their magnetization subspace | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_disjoint` | distinct sectors `H_M вҠ“ H_{M'} = вҠҘ` (`M вү  M'`) вҖ” eigenvalue uniqueness | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `iSup_magnetizationSubspace_eq_top` | `вЁҶ_M H_M = вҠӨ` вҖ” every vector decomposes as a sum across sectors | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_eq_eigenspace` | bridge `H_M = (Ең_tot^(3) as End).eigenspace M` (used to inherit `iSupIndep`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_iSupIndep` | `iSupIndep`: each sector is disjoint from the supremum of all others | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_isInternal` | `DirectSum.IsInternal`: full direct-sum decomposition `H = вҠ•_M H_M` (Tasaki eqs. (2.2.9)/(2.2.10)) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |

### Two-site spin inner product (Tasaki В§2.2 eq. (2.2.16))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, В§2.2 eq. (2.2.16), p. 24.

| `spinHalfDot_eq_plus_minus` | `Ең_x В· Ең_y = (1/2)(Ең_x^+ Ең_y^- + Ең_x^- Ең_y^+) + Ең_x^(3) Ең_y^(3)` (Tasaki (2.2.16)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_comm` | `Ең_x В· Ең_y = Ең_y В· Ең_x` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `spinHalfDot_commutator_totalSpinHalfOpPlus/Minus` | `[Ең_x В· Ең_y, Ең^Вұ_tot] = 0` (ladder-operator version of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_both_up/down` | `Ең_x В· Ең_y |вҶ‘вҶ‘вҹ© = (1/4) |вҶ‘вҶ‘вҹ©`, `Ең_x В· Ең_y |вҶ“вҶ“вҹ© = (1/4) |вҶ“вҶ“вҹ©` (Tasaki (2.2.19) triplet `m = Вұ1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |

| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_singlet` | singlet eigenvalue `Ең_x В· Ең_y (|Пғвҹ© - |swap Пғвҹ©) = -(3/4) (|Пғвҹ© - |swap Пғвҹ©)` (Tasaki (2.2.19) singlet `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
