| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}` | `[H, Ең^Вұ_tot] = 0` (ladder form of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | `Commute H ЕңtotВІ` вҖ” the Casimir operator-level form of SU(2) invariance (consequence of `[H, Еңtot^Оұ] = 0` for each Оұ, via `Commute.mul_right` and `.add_right`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_preserves_totalSpinHalfSquared_eigenvalue` | if `ЕңtotВІ В· v = S В· v` then `ЕңtotВІ В· (H В· v) = S В· (H В· v)` вҖ” operator-level simultaneous diagonalisation of `H` and the SU(2) Casimir | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfOpPlus_mul_pauliZ` / `pauliZ_mul_spinHalfOpPlus` | `Пғ^+ В· Пғ^z = -Пғ^+` and `Пғ^z В· Пғ^+ = Пғ^+` вҖ” the (anti)commutation at the single-site Pauli algebra level, used for the Jordan-Wigner cross-site CAR | `Quantum/SpinHalfBasis.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `Ең_totВІ |s s вҖҰ sвҹ© = (N(N+2)/4) |s s вҖҰ sвҹ©` for any constant `s : Fin 2` (Casimir eigenvalue at maximum total spin `S = N/2`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specializations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOp{Minus,Plus}_pow_basisVec_const` | for any `s : Fin 2` and `k : в„•`, `ЕңtotВІ В· (Еңtot^вҲ“)^k В· |sвҖҰsвҹ© = (|Оӣ|В·(|Оӣ|+2)/4) В· (Еңtot^вҲ“)^k В· |sвҖҰsвҹ©` вҖ” the iterated ladder iterates remain in the maximum-total-spin SU(2) representation `S = Smax = |Оӣ|/2` (Casimir invariance, Tasaki В§2.4) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `H |sвҖҰsвҹ© = (ОЈ_{x,y} J(x,y)В·(if x=y then 3/4 else 1/4)) В· |sвҖҰsвҹ©` вҖ” every Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian acts on a uniformly-aligned basis state as a scalar; bilinear-sum lift of Tasaki В§2.4 eq. (2.4.5), p. 32 (`-Ең_xВ·Ең_y |ОҰвҶ‘вҹ© = -SВІ |ОҰвҶ‘вҹ©` for `S = 1/2`, `x вү  y`), with the diagonal `S(S+1) = 3/4` contribution recorded explicitly | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specialisations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) / `s = 1` (all-down) вҖ” the eigenvector property of the fully-polarised states; ground-state status (Tasaki's `E_GS = -|B|В·SВІ`) requires extra ferromagnetic structure on `J` and is not asserted here | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_basisVec_const` | `H В· (Ең_tot^+ В· |sвҖҰsвҹ©) = c_J В· (Ең_tot^+ В· |sвҖҰsвҹ©)` вҖ” `Ең_tot^+` preserves the H-eigenvalue on a constant-spin basis state (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki В§2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_basisVec_const` | same with `Ең_tot^-` вҖ” the canonical lowering ladder Tasaki uses to enumerate the ferromagnetic ground states `|ОҰ_Mвҹ©` (eq. (2.4.9), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_const` | iterated form: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : в„•`, `H В· ((Ең_tot^-)^k В· |sвҖҰsвҹ©) = c_J В· ((Ең_tot^-)^k В· |sвҖҰsвҹ©)`; specialised at `s = 0` this gives the unnormalised Tasaki В§2.4 (2.4.9), p. 33 вҖ” every iterate `(Ең_tot^-)^k В· |ОҰвҶ‘вҹ©` lies in the same H-eigenspace as `|ОҰвҶ‘вҹ©` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_const` | companion iterated form for `Ең_tot^+`: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : в„•`, `H В· ((Ең_tot^+)^k В· |sвҖҰsвҹ©) = c_J В· ((Ең_tot^+)^k В· |sвҖҰsвҹ©)` (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki В§2.4 (2.4.7), iterated) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}` | for any `J` and `Оё : в„қ`, `H` commutes with the global rotation `Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё = exp(-iОё Ең_tot^Оұ)` (composes `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{Оұ}` with `totalSpinHalfRot{Оұ}_commute_of_commute`; the operator-level form of Tasaki В§2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `Оё`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H В· (Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё В· |sвҖҰsвҹ©) = c_J В· (Гӣ^(Оұ)_Оё В· |sвҖҰsвҹ©)` вҖ” the rotated (single-axis) constant-spin state shares the H-eigenvalue (Tasaki В§2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot32_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `Оё`, `П•`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H В· (Гӣ^(3)_П• В· Гӣ^(2)_Оё В· |sвҖҰsвҹ©) = c_J В· (Гӣ^(3)_П• В· Гӣ^(2)_Оё В· |sвҖҰsвҹ©)` вҖ” the two-step spin-coherent state of Tasaki eq. (2.4.6) (`|Оһ_Оё,П•вҹ©` for `s = 0`) is an H-eigenvector with the same eigenvalue as the constant configuration (Tasaki eq. (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | `Ең_totВІ (|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© - |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©) = 0` for `Оӣ = Fin 2` (singlet, `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_triplet_zero` | `Ең_totВІ (|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© + |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©) = 2(|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© + |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©)` for `Оӣ = Fin 2` (triplet `m = 0`, `S = 1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | the two-site singlet has zero `Ең_tot^(3)` magnetization | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_totalOnSite` | `[onSite x SОұ, ОЈ_z onSite z SОІ] = onSite x [SОұ, SОІ]` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

### One-dimensional open-chain quantum Ising

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, В§3.3 eq. (3.3.1), p. 55 (transverse-field Ising on an open
chain). Our formalization uses the Pauli convention `Пғ = 2В·S` and an
explicit bond coupling `J`.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h` | `H = -J ОЈ Пғ^z_i Пғ^z_{i+1} - h ОЈ Пғ^x_i` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H` is Hermitian for real `J`, `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot x y` | the Ising bond operator `Пғ^z_x В· Пғ^z_y` (generic in `Оӣ`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot_isHermitian` | each bond operator is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric (J : Оӣ вҶ’ Оӣ вҶ’ в„Ӯ) (h : в„Ӯ)` | **graph-friendly** Ising Hamiltonian `ОЈ J(x,y) Пғ^z_x Пғ^z_y вҲ’ h ОЈ Пғ^x_x` for any finite `Оӣ` and pairwise coupling `J`; specialises to chain / graph / lattice via the choice of `J` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric_isHermitian` | Hermitian for entry-wise real `J` and real `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph G J h` | graph wrapper: `isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf G J) h`; double-sum convention matches Heisenberg-on-graph | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` | Hermitian for real `J, h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph G ОІ J h` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity (real `J, h`) and commute with the Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.sum_pathGraph_forward` / `sum_pathGraph_backward` / `sum_pathGraph_adj` | sum-decomposition helpers for `pathGraph (N+1)` adjacency: `ОЈ_{x,y}` over ordered adjacent pairs = `ОЈ_{i:Fin N} (f i.cs i.s + f i.s i.cs)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `pathGraphParityColoring` / `pathGraph_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of `pathGraph (N + 1)` (`i вҶҰ i.val % 2`) and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Underpins the NГ©el state (`Quantum/NeelState.lean`) and the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki В§2.5) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `cycleGraphEvenParityColoring` / `cycleGraph_even_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of the even cycle `cycleGraph (2 * K + 2)` and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Wrap-around case `(2K+1) + 1 вүЎ 0` still flips parity because the cycle length is even (odd cycles are not bipartite) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_eq_isingHamiltonianGeneric` | **generic-N bridge**: `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h = isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J/2)) h`. The proof itself is the robust regression test | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Testing infrastructure

| Lean name / location | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem/Tests/Sanity.lean` | sanity-check `example` blocks for concrete small-N instances (Pauli arithmetic, spin-1/2 ladder actions, Heisenberg / Ising Hermiticity on small chains, graph-centric bridge identities) |
| decide-based property tests (in `Tests/Sanity.lean`) | universally-quantified properties verified by `decide` on small finite types (graph adjacency symmetry / irreflexivity / connectivity on `pathGraph n` and `cycleGraph n` for small `n`); real proofs, no `sorry` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState ОІ J h N` | `gibbsState ОІ (quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h)` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_isHermitian` | the Ising-chain Gibbs state `ПҒ_ОІ` is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[ПҒ_ОІ, H_Ising] = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_zero` | infinite-temperature closed form `вҹЁAвҹ©_0 = (1/dim) В· Tr A` (independent of `J, h`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `O`, `(вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІ).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | conservation `вҹЁ[H_Ising, A]вҹ©_ОІ = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(вҹЁH_Isingвҹ©_ОІ).im = 0` (energy expectation is real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | for Hermitian `O`, `(вҹЁH_Ising В· Oвҹ©_ОІ).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_sq_im` | `(вҹЁH_Ising^2вҹ©_ОІ).im = 0` (energy-squared expectation real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_pow_im` | `(вҹЁH_Ising^nвҹ©_ОІ).im = 0` for any `n : в„•` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(вҹЁAВ·B + BВ·Aвҹ©_ОІ).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(вҹЁAВ·B вҲ’ BВ·Aвҹ©_ОІ).re = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsHamiltonianVariance_im` | `(Var_ОІ(H_Ising)).im = 0` (energy variance real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsing_partitionFn_im` | `(partitionFn ОІ H_Ising).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq` | for Hermitian `O`, `((вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІ).re : в„Ӯ) = вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІ` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(ПҒ_ОІ^n) = Z(nОІ) / Z(ОІ)^n` for the Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_self_eq` | `вҹЁH_Isingвҹ©_ОІ = -J В· вҲ‘ вҹЁПғ^z Пғ^zвҹ©_ОІ + (-h) В· вҲ‘ вҹЁПғ^xвҹ©_ОІ` (energy as bond + transverse-field decomposition) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Gibbs state (Tasaki В§3.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, В§3.3.

All theorems in this module are fully proved with **zero `sorry`**.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `gibbsExp ОІ H` | `exp(-ОІH) := Matrix.exp (-ОІ вҖў H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isHermitian` | `exp(-ОІH)` is Hermitian (when `H` is Hermitian) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_zero` | `exp(-0В·H) = 1` (Tasaki В§3.3, pp. 75вҖ“78) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add` | `exp(-(ОІвӮҒ+ОІвӮӮ)H) = exp(-ОІвӮҒH) В· exp(-ОІвӮӮH)` (one-parameter group) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add_of_commute_hamiltonians` | `exp(-ОІ(HвӮҒ+HвӮӮ)) = exp(-ОІHвӮҒ) В· exp(-ОІHвӮӮ)` for commuting `HвӮҒ, HвӮӮ` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_neg_mul_self` | `exp(ОІH) В· exp(-ОІH) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_self_mul_neg` | `exp(-ОІH) В· exp(ОІH) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isUnit` | `exp(-ОІH)` is invertible | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_ne_zero` | `exp(-ОІH) вү  0` (corollary of `gibbsExp_isUnit`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_ne_zero` | `ПҒ_ОІ вү  0` when `Z(ОІ) вү  0` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_inv` | `(ПҒ_ОІ)вҒ»В№ = Z(ОІ) В· e^{ОІH}` when `Z(ОІ) вү  0` (general matrix inverse, generalises `gibbsState_zero_inv`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_smul_gibbsState_eq_gibbsExp` | `Z(ОІ) В· ПҒ_ОІ = e^{-ОІH}` when `Z(ОІ) вү  0` (canonical rescaled identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_mul_gibbsExpectation_eq` | `Z(ОІ) В· вҹЁAвҹ©_ОІ = Tr(e^{-ОІH} В· A)` when `Z(ОІ) вү  0` (canonical unnormalised expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_natCast_mul` | `exp(-(nВ·ОІ)H) = (exp(-ОІH))^n` for `n : в„•` (exact discrete semigroup identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_two_mul` | `exp(-(2ОІ)H) = exp(-ОІH) В· exp(-ОІH)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_inv` | `(exp(-ОІH))вҒ»В№ = exp(ОІH)` (matrix inverse made explicit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_intCast_mul` | `exp(-(nВ·ОІ)H) = (exp(-ОІH))^n` for `n : в„Ө` (integer-power extension) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn ОІ H` | `Z := Matrix.trace (exp(-ОІH))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero` | `Z(0) = Fintype.card (Оӣ вҶ’ Fin 2)` (dimension of the Hilbert space) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero_ne_zero` | `Z(0) вү  0` (concrete sorry-free proof that the partition function is nonzero at ОІ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | for any Hermitian `A : Matrix n n в„Ӯ`, `A.trace.im = 0` (generic helper; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4344) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `partitionFn_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `H`, `(partitionFn ОІ H).im = 0` (Z is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_mul_self_trace` | `Tr(ПҒ_ОІВІ) = Z(2ОІ) / Z(ОІ)ВІ` (purity / RГ©nyi-2 entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(ПҒ_ОІ^n) = Z(nОІ) / Z(ОІ)^n` for any `n : в„•` (RГ©nyi-n entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero` | `ПҒ_0 = (1/dim) В· I` (maximally mixed state at infinite temperature) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero_inv` | `ПҒ_0вҒ»В№ = dim В· I` (matrix inverse at ОІ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_zero` | `вҹЁAвҹ©_0 = (1/dim) В· Tr A` (high-temperature limit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState ОІ H` | `ПҒ := (1/Z) вҖў exp(-ОІH)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_trace` | `Tr(ПҒ) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_isHermitian` | `ПҒ` is Hermitian | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation ОІ H O` | `вҹЁOвҹ© := Matrix.trace (ПҒ * O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_one` | `вҹЁ1вҹ© = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_add` | `вҹЁOвӮҒ + OвӮӮвҹ© = вҹЁOвӮҒвҹ© + вҹЁOвӮӮвҹ©` (linearity in observable) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_smul` | `вҹЁc В· Oвҹ© = c В· вҹЁOвҹ©` (scalar linearity, `c : в„Ӯ`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_neg` | `вҹЁ-Oвҹ© = -вҹЁOвҹ©` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sub` | `вҹЁA - Bвҹ© = вҹЁAвҹ© - вҹЁBвҹ©` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sum` | `вҹЁвҲ‘ i вҲҲ s, f iвҹ© = вҲ‘ i вҲҲ s, вҹЁf iвҹ©` (finite-sum linearity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_commute_hamiltonian` | `[exp(-ОІH), H] = 0` (Tasaki В§3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[ПҒ_ОІ, H] = 0`, i.e. `ПҒ_ОІ` is stationary under the dynamics generated by `H` (Tasaki В§3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `star (Tr(A В· B)) = Tr(A В· B)` for Hermitian `A, B : Matrix n n в„Ӯ` (algebraic core, Gibbs-independent; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4344) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_of_isHermitian` | `star вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІ = вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІ` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | `(вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІ).im = 0` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq_of_isHermitian` | `((вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІ).re : в„Ӯ) = вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІ` for Hermitian `H`, `O` (real-cast equality) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_comm` | `вҹЁH В· Aвҹ©_ОІ = вҹЁA В· Hвҹ©_ОІ` for any `A` (Tasaki В§3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_comm_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A` (`[A, H] = 0`), `вҹЁA В· Oвҹ©_ОІ = вҹЁO В· Aвҹ©_ОІ` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_eq_zero_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A`, `вҹЁA В· O вҲ’ O В· Aвҹ©_ОІ = 0` (selection rule) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | `вҹЁ[H, A]вҹ©_ОІ = 0` (conservation law) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(вҹЁHвҹ©_ОІ).im = 0` for Hermitian `H` (real energy expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sq_im_of_isHermitian` | `(вҹЁO В· Oвҹ©_ОІ).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (second-moment realness, variance precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_pow_im_of_isHermitian` | `(вҹЁO^nвҹ©_ОІ).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O`, any `n : в„•` (all natural-power moments real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance ОІ H O` | `Var_ОІ(O) := вҹЁO В· Oвҹ©_ОІ вҲ’ вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІВІ` (canonical-ensemble variance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsVariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Var_ОІ(O)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (variance is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_zero` | at ОІ = 0, `Var_0(O) = (1/dim) В· Tr(OВІ) вҲ’ ((1/dim) В· Tr O)ВІ` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq_centered_sq` | `Var_ОІ(O) = вҹЁ(O вҲ’ вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІ В· 1) В· (O вҲ’ вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІ В· 1)вҹ©_ОІ` (centered-square form, `Z вү  0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance ОІ H A B` | `Cov_ОІ(A, B) := вҹЁA В· Bвҹ©_ОІ вҲ’ вҹЁAвҹ©_ОІ В· вҹЁBвҹ©_ОІ` (canonical-ensemble complex covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsCovariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_self_eq_variance` | `Cov_ОІ(O, O) = Var_ОІ(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_sub_swap_eq_commutator` | `Cov_ОІ(A, B) вҲ’ Cov_ОІ(B, A) = вҹЁA В· B вҲ’ B В· Aвҹ©_ОІ` (antisymmetric part = commutator expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_left` | `Cov_ОІ(AвӮҒ + AвӮӮ, B) = Cov_ОІ(AвӮҒ, B) + Cov_ОІ(AвӮӮ, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_right` | `Cov_ОІ(A, BвӮҒ + BвӮӮ) = Cov_ОІ(A, BвӮҒ) + Cov_ОІ(A, BвӮӮ)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_left` | `Cov_ОІ(c вҖў A, B) = c В· Cov_ОІ(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_right` | `Cov_ОІ(A, c вҖў B) = c В· Cov_ОІ(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov_ОІ(c вҖў 1, B) = 0` and `Cov_ОІ(A, c вҖў 1) = 0` (when `Z вү  0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov^s_ОІ(c вҖў 1, B) = 0` and `Cov^s_ОІ(A, c вҖў 1) = 0` (when `Z вү  0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm ОІ H A B` | `Cov^s_ОІ(A, B) := (1/2) В· вҹЁA В· B + B В· Aвҹ©_ОІ вҲ’ вҹЁAвҹ©_ОІ В· вҹЁBвҹ©_ОІ` (symmetric / real-valued covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_self_eq_variance` | `Cov^s_ОІ(O, O) = Var_ОІ(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Cov^s_ОІ(A, B)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_comm` | `Cov^s_ОІ(A, B) = Cov^s_ОІ(B, A)` (symmetric in observables) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_add_left/right` | additivity of `Cov^s_ОІ` in each argument | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_smul_left/right` | scalar pull-out from each argument of `Cov^s_ОІ` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add` | `Var_ОІ(A + B) = Var_ОІ(A) + Var_ОІ(B) + 2 В· Cov^s_ОІ(A, B)` (sum-of-observables variance identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_one` | `Var_ОІ(1) = 0` (when `Z вү  0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul` | `Var_ОІ(c вҖў A) = cВІ В· Var_ОІ(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul_one` | `Var_ОІ(c вҖў 1) = 0` (when `Z вү  0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_neg` | `Var_ОІ(вҲ’A) = Var_ОІ(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add_const_smul_one` | `Var_ОІ(A + c вҖў 1) = Var_ОІ(A)` (when `Z вү  0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_add_half_commutator` | `Cov_ОІ(A, B) = Cov^s_ОІ(A, B) + (1/2) В· вҹЁA В· B вҲ’ B В· Aвҹ©_ОІ` (symmetric / antisymmetric decomposition) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_eq_half_add_swap` | `Cov^s_ОІ(A, B) = (1/2) В· (Cov_ОІ(A, B) + Cov_ОІ(B, A))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_of_commute` | for commuting `A, B`, `Cov_ОІ(A, B) = Cov^s_ОІ(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_conjTranspose_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star Tr(ПҒ В· X) = Tr(ПҒ В· Xбҙҙ)` for Hermitian `ПҒ` (generic helper) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star вҹЁA В· Bвҹ©_ОІ = вҹЁB В· Aвҹ©_ОІ` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | `(вҹЁAВ·B + BВ·Aвҹ©_ОІ).im = 0` (anticommutator is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | `(вҹЁAВ·B вҲ’ BВ·Aвҹ©_ОІ).re = 0` (commutator is purely imaginary) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | `(вҹЁH В· Oвҹ©_ОІ).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

### Heisenberg chain (Tasaki В§3.5)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, В§3.5, p. 89.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf G J` | the canonical pairwise coupling `Оӣ вҶ’ Оӣ вҶ’ в„Ӯ` of a `SimpleGraph G` with uniform edge weight `J`: returns `J` on edges of `G`, zero otherwise (graph-centric bridge) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf_symm` / `_self` / `_real` | symmetry (from `G.Adj` symmetry), vanishing on the diagonal (from irreflexivity), and reality (for real edge weight) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.pathGraph_adj_iff` / `cycleGraph_adj_iff` | path / cycle graph adjacency in the explicit `x.val + 1 = y.val вҲЁ ...` form used elsewhere in the codebase | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+1) вҶ’ Fin (N+1) вҶ’ в„Ӯ`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds, zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+2) вҶ’ Fin (N+2) вҶ’ в„Ӯ`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds (mod N+2), zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the open-chain coupling is `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the periodic-chain coupling is `couplingOf (cycleGraph (N+2)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_isHermitian_of_real_symm` | for any real symmetric coupling `J` the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ОЈ_{x,y} J(x,y) Ең_x В· Ең_y` is Hermitian | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_couplingOf_isHermitian` | **graph-centric** Hermiticity: for any `SimpleGraph G` and real edge weight `J : в„Ӯ`, the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` is Hermitian. The chain instances are corollaries via the bridge theorems | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph G J` | named wrapper = `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` (parallel to `isingHamiltonianOnGraph`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | corollaries re-exposed under the named wrapper | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` / `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridges: chain Gibbs = graph Gibbs on pathGraph/cycleGraph | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_eq_isingGibbsStateOnGraph` | chain Ising Gibbs = `isingGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) ОІ (-J/2) h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | the periodic Ising Gibbs state commutes with the periodic Ising Hamiltonian (free corollary of `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsExpectation_zero` / `_im_of_isHermitian` / `_commutator_hamiltonian` / `_hamiltonian_im` / `_hamiltonian_pow_im` / `isingCycle_partitionFn_im` / `_ofReal_re_eq` / `isingCycleGibbsState_pow_trace` | periodic-Ising expectation companions of the open-chain `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation*` family: ОІ = 0 closed form, real-valuedness for Hermitian observables, conservation `вҹЁ[H, A]вҹ© = 0`, energy / energy-power expectations real, partition-function real, real-cast `((вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІ).re : в„Ӯ) = вҹЁOвҹ©_ОІ`, RГ©nyi-n trace `Tr(ПҒ_ОІ^n) = Z(nОІ) / Z(ОІ)^n` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.integerChainGraph_adj_iff` | adjacency: `a ~ b вҶ” b = a + 1 вҲЁ a = b + 1` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |

(4.3.1)) + a `DecidableRel` instance; the substrate of the thermodynamic-limit / infinite-volume frontier (Issue #4557) | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBox` / `mem_hypercubicBox` | the centered finite box `Оӣ_n = {x вҲҲ в„ӨбөҲ : вҲ’n < xбөў вүӨ n}` (even side `2n`, Tasaki eq. (3.1.2)) as a `Finset`, with its coordinatewise membership criterion | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBox_subset_succ` / `hypercubicBox_monotone` / `exists_mem_hypercubicBox` / `iUnion_hypercubicBox` | the **monotone exhaustion** of `в„ӨбөҲ` by the boxes: nesting `Оӣ_n вҠҶ Оӣ_{n+1}`, monotonicity, every site lies in some box, and `вӢғвӮҷ Оӣ_n = в„ӨбөҲ`; the increasing-region API for constructing the thermodynamic limit | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBox_card` | the box **volume** `\|Оӣ_n\| = (2n)бөҲ` (side `2n` in each of `d` directions); the volume normalization `LбөҲ = (2n)бөҲ` of the bulk-density / energy-density limits (Tasaki eqs. (4.3.4), (4.3.6)) | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBoxGraph` / `hypercubicBoxVertex` / `hypercubicBoxGraph_adj` | the **finite-volume graph** on the box `Оӣ_n` (a `Fintype` subtype of `в„ӨбөҲ`): the induced subgraph `(hypercubicLatticeGraph d).induce вҶ‘Оӣ_n`, the graph-centric finite-volume substrate `Оӣ_n вҶӘ в„ӨбөҲ` of the thermodynamic limit | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.hypercubicBoxCoupling` / `hypercubicBoxParityColoring` / `hypercubicBoxGraph_isBipartite` | the uniform nearest-neighbor coupling `couplingOf` on `Оӣ_n` (finite-volume many-body Hamiltonian input), and the inherited bipartite structure (parity coloring restricted from `в„ӨбөҲ`) | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |
| `boxHeisenbergHamiltonianS` / `boxAFMHeisenbergHamiltonianS` (+ `_isHermitian`, `_eq_heisenbergHamiltonianS_boxCoupling`) | the **concrete finite-volume spin-`S` Heisenberg model** on the box `Оӣ_n вҠӮ в„ӨбөҲ` (`ManyBodyOpS (hypercubicBoxVertex d n) N`), via the graph Hamiltonian on `hypercubicBoxGraph`; the AFM specialization `ДӨ_{Оӣ_n} = ОЈ_вҹЁx,yвҹ© Ең_xВ·Ең_y` (`J=1/2`, Tasaki unordered-bond convention) is the finite-volume model whose `LвҶ‘вҲһ` limit is the В§4.3 `InfiniteSpinSystem`. Hermitian for real `J`; ties to PR5's `hypercubicBoxCoupling` by `rfl`. Axiom-free | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxModel.lean` |
| `boxGroundEnergyS` / `boxGroundEnergyS_le_eigenvalues` / `boxBondCount` / `boxGroundEnergyDensityS` | **finite-volume ground-state energy** `E_{GS,n}` (least eigenvalue of the Hermitian box AFM Hamiltonian, via `hermitianMinEigenvalue`), the **bond count** `\|B_n\|` (edges of `hypercubicBoxGraph`), and the **energy density** `E_{GS,n}/\|B_n\|` (Tasaki В§4.3 eq. (4.3.4)) вҖ” the provable finite-volume groundwork for the thermodynamic-limit bridge (Issue #4564). Axiom-free | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxModel.lean` |
| `boxBondCount_pos` / `boxGroundEnergyDensityS_tendsto` | `boxBondCount_pos` (**proved**): for `0 < d`, `1 вүӨ n` the box has вүҘ1 bond (origin & `e_i` adjacent), so the energy-density denominator is positive on the tail (non-vacuity). `boxGroundEnergyDensityS_tendsto` (**documented AXIOM**, Tasaki В§4.3 eq. (4.3.4)): for `0 < d`, `0 < N` the finite-volume per-bond ground-state energy density converges as `nвҶ’вҲһ` to the infinite-volume energy density `Оө_GS(d,N)` (existentially pinned to the genuine limit вҖ” the thermodynamic-limit existence is the deep analytic content) | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxModel.lean` |
| `boxGroundEnergyDensitySLimit` / `boxGroundEnergyDensityS_tendsto_limit` / `IsAFMThermodynamicLimit` / `afmThermodynamicLimit_energyDensity` / `afmThermodynamicLimit_exists_omega0` | **thermodynamic-limit bridge** (Issue #4564 capstone): names the box energy-density limit `Оө_GS(d,N)` (`boxGroundEnergyDensitySLimit`, proved to be the limit); the **documented predicate** `IsAFMThermodynamicLimit S N` (`S` is the `LвҶ‘вҲһ` limit of the concrete box AFM model вҖ” not an identification of matrix algebras with the abstract C*-algebra); the **documented AXIOM** `afmThermodynamicLimit_energyDensity` (such `S`'s abstract energy density = the concrete box limit, Tasaki eq. (4.3.4)); the **PROVED** `afmThermodynamicLimit_exists_omega0` (symmetric infinite-volume ground state `Пү_0` exists for the box AFM model's limit, via Theorem 4.20, eqs. (4.3.7)/(4.3.9)); and the **PROVED** `afmThermodynamicLimit_exists_omegaN` (the symmetry-breaking ground states `Пү_n` with NГ©el magnetization `(вҲ’1)^x mвҲ— n_Оұ`, given `HasStaggeredLRO`, eqs. (4.3.8)/(4.3.10) вҖ” vacuous in 1D) | `Quantum/SpinS/HypercubicBoxThermodynamicLimit.lean` |

(4.3.2)) and its odd complement (B-sublattice) | `Lattice/HypercubicLattice.lean` |

| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_eq` | for `N = 1` (the 2-site open chain on `Fin 2`), `H_open = -2J В· spinHalfDot 0 1` (explicit one-bond reduction; Tasaki В§2.4 simplest concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=1) В· |вҶ‘вҶ‘вҹ© = -(J/2) В· |вҶ‘вҶ‘вҹ©` вҖ” `|вҶ‘вҶ‘вҹ©` lies in the `S = 1` triplet sector and is an exact eigenvector with eigenvalue `-J/2` (this is the ferromagnetic ground state for `J < 0`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_singlet` | `H_open(N=1) В· (|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© - |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©) = (3J/2) В· (|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© - |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©)` вҖ” singlet eigenvalue, the antiferromagnetic ground state for `J > 0` (Tasaki В§2.5 concrete instance) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `H_open(N=1) В· |вҶ“вҶ“вҹ© = -(J/2) В· |вҶ“вҶ“вҹ©` вҖ” all-down state has the same eigenvalue as all-up (both are `S = 1` triplet states) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_two_site_mulVec_basisVec_triplet_zero` | `H_open(N=1) В· (|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© + |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©) = -(J/2) В· (|вҶ‘вҶ“вҹ© + |вҶ“вҶ‘вҹ©)` вҖ” triplet `m = 0` state, completing the 3-fold degenerate triplet representation `S = 1` with eigenvalue `-J/2` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_eq` | for `N = 2` (the 3-site open chain on `Fin 3`, 2 bonds), `H_open = -2J В· (spinHalfDot 0 1 + spinHalfDot 1 2)` вҖ” explicit two-bond reduction | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_three_site_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `H_open(N=2) В· |вҶ‘вҶ‘вҶ‘вҹ© = -J В· |вҶ‘вҶ‘вҶ‘вҹ©` вҖ” confirming the linear scaling `E(|вҶ‘..вҶ‘вҹ©) = -NВ·J/2` (here `N = 2` bonds, `J = 1` per bond) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_sum_eq` | for any `N : в„•`, `ОЈ_{x,y вҲҲ Fin (N+1)} openChainCoupling N J x y = -(2N В· J : в„Ӯ)` (the bond-counting lemma: each of the `N` unordered nearest-neighbour bonds is counted in both orientations) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | for any `N : в„•`, `J : в„қ`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H_open В· |s..sвҹ© = -(NВ·J/2 : в„Ӯ) В· |s..sвҹ©` вҖ” both `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) share the same eigenvalue by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up` | `s = 0` specialisation of the above (Tasaki В§2.4 (2.4.5)/(2.4.1) ferromagnetic ground-state energy `E_GS = -|B|В·SВІ` for `S = 1/2`, `|B| = N` bonds) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_down` | `s = 1` specialisation: same eigenvalue `-(NВ·J/2)` for the all-down state by SU(2) symmetry | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_all_up` | for any `N : в„•`, `J : в„қ`, `k : в„•`, `H_open В· ((Еңtot^-)^k В· |вҶ‘..вҶ‘вҹ©) = -(NВ·J/2 : в„Ӯ) В· ((Еңtot^-)^k В· |вҶ‘..вҶ‘вҹ©)` вҖ” the unnormalised Tasaki В§2.4 (2.4.9) ferromagnetic ground states `|ОҰ_Mвҹ©` made explicit on the chain (combines PRs #82 + #98) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_all_down` | dual ladder from `|вҶ“..вҶ“вҹ©`, same eigenvalue `-(NВ·J/2)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_open` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainHeisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | `H_periodic` preserves every magnetisation subspace `H_M` (chain specialisation of PR #91) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |

### Perron-Frobenius theorem (`Math/PerronFrobenius.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean`, `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean`)

Perron-Frobenius theorem for nonneg irreducible/primitive matrices (Issue #405, closed).
The sorry in `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` is eliminated via the Collatz-Wielandt port (PRs AвҖ“C).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.IsPrimitive.of_irreducible_pos_diagonal` | irreducible nonneg + positive diagonal вҶ’ primitive (Seneta В§1.1, Prop. 1.3, p. 17) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.collatzWielandtFn` | CW function `min_{i\|x_i>0} (Ax)_i/x_i` (Seneta В§1.2, p. 27) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_any_ratio` | `CW(x) вүӨ (Ax)_i/x_i` for `x_i > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_mulVec` | fundamental inequality `CW(x)В·x вүӨ Ax` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.smul_eq` | scale invariance `CW(cx) = CW(x)` for `c > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.upperSemicontinuousOn` | CW is upper-semicontinuous on stdSimplex | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.exists_maximizer` | CW attains its max on stdSimplex (EVT for USC, Seneta В§1.2, p. 28) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.eq_eigenvalue` | `CW(v) = r` when `Av = rВ·v`, `v > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.lt_of_all_ratios_gt` | all ratios `> c` вҹ№ `CW(x) > c` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.pos_of_nonneg_eigenvec` | irreducible nonneg + `Av = Ојv`, `v вүҘ 0`, `v вү  0` вҹ№ `v > 0` (standard propagation argument) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_primitive` | primitive nonneg вҹ№ вҲғ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta В§1.2, pp. 27вҖ“28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_irreducible` | irreducible nonneg вҹ№ вҲғ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta В§1.2, pp. 27вҖ“28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry-free**) irreducible nonneg Hermitian вҹ№ max eigenvalue has strictly positive eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `pos_eigenvec_unique` | strictly positive eigenvector unique up to positive scalar | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `PerronFrobenius.eigenvec_proportional_of_pos_eigenvec` | **geometric simplicity of the Perron eigenvalue**: irreducible nonneg with a strictly positive eigenvector `v` at `Ој` вҹ№ every real eigenvector at `Ој` is `s вҖў v` for some scalar `s` (the `Ој`-eigenspace is 1-dimensional). Perturbation trick: `v + t вҖў u > 0` for small `t > 0` is an eigenvector at `Ој`, hence `вҲқ v` by `pos_eigenvec_unique`, forcing `u вҲқ v`. Used to show a Perron ground state is a joint eigenvector of any commuting operator (e.g. the total Casimir) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusSimple.lean` |

References: E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains* (3rd ed.), Springer 2006, В§1.2 (pp. 27вҖ“28);
or4nge19/MCMC: `MCMC/PF/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PerronFrobenius/`.

### Spin-`S` MarshallвҖ“LiebвҖ“Mattis on the magnetization sector (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.2 generic S, sector form)

> **Route correction (2026-05-26).** The earlier "saturated-ladder-iterate"
> route toward `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` was found **unsound**: it reduced the
> theorem to a leaf hypothesis (lowerable attach-sum dominance) that is **false**
> вҖ” the ferromagnetic ladder iterate `(ЕңвҒ»_tot)^M|all-upвҹ©` is *not* the
> Marshall-positive antiferromagnetic ground state (concrete counterexample:
> spin-1/2, `V={a,b}`, `A={a}`, `B={b}`, `M=0вҶ’1`). The 40 conditional-wrapper
> modules of that route were removed; their detailed rows below were deleted with
> them. The **sound** route keeps the per-sector PerronвҖ“Frobenius ground state
> (`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_groundState_full`, sorry-free) and chains sectors by
> SU(2) invariance `[ДӨ, ЕңвҒ»_tot] = 0`; some rows below still reference helper
> lemmas from the removed route pending re-documentation. See
> `.self-local/docs/tasaki-2-5-pf-route-design.md` and Issue #3542.

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.2 applied to the
**magnetization-`M` sector** of the un-dressed antiferromagnetic
Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a bipartite graph. The sector subtype
`magConfigS V N M := { Пғ : V вҶ’ Fin (N + 1) // magSumS Пғ = M }` is the
natural index type since the dressed Heisenberg matrix is irreducible
on each sector. The core sector-matrix API lives in
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSector.lean`; the trailing
eigenvalue-uniqueness + complex-form MLM Theorem 2.2 statements
were moved to the companion
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSectorEigenvalueUnique.lean` in
refactor #30 (PR #2829). The two bundled sector-level MLM Theorem 2.2
ground-state results (`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_groundState`,
`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full`) were
subsequently relocated to
`Quantum/SpinS/MarshallLiebMattisSectorBundled.lean` (#4569, re-proved via the
canonical `*Structural*` twins). Tracked in Issue #412.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (lowering)**: if a magnetization-`M` sector vector `magSectorEmbedding ОҰ` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at energy `Ој` and a total-Casimir eigenvector at `Оі` away from the sector's lowering-kernel value `(cardВ·N/2вҲ’M)(cardВ·N/2вҲ’MвҲ’1)`, then `ЕңвҒ»_tot В· magSectorEmbedding ОҰ` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the **same** `Ој` (via `[ДӨ, ЕңвҒ»_tot]=0`), is non-zero (total-Casimir non-vanishing criterion), and lies in the next magnetization sector. This is the energy-preserving step that chains the per-sector PerronвҖ“Frobenius ground states (`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_groundState_full`) across the admissible range; it uses **no** Marshall positivity of the lowered vector (the false hypothesis of the removed ferromagnetic-ladder route). Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred` / `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred_of_mem_predictedGS` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (raising)** and its predicted-GS specialisation: the raising companions of `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ(_of_mem_predictedGS)`. `ЕңвҒә_tot` maps a sector-`K` Heisenberg eigenvector (total-Casimir away from the raising-kernel value `(cardВ·N/2вҲ’K)(cardВ·N/2вҲ’K+1)`) to a non-zero same-energy eigenvector in the previous sector; predicted-GS membership in sector `M+1` strictly above the left endpoint discharges the Casimir hypotheses via `tasaki23_predictedCasimirValue_ne_raising_kernel_value_of_mem_of_left_lt`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagSubspaceS` / `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS` (with `mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_iff`, `basisVecS_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`, `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_disjoint`) | **Sublattice magnetization grading**: the `Ең_A^(3)`-eigenspaces `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` and the diagonal eigenvalue `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A Пғ = вҲ‘_{xвҲҲA}(N/2вҲ’Пғ_x)`. Disjointness of distinct eigenvalues and basis-state membership (Submodule membership uses mathlib's standard `.zero_mem`/`.add_mem`/`.smul_mem`/вҖҰ directly вҖ” no dedicated wrapper lemmas). Sublattice analogue of `magSubspaceS`/`magEigenvalueS`; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 5) for the sublattice highest-weight argument bounding `(Ең_A)ВІ`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagnetization.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_sublattice_casimir_re_le` | **Sublattice Casimir bounds for the toy ground state**: the per-sector Marshall-positive toy GS is a joint Casimir eigenvector (#3657) whose `(Ең_A)ВІ`/`(Ең_В¬A)ВІ` eigenvalues `Оі_A, Оі_B` obey `Оі_A.re вүӨ s_A(s_A+1)`, `Оі_B.re вүӨ s_B(s_B+1)` (apply the spectral max bound #3672 to the non-zero Marshall-dressed embedding). Discharges two hypotheses of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 3). Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23StructuralPFJointCasimir.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_sector_magnetization_re_eq_predicted` | **Extremal sector realizes the predicted total spin**: for the left-endpoint sector `M = min(|A|,|В¬A|)В·N`, the magnetization eigenvalue `|V|В·N/2 вҲ’ M` has real part `= tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N = |s_A вҲ’ s_B|`. Supplies the extremal-sector hypothesis `hM` of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 4). Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ExtremalSector.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_of_energy_le` | **Toy ground state is the predicted-Casimir witness (modulo the energy bound)**: in the extremal sector `M = min(|A|,|В¬A|)В·N`, the Marshall-positive toy GS with toy energy `Ој вүӨ predicted вҲ’ s_A(s_A+1) вҲ’ s_B(s_B+1)` is a `(Ең_tot)ВІ`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. Capstone assembling the joint eigenvector (#3657), sublattice bounds (#3677), toy energy formula (#3673), extremal sector (#3678), the two-sided pin (#3676), and Hermitian eigenvalue realness вҖ” exactly the witness form required by #3656. The single remaining obligation is the reference energy bound `hОј` (Issue #3674, PR 5). Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyWitness.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness` | **Toy ground energy is minimal in its sector**: the Marshall-positive toy ground state (energy `Ој`) has `Ој вүӨ E` for every sector eigenvector `ПҶ вү  0` of the toy Heisenberg sector matrix at real energy `E`. Specialises `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive` to `J = bipartiteCoupling A`; reduces the reference energy bound `hОј` (and hence the whole route, via #3679) to exhibiting one sector eigenvector at the predicted ground energy `predicted вҲ’ s_A(s_A+1) вҲ’ s_B(s_B+1)` (Issue #3674, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyGroundEnergyBound.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_total_casimir_re_eq_predicted_of_bounds` (with `tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin_nonneg`) | **Toy total-Casimir pin (extremal sector)**: in the extremal magnetization sector `M.re = |s_AвҲ’s_B|`, a non-zero `(Ең_tot)ВІ`-eigenvector at `Оі_tot` with sublattice Casimir max bounds `Оі_A вүӨ s_A(s_A+1)`, `Оі_B вүӨ s_B(s_B+1)` and reference toy energy bound `(Оі_totвҲ’Оі_AвҲ’Оі_B).re вүӨ predicted вҲ’ s_A(s_A+1) вҲ’ s_B(s_B+1)` has `Оі_tot.re = predicted`. A two-sided squeeze (magnetization lower bound #3675 + sublattice upper bounds #3672 + energy formula #3673), isolating the extremal-sector and reference-energy obligations as hypotheses; no ClebschвҖ“Gordan (Issue #3674, PR 2). Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyCasimirPin.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_ge_of_mem_magSubspaceS` (with `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector_ge`) | **Total-Casimir magnetization lower bound** `(Ең_tot)ВІ вүҘ m(m+1)`: a non-zero `(Ең_tot)ВІ`-eigenvector at `Оі` in `magSubspaceS V N M` with `0 вүӨ M.re` has `M.reВ·(M.re+1) вүӨ Оі.re`. The total spin dominates its `z`-projection: raising with `ЕңвҒә_tot` only increases the magnetization, so the highest weight `M'` reached has `M' вүҘ M`, giving `Оі = M'(M'+1) вүҘ M(M+1)`. Lower-bound companion of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; in the extremal sector `m = s_AвҲ’s_B` it pins the toy GS total Casimir to predicted, bypassing the ClebschвҖ“Gordan triangle inequality (Issue #3674, PR 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirSpectralLowerBound.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_joint_casimir_eigenvector` | **Toy energy on a joint Casimir eigenvector**: a simultaneous eigenvector of `(Ең_tot)ВІ`, `(Ең_A)ВІ`, `(Ең_В¬A)ВІ` at `(Оі_tot, Оі_A, Оі_B)` is a `ДӨ_toy`-eigenvector at `Оі_tot вҲ’ Оі_A вҲ’ Оі_B` (from `ДӨ_toy = (Ең_tot)ВІвҲ’(Ең_A)ВІвҲ’(Ең_В¬A)ВІ`). The energy formula underlying the variational identification of the toy ground state's predicted total Casimir (Issue #3658, PR 4 step 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianJointEnergy.lean`) |

Hence `finrank в„Ӯ (eig M ОҪ вҠ“ ker(P=(-1)^p)) вүӨ finrank в„Ӯ (eig M.submatrix ОҪ)` for `p < 2`. The bridge for lifting the per-block PF bound (#3831) to the full eigenspace вҠ“ parity ker. | `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one` (with `..._apply_eq_zero_of_magSum_parity_ne`, `..._commute_magParityDiagS`, `..._eigenspace_finrank_le_parity_blocks`) | **(g.1) dressed `ДӨ'` eigenspace `finrank в„Ӯ вүӨ 2` via parity-block decomposition** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3833): the dressed axis-swapped Hamiltonian (i) inherits parity-block-diagonality from the bare via `dressed Пғ П„ = m_A(Пғ) m_A(П„) * bare Пғ П„`, (ii) commutes with `P = magParityDiagS` entrywise (since `Оҳ_A` and `P` are both diagonal, hence the entire `Оҳ_A ДӨ' Оҳ_A` commutes with `P`), (iii) so `eigenspace_finrank_le_of_commuting_involution` (#3776) applies and the eigenspace splits across `Вұ1` parity blocks. Given per-block `вүӨ 1` bounds, full dressed eig `вүӨ 2`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43вҖ“44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/DressedAxisSwapDegeneracyBound.lean`) |
| `dressed_full_eigenspace_inter_parity_finrank_le_one` | **(g.2) dressed `ДӨ'` per-block full-eigenspace `finrank в„Ӯ вүӨ 1` at the PF ОҪ** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3834): combines #3831 (parity-submatrix eig вүӨ 1) with #3832 (block-diag bridge) under the dressed parity-block-diagonality (#3833 step 1). Result: `вҲғ ОҪ : в„қ, finrank в„Ӯ (eig dressed_ДӨ' ОҪ вҠ“ ker(P=(-1)^p)) вүӨ 1` for `p < 2`. The per-block input consumed by the dressed eig вүӨ 2 assembly. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43вҖ“44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/DressedFullEigInterParityLeOne.lean`) |
| `matrix_similar_eigenspace_inter_finrank_eq` | **(g.3) similarity-invariant intersection eigenspace finrank** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3835): generalises `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq` (#3746 family) to intersections with a second operator's eigenspace, provided the similarity `U` commutes with that operator `Q`. Given `U * Uinv = 1`, `Uinv * U = 1`, `H' = Uinv * H * U`, AND `Q * U = U * Q`, then `finrank в„Ӯ (eig H' Ој вҠ“ ker(Q=c)) = finrank в„Ӯ (eig H Ој вҠ“ ker(Q=c))`. The core technical step for transferring per-parity-block PF bounds from the dressed `ДӨ'_dressed` to the bare `ДӨ'`, since the Marshall sign diagonal `Оҳ_A` commutes with the diagonal `magParityDiagS P`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43вҖ“44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/GaugeIntersectionEigenspaceFinrank.lean`) |
| `bare_axisSwapped_full_eigenspace_inter_parity_finrank_le_one` (with `marshallDiagonal_commute_magParityDiagS`) | **(g.4) bare `ДӨ'` per-block PF `finrank в„Ӯ вүӨ 1` via Marshall similarity** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3836): transfers the dressed `ДӨ'_dressed` per-block bound (#3834) to the bare axis-swapped `ДӨ'` via the Marshall similarity for intersected eigenspaces (#3835). Key observation: the Marshall sign diagonal `Оҳ_A` and the magnetization parity diagonal `P = magParityDiagS` are both diagonal and hence commute, so the intersection `eig ДӨ' Ој вҠ“ ker(P=c)` is preserved by the Marshall similarity dressed вҶ’ bare. Result for `p < 2`: `вҲғ ОҪ : в„қ, finrank в„Ӯ (eig (axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS J lam D N) (ОҪ : в„Ӯ) вҠ“ ker(P=(-1)^p)) вүӨ 1`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43вҖ“44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BareAxisSwapFullEigInterParityLeOne.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one` (with `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one`) | **(g.5) bare anisotropic `ДӨ` eigenspace `finrank в„Ӯ вүӨ 2` (conditional, via axis-swap)** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3837): wraps the conditional `bare ДӨ' eig вүӨ 2 from per-block вүӨ 1` (`axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_blocks_le_one`, #3776 family) with the axis-swap unitary equivalence (`AxisSwapUnitaryS.anisotropic_axisSwapped_eigenspace_finrank_eq`, #3753) to give the same conditional bound for the bare anisotropic Hamiltonian `ДӨ`. General `N` version requires an explicit `AxisSwapUnitaryS N` instance; spin-1/2 (`N = 1`) instance via `axisSwapUnitarySpinHalf`. This **closes the bare `ДӨ` вүӨ 2 link in the (g) chain**, conditional on per-parity-block PF hypotheses (discharged separately per sector by #3836). The remaining gap to unconditional obligation (1) is the ОҪ-sector matching (PF eigenvalues of even/odd sectors coinciding at the ground-state energy), which is mathematically distinct (Hermitian spectral minimisation) and deferred. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43вҖ“44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BareAnisotropicEigLeTwoConditional.lean`) |
| `parity_block_submatrix_full_inter_finrank_eq` (with `parity_block_submatrix_eigenspace_finrank_le_full_inter`, `parityEmbedMap`) | **(h.1) reverse block-diag bridge** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3840): for a parity-block-diagonal complex `M`, the parity-`p` submatrix's ОҪ-eigenspace embeds INJECTIVELY into `eig M ОҪ вҠ“ ker(P=(-1)^p)` via the zero-extension map (extend by 0 off `parityConfigS p`). Combined with the forward direction (#3832), gives the finrank equality `finrank в„Ӯ (eig M.submatrix ОҪ) = finrank в„Ӯ (eig M ОҪ вҠ“ ker(P=(-1)^p))` for `p < 2`. The equality unlocks ОҪ-sector matching arguments: per-block submatrix eig finrank equals the full eig вҠ“ ker(P) finrank, so per-sector PF simplicity (#3834/#3836) directly transfers to constraints on the FULL eigenspace at that energy. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43вҖ“44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/BlockDiagSubmatrixEmbed.lean`) |
| `eigenspace_finrank_eq_of_commuting_involution` | **(h.2) involution eigenspace decomposition вҖ” finrank EQUALITY** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3841): strengthens `eigenspace_finrank_le_of_commuting_involution` (#3776) from `вүӨ` to `=`. For finite-dim `T, P` with `TвҲҳP = PвҲҳT` and `PвҲҳP = id`, the `T`-eigenspace at any `Ој` splits as a **direct sum** across the two `P`-eigenspaces `Вұ1`. The `вүӨ` was tight because the two intersected `Вұ1` eigenspaces of `P` are disjoint (a single eigenvector cannot have two distinct `P`-eigenvalues), so `Submodule.finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq` collapses to a sum (inf = 0). Used by (h.3) to derive the block-sum equality. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43вҖ“44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/InvolutionEigenspaceDecompEq.lean`) |
| `matrix_eigenspace_finrank_eq_sum_parity_blocks` (with `dressedAxisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._sum_parity_blocks`, `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_..._sum_parity_blocks`) | **(h.3) block-sum finrank equality** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3842): combines (h.1) #3840 and (h.2) #3841 to give the block-sum equality for any parity-block-diagonal `M` commuting with `magParityDiagS`: `finrank в„Ӯ (eig M Ој) = finrank в„Ӯ (eig M.submatrix_0 Ој) + finrank в„Ӯ (eig M.submatrix_1 Ој)`. Specialised to the dressed `ДӨ'_dressed` and the bare `ДӨ'` (both block-diagonal and commuting with `P`). Central tool for unconditional `вүӨ 2` at any Ој where both per-block bounds hold simultaneously. Combined with PF (per-sector simplicity at the per-sector spectrum minimum) and a Hermitian spectral identification of the GS energy as `min(ОҪ_0, ОҪ_1)`, gives the unconditional bare `ДӨ'` GS degeneracy `вүӨ 2`. The PR exposes no-argument wrappers for the axis-swap eigenspace-dimension equality, the conditional parity-block and submatrix-block `вүӨ 2` bounds, the min-block `вүӨ 2` bound, the Hermitian-minimum equality, and the structural general-N capstone `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_unconditional_general`. This discharges the explicit axis-swap-unitary argument in the strict case-(i) degeneracy chain; the SU(2)-uniqueness target wrappers are handled in the later obligation-(2) row, and Tasaki case (ii) now has conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges recorded in the case-(ii) rows, with the strict-gap derivation still explicit. | `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_sector_pf` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_balanced_sector_pf_at_target` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_balanced_eq_full` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_balanced_eq_full` | **General spin-S target conditional bridge with balanced-sector PF discharged** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): packages the final linear-algebra step after the general spin-S `finrank вүӨ 2` degeneracy capstone. If the target full ground eigenspace has `finrank вүӨ 2`, the balanced sector attains the full ground energy, and the balanced-sector ground eigenspace has `finrank вүӨ 1`, then the full target ground eigenspace has `finrank вүӨ 1`; the companion wrapper then gives zero total `ЕңВі` magnetization for every non-zero target ground state. The new general spin-S PF wrapper chooses a finite strict diagonal shift and applies the existing anisotropic sector PF/minimum theorem, so the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit balanced-sector PF callback. The direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input is superseded by the strict-gap wrappers in the next row; for case (ii), the conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges are recorded in the case-(ii) rows and the strict-gap derivation remains explicit. These first case-(ii) wrappers keep the remaining target inputs explicit as balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2`; they do not reuse the case-(i) axis-swap sign argument. This is a case-(ii) bridge step, not the final case-(ii) endpoint. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIStrictGapBridge.lean`) |
| `anisotropicHeisenbergS_groundState_mem_balanced_sector_of_strict_gap` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_strict_gap_no_full_le_two` | **General spin-S target bridge from strict sector gap without the full `finrank <= 2` input** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): proves that if the balanced sector attains the full ground energy and every non-balanced non-empty magnetization sector has strictly larger minimum, then every full ground vector lies in the balanced magnetization sector. This uses the sector-projection eigenvector bridge and `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min`, not the case-(i) parity-block `finrank <= 2` contradiction. Composing this containment with balanced-sector Perron--Frobenius simplicity gives full target uniqueness and zero total magnetization from strict sector gap alone; the case-(ii)-named wrappers keep only the theorem-facing `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0` hypotheses plus the strict-gap input. This removes the explicit case-(ii) full `finrank <= 2` input; deriving the case-(ii) strict gap remains future work. If every non-balanced target crossing `E_M <= E_balanced` contradicts the case-(ii) hypotheses `1 <= lambda`, `D <= 0`, then order duality gives the strict gap `E_balanced < E_M` for every non-balanced non-empty sector. The target wrappers feed that strict gap to the no-full-finrank bridge above, so the public endpoint surface no longer exposes either balanced/full equality, full `finrank <= 2`, or strict gap directly; the remaining mathematical task is proving the crossing contradiction callback. Therefore, if every non-balanced non-empty sector has contradictory crossing-set non-emptiness, every path crossing is impossible, and the target wrappers feed that path contradiction to the path-callback bridge above. The target wrappers therefore leave the remaining case-(ii) mathematical input as a path-global `finrank <= 2` theorem, rather than a selected-sector `sInf` hypothesis. | `caseII_path_global_finrank_bound_of_axisSwapped_submatrix_blocks_le_one` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_axisSwapped_submatrix_blocks_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_axisSwapped_submatrix_blocks_path` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from pathwise axis-swapped parity-block simplicity** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): derives the path-global full ground eigenspace `finrank <= 2` input from the standard block-sum API. At each path time `t вҲҲ Icc 0 1`, the arbitrary-eigenvalue wrapper `anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_of_submatrix_blocks_le_one_general` is applied with `Ој` equal to the full Hermitian minimum, so two axis-swapped parity-block submatrix `finrank <= 1` hypotheses imply the full pathwise `finrank <= 2` bound. The target wrappers feed this callback to the path-global bridge above. The remaining case-(ii) mathematical input is now pathwise axis-swapped parity-block submatrix simplicity at the full ground energy. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (files `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIBlockPathFinrankCore.lean` for the path-global finrank bound + `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIBlockPathFinrank.lean` for the case-(ii) target wrappers, split for build speed) |
| `axisSwappedParityBlockPFMinAt` / `axisSwappedParityBlockPFMinPath` / `axisSwappedParityBlockStrictRawSupportPath` / `axisSwappedParityBlockLambdaOneRawSupportPath` / `axisSwappedParityBlockDZeroRawSupportPath` / `exists_parityBlock_dressed_diag_strict_upper_bound` / `axisSwappedParityBlockStrictRawSupportPath_of_reachability` / `axisSwappedParityBlockLambdaOneRawSupportPath_of_reachability` / `axisSwappedParityBlockDZeroRawSupportPath_of_reachability` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_finrank_le_one_at_full_min_of_pf_min` / `caseII_axisSwapped_submatrix_blocks_path_of_pf_min` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_pf_min_path_of_caseII_raw_support` / `caseII_axisSwapped_parityBlockPFMinPath_of_raw_support` / `caseII_axisSwapped_submatrix_blocks_path_of_raw_support_pf_min` / `caseII_axisSwapped_parityBlockPFMinPath_of_reachability` / `caseII_axisSwapped_submatrix_blocks_path_of_reachability_pf_min` / `caseII_coupling_eq_zero_of_not_bipartiteCompleteGraph_adj` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_block_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_block_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_raw_support_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_raw_support_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_finrank_le_one_of_reachability_pf_min_path` / `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_zero_magnetization_of_reachability_pf_min_path` | **General spin-S case-(ii) target bridge from pathwise parity-block PF/min callbacks** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): turns the previous row's full-ground-energy parity-block simplicity input into the existing bare-submatrix conditional form. For each parity block, if a PF eigenvalue `ОҪ` has `finrank <= 1` and equals that block's Hermitian minimum, then the block has `finrank <= 1` at the full anisotropic ground energy: if the full ground energy equals the block minimum, this is the conditional theorem; if it is lower, `hermitian_eigenspace_eq_bot_of_real_lt_min` makes the block eigenspace zero. The path wrapper supplies both parity blocks along `Оі(t)`, and the target wrappers feed the result to the block-path bridge above. The raw-support abbreviations name the strict, `lambda = 1`, and `D = 0` reachability/PF inputs for one parity block. The one-block selector chooses among the fixed raw-support consumers by the case-(ii) path inequalities and leaves only the corner `(lambda,D)=(1,0)` PF/min callback explicit; the even/odd raw-support wrapper applies this selector to both parity blocks, and the full-min wrapper feeds those callbacks into the block-path PF/min transfer. The raw-support target wrappers derive the support-zero hypothesis from `hJself` and `hJbip`, then expose target uniqueness and zero magnetization directly from the raw-support input surface. The reachability wrappers use finite parity blocks to supply strict diagonal shifts automatically, so the public reachability-level target wrappers require only the even and odd strict, ion-only, and bond-only reachability totality hypotheses plus the two explicit corner callbacks. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (foundational parity-block PF/min path lemmas in `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIBlockPFMinCore.lean`; the packaged `caseII_coupling_eq_zero_of_not_bipartiteCompleteGraph_adj` helper and the six `anisotropicHeisenbergS_case_ii_target_*` endpoints in `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIBlockPFMin.lean`) |

The bipartite complete graph totality wrappers then supply the block-level reachability hypotheses from `hA_ne`, `hB_ne`, and the spin-size hypotheses (`1 <= N` for strict and bond-only, `2 <= N` for ion-only). The final wrappers feed those totality results into the case-(ii) reachability-level PF/min bridge; the later corner and total-reachability target bridges reuse this layer to close the current `2 <= N` case-(ii) endpoint. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (files `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIBlockReachabilityCore.lean` for the lifts + totality reachability bridge + `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIBlockReachability.lean` for the case-(ii) wrappers, split for build speed) |

Away from the corner, the pointwise selector uses the strict, ion-only, or bond-only raw-support PF/min consumer together with the total reachability bridge; at the corner, the path-global full `finrank <= 2` input is supplied directly from a full SU(2)-point ground-eigenspace `finrank <= 1` callback. This avoids imposing the stronger and unnecessary assertion that each whole parity block is PF-simple at its own block minimum at the SU(2) point. The MLM/Casimir/Theorem 2.3 endpoint supplies full SU(2)-point uniqueness; the same uniqueness input is reused for the exact-corner `finrank <= 2` branch, for the balanced SU(2) sector/full-ground equality, and for the strict non-balanced sector gap at `t = 0`. Thus, for `2 <= N`, total reachability on the bipartite complete graph now feeds target uniqueness and zero magnetization directly, modulo the standard balanced-sector bookkeeping and Theorem 2.3 endpoint data. The wrapper dispatches `-1 < О» < 1`, `D >= 0` to the `D_nonneg` case-(i) endpoint; `О» = 1`, `D >= 0` to the explicit SU(2) endpoint when `D = 0` and to the `lambda_one_D_pos` endpoint when `D > 0`; and `О» >= 1`, `D <= 0` to the case-(ii) total-reachability endpoint. The second theorem applies the packaged uniqueness statement and the existing uniqueness-implies-zero-magnetization theorem, yielding `Ең^3_tot ОҰ = 0` for every non-zero target ground state in the covered parameter region. Hence the non-corner path never enters the `О» = 1`, `D < 0` ion-only branch, whose totality requires `2 <= N`; strict parity reachability and the `D = 0` bond-only branch both need only `1 <= N`. The new spin-`1/2` target wrappers combine this strict case-(ii) route with the existing `D >= 0` case-(i) endpoint and the spin-`1/2` `О» = 1` scalar-shift endpoint, giving the exact `N = 1` region wrapper for `-1 < О» < 1`, `D >= 0`; `О» = 1`; and `1 < О»`, `D <= 0`. | `caseIIParityGaugeSign` / `caseIIParityGaugeSign_mul_eq_one_of_magSumS_eq` / `caseIIParityGaugeSign_mul_eq_neg_one_of_magSumS_add_two` / `caseIIParityGaugeSign_mul_eq_neg_one_of_add_two_magSumS` / `caseIIParityGaugeDiagonalOnParity_mul_self` | **General spin-S case-(ii) parity-block gauge sign layer** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): introduces the additional parity-block gauge `(-1)^(magSumS / 2)` needed for the `О» >= 1`, `D <= 0` PF route. On a fixed parity block it agrees with Tasaki's `(-1)^((magSumS - p) / 2)` convention up to a constant block sign. The gauge product is `1` when `magSumS` is preserved and `-1` when one configuration has `magSumS` two larger than the other, so it preserves transverse raise-lower signs while flipping the parity-bond and single-ion `Вұ2` signs. The diagonal gauge squares to the identity and is ready to combine with the Marshall diagonal in the next shifted PF matrix layer. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIParityGauge.lean`) |
| `caseIIParityGaugeSignReal` / `caseIIParityGaugeSignReal_mul_self` / `caseIICombinedGaugeSignOnParity` / `caseIICombinedGaugeSignOnParity_mul_self` / `caseIICombinedGaugeDiagonalOnParity` / `caseIICombinedGaugeDiagonalOnParity_mul_self` / `caseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock` / `caseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_apply` / `caseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_apply_diag` / `caseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_isSymm_of_real` / `shiftedCaseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock` / `shiftedCaseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_apply` / `shiftedCaseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_apply_off_diag` / `shiftedCaseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_apply_diag` / `shiftedCaseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_diag_pos` / `shiftedCaseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_isSymm_of_real` | **General spin-S case-(ii) parity-gauged shifted matrix layer** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): combines the extra parity gauge with the Marshall-dressed axis-swapped real matrix on each parity block. The real combined gauge diagonal squares to the identity, the parity-gauged real block matrix has the same diagonal as the dressed matrix and is symmetric for real coupling data, and the shifted matrix `c вҖў 1 - R` has the expected diagonal/off-diagonal unfoldings plus strict diagonal positivity under a strict shift. The next case-(ii) PF steps are local sign transfer, reachability/irreducibility, and PF/min identification for this shifted matrix. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIParityGaugedMatrix.lean`) |
| `neg_one_pow_mul_self_real` / `neg_one_pow_succ_mul_self_real` / `neg_one_pow_mul_succ_self_real` / `caseIIParityGaugeSignReal_mul_eq_one_of_magSumS_eq` / `caseIIParityGaugeSignReal_mul_eq_neg_one_of_magSumS_add_two` / `caseIIParityGaugeSignReal_mul_eq_neg_one_of_add_two_magSumS` / `caseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_eq_of_magSumS_eq` / `caseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_eq_neg_of_magSumS_add_two` / `caseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_eq_neg_of_add_two_magSumS` / `caseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_nonpos_of_magSumS_eq` / `caseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_nonpos_of_magSumS_add_two` / `caseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_nonpos_of_add_two_magSumS` / `shiftedCaseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_nonneg` / `shiftedCaseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_pos_of_gauged_entry_neg` / `shiftedCaseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_pos_of_magSumS_eq` / `shiftedCaseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_pos_of_magSumS_add_two` / `shiftedCaseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_pos_of_add_two_magSumS` | **General spin-S case-(ii) parity-gauged sign-transfer layer** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): adds the real parity-gauge product API and structural sign-transfer lemmas for the case-(ii) shifted PF matrix. Equal-`magSumS` pairs leave the Marshall-dressed real entry unchanged, while +/-2 pairs negate it; dressed negativity on transverse moves and dressed positivity on parity-bond or single-ion moves therefore become parity-gauged off-diagonal non-positivity. A diagonal shift bound plus this off-diagonal non-positivity gives entrywise non-negativity of `c вҖў 1 - R`, and strict dressed signs transfer to strict shifted entries. The next local case-(ii) task is to prove those dressed signs under the strict local hypotheses (`-1 < lambda` for transverse moves, `1 < lambda` for parity-bond moves, and `D < 0` for single-ion moves), then discharge irreducibility and PF/min identification. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (files `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIParityGaugedSignsCore.lean` for the real parity-gauge products and parity-gauged entry sign transfer + `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIParityGaugedSigns.lean` for the shifted-matrix sign transfer, split for build speed) |

Transverse raise/lower moves use the positive `(1 + lambda.re) / 4` coefficient and the Marshall sign flip; parity-bond `Вұ2` moves use `1 < lambda.re`, the negative `(1 - lambda.re) / 4` coefficient, and the extra parity gauge; same-site single-ion `Вұ2` moves use `D.re < 0` against the negative `(S^2)^2` off-diagonal entry. The remaining case-(ii) shifted-matrix inputs are reachability/irreducibility and PF/min identification. The block relation is the reflexive-transitive closure of `ParityStepS` on `parityConfigS`; in the strict interior (`-1 < lambda.re`, `1 < lambda.re`, and `D.re < 0`) every elementary parity step gives a strictly positive shifted block entry. Entrywise non-negativity plus block reachability therefore gives a positive matrix-power entry, and reachability totality plus strict diagonal shift gives `Matrix.IsIrreducible`. The following support and boundary rows lower its remaining shifted-matrix inputs; the following PF/min row supplies the fixed-block conversion, including the zero-coefficient boundary consumers. Equal-`magSumS` off-diagonal block pairs use dressed non-positivity; target/source `magSumS` raised by two use dressed non-negativity plus the parity-gauge sign flip; all other off-diagonal block pairs are supplied as zero-support. A diagonal shift bound then gives entrywise non-negativity of the shifted parity-gauged block matrix, and the final wrapper feeds that non-negativity into conditional irreducibility. The following step-support, raw-support, and boundary rows supply the total-Hamiltonian support/sign split; the following PF/min row supplies the fixed-block conversion, including the zero-coefficient boundary consumers. At `lambda = 1`, the parity-bond coefficient vanishes, so the raw support classification reduces to ion-only support; the wrappers use transverse steps plus `SingleIonStepS`, with conditional irreducibility from `ionParityReachableSOnBlock`. At `D = 0`, the single-ion branch vanishes; the raw support classification reduces to bond-only support, and the wrappers use transverse steps plus `ParityBondStepS` with conditional irreducibility from `bondParityReachableSOnBlock`. The following PF/min row supplies the bare-block conversion for the strict and zero-coefficient boundary consumers. | `caseIIParityGaugeSign_eq_ofReal_real` / `caseIICombinedGaugeDiagonalOnParity_map_eq` / `caseIICombinedGaugeDiagonalOnParity_map_mul_self` / `caseIIParityGaugedAxisSwappedReMatrixOnParityBlock_map_eq_combinedGauge_conj_bare` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_pf_min_of_caseII_shifted_block` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_pf_min_of_caseII_raw_support` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_pf_min_of_caseII_raw_support_lambda_one` / `axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS_submatrix_pf_min_of_caseII_raw_support_D_zero` | **General spin-S case-(ii) parity-block PF/min bridge** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): converts the shifted case-(ii) parity-gauged real block into the bare axis-swapped complex parity-block PF/min callback. The combined parity/Marshall gauge diagonal is complexified and shown to square to the identity; the complexified parity-gauged block is similar to the bare axis-swapped block. Perron--Frobenius gives a one-dimensional positive eigenspace for the shifted non-negative irreducible real block, the shift changes the eigenvalue from `r` to `c - r`, Collatz--Wielandt identifies `c - r` with the Hermitian minimum, and similarity transfers the `finrank <= 1` bound to the bare block. Raw-support consumers cover the strict case-(ii) region, the `lambda = 1` ion-only boundary, and the `D = 0` bond-only boundary. The remaining pathwise task is to select and supply the appropriate strict or zero-coefficient reachability inputs along the case-(ii) deformation path. Tasaki, Springer 2020, В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, pp. 43--44 (file `Quantum/SpinS/AnisotropicHeisenbergSpinSCaseIIParityBlockPFMin.lean`) |

The spin-1/2 target wrapper chooses a diagonal-dominating shift and supplies the balanced-sector PF bound directly, so the public target uniqueness and zero-magnetization wrappers no longer require an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` hypothesis. | `singleIonStepS_spinHalf_false` / `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_eigenspace_finrank_le_two_at_global_min_D_nonneg` / `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_obligation_2_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg` / `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_balanced_eq_full_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg` / `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg` / `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_groundState_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg` | **Spin-1/2 case-(i) `D вүҘ 0` boundary extension** (Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #412): specializes the parity-block PerronвҖ“Frobenius route to spin `1/2`, where the single-ion `Вұ2` parity step is impossible. The raise/lower and parity-bond positivity inputs only require `D.re вүҘ 0`, so the parity-block irreducibility, global `finrank в„Ӯ вүӨ 2`, deformation contradiction, balanced-sector ground-energy equality, and final target uniqueness/zero-magnetization wrappers extend from `D > 0` to `D вүҘ 0` while retaining `-1 < О» < 1`. The strict parity-block route does not cover the `О» = 1` edge; the following row records the separate scalar-shift boundary. Case (ii) now has conditional, strict-gap, and no-full-finrank bridges, with the strict-gap derivation still explicit; the general spin-S `D >= 0` endpoint is recorded in the general boundary row above. The hypothesis bundle and conclusion match the per-sector bundled Theorem 2.2 `marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergHamiltonianS_groundState_full` (#869) exactly вҖ” real symmetric coupling (`(J x y).im = 0`, `star (J x y) = J x y`, `J x y = J y x`, `0 вүӨ (J x y).re`), bipartite support, positivity on `bipartiteCompleteGraphOf A`, non-empty sublattices, a spectral shift `c` strictly above the dressed diagonal, the #869 intermediate-existence hypothesis, plus sector non-emptiness вҖ” and asserts existence of a common GS energy `Ој` realised on every admissible sector `M вҲҲ tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N` by a Marshall-positive eigenvector (Tasaki (2.5.4) with `Пғ = M`), with within-sector uniqueness up to positive scalar, plus global minimality of `Ој`. Together with `tasaki23PredictedDegeneracy A N = \|\|A\| вҲ’ \|В¬A\|\|В·N + 1 = 2 S_tot + 1` (cardinality of `tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N`), this packages the predicted total spin `S_tot = \|\|A\| вҲ’ \|В¬A\|\|В·N/2` and the predicted `2 S_tot + 1` degeneracy. Proof: iterate #869 across `tasaki23GroundStateSectors A N`. | `totalSpinSOp3_mulVec_apply_eq_magEigenvalueS_mul` / `magSectorEmbedding_mem_magSubspaceS` / `magSubspaceS_apply_eq_zero_of_magSumS_ne` / `magSectorEmbedding_magSectorRestriction_of_mem_magSubspaceS` / `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_magSectorEmbedding_supported_succ` / `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_magSectorEmbedding_supported_pred` | **Tasaki В§2.5 Theorem 2.3 sector-support bridge and adjacent-sector ladder shifts**: `Ең_tot^(3)` acts diagonally on arbitrary full spin-S vectors, zero-extended `magSumS = M` sector vectors lie in the `Ең_tot^(3)` eigenspace `\|V\|В·N/2 - M`, and an `Ең_tot^(3)` eigenspace vector vanishes outside the matching `magSumS` sector. Such a full eigenspace vector is therefore exactly the zero-extension of its sector restriction, letting later component arguments reuse the sector-embedding APIs for vectors obtained from successor `magSubspaceS` support. Consequently `Ең^-_tot` maps an embedded sector vector supported on `M` to a full vector supported on `M + 1`, while `Ең^+_tot` maps an embedded sector vector supported on `M + 1` back to sector `M`. These are the support halves of the adjacent-sector comparisons used with ladder eigenvalue preservation.
